## File: src/khora/pipelines/manager.py
````python
  1: """Pipeline manager for orchestrating pipeline flows."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  6: from datetime import datetime
  7: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
  8: from uuid import UUID
  9: 
 10: from loguru import logger
 11: 
 12: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 13:     from khora.config import KhoraConfig
 14:     from khora.storage import StorageCoordinator
 15: 
 16: 
 17: @dataclass
 18: class PipelineRun:
 19:     """Information about a pipeline run."""
 20: 
 21:     run_id: str
 22:     pipeline_name: str
 23:     namespace_id: UUID
 24:     status: str  # pending, running, completed, failed
 25:     started_at: datetime | None = None
 26:     completed_at: datetime | None = None
 27:     error: str | None = None
 28:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 29: 
 30: 
 31: class PipelineManager:
 32:     """Manager for running and tracking pipelines.
 33: 
 34:     Orchestrates pipeline flows and tracks their execution
 35:     within the context of a memory namespace.
 36:     """
 37: 
 38:     def __init__(
 39:         self,
 40:         storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None,
 41:         config: KhoraConfig | None = None,
 42:     ) -> None:
 43:         """Initialize the pipeline manager.
 44: 
 45:         Args:
 46:             storage: StorageCoordinator for data access
 47:             config: Khora configuration
 48:         """
 49:         self._storage = storage
 50:         self._config = config
 51:         self._runs: dict[str, PipelineRun] = {}
 52: 
 53:     async def run_pipeline(
 54:         self,
 55:         pipeline_name: str,
 56:         namespace_id: UUID,
 57:         *,
 58:         parameters: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 59:         wait: bool = True,
 60:     ) -> PipelineRun:
 61:         """Run a registered pipeline.
 62: 
 63:         Args:
 64:             pipeline_name: Name of the pipeline to run
 65:             namespace_id: Namespace to run in
 66:             parameters: Pipeline parameters
 67:             wait: Wait for completion
 68: 
 69:         Returns:
 70:             PipelineRun with execution info
 71:         """
 72:         from .registry import get_registry
 73: 
 74:         registry = get_registry()
 75:         pipeline_info = registry.get(pipeline_name)
 76: 
 77:         if not pipeline_info:
 78:             raise ValueError(f"Unknown pipeline: {pipeline_name}")
 79: 
 80:         # Create run record
 81:         import uuid
 82: 
 83:         run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
 84:         run = PipelineRun(
 85:             run_id=run_id,
 86:             pipeline_name=pipeline_name,
 87:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 88:             status="running",
 89:             started_at=datetime.now(),
 90:         )
 91:         self._runs[run_id] = run
 92: 
 93:         logger.info(f"Starting pipeline {pipeline_name} (run_id={run_id})")
 94: 
 95:         try:
 96:             # Prepare parameters
 97:             params = parameters or {}
 98:             params["namespace_id"] = namespace_id
 99:             params["storage"] = self._storage
100:             params["config"] = self._config
101: 
102:             # Run the pipeline
103:             func = pipeline_info.func
104:             result = await func(**params)
105: 
106:             run.status = "completed"
107:             run.completed_at = datetime.now()
108:             run.metadata["result"] = result
109: 
110:             logger.info(f"Pipeline {pipeline_name} completed (run_id={run_id})")
111: 
112:         except Exception as e:
113:             run.status = "failed"
114:             run.error = str(e)
115:             run.completed_at = datetime.now()
116:             logger.error(f"Pipeline {pipeline_name} failed (run_id={run_id}): {e}")
117:             raise
118: 
119:         return run
120: 
121:     async def run_ingestion(
122:         self,
123:         namespace_id: UUID,
124:         documents: list[dict[str, Any]],
125:         *,
126:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
127:     ) -> PipelineRun:
128:         """Run the standard ingestion pipeline.
129: 
130:         Args:
131:             namespace_id: Namespace to ingest into
132:             documents: List of documents to ingest
133:             skill_name: Extraction skill to use
134: 
135:         Returns:
136:             PipelineRun with execution info
137:         """
138:         return await self.run_pipeline(
139:             "ingest",
140:             namespace_id,
141:             parameters={
142:                 "documents": documents,
143:                 "skill_name": skill_name,
144:             },
145:         )
146: 
147:     async def run_sync(
148:         self,
149:         namespace_id: UUID,
150:         source: str,
151:         *,
152:         connector_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
153:     ) -> PipelineRun:
154:         """Run a sync pipeline for an external source.
155: 
156:         Args:
157:             namespace_id: Namespace to sync into
158:             source: Source name (e.g., "github", "notion")
159:             connector_config: Connector configuration
160: 
161:         Returns:
162:             PipelineRun with execution info
163:         """
164:         return await self.run_pipeline(
165:             f"sync_{source}",
166:             namespace_id,
167:             parameters={
168:                 "source": source,
169:                 "connector_config": connector_config or {},
170:             },
171:         )
172: 
173:     def get_run(self, run_id: str) -> PipelineRun | None:
174:         """Get a pipeline run by ID.
175: 
176:         Args:
177:             run_id: Run ID
178: 
179:         Returns:
180:             PipelineRun or None if not found
181:         """
182:         return self._runs.get(run_id)
183: 
184:     def list_runs(
185:         self,
186:         *,
187:         namespace_id: UUID | None = None,
188:         pipeline_name: str | None = None,
189:         status: str | None = None,
190:     ) -> list[PipelineRun]:
191:         """List pipeline runs with optional filtering.
192: 
193:         Args:
194:             namespace_id: Filter by namespace
195:             pipeline_name: Filter by pipeline name
196:             status: Filter by status
197: 
198:         Returns:
199:             List of matching PipelineRun objects
200:         """
201:         runs = list(self._runs.values())
202: 
203:         if namespace_id:
204:             runs = [r for r in runs if r.namespace_id == namespace_id]
205:         if pipeline_name:
206:             runs = [r for r in runs if r.pipeline_name == pipeline_name]
207:         if status:
208:             runs = [r for r in runs if r.status == status]
209: 
210:         return runs
````

## File: src/khora/pipelines/registry.py
````python
  1: """Pipeline registry with decorators for Khora Memory Lake."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from collections.abc import Callable
  6: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  7: from typing import Any
  8: 
  9: from loguru import logger
 10: 
 11: 
 12: @dataclass
 13: class PipelineInfo:
 14:     """Information about a registered pipeline."""
 15: 
 16:     name: str
 17:     description: str
 18:     func: Callable
 19:     tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 20:     version: str = "1.0.0"
 21:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 22: 
 23: 
 24: class PipelineRegistry:
 25:     """Registry for managing pipeline flows.
 26: 
 27:     Provides registration and discovery of pipeline flows
 28:     that can be run by the PipelineManager.
 29:     """
 30: 
 31:     _instance: PipelineRegistry | None = None
 32:     _pipelines: dict[str, PipelineInfo]
 33: 
 34:     def __new__(cls) -> PipelineRegistry:
 35:         """Singleton pattern for global registry."""
 36:         if cls._instance is None:
 37:             cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
 38:             cls._instance._pipelines = {}
 39:         return cls._instance
 40: 
 41:     def register(
 42:         self,
 43:         name: str,
 44:         *,
 45:         description: str = "",
 46:         tags: list[str] | None = None,
 47:         version: str = "1.0.0",
 48:         **metadata: Any,
 49:     ) -> Callable[[Callable], Callable]:
 50:         """Decorator to register a pipeline flow.
 51: 
 52:         Args:
 53:             name: Pipeline name
 54:             description: Pipeline description
 55:             tags: Pipeline tags for categorization
 56:             version: Pipeline version
 57:             **metadata: Additional metadata
 58: 
 59:         Returns:
 60:             Decorator function
 61:         """
 62: 
 63:         def decorator(func: Callable) -> Callable:
 64:             info = PipelineInfo(
 65:                 name=name,
 66:                 description=description or func.__doc__ or "",
 67:                 func=func,
 68:                 tags=tags or [],
 69:                 version=version,
 70:                 metadata=metadata,
 71:             )
 72:             self._pipelines[name] = info
 73:             logger.debug(f"Registered pipeline: {name}")
 74:             return func
 75: 
 76:         return decorator
 77: 
 78:     def get(self, name: str) -> PipelineInfo | None:
 79:         """Get a pipeline by name.
 80: 
 81:         Args:
 82:             name: Pipeline name
 83: 
 84:         Returns:
 85:             PipelineInfo or None if not found
 86:         """
 87:         return self._pipelines.get(name)
 88: 
 89:     def list_pipelines(self) -> list[str]:
 90:         """List all registered pipeline names.
 91: 
 92:         Returns:
 93:             List of pipeline names
 94:         """
 95:         return list(self._pipelines.keys())
 96: 
 97:     def get_by_tag(self, tag: str) -> list[PipelineInfo]:
 98:         """Get pipelines by tag.
 99: 
100:         Args:
101:             tag: Tag to filter by
102: 
103:         Returns:
104:             List of matching PipelineInfo objects
105:         """
106:         return [p for p in self._pipelines.values() if tag in p.tags]
107: 
108:     def all_pipelines(self) -> list[PipelineInfo]:
109:         """Get all registered pipelines.
110: 
111:         Returns:
112:             List of PipelineInfo objects
113:         """
114:         return list(self._pipelines.values())
115: 
116:     def unregister(self, name: str) -> bool:
117:         """Unregister a pipeline.
118: 
119:         Args:
120:             name: Pipeline name to unregister
121: 
122:         Returns:
123:             True if removed, False if not found
124:         """
125:         if name in self._pipelines:
126:             del self._pipelines[name]
127:             logger.debug(f"Unregistered pipeline: {name}")
128:             return True
129:         return False
130: 
131: 
132: # Global registry instance
133: _registry = PipelineRegistry()
134: 
135: 
136: def pipeline(
137:     name: str,
138:     *,
139:     description: str = "",
140:     tags: list[str] | None = None,
141:     version: str = "1.0.0",
142:     **metadata: Any,
143: ) -> Callable[[Callable], Callable]:
144:     """Decorator to register a pipeline flow.
145: 
146:     Usage:
147:         @pipeline("my_pipeline", description="My pipeline", tags=["ingestion"])
148:         async def my_pipeline(param: str):
149:             ...
150: 
151:     Args:
152:         name: Pipeline name
153:         description: Pipeline description
154:         tags: Pipeline tags
155:         version: Pipeline version
156:         **metadata: Additional metadata
157: 
158:     Returns:
159:         Decorator function
160:     """
161:     return _registry.register(name, description=description, tags=tags, version=version, **metadata)
162: 
163: 
164: def get_registry() -> PipelineRegistry:
165:     """Get the global pipeline registry.
166: 
167:     Returns:
168:         PipelineRegistry instance
169:     """
170:     return _registry
````

## File: src/khora/query/cache.py
````python
  1: """Query result cache with TTL for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Provides an in-memory LRU cache that avoids re-executing identical
  4: queries within a configurable time-to-live window.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import asyncio
 10: from datetime import datetime, timedelta
 11: from hashlib import sha256
 12: from typing import Any
 13: from uuid import UUID
 14: 
 15: 
 16: class QueryCache:
 17:     """LRU cache for query results with TTL."""
 18: 
 19:     def __init__(self, max_size: int = 1000, ttl_seconds: int = 300) -> None:
 20:         """Initialize the cache.
 21: 
 22:         Args:
 23:             max_size: Maximum cached entries before eviction.
 24:             ttl_seconds: Time-to-live for each entry in seconds.
 25:         """
 26:         self._cache: dict[str, tuple[datetime, Any]] = {}
 27:         self._max_size = max_size
 28:         self._ttl = timedelta(seconds=ttl_seconds)
 29:         self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
 30:         self._hits = 0
 31:         self._misses = 0
 32: 
 33:     @staticmethod
 34:     def _make_key(query: str, namespace_id: UUID, mode: str) -> str:
 35:         return sha256(f"{query}:{namespace_id}:{mode}".encode()).hexdigest()
 36: 
 37:     async def get(self, query: str, namespace_id: UUID, mode: str) -> Any | None:
 38:         """Look up a cached result.
 39: 
 40:         Returns None on miss or expiry.
 41:         """
 42:         key = self._make_key(query, namespace_id, mode)
 43:         async with self._lock:
 44:             entry = self._cache.get(key)
 45:             if entry is not None:
 46:                 timestamp, result = entry
 47:                 if datetime.now() - timestamp < self._ttl:
 48:                     self._hits += 1
 49:                     self._record_cache_event(True, namespace_id)
 50:                     return result
 51:                 del self._cache[key]
 52:             self._misses += 1
 53:             self._record_cache_event(False, namespace_id)
 54:             return None
 55: 
 56:     @staticmethod
 57:     def _record_cache_event(hit: bool, namespace_id: UUID) -> None:
 58:         """Record a cache hit/miss to telemetry."""
 59:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
 60: 
 61:         get_collector().record_llm_call(
 62:             operation="query_cache",
 63:             cache_hit=hit,
 64:             latency_ms=0.0,
 65:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 66:             metadata={"cache_type": "query"},
 67:         )
 68: 
 69:     async def set(self, query: str, namespace_id: UUID, mode: str, result: Any) -> None:
 70:         """Store a result in the cache, evicting the oldest entry if full."""
 71:         key = self._make_key(query, namespace_id, mode)
 72:         async with self._lock:
 73:             if len(self._cache) >= self._max_size:
 74:                 oldest_key = min(self._cache, key=lambda k: self._cache[k][0])
 75:                 del self._cache[oldest_key]
 76:             self._cache[key] = (datetime.now(), result)
 77: 
 78:     async def invalidate(self, namespace_id: UUID | None = None) -> int:
 79:         """Remove entries, optionally filtered by namespace.
 80: 
 81:         Args:
 82:             namespace_id: If provided, only remove entries whose key
 83:                           was generated with this namespace. If None,
 84:                           clears the entire cache.
 85: 
 86:         Returns:
 87:             Number of entries removed.
 88:         """
 89:         async with self._lock:
 90:             if namespace_id is None:
 91:                 count = len(self._cache)
 92:                 self._cache.clear()
 93:                 return count
 94:             # Namespace is hashed into the key, so we can't filter efficiently.
 95:             # Clear everything as a safe fallback.
 96:             count = len(self._cache)
 97:             self._cache.clear()
 98:             return count
 99: 
100:     @property
101:     def stats(self) -> dict[str, int]:
102:         """Return hit/miss statistics."""
103:         return {
104:             "size": len(self._cache),
105:             "hits": self._hits,
106:             "misses": self._misses,
107:         }
````

## File: src/khora/query/hyde.py
````python
 1: """Hypothetical Document Embeddings (HyDE) for query expansion.
 2: 
 3: Generates a hypothetical answer document using an LLM, embeds it,
 4: and averages with the original query embedding to improve recall
 5: for queries that use different vocabulary than stored documents.
 6: """
 7: 
 8: from __future__ import annotations
 9: 
10: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
11: 
12: import numpy as np
13: from loguru import logger
14: 
15: if TYPE_CHECKING:
16:     from khora.config.llm import LiteLLMConfig
17:     from khora.extraction.embedders import Embedder
18: 
19: 
20: class HyDEExpander:
21:     """Expands query embeddings using Hypothetical Document Embeddings."""
22: 
23:     def __init__(
24:         self,
25:         embedder: Embedder,
26:         llm_config: LiteLLMConfig | None = None,
27:         *,
28:         num_hypotheticals: int = 1,
29:     ) -> None:
30:         self._embedder = embedder
31:         self._llm_config = llm_config
32:         self._num_hypotheticals = num_hypotheticals
33: 
34:     async def generate_hypothetical(self, query: str) -> str:
35:         """Generate a hypothetical document that answers the query."""
36:         from khora.config.llm import acompletion
37: 
38:         system_prompt = (
39:             "You are a knowledgeable assistant. Given a question, write a short, "
40:             "factual passage (2-3 sentences) that directly answers it. "
41:             "Do not include any preamble or meta-commentary, just the answer content."
42:         )
43: 
44:         return await acompletion(
45:             query,
46:             self._llm_config,
47:             system_prompt=system_prompt,
48:             temperature=0.7,
49:             max_tokens=200,
50:         )
51: 
52:     async def expand_query_embedding(
53:         self,
54:         query: str,
55:         query_embedding: list[float],
56:     ) -> list[float]:
57:         """Expand a query embedding by averaging with hypothetical doc embeddings.
58: 
59:         On any failure, returns the original embedding unchanged.
60:         """
61:         from khora.telemetry.instrument import pipeline_stage
62: 
63:         try:
64:             async with pipeline_stage("query", "hyde", extra_metadata={"num_hypotheticals": self._num_hypotheticals}):
65:                 hypotheticals: list[str] = []
66:                 for _ in range(self._num_hypotheticals):
67:                     doc = await self.generate_hypothetical(query)
68:                     hypotheticals.append(doc)
69: 
70:                 # Embed hypothetical documents
71:                 hyde_embeddings: list[list[float]] = []
72:                 for doc in hypotheticals:
73:                     emb = await self._embedder.embed(doc)
74:                     hyde_embeddings.append(emb)
75: 
76:                 # Average: original query embedding + hypothetical embeddings
77:                 all_embeddings = [query_embedding, *hyde_embeddings]
78:                 avg = np.mean(all_embeddings, axis=0)
79:                 return avg.tolist()
80: 
81:         except Exception as e:
82:             logger.warning(f"HyDE expansion failed, using original embedding: {e}")
83:             return query_embedding
````

## File: src/khora/query/keyword.py
````python
  1: """Keyword search module for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Provides BM25-based keyword search for improved recall alongside
  4: vector search.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import math
 10: import re
 11: from collections import Counter
 12: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 13: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
 14: 
 15: from loguru import logger
 16: 
 17: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 18:     from khora.core.models import Chunk
 19: 
 20: 
 21: # Common English stopwords
 22: STOPWORDS = {
 23:     "a",
 24:     "an",
 25:     "and",
 26:     "are",
 27:     "as",
 28:     "at",
 29:     "be",
 30:     "by",
 31:     "for",
 32:     "from",
 33:     "has",
 34:     "he",
 35:     "in",
 36:     "is",
 37:     "it",
 38:     "its",
 39:     "of",
 40:     "on",
 41:     "that",
 42:     "the",
 43:     "to",
 44:     "was",
 45:     "were",
 46:     "will",
 47:     "with",
 48:     "the",
 49:     "this",
 50:     "but",
 51:     "they",
 52:     "have",
 53:     "had",
 54:     "what",
 55:     "when",
 56:     "where",
 57:     "who",
 58:     "which",
 59:     "why",
 60:     "how",
 61:     "all",
 62:     "each",
 63:     "every",
 64:     "both",
 65:     "few",
 66:     "more",
 67:     "most",
 68:     "other",
 69:     "some",
 70:     "such",
 71:     "no",
 72:     "nor",
 73:     "not",
 74:     "only",
 75:     "own",
 76:     "same",
 77:     "so",
 78:     "than",
 79:     "too",
 80:     "very",
 81:     "just",
 82:     "can",
 83:     "should",
 84:     "now",
 85:     "i",
 86:     "you",
 87:     "we",
 88:     "our",
 89:     "your",
 90:     "my",
 91:     "me",
 92:     "him",
 93:     "her",
 94:     "them",
 95:     "their",
 96:     "been",
 97:     "being",
 98:     "do",
 99:     "does",
100:     "did",
101:     "doing",
102:     "would",
103:     "could",
104:     "if",
105:     "then",
106:     "else",
107:     "or",
108:     "because",
109:     "until",
110:     "while",
111:     "am",
112: }
113: 
114: 
115: def tokenize(text: str, use_stemming: bool = True, remove_stopwords: bool = True) -> list[str]:
116:     """Tokenize text for keyword search.
117: 
118:     Args:
119:         text: Text to tokenize
120:         use_stemming: Apply basic stemming
121:         remove_stopwords: Remove common stopwords
122: 
123:     Returns:
124:         List of tokens
125:     """
126:     # Lowercase and split on non-alphanumeric
127:     tokens = re.findall(r"\b[a-zA-Z0-9]+\b", text.lower())
128: 
129:     # Remove stopwords
130:     if remove_stopwords:
131:         tokens = [t for t in tokens if t not in STOPWORDS]
132: 
133:     # Basic stemming (simple suffix removal)
134:     if use_stemming:
135:         tokens = [_basic_stem(t) for t in tokens]
136: 
137:     # Filter short tokens
138:     tokens = [t for t in tokens if len(t) > 2]
139: 
140:     return tokens
141: 
142: 
143: def _basic_stem(word: str) -> str:
144:     """Basic Porter-like stemming.
145: 
146:     Args:
147:         word: Word to stem
148: 
149:     Returns:
150:         Stemmed word
151:     """
152:     # Simple suffix removal
153:     suffixes = ["ing", "ed", "tion", "ness", "ment", "able", "ible", "ful", "less", "ly", "er", "est", "es", "s"]
154: 
155:     for suffix in suffixes:
156:         if word.endswith(suffix) and len(word) > len(suffix) + 2:
157:             return word[: -len(suffix)]
158: 
159:     return word
160: 
161: 
162: @dataclass
163: class BM25Index:
164:     """BM25 index for keyword search.
165: 
166:     Implements the BM25 ranking function for full-text search.
167:     """
168: 
169:     # BM25 parameters
170:     k1: float = 1.5
171:     b: float = 0.75
172: 
173:     # Index data
174:     doc_lengths: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
175:     doc_freqs: dict[str, Counter] = field(default_factory=dict)  # doc_id -> term -> count
176:     term_doc_freqs: Counter = field(default_factory=Counter)  # term -> num_docs
177:     avg_doc_length: float = 0.0
178:     total_docs: int = 0
179: 
180:     # Inverted index: term -> set of doc_ids containing that term
181:     _inverted_index: dict[str, set[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
182: 
183:     # Running sum for O(1) average length updates
184:     _total_length: int = 0
185: 
186:     # Stemming and stopwords
187:     use_stemming: bool = True
188:     remove_stopwords: bool = True
189: 
190:     def add_document(self, doc_id: str, text: str) -> None:
191:         """Add a document to the index.
192: 
193:         Args:
194:             doc_id: Document identifier
195:             text: Document text
196:         """
197:         tokens = tokenize(text, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
198: 
199:         self.doc_lengths[doc_id] = len(tokens)
200:         self.doc_freqs[doc_id] = Counter(tokens)
201: 
202:         # Update term document frequencies and inverted index
203:         for term in set(tokens):
204:             self.term_doc_freqs[term] += 1
205:             if term not in self._inverted_index:
206:                 self._inverted_index[term] = set()
207:             self._inverted_index[term].add(doc_id)
208: 
209:         self.total_docs += 1
210:         self._total_length += len(tokens)
211:         self._update_avg_length()
212: 
213:     def add_documents(self, documents: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> None:
214:         """Add multiple documents to the index.
215: 
216:         Args:
217:             documents: List of (doc_id, text) tuples
218:         """
219:         for doc_id, text in documents:
220:             tokens = tokenize(text, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
221:             self.doc_lengths[doc_id] = len(tokens)
222:             self.doc_freqs[doc_id] = Counter(tokens)
223:             for term in set(tokens):
224:                 self.term_doc_freqs[term] += 1
225:                 if term not in self._inverted_index:
226:                     self._inverted_index[term] = set()
227:                 self._inverted_index[term].add(doc_id)
228:             self.total_docs += 1
229:             self._total_length += len(tokens)
230: 
231:         self._update_avg_length()
232: 
233:     def _update_avg_length(self) -> None:
234:         """Update average document length using running sum (O(1))."""
235:         if self.total_docs > 0:
236:             self.avg_doc_length = self._total_length / self.total_docs
237: 
238:     def _idf(self, term: str) -> float:
239:         """Calculate inverse document frequency for a term.
240: 
241:         Args:
242:             term: Term to calculate IDF for
243: 
244:         Returns:
245:             IDF score
246:         """
247:         n = self.total_docs
248:         df = self.term_doc_freqs.get(term, 0)
249:         if df == 0:
250:             return 0.0
251:         return math.log((n - df + 0.5) / (df + 0.5) + 1)
252: 
253:     def score(self, query: str, doc_id: str) -> float:
254:         """Calculate BM25 score for a query-document pair.
255: 
256:         Args:
257:             query: Query text
258:             doc_id: Document identifier
259: 
260:         Returns:
261:             BM25 score
262:         """
263:         if doc_id not in self.doc_freqs:
264:             return 0.0
265: 
266:         query_tokens = tokenize(query, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
267:         doc_freq = self.doc_freqs[doc_id]
268:         doc_len = self.doc_lengths[doc_id]
269: 
270:         score = 0.0
271:         for term in query_tokens:
272:             if term not in doc_freq:
273:                 continue
274: 
275:             tf = doc_freq[term]
276:             idf = self._idf(term)
277: 
278:             # BM25 scoring formula
279:             numerator = tf * (self.k1 + 1)
280:             denominator = tf + self.k1 * (1 - self.b + self.b * doc_len / max(self.avg_doc_length, 1))
281:             score += idf * numerator / denominator
282: 
283:         return score
284: 
285:     def search(
286:         self,
287:         query: str,
288:         limit: int = 10,
289:         min_score: float = 0.0,
290:     ) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
291:         """Search the index for relevant documents.
292: 
293:         Uses the inverted index to only score documents containing at
294:         least one query term, reducing complexity from O(D*Q) to
295:         O(postings*Q).
296: 
297:         Args:
298:             query: Query text
299:             limit: Maximum results to return
300:             min_score: Minimum score threshold
301: 
302:         Returns:
303:             List of (doc_id, score) tuples sorted by score
304:         """
305:         query_tokens = tokenize(query, self.use_stemming, self.remove_stopwords)
306:         if not query_tokens:
307:             return []
308: 
309:         # Gather candidate doc IDs via inverted index
310:         candidate_doc_ids: set[str] = set()
311:         for term in query_tokens:
312:             candidate_doc_ids.update(self._inverted_index.get(term, set()))
313: 
314:         results = []
315:         for doc_id in candidate_doc_ids:
316:             s = self.score(query, doc_id)
317:             if s > min_score:
318:                 results.append((doc_id, s))
319: 
320:         # Sort by score descending
321:         results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
322:         return results[:limit]
323: 
324: 
325: class KeywordSearcher:
326:     """Keyword search for chunks.
327: 
328:     Provides BM25-based search over chunk content.
329:     """
330: 
331:     def __init__(
332:         self,
333:         use_stemming: bool = True,
334:         remove_stopwords: bool = True,
335:         k1: float = 1.5,
336:         b: float = 0.75,
337:     ) -> None:
338:         """Initialize the keyword searcher.
339: 
340:         Args:
341:             use_stemming: Apply stemming
342:             remove_stopwords: Remove stopwords
343:             k1: BM25 k1 parameter
344:             b: BM25 b parameter
345:         """
346:         self._index = BM25Index(
347:             k1=k1,
348:             b=b,
349:             use_stemming=use_stemming,
350:             remove_stopwords=remove_stopwords,
351:         )
352:         self._chunks: dict[str, Chunk] = {}  # doc_id -> Chunk
353: 
354:     def index_chunks(self, chunks: list[Chunk]) -> None:
355:         """Index chunks for keyword search.
356: 
357:         Args:
358:             chunks: Chunks to index
359:         """
360:         for chunk in chunks:
361:             doc_id = str(chunk.id)
362:             self._chunks[doc_id] = chunk
363:             self._index.add_document(doc_id, chunk.content)
364: 
365:         logger.debug(f"Indexed {len(chunks)} chunks for keyword search")
366: 
367:     def search(
368:         self,
369:         query: str,
370:         limit: int = 10,
371:         min_score: float = 0.0,
372:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
373:         """Search for chunks matching the query.
374: 
375:         Args:
376:             query: Query text
377:             limit: Maximum results
378:             min_score: Minimum BM25 score
379: 
380:         Returns:
381:             List of (chunk, score) tuples
382:         """
383:         results = self._index.search(query, limit=limit, min_score=min_score)
384: 
385:         chunk_results = []
386:         for doc_id, score in results:
387:             if doc_id in self._chunks:
388:                 chunk_results.append((self._chunks[doc_id], score))
389: 
390:         return chunk_results
391: 
392:     def search_with_keywords(
393:         self,
394:         keywords: list[str],
395:         limit: int = 10,
396:         min_score: float = 0.0,
397:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
398:         """Search using pre-extracted keywords.
399: 
400:         Args:
401:             keywords: Keywords to search for
402:             limit: Maximum results
403:             min_score: Minimum BM25 score
404: 
405:         Returns:
406:             List of (chunk, score) tuples
407:         """
408:         # Join keywords into query
409:         query = " ".join(keywords)
410:         return self.search(query, limit=limit, min_score=min_score)
411: 
412: 
413: async def build_keyword_index(
414:     chunks: list[Chunk],
415:     use_stemming: bool = True,
416:     remove_stopwords: bool = True,
417: ) -> KeywordSearcher:
418:     """Build a keyword search index from chunks.
419: 
420:     Args:
421:         chunks: Chunks to index
422:         use_stemming: Apply stemming
423:         remove_stopwords: Remove stopwords
424: 
425:     Returns:
426:         KeywordSearcher instance
427:     """
428:     searcher = KeywordSearcher(
429:         use_stemming=use_stemming,
430:         remove_stopwords=remove_stopwords,
431:     )
432:     searcher.index_chunks(chunks)
433:     return searcher
434: 
435: 
436: def normalize_bm25_score(score: float, max_score: float = 10.0) -> float:
437:     """Normalize BM25 score to 0-1 range.
438: 
439:     Uses sigmoid-like normalization.
440: 
441:     Args:
442:         score: Raw BM25 score
443:         max_score: Expected max score for normalization
444: 
445:     Returns:
446:         Normalized score between 0 and 1
447:     """
448:     if score <= 0:
449:         return 0.0
450:     return min(1.0, score / max_score)
````

## File: src/khora/query/metrics.py
````python
  1: """Search quality metrics for observability.
  2: 
  3: Captures per-stage latency, result counts, score distributions,
  4: and feature flags for every query execution.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import time
 10: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 11: from typing import Any
 12: 
 13: from loguru import logger
 14: 
 15: 
 16: @dataclass
 17: class StageTimer:
 18:     """Tracks start/end of a pipeline stage."""
 19: 
 20:     _start: float = 0.0
 21:     _end: float = 0.0
 22: 
 23:     def start(self) -> None:
 24:         self._start = time.perf_counter()
 25: 
 26:     def stop(self) -> None:
 27:         self._end = time.perf_counter()
 28: 
 29:     @property
 30:     def elapsed_ms(self) -> float:
 31:         if self._end <= self._start:
 32:             return 0.0
 33:         return (self._end - self._start) * 1000
 34: 
 35: 
 36: @dataclass
 37: class SearchMetrics:
 38:     """Metrics collected during a single query execution."""
 39: 
 40:     # Stage timers
 41:     understanding_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 42:     linking_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 43:     search_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 44:     fusion_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 45:     reranking_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 46:     total_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 47: 
 48:     # Multi-stage pipeline timers
 49:     stage1_recall_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 50:     stage2_normalize_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 51:     stage3_filter_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 52:     stage4_rerank_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 53:     stage5_diversity_timer: StageTimer = field(default_factory=StageTimer)
 54: 
 55:     # Result counts per source
 56:     vector_chunk_count: int = 0
 57:     graph_chunk_count: int = 0
 58:     keyword_chunk_count: int = 0
 59:     vector_entity_count: int = 0
 60:     graph_entity_count: int = 0
 61: 
 62:     # Post-fusion counts
 63:     fused_chunk_count: int = 0
 64:     fused_entity_count: int = 0
 65:     final_chunk_count: int = 0
 66:     final_entity_count: int = 0
 67: 
 68:     # Multi-stage pipeline counts
 69:     stage1_candidate_count: int = 0
 70:     stage2_normalized_count: int = 0
 71:     stage3_filtered_count: int = 0
 72:     stage4_reranked_count: int = 0
 73:     stage5_final_count: int = 0
 74: 
 75:     # Multi-stage enabled flag
 76:     multi_stage_enabled: bool = False
 77: 
 78:     # Score distributions (after fusion)
 79:     chunk_score_min: float = 0.0
 80:     chunk_score_max: float = 0.0
 81:     chunk_score_mean: float = 0.0
 82: 
 83:     # Feature flags active during this query
 84:     features: dict[str, bool] = field(default_factory=dict)
 85: 
 86:     def set_chunk_scores(self, scores: list[float]) -> None:
 87:         """Compute score statistics from a list of fused chunk scores."""
 88:         if not scores:
 89:             return
 90:         self.chunk_score_min = min(scores)
 91:         self.chunk_score_max = max(scores)
 92:         self.chunk_score_mean = sum(scores) / len(scores)
 93: 
 94:     def log(self) -> None:
 95:         """Emit a structured log line with all metrics."""
 96:         log_data = {
 97:             "latency_total_ms": round(self.total_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
 98:             "latency_understanding_ms": round(self.understanding_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
 99:             "latency_linking_ms": round(self.linking_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
100:             "latency_search_ms": round(self.search_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
101:             "latency_fusion_ms": round(self.fusion_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
102:             "latency_reranking_ms": round(self.reranking_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
103:             "vector_chunks": self.vector_chunk_count,
104:             "graph_chunks": self.graph_chunk_count,
105:             "keyword_chunks": self.keyword_chunk_count,
106:             "fused_chunks": self.fused_chunk_count,
107:             "final_chunks": self.final_chunk_count,
108:             "final_entities": self.final_entity_count,
109:             "score_min": round(self.chunk_score_min, 4),
110:             "score_max": round(self.chunk_score_max, 4),
111:             "score_mean": round(self.chunk_score_mean, 4),
112:         }
113: 
114:         # Add multi-stage metrics if enabled
115:         if self.multi_stage_enabled:
116:             log_data.update(
117:                 {
118:                     "multi_stage": True,
119:                     "stage1_recall_ms": round(self.stage1_recall_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
120:                     "stage2_normalize_ms": round(self.stage2_normalize_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
121:                     "stage3_filter_ms": round(self.stage3_filter_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
122:                     "stage4_rerank_ms": round(self.stage4_rerank_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
123:                     "stage5_diversity_ms": round(self.stage5_diversity_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
124:                     "stage1_candidates": self.stage1_candidate_count,
125:                     "stage3_filtered": self.stage3_filtered_count,
126:                     "stage4_reranked": self.stage4_reranked_count,
127:                     "stage5_final": self.stage5_final_count,
128:                 }
129:             )
130: 
131:         log_data.update(self.features)
132:         logger.bind(search_metrics=True).info("Search query completed", **log_data)
133: 
134:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
135:         """Serialize metrics for inclusion in API response metadata."""
136:         result = {
137:             "latency_ms": {
138:                 "total": round(self.total_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
139:                 "understanding": round(self.understanding_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
140:                 "linking": round(self.linking_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
141:                 "search": round(self.search_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
142:                 "fusion": round(self.fusion_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
143:                 "reranking": round(self.reranking_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
144:             },
145:             "counts": {
146:                 "vector_chunks": self.vector_chunk_count,
147:                 "graph_chunks": self.graph_chunk_count,
148:                 "keyword_chunks": self.keyword_chunk_count,
149:                 "vector_entities": self.vector_entity_count,
150:                 "graph_entities": self.graph_entity_count,
151:                 "fused_chunks": self.fused_chunk_count,
152:                 "fused_entities": self.fused_entity_count,
153:                 "final_chunks": self.final_chunk_count,
154:                 "final_entities": self.final_entity_count,
155:             },
156:             "scores": {
157:                 "min": round(self.chunk_score_min, 4),
158:                 "max": round(self.chunk_score_max, 4),
159:                 "mean": round(self.chunk_score_mean, 4),
160:             },
161:             "features": self.features,
162:         }
163: 
164:         # Add multi-stage metrics if enabled
165:         if self.multi_stage_enabled:
166:             result["multi_stage"] = {
167:                 "enabled": True,
168:                 "latency_ms": {
169:                     "stage1_recall": round(self.stage1_recall_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
170:                     "stage2_normalize": round(self.stage2_normalize_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
171:                     "stage3_filter": round(self.stage3_filter_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
172:                     "stage4_rerank": round(self.stage4_rerank_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
173:                     "stage5_diversity": round(self.stage5_diversity_timer.elapsed_ms, 2),
174:                 },
175:                 "counts": {
176:                     "stage1_candidates": self.stage1_candidate_count,
177:                     "stage2_normalized": self.stage2_normalized_count,
178:                     "stage3_filtered": self.stage3_filtered_count,
179:                     "stage4_reranked": self.stage4_reranked_count,
180:                     "stage5_final": self.stage5_final_count,
181:                 },
182:             }
183: 
184:         return result
````

````python
 1: """Storage backend implementations for Khora Memory Lake."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from .base import (
 6:     EventStoreProtocol,
 7:     GraphBackendProtocol,
 8:     RelationalBackendProtocol,
 9:     VectorBackendProtocol,
10: )
11: from .mixins import GraphBackendBase, VectorBackendBase
12: from .neo4j import Neo4jBackend
13: from .pgvector import PgVectorBackend
14: from .postgresql import PostgreSQLBackend
15: 
16: # Lazy imports for optional backends
17: try:
18:     from .kuzu import KuzuBackend
19: except ImportError:
20:     KuzuBackend = None  # type: ignore[assignment,misc]
21: 
22: try:
23:     from .memgraph import MemgraphBackend
24: except ImportError:
25:     MemgraphBackend = None  # type: ignore[assignment,misc]
26: 
27: try:
28:     from .arcadedb import ArcadeDBBackend
29: except ImportError:
30:     ArcadeDBBackend = None  # type: ignore[assignment,misc]

33:     # Protocols
34:     "RelationalBackendProtocol",
35:     "VectorBackendProtocol",
36:     "GraphBackendProtocol",
37:     "EventStoreProtocol",
38:     # Mixins
39:     "GraphBackendBase",
40:     "VectorBackendBase",
41:     # Implementations
42:     "PostgreSQLBackend",
43:     "PgVectorBackend",
44:     "Neo4jBackend",
45:     "KuzuBackend",
46:     "MemgraphBackend",
47:     "ArcadeDBBackend",
48: ]
````

## File: src/khora/storage/backends/mixins.py
````python
  1: """Mixin base classes for storage backends.
  2: 
  3: Provide default implementations for batch/aggregate operations so new
  4: backends get them for free and can override with optimized versions.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import json
 10: from datetime import datetime
 11: from typing import Any
 12: from uuid import UUID
 13: 
 14: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 15: # Serialization helpers (shared across graph backends)
 16: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 17: 
 18: 
 19: def serialize_dict(value: dict[str, Any] | None) -> str | None:
 20:     """Serialize a dict to JSON string for property-store backends.
 21: 
 22:     Graph databases typically only support primitive property values;
 23:     nested dicts must be stored as JSON strings.
 24:     """
 25:     if value is None:
 26:         return None
 27:     return json.dumps(value)
 28: 
 29: 
 30: def deserialize_dict(value: str | dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
 31:     """Deserialize a JSON string back to dict.
 32: 
 33:     Handles both string (serialized) and dict (legacy/native) values.
 34:     """
 35:     if value is None:
 36:         return {}
 37:     if isinstance(value, dict):
 38:         return value
 39:     try:
 40:         return json.loads(value)
 41:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
 42:         return {}
 43: 
 44: 
 45: def element_to_dict(element: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
 46:     """Safely convert a graph element (Node, Relationship, or raw value) to a dict.
 47: 
 48:     Handles Neo4j Node/Relationship objects, Kùzu results, plain dicts, etc.
 49:     """
 50:     if isinstance(element, dict):
 51:         return element
 52:     # neo4j.graph.Node / Relationship expose _properties
 53:     if hasattr(element, "_properties"):
 54:         return dict(element._properties)
 55:     # Some driver versions make Node/Relationship a Mapping
 56:     if hasattr(element, "items"):
 57:         try:
 58:             return dict(element.items())
 59:         except Exception:
 60:             pass
 61:     # Last resort — avoid crashing on unexpected types
 62:     return {"_raw": str(element)}
 63: 
 64: 
 65: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 66: # Parse helpers for record→domain model conversions
 67: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 68: 
 69: 
 70: def parse_uuid(val: Any) -> UUID:
 71:     """Parse a UUID from a string or UUID."""
 72:     if isinstance(val, UUID):
 73:         return val
 74:     return UUID(str(val))
 75: 
 76: 
 77: def parse_uuid_list(val: list | None) -> list[UUID]:
 78:     """Parse a list of UUIDs."""
 79:     if not val:
 80:         return []
 81:     return [parse_uuid(v) for v in val]
 82: 
 83: 
 84: def parse_datetime(val: str | datetime | None, default: datetime | None = None) -> datetime | None:
 85:     """Parse an ISO datetime string or pass through a datetime."""
 86:     if val is None:
 87:         return default
 88:     if isinstance(val, datetime):
 89:         return val
 90:     return datetime.fromisoformat(val)
 91: 
 92: 
 93: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 94: # GraphBackendBase
 95: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 96: 
 97: 
 98: class GraphBackendBase:
 99:     """Mixin providing default N+1 implementations for batch/aggregate graph ops.
100: 
101:     Concrete graph backends inherit this alongside their protocol implementation.
102:     Backends that support native batch queries should override these methods.
103:     """
104: 
105:     async def get_entities_batch(self, entity_ids: list[UUID]) -> dict[UUID, Entity]:  # noqa: F821
106:         """Default N+1 implementation — subclasses should override for efficiency."""
107:         if not entity_ids:
108:             return {}
109:         result: dict[UUID, Any] = {}
110:         for eid in entity_ids:
111:             entity = await self.get_entity(eid)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
112:             if entity is not None:
113:                 result[eid] = entity
114:         return result
115: 
116:     async def get_neighborhoods_batch(
117:         self,
118:         entity_ids: list[UUID],
119:         *,
120:         depth: int = 1,
121:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
122:         limit_per_entity: int = 20,
123:     ) -> dict[UUID, dict[str, Any]]:
124:         """Default N+1 implementation — subclasses should override for efficiency."""
125:         if not entity_ids:
126:             return {}
127:         result: dict[UUID, dict[str, Any]] = {}
128:         for eid in entity_ids:
129:             neighborhood = await self.get_neighborhood(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
130:                 eid,
131:                 depth=depth,
132:                 relationship_types=relationship_types,
133:                 limit=limit_per_entity,
134:             )
135:             result[eid] = neighborhood
136:         return result
137: 
138:     async def count_entities(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
139:         """Default implementation using list_entities — subclasses should override."""
140:         entities = await self.list_entities(namespace_id, limit=100_000)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
141:         return len(entities)
142: 
143: 
144: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145: # VectorBackendBase
146: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147: 
148: 
149: class VectorBackendBase:
150:     """Mixin providing default implementations for aggregate vector ops.
151: 
152:     Concrete vector backends inherit this alongside their protocol implementation.
153:     """
154: 
155:     async def count_chunks(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
156:         """Default implementation — subclasses should override for efficiency."""
157:         # Fallback: this is intentionally inefficient; override in real backends
158:         return 0
159: 
160:     async def list_chunks(
161:         self,
162:         namespace_id: UUID,
163:         *,
164:         limit: int = 100,
165:         offset: int = 0,
166:     ) -> list:
167:         """Default implementation — subclasses should override."""
168:         return []
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/config.py
````python
 1: """Telemetry configuration."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: import os
 6: 
 7: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
 8: 
 9: 
10: class TelemetryConfig(BaseModel):
11:     """Configuration for the telemetry subsystem."""
12: 
13:     database_url: str | None = Field(
14:         default=None,
15:         description="PostgreSQL URL for telemetry database",
16:     )
17:     service_name: str = Field(
18:         default="khora",
19:         description="Service name tag for telemetry events",
20:     )
21:     flush_interval_seconds: float = Field(default=5.0, description="Seconds between background flushes")
22:     flush_threshold: int = Field(default=100, description="Buffer size that triggers an immediate flush")
23: 
24:     @classmethod
25:     def from_env(cls) -> TelemetryConfig:
26:         """Build config from environment variables."""
27:         return cls(
28:             database_url=os.getenv("KHORA_TELEMETRY_DATABASE_URL"),
29:             service_name=os.getenv("KHORA_TELEMETRY_SERVICE_NAME", "khora"),
30:         )
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/context.py
````python
 1: """Trace propagation via contextvars.
 2: 
 3: Provides request-level trace_id and parent_event_id propagation through
 4: the async call stack without threading parameters through every function.
 5: 
 6: Usage::
 7: 
 8:     from khora.telemetry.context import set_trace_id, ensure_trace_id
 9: 
10:     # At request entry point (remember/recall/chat):
11:     set_trace_id(uuid4())
12: 
13:     # Downstream code automatically inherits trace_id:
14:     trace_id = get_trace_id()  # returns the UUID set above
15: """
16: 
17: from __future__ import annotations
18: 
19: from contextvars import ContextVar
20: from uuid import UUID, uuid4
21: 
22: _trace_id_var: ContextVar[UUID | None] = ContextVar("khora_trace_id", default=None)
23: _parent_event_id_var: ContextVar[int | None] = ContextVar("khora_parent_event_id", default=None)
24: 
25: 
26: def get_trace_id() -> UUID | None:
27:     """Get the current trace ID, or None if not set."""
28:     return _trace_id_var.get()
29: 
30: 
31: def set_trace_id(trace_id: UUID | None) -> None:
32:     """Set the trace ID for the current async context."""
33:     _trace_id_var.set(trace_id)
34: 
35: 
36: def ensure_trace_id() -> UUID:
37:     """Get or create a trace ID for the current context."""
38:     tid = _trace_id_var.get()
39:     if tid is None:
40:         tid = uuid4()
41:         _trace_id_var.set(tid)
42:     return tid
43: 
44: 
45: def clear_trace_id() -> None:
46:     """Clear the trace ID (call at end of request)."""
47:     _trace_id_var.set(None)
48: 
49: 
50: def get_parent_event_id() -> int | None:
51:     """Get the current parent event ID, or None."""
52:     return _parent_event_id_var.get()
53: 
54: 
55: def set_parent_event_id(event_id: int | None) -> None:
56:     """Set the parent event ID for child event linking."""
57:     _parent_event_id_var.set(event_id)
58: 
59: 
60: def clear_parent_event_id() -> None:
61:     """Clear the parent event ID."""
62:     _parent_event_id_var.set(None)
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/noop.py
````python
 1: """No-op telemetry collector -- zero overhead when telemetry is disabled."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from typing import Any
 6: 
 7: 
 8: class NoOpCollector:
 9:     """Drop-in replacement for TelemetryCollector that does nothing."""
10: 
11:     async def start(self) -> None:
12:         pass
13: 
14:     async def shutdown(self) -> None:
15:         pass
16: 
17:     def record_llm_call(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
18:         pass
19: 
20:     def record_storage_op(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
21:         pass
22: 
23:     def record_pipeline_stage(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
24:         pass
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/session.py
````python
 1: """Separate async engine for the telemetry database."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncEngine, create_async_engine
 6: 
 7: 
 8: def create_telemetry_engine(database_url: str) -> AsyncEngine:
 9:     """Create an async engine for the telemetry database.
10: 
11:     The engine is completely independent from khora's main DB engine.
12: 
13:     Args:
14:         database_url: PostgreSQL connection URL.
15: 
16:     Returns:
17:         AsyncEngine configured for telemetry writes.
18:     """
19:     url = database_url
20:     if url.startswith("postgresql://"):
21:         url = url.replace("postgresql://", "postgresql+asyncpg://", 1)
22:     elif url.startswith("postgres://"):
23:         url = url.replace("postgres://", "postgresql+asyncpg://", 1)
24: 
25:     return create_async_engine(
26:         url,
27:         echo=False,
28:         pool_size=5,
29:         max_overflow=5,
30:         pool_pre_ping=True,
31:     )
````

````python
  1: """Unit tests for the Skeleton Construction engine."""
  2: 
  3: from datetime import UTC, datetime
  4: from unittest.mock import MagicMock
  5: from uuid import uuid4

  7: import pytest

  9: from khora.engines.skeleton.backends import TemporalChunk, TemporalFilter, TemporalSearchResult

 11: 
 12: class TestTemporalFilter:
 13:     """Tests for TemporalFilter dataclass."""
 14: 
 15:     def test_empty_filter(self):
 16:         """Test creating an empty filter."""
 17:         f = TemporalFilter()
 18:         assert f.occurred_after is None
 19:         assert f.occurred_before is None
 20:         assert f.source_system is None
 21:         assert f.tags is None
 22:         assert f.additional == {}
 23: 
 24:     def test_filter_with_time_range(self):
 25:         """Test filter with time range."""
 26:         start = datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
 27:         end = datetime(2024, 2, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
 28: 
 29:         f = TemporalFilter(occurred_after=start, occurred_before=end)
 30: 
 31:         assert f.occurred_after == start
 32:         assert f.occurred_before == end
 33: 
 34:     def test_filter_with_metadata(self):
 35:         """Test filter with metadata fields."""
 36:         f = TemporalFilter(
 37:             source_system="slack",
 38:             author="alice",
 39:             channel="general",
 40:             tags=["important", "followup"],
 41:         )
 42: 
 43:         assert f.source_system == "slack"
 44:         assert f.author == "alice"
 45:         assert f.channel == "general"
 46:         assert f.tags == ["important", "followup"]
 47: 
 48:     def test_filter_with_additional(self):
 49:         """Test filter with additional structured filters."""
 50:         f = TemporalFilter(
 51:             additional={
 52:                 "confidence": {"gte": 0.8},
 53:                 "metadata.priority": {"eq": "high"},
 54:             }
 55:         )
 56: 
 57:         assert f.additional["confidence"] == {"gte": 0.8}
 58:         assert f.additional["metadata.priority"] == {"eq": "high"}

158: class TestSkeletonConstructionEngineFilterBuilding:
159:     """Tests for SkeletonConstructionEngine filter building."""

161:     @pytest.fixture
162:     def mock_config(self):
163:         """Create a mock config."""
164:         config = MagicMock()
165:         config.get_postgresql_url.return_value = "postgresql://localhost/test"
166:         config.get_neo4j_url.return_value = None
167:         config.get_neo4j_user.return_value = None
168:         config.get_neo4j_password.return_value = None
169:         config.get_neo4j_database.return_value = None
170:         config.get_graph_config.return_value = None
171:         config.get_vector_config.return_value = None
172:         config.storage.embedding_dimension = 1536
173:         config.llm.model = "gpt-4o-mini"
174:         config.llm.embedding_model = "text-embedding-3-small"
175:         config.llm.embedding_dimension = 1536
176:         config.llm.timeout = 30
177:         config.llm.max_retries = 3
178:         config.llm.extraction_model = None
179:         config.pipeline.chunking_strategy = "recursive"
180:         config.pipeline.chunk_size = 1000
181:         config.pipeline.chunk_overlap = 200
182:         config.telemetry_database_url = None
183:         config.telemetry_service_name = "test"
184:         return config
185: 
186:     def test_build_temporal_filter_from_dict(self, mock_config):
187:         """Test building TemporalFilter from dict."""
188:         from khora.engines.skeleton.engine import SkeletonConstructionEngine
189: 
190:         engine = SkeletonConstructionEngine(mock_config, backend="pgvector")
191: 
192:         filters = {
193:             "occurred_at": {"gte": "2024-01-01", "lt": "2024-02-01"},
194:             "author": {"eq": "alice"},
195:             "source_system": {"eq": "slack"},
196:             "tags": {"contains": ["important"]},
197:         }
198: 
199:         tf = engine._build_temporal_filter_from_dict(filters)
200: 
201:         assert tf.occurred_after == datetime(2024, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
202:         assert tf.occurred_before == datetime(2024, 2, 1, tzinfo=UTC)
203:         assert tf.author == "alice"
204:         assert tf.source_system == "slack"
205:         assert tf.tags == ["important"]
206: 
207:     def test_build_temporal_filter_simple_values(self, mock_config):
208:         """Test building TemporalFilter with simple values (not dicts)."""
209:         from khora.engines.skeleton.engine import SkeletonConstructionEngine
210: 
211:         engine = SkeletonConstructionEngine(mock_config, backend="pgvector")
212: 
213:         filters = {
214:             "author": "alice",
215:             "channel": "general",
216:         }
217: 
218:         tf = engine._build_temporal_filter_from_dict(filters)
219: 
220:         # Simple values are converted to {"eq": value}
221:         assert tf.author == "alice"
222:         assert tf.channel == "general"
223: 
224:     def test_parse_datetime_iso(self, mock_config):
225:         """Test parsing ISO datetime strings."""
226:         from khora.engines.skeleton.engine import SkeletonConstructionEngine
227: 
228:         engine = SkeletonConstructionEngine(mock_config, backend="pgvector")
229: 
230:         # ISO format with timezone
231:         dt = engine._parse_datetime("2024-01-15T10:30:00+00:00")
232:         assert dt.year == 2024
233:         assert dt.month == 1
234:         assert dt.day == 15
235:         assert dt.hour == 10
236: 
237:         # ISO format with Z
238:         dt = engine._parse_datetime("2024-01-15T10:30:00Z")
239:         assert dt.year == 2024
240: 
241:         # Date only
242:         dt = engine._parse_datetime("2024-01-15")
243:         assert dt.year == 2024
244:         assert dt.month == 1
245:         assert dt.day == 15
246: 
247:     def test_parse_datetime_object(self, mock_config):
248:         """Test that datetime objects pass through."""
249:         from khora.engines.skeleton.engine import SkeletonConstructionEngine
250: 
251:         engine = SkeletonConstructionEngine(mock_config, backend="pgvector")
252: 
253:         now = datetime.now(UTC)
254:         result = engine._parse_datetime(now)
255:         assert result == now
````

## File: tests/unit/test_attribute_schemas.py
````python
  1: """Tests for entity attribute schema validation."""
  2: 
  3: from pydantic import BaseModel
  4: 
  5: from khora.core.models.entity import Entity, EntityType
  6: from khora.core.models.schemas import (
  7:     ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMAS,
  8:     register_attribute_schema,
  9:     validate_attributes,
 10: )
 11: 
 12: 
 13: class TestAttributeSchemas:
 14:     """Test the attribute schema registry and validation."""
 15: 
 16:     def test_registry_has_standard_types(self):
 17:         """All standard entity types should have schemas."""
 18:         expected = {"PERSON", "ORGANIZATION", "LOCATION", "CONCEPT", "EVENT", "TECHNOLOGY", "PRODUCT", "DATE"}
 19:         assert expected.issubset(set(ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMAS.keys()))
 20: 
 21:     def test_register_attribute_schema(self):
 22:         """Downstream projects can register custom schemas."""
 23: 
 24:         class CustomAttributes(BaseModel):
 25:             name: str
 26:             custom_field: str | None = None
 27: 
 28:         register_attribute_schema("CUSTOM_TYPE", CustomAttributes, aliases=["CUSTOM_ALIAS"])
 29:         assert ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMAS["CUSTOM_TYPE"] is CustomAttributes
 30:         assert ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMAS["CUSTOM_ALIAS"] is CustomAttributes
 31: 
 32:         # Validate works with the registered schema
 33:         result = validate_attributes("CUSTOM_TYPE", {"name": "test", "custom_field": "val"})
 34:         assert result["name"] == "test"
 35:         assert result["custom_field"] == "val"
 36: 
 37:         # Clean up
 38:         del ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMAS["CUSTOM_TYPE"]
 39:         del ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMAS["CUSTOM_ALIAS"]
 40: 
 41: 
 42: class TestValidateAttributes:
 43:     """Test the validate_attributes function."""
 44: 
 45:     def test_valid_person_attributes(self):
 46:         """Valid person attributes pass validation."""
 47:         attrs = {"name": "Alice Smith", "title": "Engineer", "email": "alice@example.com"}
 48:         result = validate_attributes("PERSON", attrs)
 49:         assert result["name"] == "Alice Smith"
 50:         assert result["title"] == "Engineer"
 51:         assert result["email"] == "alice@example.com"
 52: 
 53:     def test_person_attributes_strips_none(self):
 54:         """None values are excluded from validated output."""
 55:         attrs = {"name": "Bob", "title": None, "role": None}
 56:         result = validate_attributes("PERSON", attrs)
 57:         assert result == {"name": "Bob"}
 58: 
 59:     def test_unknown_entity_type_passthrough(self):
 60:         """Unknown entity types pass through without validation."""
 61:         attrs = {"foo": "bar", "baz": 42}
 62:         result = validate_attributes("UNKNOWN_TYPE", attrs)
 63:         assert result == attrs
 64: 
 65:     def test_extra_fields_ignored(self):
 66:         """Extra fields not in schema are silently dropped."""
 67:         attrs = {"name": "Alice", "unknown_field": "value"}
 68:         result = validate_attributes("PERSON", attrs)
 69:         assert "name" in result
 70:         # Pydantic by default ignores extra fields
 71:         assert "unknown_field" not in result
 72: 
 73:     def test_case_insensitive_type_lookup(self):
 74:         """Entity type lookup should be case-insensitive."""
 75:         attrs = {"name": "Test Person"}
 76:         result = validate_attributes("person", attrs)
 77:         assert result["name"] == "Test Person"
 78: 
 79:     def test_empty_attributes_with_required_fields(self):
 80:         """Empty attributes for a type with required fields degrades gracefully."""
 81:         result = validate_attributes("PERSON", {})
 82:         assert result == {}  # Falls back to original
 83: 
 84: 
 85: class TestEntityValidation:
 86:     """Test Entity.validate() method."""
 87: 
 88:     def test_entity_validate_cleans_attributes(self):
 89:         """Entity.validate() should clean attributes via schema."""
 90:         entity = Entity(
 91:             name="Alice",
 92:             entity_type=EntityType.PERSON,
 93:             attributes={"name": "Alice", "title": "CTO", "garbage": None},
 94:         )
 95:         entity.validate()
 96:         assert entity.attributes["name"] == "Alice"
 97:         assert entity.attributes["title"] == "CTO"
 98: 
 99:     def test_entity_validate_unknown_type(self):
100:         """Entity.validate() should pass through for CUSTOM type."""
101:         entity = Entity(
102:             name="Something",
103:             entity_type=EntityType.CUSTOM,
104:             attributes={"foo": "bar"},
105:         )
106:         entity.validate()
107:         assert entity.attributes == {"foo": "bar"}
108: 
109:     def test_entity_source_tool_field(self):
110:         """Entity should have source_tool field."""
111:         entity = Entity(name="Test", source_tool="linear")
112:         assert entity.source_tool == "linear"
113: 
114:     def test_entity_source_tool_default_empty(self):
115:         """Entity source_tool should default to empty string."""
116:         entity = Entity(name="Test")
117:         assert entity.source_tool == ""
````

````python
  1: """Unit tests for extraction/embedders/litellm.py — LiteLLM embedder."""

 12: class TestLiteLLMEmbedder:
 13:     """Tests for LiteLLMEmbedder initialization and properties."""

 15:     def test_init_defaults(self) -> None:

 18:         assert embedder.model_name == "text-embedding-3-small"
 19:         assert embedder.dimension == 1536
 20:         assert embedder._batch_size == 100
 21:         assert embedder._cache_max_size == 10000

 27:             dimension=768,
 28:             batch_size=50,

 32:         assert embedder.dimension == 768
 33:         assert embedder._batch_size == 50
 34:         assert embedder._cache_max_size == 500

 41:         config.timeout = 60
 42:         config.max_retries = 5
 43:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder.from_config(config)

 47: 
 48: class TestCache:
 49:     """Tests for embedding cache."""
 50: 
 51:     def test_cache_key_deterministic(self) -> None:
 52:         """Same text produces same cache key."""
 53:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
 54:         k1 = embedder._cache_key("hello")
 55:         k2 = embedder._cache_key("hello")
 56:         assert k1 == k2
 57: 
 58:     def test_cache_key_different(self) -> None:
 59:         """Different text produces different key."""
 60:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
 61:         k1 = embedder._cache_key("hello")
 62:         k2 = embedder._cache_key("world")
 63:         assert k1 != k2
 64: 
 65:     def test_cache_miss(self) -> None:
 66:         """Cache get for uncached text returns None."""
 67:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
 68:         assert embedder._cache_get("uncached") is None
 69:         assert embedder._cache_misses == 1
 70: 
 71:     def test_cache_hit(self) -> None:
 72:         """Cache put then get returns embedding."""
 73:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
 74:         embedding = [0.1, 0.2, 0.3]
 75:         embedder._cache_put("test", embedding)
 76:         result = embedder._cache_get("test")
 77:         assert result == embedding
 78:         assert embedder._cache_hits == 1
 79: 
 80:     def test_cache_eviction(self) -> None:
 81:         """Cache evicts oldest entries when max_size is exceeded."""
 82:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(cache_max_size=2)
 83:         embedder._cache_put("a", [1.0])
 84:         embedder._cache_put("b", [2.0])
 85:         embedder._cache_put("c", [3.0])  # Should evict "a"
 86:         assert embedder._cache_get("a") is None
 87:         assert embedder._cache_get("c") == [3.0]
 88: 
 89:     def test_cache_disabled(self) -> None:
 90:         """Cache disabled when max_size=0."""
 91:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(cache_max_size=0)
 92:         embedder._cache_put("test", [1.0])
 93:         assert embedder._cache_get("test") is None
 94: 
 95:     def test_cache_stats(self) -> None:
 96:         """Cache stats report correct values."""
 97:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
 98:         embedder._cache_put("a", [1.0])
 99:         embedder._cache_get("a")  # hit
100:         embedder._cache_get("b")  # miss
101:         stats = embedder.cache_stats
102:         assert stats["size"] == 1
103:         assert stats["hits"] == 1
104:         assert stats["misses"] == 1
105: 
106: 
107: class TestEmbed:
108:     """Tests for the embed method."""
109: 
110:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
111:     async def test_single_text(self) -> None:
112:         """embed delegates to embed_batch."""
113:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", max_retries=1)
114:         expected = [0.1, 0.2, 0.3]
115: 
116:         mock_response = MagicMock()
117:         mock_response.data = [{"embedding": expected}]
118:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, total_tokens=10)
119: 
120:         with (
121:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response),
122:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
123:         ):
124:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
125:             result = await embedder.embed("hello world")
126: 
127:         assert result == expected
128: 
129:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
130:     async def test_cached_embed(self) -> None:
131:         """embed returns cached result without API call."""
132:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
133:         embedder._cache_put("hello", [0.1, 0.2])
134:         result = await embedder.embed("hello")
135:         assert result == [0.1, 0.2]

138: class TestEmbedBatch:
139:     """Tests for embed_batch method."""
140: 
141:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
142:     async def test_empty_texts(self) -> None:
143:         """Empty list returns empty list."""
144:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder()
145:         result = await embedder.embed_batch([])
146:         assert result == []
147: 
148:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
149:     async def test_small_batch(self) -> None:
150:         """Small batch (no chunking needed) calls API once."""
151:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", batch_size=100, max_retries=1)
152: 
153:         mock_response = MagicMock()
154:         mock_response.data = [
155:             {"embedding": [0.1, 0.2]},
156:             {"embedding": [0.3, 0.4]},
157:         ]
158:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=20, total_tokens=20)
159: 
160:         with (
161:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response),
162:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
163:         ):
164:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
165:             result = await embedder.embed_batch(["text1", "text2"])
166: 
167:         assert len(result) == 2
168:         assert result[0] == [0.1, 0.2]
169: 
170:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
171:     async def test_caching_integration(self) -> None:
172:         """Previously cached texts are not re-embedded."""
173:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", batch_size=100, max_retries=1)
174:         embedder._cache_put("cached", [0.5, 0.6])
175: 
176:         mock_response = MagicMock()
177:         mock_response.data = [{"embedding": [0.7, 0.8]}]
178:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, total_tokens=10)
179: 
180:         with (
181:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response),
182:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
183:         ):
184:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
185:             result = await embedder.embed_batch(["cached", "new_text"])
186: 
187:         assert result[0] == [0.5, 0.6]  # From cache
188:         assert result[1] == [0.7, 0.8]  # From API
189:

191: class TestEmbedBatchInternal:
192:     """Tests for _embed_batch_internal (input sanitization)."""
193: 
194:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
195:     async def test_sanitizes_empty_inputs(self) -> None:
196:         """Empty/None inputs are replaced with space placeholder."""
197:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", max_retries=1)
198: 
199:         mock_response = MagicMock()
200:         mock_response.data = [
201:             {"embedding": [0.1]},
202:             {"embedding": [0.2]},
203:         ]
204:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, total_tokens=10)
205: 
206:         with (
207:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=mock_response) as mock_api,
208:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
209:         ):
210:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
211:             result = await embedder._embed_batch_internal(["", "  "])
212: 
213:         # Verify sanitized inputs were sent to API
214:         call_args = mock_api.call_args
215:         assert call_args.kwargs["input"] == [" ", " "]
216:         assert len(result) == 2
217: 
218:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
219:     async def test_retry_on_failure(self) -> None:
220:         """Retries on transient failure."""
221:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", max_retries=2)
222: 
223:         mock_response = MagicMock()
224:         mock_response.data = [{"embedding": [0.1]}]
225:         mock_response.usage = MagicMock(prompt_tokens=10, total_tokens=10)
226: 
227:         with (
228:             patch(
229:                 "litellm.aembedding",
230:                 new_callable=AsyncMock,
231:                 side_effect=[Exception("transient"), mock_response],
232:             ),
233:             patch("asyncio.sleep", new_callable=AsyncMock),
234:             patch("khora.telemetry.get_collector") as mock_telem,
235:         ):
236:             mock_telem.return_value.record_llm_call = MagicMock()
237:             result = await embedder._embed_batch_internal(["test"])
238: 
239:         assert result == [[0.1]]
240: 
241:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
242:     async def test_raises_after_max_retries(self) -> None:
243:         """Raises after exhausting retries."""
244:         embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model="test-model", max_retries=2)
245: 
246:         with (
247:             patch("litellm.aembedding", new_callable=AsyncMock, side_effect=Exception("persistent")),
248:             patch("asyncio.sleep", new_callable=AsyncMock),
249:         ):
250:             with pytest.raises(Exception, match="persistent"):
251:                 await embedder._embed_batch_internal(["test"])
````
