````python
 91: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 92: # TelemetryCollector
 93: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 94: 
 95: 
 96: class TestTelemetryCollector:
 97:     def test_record_llm_call_buffers(self):
 98:         engine = MagicMock()
 99:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
100:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="embedding", model="gpt-4o", prompt_tokens=100)
101:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 1
102:         kind, data = collector._buffer[0]
103:         assert kind == "llm"
104:         assert data["operation"] == "embedding"
105:         assert data["model"] == "gpt-4o"
106: 
107:     def test_record_storage_op_buffers(self):
108:         engine = MagicMock()
109:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
110:         collector.record_storage_op(backend="pgvector", operation="search_similar_chunks", record_count=10)
111:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 1
112:         kind, data = collector._buffer[0]
113:         assert kind == "storage"
114:         assert data["backend"] == "pgvector"
115:         assert data["record_count"] == 10
116: 
117:     def test_record_pipeline_stage_buffers(self):
118:         engine = MagicMock()
119:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
120:         run_id = uuid4()
121:         collector.record_pipeline_stage(pipeline="ingestion", stage="chunking", run_id=run_id)
122:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 1
123:         kind, data = collector._buffer[0]
124:         assert kind == "pipeline"
125:         assert data["pipeline"] == "ingestion"
126: 
127:     def test_service_name_applied(self):
128:         engine = MagicMock()
129:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="genesis")
130:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="chat")
131:         _, data = collector._buffer[0]
132:         assert data["service_name"] == "genesis"
133: 
134:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
135:     async def test_flush_drains_buffer(self):
136:         engine = AsyncMock()
137:         conn = AsyncMock()
138:         engine.begin.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=conn)
139:         engine.begin.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
140: 
141:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
142:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="test")
143:         collector.record_storage_op(backend="pg", operation="test")
144:         collector.record_pipeline_stage(pipeline="test", stage="test")
145: 
146:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 3
147:         await collector._flush()
148:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 0
149: 
150:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
151:     async def test_flush_handles_errors_gracefully(self):
152:         engine = AsyncMock()
153:         engine.begin.side_effect = Exception("connection failed")
154: 
155:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
156:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="test")
157: 
158:         # Should not raise
159:         await collector._flush()
160:         # Buffer is drained even on error (events are dropped)
161:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 0
162: 
163: 
164: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
165: # init_telemetry / shutdown_telemetry
166: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
167: 
168: 
169: class TestInitTelemetry:
170:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
171:     async def test_init_returns_noop_when_no_url(self):
172:         cfg = TelemetryConfig(database_url=None)
173:         collector = await init_telemetry(cfg)
174:         assert isinstance(collector, NoOpCollector)
175:         assert isinstance(get_collector(), NoOpCollector)
176: 
177:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
178:     async def test_init_returns_collector_when_url_set(self):
179:         cfg = TelemetryConfig(database_url="postgresql://localhost/telemetry")
180: 
181:         with patch("khora.telemetry.session.create_telemetry_engine") as mock_engine_factory:
182:             mock_engine = MagicMock()
183:             # Make begin() return an async context manager
184:             mock_conn = AsyncMock()
185:             cm = AsyncMock()
186:             cm.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_conn)
187:             cm.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
188:             mock_engine.begin.return_value = cm
189:             mock_engine.dispose = AsyncMock()
190:             mock_engine_factory.return_value = mock_engine
191: 
192:             collector = await init_telemetry(cfg)
193:             assert isinstance(collector, TelemetryCollector)
194:             assert isinstance(get_collector(), TelemetryCollector)
195: 
196:             # Clean up
197:             await shutdown_telemetry()
198:             assert isinstance(get_collector(), NoOpCollector)
199: 
200:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
201:     async def test_init_from_env_defaults(self):
202:         with patch.dict("os.environ", {}, clear=True):
203:             collector = await init_telemetry()
204:             assert isinstance(collector, NoOpCollector)
````

## File: tests/unit/test_telemetry_instrument.py
````python
  1: """Tests for telemetry instrumentation helpers."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: import asyncio
  6: from unittest.mock import patch
  7: from uuid import uuid4
  8: 
  9: import pytest
 10: 
 11: from khora.telemetry import NoOpCollector
 12: from khora.telemetry.instrument import instrument_llm, instrument_storage, pipeline_stage
 13: 
 14: 
 15: class _RecordingCollector(NoOpCollector):
 16:     """Collector that records calls for assertions."""
 17: 
 18:     def __init__(self):
 19:         self.llm_calls: list[dict] = []
 20:         self.storage_calls: list[dict] = []
 21:         self.pipeline_calls: list[dict] = []
 22: 
 23:     def record_llm_call(self, **kwargs):
 24:         self.llm_calls.append(kwargs)
 25: 
 26:     def record_storage_op(self, **kwargs):
 27:         self.storage_calls.append(kwargs)
 28: 
 29:     def record_pipeline_stage(self, **kwargs):
 30:         self.pipeline_calls.append(kwargs)
 31: 
 32: 
 33: @pytest.fixture
 34: def recording_collector():
 35:     collector = _RecordingCollector()
 36:     with patch("khora.telemetry._collector", collector):
 37:         yield collector
 38: 
 39: 
 40: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 41: # @instrument_llm
 42: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 43: 
 44: 
 45: class TestInstrumentLLM:
 46:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 47:     async def test_records_success(self, recording_collector):
 48:         @instrument_llm("test_op")
 49:         async def my_llm_call():
 50:             return "result"
 51: 
 52:         result = await my_llm_call()
 53:         assert result == "result"
 54:         assert len(recording_collector.llm_calls) == 1
 55:         call = recording_collector.llm_calls[0]
 56:         assert call["operation"] == "test_op"
 57:         assert call["status"] == "success"
 58:         assert call["latency_ms"] > 0
 59:         assert call["error_message"] is None
 60: 
 61:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 62:     async def test_records_error(self, recording_collector):
 63:         @instrument_llm("failing_op")
 64:         async def my_llm_call():
 65:             raise ValueError("boom")
 66: 
 67:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="boom"):
 68:             await my_llm_call()
 69: 
 70:         assert len(recording_collector.llm_calls) == 1
 71:         call = recording_collector.llm_calls[0]
 72:         assert call["status"] == "error"
 73:         assert "boom" in call["error_message"]
 74: 
 75: 
 76: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 77: # @instrument_storage
 78: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 79: 
 80: 
 81: class TestInstrumentStorage:
 82:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 83:     async def test_records_success_with_list_result(self, recording_collector):
 84:         @instrument_storage("pgvector", "search_similar")
 85:         async def search():
 86:             return [1, 2, 3]
 87: 
 88:         result = await search()
 89:         assert result == [1, 2, 3]
 90:         assert len(recording_collector.storage_calls) == 1
 91:         call = recording_collector.storage_calls[0]
 92:         assert call["backend"] == "pgvector"
 93:         assert call["operation"] == "search_similar"
 94:         assert call["record_count"] == 3
 95:         assert call["status"] == "success"
 96: 
 97:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 98:     async def test_records_single_result(self, recording_collector):
 99:         @instrument_storage("postgresql", "create_document")
100:         async def create():
101:             return {"id": 1}
102: 
103:         await create()
104:         call = recording_collector.storage_calls[0]
105:         assert call["record_count"] == 1
106: 
107:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
108:     async def test_records_error(self, recording_collector):
109:         @instrument_storage("neo4j", "create_entity")
110:         async def failing():
111:             raise ConnectionError("db down")
112: 
113:         with pytest.raises(ConnectionError):
114:             await failing()
115: 
116:         call = recording_collector.storage_calls[0]
117:         assert call["status"] == "error"
118:         assert "db down" in call["error_message"]
119: 
120: 
121: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122: # pipeline_stage context manager
123: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
124: 
125: 
126: class TestPipelineStage:
127:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
128:     async def test_records_success(self, recording_collector):
129:         run_id = uuid4()
130:         ns_id = uuid4()
131:         async with pipeline_stage("ingestion", "chunking", run_id, namespace_id=ns_id):
132:             await asyncio.sleep(0.001)
133: 
134:         assert len(recording_collector.pipeline_calls) == 1
135:         call = recording_collector.pipeline_calls[0]
136:         assert call["pipeline"] == "ingestion"
137:         assert call["stage"] == "chunking"
138:         assert call["run_id"] == run_id
139:         assert call["namespace_id"] == ns_id
140:         assert call["status"] == "success"
141:         assert call["latency_ms"] > 0
142: 
143:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
144:     async def test_records_error(self, recording_collector):
145:         with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="stage failed"):
146:             async with pipeline_stage("query", "understanding", uuid4()):
147:                 raise RuntimeError("stage failed")
148: 
149:         call = recording_collector.pipeline_calls[0]
150:         assert call["status"] == "error"
151:         assert "stage failed" in call["error_message"]
152: 
153:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
154:     async def test_extra_metadata(self, recording_collector):
155:         async with pipeline_stage("ingestion", "embedding", uuid4(), extra_metadata={"chunk_count": 42}):
156:             pass
157: 
158:         call = recording_collector.pipeline_calls[0]
159:         assert call["metadata"] == {"chunk_count": 42}
````

## File: src/khora/engines/graphrag/engine.py
````python
  1: """GraphRAG engine implementation.
  2: 
  3: This is the default memory engine for Khora, providing:
  4: - Knowledge graph storage (Neo4j, Kuzu, Memgraph, ArcadeDB)
  5: - Vector embeddings (pgvector, ArcadeDB)
  6: - LLM-based entity extraction
  7: - Hybrid search (vector + graph + keyword)
  8: """
  9: 
 10: from __future__ import annotations
 11: 
 12: import hashlib
 13: import time
 14: from collections.abc import Callable
 15: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 16: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 17: from uuid import UUID
 18: 
 19: from loguru import logger
 20: 
 21: from khora.config import KhoraConfig, LiteLLMConfig
 22: from khora.core.models import Document, DocumentMetadata, Entity, MemoryNamespace, Organization, Workspace
 23: from khora.extraction.embedders import LiteLLMEmbedder
 24: from khora.memory_lake import BatchResult, RecallResult, RememberResult, Stats
 25: from khora.query import HybridQueryEngine, QueryConfig, SearchMode
 26: from khora.storage import StorageConfig, StorageCoordinator, create_storage_coordinator
 27: 
 28: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 29:     pass
 30: 
 31: 
 32: @dataclass
 33: class ExtractionQualityMetrics:
 34:     """Track extraction quality for monitoring.
 35: 
 36:     This dataclass captures metrics about the quality of entity and
 37:     relationship extraction during document ingestion, enabling
 38:     monitoring and quality control.
 39:     """
 40: 
 41:     total_chunks: int = 0
 42:     chunks_with_entities: int = 0
 43:     total_entities: int = 0
 44:     total_relationships: int = 0
 45:     avg_entities_per_chunk: float = 0.0
 46:     avg_confidence: float = 0.0
 47:     entity_type_distribution: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
 48:     low_confidence_entities: int = 0
 49:     extraction_time_ms: float = 0.0
 50: 
 51:     def compute_averages(self) -> None:
 52:         """Compute average metrics from totals."""
 53:         if self.total_chunks > 0:
 54:             self.avg_entities_per_chunk = self.total_entities / self.total_chunks
 55: 
 56:     def log_quality_summary(self, document_id: UUID) -> None:
 57:         """Log a quality summary for monitoring."""
 58:         if self.total_chunks == 0:
 59:             logger.debug(f"Document {document_id}: no chunks processed")
 60:             return
 61: 
 62:         entity_ratio = self.chunks_with_entities / self.total_chunks if self.total_chunks > 0 else 0
 63:         logger.info(
 64:             f"Document {document_id} extraction quality: "
 65:             f"{self.total_entities} entities from {self.chunks_with_entities}/{self.total_chunks} chunks "
 66:             f"({entity_ratio:.1%} coverage), "
 67:             f"{self.total_relationships} relationships, "
 68:             f"avg confidence: {self.avg_confidence:.2f}"
 69:         )
 70: 
 71:         if self.low_confidence_entities > 0:
 72:             logger.warning(f"Document {document_id}: {self.low_confidence_entities} low-confidence entities detected")
 73: 
 74:         if entity_ratio < 0.1 and self.total_chunks > 5:
 75:             logger.warning(
 76:                 f"Document {document_id}: low entity extraction rate ({entity_ratio:.1%}). "
 77:                 "Consider reviewing extraction skill or content quality."
 78:             )
 79: 
 80: 
 81: class GraphRAGEngine:
 82:     """GraphRAG engine - full-featured engine using knowledge graphs, vectors, and LLM extraction.
 83: 
 84:     This is the default engine for MemoryLake. It provides:
 85:     - Document chunking (fixed, semantic, recursive, conversation-aware)
 86:     - Entity and relationship extraction using LLMs
 87:     - Vector similarity search via pgvector
 88:     - Graph traversal via configurable graph backends
 89:     - Hybrid search with RRF fusion
 90:     - Query understanding and entity linking
 91:     - Neural reranking
 92:     """
 93: 
 94:     def __init__(
 95:         self,
 96:         config: KhoraConfig,
 97:         *,
 98:         storage_config: StorageConfig | None = None,
 99:     ) -> None:
100:         """Initialize the GraphRAG engine.
101: 
102:         Args:
103:             config: KhoraConfig instance
104:             storage_config: Storage configuration (derived from config if None) - deprecated
105:         """
106:         self._config = config
107: 
108:         # Set up storage config
109:         if storage_config:
110:             self._storage_config = storage_config
111:         else:
112:             postgresql_url = config.get_postgresql_url()
113:             graph_config = config.get_graph_config()
114:             vector_config = config.get_vector_config()
115:             self._storage_config = StorageConfig(
116:                 postgresql_url=postgresql_url,
117:                 pgvector_url=postgresql_url,  # pgvector uses same database as relational
118:                 neo4j_url=config.get_neo4j_url(),
119:                 neo4j_user=config.get_neo4j_user(),
120:                 neo4j_password=config.get_neo4j_password(),
121:                 neo4j_database=config.get_neo4j_database(),
122:                 pgvector_embedding_dimension=config.storage.embedding_dimension,
123:                 graph_config=graph_config,
124:                 vector_config=vector_config,
125:             )
126: 
127:         self._storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None
128:         self._embedder: LiteLLMEmbedder | None = None
129:         self._query_engine: HybridQueryEngine | None = None
130:         self._connected = False
131: 
132:         # Default namespace for simple usage
133:         self._default_namespace_id: UUID | None = None
134: 
135:     async def connect(self) -> None:
136:         """Connect to all storage backends."""
137:         if self._connected:
138:             return
139: 
140:         logger.info("Connecting GraphRAG engine...")
141: 
142:         # Create and connect storage
143:         self._storage = create_storage_coordinator(self._storage_config)
144:         await self._storage.connect()
145: 
146:         # Create embedder
147:         llm_config = LiteLLMConfig(
148:             model=self._config.llm.model,
149:             embedding_model=self._config.llm.embedding_model,
150:             embedding_dimension=self._config.llm.embedding_dimension,
151:             timeout=self._config.llm.timeout,
152:             max_retries=self._config.llm.max_retries,
153:         )
154:         self._embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder.from_config(llm_config)
155: 
156:         # Create query engine
157:         self._query_engine = HybridQueryEngine(
158:             storage=self._storage,
159:             embedder=self._embedder,
160:         )
161: 
162:         # Initialize telemetry (no-op if KHORA_TELEMETRY_DATABASE_URL not set)
163:         from khora.telemetry import init_telemetry
164:         from khora.telemetry.config import TelemetryConfig
165: 
166:         telemetry_cfg = TelemetryConfig(
167:             database_url=self._config.telemetry_database_url,
168:             service_name=self._config.telemetry_service_name,
169:         )
170:         await init_telemetry(telemetry_cfg)
171: 
172:         self._connected = True
173:         logger.info("GraphRAG engine connected")
174: 
175:     async def disconnect(self) -> None:
176:         """Disconnect from all storage backends."""
177:         if not self._connected:
178:             return
179: 
180:         logger.info("Disconnecting GraphRAG engine...")
181: 
182:         # Shutdown telemetry
183:         from khora.telemetry import shutdown_telemetry
184: 
185:         await shutdown_telemetry()
186: 
187:         if self._storage:
188:             await self._storage.disconnect()
189:             self._storage = None
190: 
191:         self._embedder = None
192:         self._query_engine = None
193:         self._connected = False
194: 
195:         logger.info("GraphRAG engine disconnected")
196: 
197:     def _get_storage(self) -> StorageCoordinator:
198:         """Get storage coordinator (internal use)."""
199:         if self._storage is None:
200:             raise RuntimeError("GraphRAG engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
201:         return self._storage
202: 
203:     def _get_query_engine(self) -> HybridQueryEngine:
204:         """Get query engine (internal use)."""
205:         if self._query_engine is None:
206:             raise RuntimeError("GraphRAG engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
207:         return self._query_engine
208: 
209:     # =========================================================================
210:     # Core API: remember, recall, forget
211:     # =========================================================================
212: 
213:     async def remember(
214:         self,
215:         content: str,
216:         namespace_id: UUID,
217:         *,
218:         title: str = "",
219:         source: str = "",
220:         metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
221:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
222:     ) -> RememberResult:
223:         """Store content in the memory engine.
224: 
225:         Processes document through the ingestion pipeline with parallel
226:         chunking, embedding, and entity extraction for optimal performance.
227: 
228:         Returns:
229:             RememberResult with document_id, counts, and timing metrics in metadata.
230:         """
231:         timings: dict[str, float] = {}
232:         total_start = time.perf_counter()
233: 
234:         # Compute checksum
235:         start = time.perf_counter()
236:         checksum = hashlib.sha256(content.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
237:         timings["checksum_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
238: 
239:         storage = self._get_storage()
240: 
241:         # Check for duplicate - skip if any document with same checksum exists
242:         start = time.perf_counter()
243:         existing = await storage.get_document_by_checksum(namespace_id, checksum)
244:         timings["dedup_check_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
245: 
246:         if existing:
247:             timings["total_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - total_start) * 1000
248:             logger.debug(f"Document already exists (checksum={checksum[:8]}..., status={existing.status})")
249:             return RememberResult(
250:                 document_id=existing.id,
251:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
252:                 chunks_created=existing.chunk_count,
253:                 entities_extracted=existing.entity_count,
254:                 relationships_created=0,
255:                 metadata={"duplicate": True, "status": str(existing.status), "timings": timings},
256:             )
257: 
258:         # Create document
259:         start = time.perf_counter()
260:         doc_metadata = DocumentMetadata(
261:             title=title,
262:             source=source,
263:             source_type="api",
264:             checksum=checksum,
265:             size_bytes=len(content.encode("utf-8")),
266:             custom=metadata or {},
267:         )
268:         document = Document(
269:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
270:             content=content,
271:             metadata=doc_metadata,
272:         )
273:         document = await storage.create_document(document)
274:         timings["document_create_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
275: 
276:         # Process through pipeline (handles chunking, embedding, extraction in parallel)
277:         from khora.pipelines.flows.ingest import process_document
278: 
279:         start = time.perf_counter()
280:         result = await process_document(
281:             document,
282:             storage,
283:             skill_name=skill_name,
284:             embedding_model=self._config.llm.embedding_model,
285:             extraction_model=self._config.llm.extraction_model or self._config.llm.model,
286:         )
287:         timings["pipeline_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
288:         timings["total_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - total_start) * 1000
289: 
290:         # Track extraction quality metrics
291:         quality_metrics = ExtractionQualityMetrics(
292:             total_chunks=result["chunks"],
293:             total_entities=result["entities"],
294:             total_relationships=result["relationships"],
295:             extraction_time_ms=timings.get("pipeline_ms", 0),
296:         )
297:         quality_metrics.compute_averages()
298:         quality_metrics.log_quality_summary(document.id)
299: 
300:         # Log performance summary
301:         logger.debug(
302:             f"remember() completed: {result['chunks']} chunks, {result['entities']} entities, "
303:             f"{result['relationships']} relationships in {timings['total_ms']:.1f}ms"
304:         )
305: 
306:         return RememberResult(
307:             document_id=document.id,
308:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
309:             chunks_created=result["chunks"],
310:             entities_extracted=result["entities"],
311:             relationships_created=result["relationships"],
312:             metadata={
313:                 "timings": timings,
314:                 "quality_metrics": {
315:                     "avg_entities_per_chunk": quality_metrics.avg_entities_per_chunk,
316:                     "extraction_time_ms": quality_metrics.extraction_time_ms,
317:                 },
318:             },
319:         )
320: 
321:     async def recall(
322:         self,
323:         query: str,
324:         namespace_id: UUID,
325:         *,
326:         limit: int = 10,
327:         mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.HYBRID,
328:         min_similarity: float = 0.0,
329:         agentic: bool = False,
330:         raw: bool = False,
331:     ) -> RecallResult:
332:         """Recall memories relevant to a query."""
333:         config = QueryConfig(
334:             mode=mode,
335:             max_chunks=limit,
336:             max_entities=limit,
337:             min_chunk_similarity=min_similarity,
338:             min_entity_similarity=min_similarity,
339:         )
340: 
341:         # Raw mode: disable all LLM features
342:         if raw:
343:             config.enable_query_understanding = False
344:             config.enable_query_expansion = False
345:             config.enable_entity_extraction = False
346:             config.enable_temporal_detection = False
347:             config.enable_entity_linking = False
348:             config.enable_reranking = False
349:             config.enable_hyde = False
350: 
351:         result = await self._get_query_engine().query(query, namespace_id, config=config, agentic=agentic)
352: 
353:         return RecallResult(
354:             query=query,
355:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
356:             chunks=result.chunks,
357:             entities=result.entities,
358:             context_text=result.get_context_text(max_chunks=limit),
359:             metadata=result.metadata,
360:         )
361: 
362:     async def forget(self, document_id: UUID, namespace_id: UUID | None) -> bool:
363:         """Remove a memory from the engine."""
364:         storage = self._get_storage()
365: 
366:         # Verify namespace if provided
367:         if namespace_id:
368:             document = await storage.get_document(document_id)
369:             if document and document.namespace_id != namespace_id:
370:                 logger.warning(f"Document {document_id} not in namespace {namespace_id}")
371:                 return False
372: 
373:         return await storage.delete_document(document_id)
374: 
375:     async def remember_batch(
376:         self,
377:         documents: list[dict[str, Any]],
378:         namespace_id: UUID,
379:         *,
380:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
381:         max_concurrent: int = 5,
382:         deduplicate: bool = True,
383:         infer_relationships: bool = True,
384:         on_progress: Callable[[int, int], None] | None = None,
385:     ) -> BatchResult:
386:         """Store multiple documents with automatic optimization.
387: 
388:         Processes documents in parallel with configurable concurrency.
389:         Uses shared embedder and entity index for efficiency.
390: 
391:         Returns:
392:             BatchResult with counts and timing metrics.
393:         """
394:         timings: dict[str, float] = {}
395:         total_start = time.perf_counter()
396: 
397:         if not documents:
398:             return BatchResult(
399:                 total=0,
400:                 processed=0,
401:                 skipped=0,
402:                 failed=0,
403:                 chunks=0,
404:                 entities=0,
405:                 relationships=0,
406:             )
407: 
408:         # Build doc dicts for ingest_documents
409:         start = time.perf_counter()
410:         doc_inputs = []
411:         for doc_data in documents:
412:             doc_inputs.append(
413:                 {
414:                     "content": doc_data.get("content", ""),
415:                     "title": doc_data.get("title", ""),
416:                     "source": doc_data.get("source", ""),
417:                     "source_type": "api",
418:                     "metadata": doc_data.get("metadata", {}),
419:                 }
420:             )
421:         timings["prepare_inputs_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
422: 
423:         from khora.pipelines.flows.ingest import ingest_documents
424: 
425:         # Create a shared embedder for efficiency (uses LRU cache internally)
426:         shared_embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder(model=self._config.llm.embedding_model)
427: 
428:         # Create shared EntityIndex for cross-document deduplication if enabled
429:         shared_entity_index = None
430:         if deduplicate:
431:             start = time.perf_counter()
432:             from khora.extraction.expansion.entity_index import EntityIndex
433: 
434:             shared_entity_index = EntityIndex()
435: 
436:             # Optionally preload existing entities for dedup against stored data
437:             existing_entities = await self._get_storage().list_entities(namespace_id, limit=50000)
438:             for entity in existing_entities:
439:                 shared_entity_index.add(entity)
440: 
441:             timings["entity_preload_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
442:             if existing_entities:
443:                 logger.debug(f"Preloaded {len(existing_entities)} existing entities into EntityIndex")
444: 
445:         start = time.perf_counter()
446:         result = await ingest_documents(
447:             namespace_id,
448:             doc_inputs,
449:             self._get_storage(),
450:             skill_name=skill_name,
451:             embedding_model=self._config.llm.embedding_model,
452:             extraction_model=self._config.llm.extraction_model or self._config.llm.model,
453:             max_concurrent_documents=max_concurrent,
454:             shared_embedder=shared_embedder,
455:             shared_entity_index=shared_entity_index,
456:             enable_expansion=infer_relationships,
457:         )
458:         timings["ingest_pipeline_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000
459:         timings["total_ms"] = (time.perf_counter() - total_start) * 1000
460: 
461:         # Calculate throughput metrics
462:         processed = result.get("processed_documents", 0)
463:         if processed > 0 and timings["total_ms"] > 0:
464:             timings["docs_per_second"] = processed / (timings["total_ms"] / 1000)
465:             timings["avg_doc_ms"] = timings["ingest_pipeline_ms"] / processed
466: 
467:         logger.info(
468:             f"remember_batch() completed: {processed}/{len(documents)} docs, "
469:             f"{result.get('total_chunks', 0)} chunks, {result.get('total_entities', 0)} entities "
470:             f"in {timings['total_ms']:.1f}ms ({timings.get('docs_per_second', 0):.1f} docs/sec)"
471:         )
472: 
473:         # Call progress callback if provided
474:         if on_progress:
475:             processed = result.get("processed_documents", 0)
476:             total = result.get("total_documents", len(documents))
477:             on_progress(processed, total)
478: 
479:         # Build BatchResult from aggregated stats
480:         return BatchResult(
481:             total=result.get("total_documents", len(documents)),
482:             processed=result.get("processed_documents", 0),
483:             skipped=result.get("skipped_documents", 0),
484:             failed=result.get("failed_documents", 0),
485:             chunks=result.get("total_chunks", 0),
486:             entities=result.get("total_entities", 0),
487:             relationships=result.get("total_relationships", 0) + result.get("total_inferred_relationships", 0),
488:             metadata={"timings": timings},
489:         )
490: 
491:     # =========================================================================
492:     # Namespace Management
493:     # =========================================================================
494: 
495:     async def get_or_create_default_namespace(self) -> UUID:
496:         """Get or create a default namespace for simple usage."""
497:         if self._default_namespace_id:
498:             return self._default_namespace_id
499: 
500:         # Try to find existing default namespace
501:         # For simplicity, we'll create a default org/workspace/namespace
502:         storage = self._get_storage()
503:         default_org = await storage.get_organization_by_slug("default")
504:         if not default_org:
505:             default_org = await storage.create_organization(Organization(name="Default", slug="default"))
506: 
507:         workspaces = await storage.list_workspaces(default_org.id)
508:         if workspaces:
509:             default_workspace = workspaces[0]
510:         else:
511:             default_workspace = await storage.create_workspace(
512:                 Workspace(
513:                     organization_id=default_org.id,
514:                     name="Default",
515:                     slug="default",
516:                 )
517:             )
518: 
519:         namespaces = await storage.list_namespaces(default_workspace.id)
520:         if namespaces:
521:             default_namespace = namespaces[0]
522:         else:
523:             default_namespace = await storage.create_namespace(
524:                 MemoryNamespace(
525:                     workspace_id=default_workspace.id,
526:                     name="Default",
527:                     slug="default",
528:                 )
529:             )
530: 
531:         self._default_namespace_id = default_namespace.id
532:         return self._default_namespace_id
533: 
534:     async def create_namespace(
535:         self,
536:         name: str,
537:         workspace_id: UUID,
538:         *,
539:         description: str = "",
540:         config_overrides: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
541:     ) -> MemoryNamespace:
542:         """Create a new memory namespace."""
543:         namespace = MemoryNamespace(
544:             workspace_id=workspace_id,
545:             name=name,
546:             description=description,
547:             config_overrides=config_overrides or {},
548:         )
549:         return await self._get_storage().create_namespace(namespace)
550: 
551:     async def get_namespace(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> MemoryNamespace | None:
552:         """Get a namespace by ID."""
553:         return await self._get_storage().get_namespace(namespace_id)
554: 
555:     async def ensure_namespace(
556:         self,
557:         name: str,
558:         *,
559:         description: str = "",
560:     ) -> UUID:
561:         """Get or create a namespace by name."""
562:         storage = self._get_storage()
563: 
564:         # Ensure default org/workspace exists
565:         await self.get_or_create_default_namespace()
566: 
567:         # Get default workspace
568:         default_org = await storage.get_organization_by_slug("default")
569:         if not default_org:
570:             raise RuntimeError("Default organization not found")
571: 
572:         workspaces = await storage.list_workspaces(default_org.id)
573:         if not workspaces:
574:             raise RuntimeError("Default workspace not found")
575: 
576:         default_workspace = workspaces[0]
577: 
578:         # Try to find namespace by slug
579:         slug = name.lower().replace(" ", "-")
580:         existing_ns = await storage.get_namespace_by_slug(default_workspace.id, slug)
581:         if existing_ns:
582:             return existing_ns.id
583: 
584:         # Create new namespace
585:         new_ns = await storage.create_namespace(
586:             MemoryNamespace(
587:                 workspace_id=default_workspace.id,
588:                 name=name,
589:                 slug=slug,
590:                 description=description,
591:             )
592:         )
593:         return new_ns.id
594: 
595:     # =========================================================================
596:     # Entity Operations
597:     # =========================================================================
598: 
599:     async def get_entity(self, entity_id: UUID) -> Entity | None:
600:         """Get an entity by ID."""
601:         return await self._get_storage().get_entity(entity_id)
602: 
603:     async def list_entities(
604:         self,
605:         namespace_id: UUID,
606:         *,
607:         entity_type: str | None = None,
608:         limit: int = 100,
609:     ) -> list[Entity]:
610:         """List entities in a namespace."""
611:         return await self._get_storage().list_entities(namespace_id, entity_type=entity_type, limit=limit)
612: 
613:     async def find_related_entities(
614:         self,
615:         entity_id: UUID,
616:         namespace_id: UUID,
617:         *,
618:         max_depth: int = 2,
619:         limit: int = 20,
620:     ) -> list[tuple[Entity, float]]:
621:         """Find entities related to a given entity."""
622:         return await self._get_query_engine().find_related_entities(
623:             entity_id,
624:             namespace_id,
625:             max_depth=max_depth,
626:             limit=limit,
627:         )
628: 
629:     # =========================================================================
630:     # Document Operations
631:     # =========================================================================
632: 
633:     async def get_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> Document | None:
634:         """Get a document by ID."""
635:         return await self._get_storage().get_document(document_id)
636: 
637:     async def list_documents(
638:         self,
639:         namespace_id: UUID,
640:         *,
641:         limit: int = 100,
642:     ) -> list[Document]:
643:         """List documents in a namespace."""
644:         return await self._get_storage().list_documents(namespace_id, limit=limit)
645: 
646:     async def search_entities(
647:         self,
648:         query: str,
649:         namespace_id: UUID,
650:         *,
651:         limit: int = 10,
652:     ) -> list[Entity]:
653:         """Search entities by query text using embedding similarity.
654: 
655:         Uses batch entity fetching to avoid N+1 queries for better performance.
656:         """
657:         # Embed the query
658:         if self._embedder is None:
659:             raise RuntimeError("GraphRAG engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
660: 
661:         query_embedding = await self._embedder.embed(query)
662: 
663:         # Search similar entities
664:         storage = self._get_storage()
665:         entity_ids_scores = await storage.search_similar_entities(
666:             namespace_id,
667:             query_embedding,
668:             limit=limit,
669:             min_similarity=0.0,
670:         )
671: 
672:         if not entity_ids_scores:
673:             return []
674: 
675:         # Batch fetch all entities in a single query (avoids N+1)
676:         entity_ids = [entity_id for entity_id, _ in entity_ids_scores]
677:         entities_map = await storage.get_entities_batch(entity_ids)
678: 
679:         # Return entities in score order, filtering out any that weren't found
680:         entities = []
681:         for entity_id, _score in entity_ids_scores:
682:             if entity_id in entities_map:
683:                 entities.append(entities_map[entity_id])
684: 
685:         return entities
686: 
687:     async def stats(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> Stats:
688:         """Get document/chunk/entity/relationship counts for a namespace."""
689:         storage = self._get_storage()
690: 
691:         # Get counts
692:         documents = await storage.list_documents(namespace_id, limit=0)
693:         doc_count = len(documents) if documents else 0
694: 
695:         # For more accurate counts, query directly
696:         # These may vary by backend implementation
697:         try:
698:             doc_count = await storage.count_documents(namespace_id)
699:         except (AttributeError, NotImplementedError):
700:             pass
701: 
702:         try:
703:             chunk_count = await storage.count_chunks(namespace_id)
704:         except (AttributeError, NotImplementedError):
705:             chunks = await storage.list_chunks(namespace_id, limit=0)
706:             chunk_count = len(chunks) if chunks else 0
707: 
708:         try:
709:             entity_count = await storage.count_entities(namespace_id)
710:         except (AttributeError, NotImplementedError):
711:             entities = await storage.list_entities(namespace_id, limit=0)
712:             entity_count = len(entities) if entities else 0
713: 
714:         try:
715:             relationship_count = await storage.count_relationships(namespace_id)
716:         except (AttributeError, NotImplementedError):
717:             rels = await storage.list_relationships(namespace_id, limit=0)
718:             relationship_count = len(rels) if rels else 0
719: 
720:         return Stats(
721:             documents=doc_count,
722:             chunks=chunk_count,
723:             entities=entity_count,
724:             relationships=relationship_count,
725:         )
726: 
727:     async def health_check(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
728:         """Check health of all components and dependencies.
729: 
730:         Returns a dict with:
731:         - status: 'healthy', 'degraded', or 'disconnected'
732:         - checks: Individual component check results
733:         - engine: Engine name for identification
734:         """
735:         if not self._connected:
736:             return {"status": "disconnected", "engine": "graphrag"}
737: 
738:         health: dict[str, Any] = {
739:             "engine": "graphrag",
740:             "status": "healthy",
741:             "checks": {},
742:         }
743: 
744:         # Check storage coordinator (PostgreSQL + backends)
745:         try:
746:             storage_health = await self._get_storage().health_check()
747:             health["checks"]["storage"] = storage_health.summary
748:             if not storage_health.is_healthy:
749:                 health["status"] = "degraded"
750:         except Exception as e:
751:             health["checks"]["storage"] = f"error: {e}"
752:             health["status"] = "degraded"
753: 
754:         # Check embedder availability
755:         try:
756:             if self._embedder is not None:
757:                 # Simple test - just verify the embedder is configured
758:                 health["checks"]["embedder"] = "ok"
759:             else:
760:                 health["checks"]["embedder"] = "not configured"
761:                 health["status"] = "degraded"
762:         except Exception as e:
763:             health["checks"]["embedder"] = f"error: {e}"
764:             health["status"] = "degraded"
765: 
766:         # Check query engine
767:         try:
768:             if self._query_engine is not None:
769:                 health["checks"]["query_engine"] = "ok"
770:             else:
771:                 health["checks"]["query_engine"] = "not configured"
772:                 health["status"] = "degraded"
773:         except Exception as e:
774:             health["checks"]["query_engine"] = f"error: {e}"
775:             health["status"] = "degraded"
776: 
777:         return health
````

## File: src/khora/engines/skeleton/skeleton.py
````python
  1: """Skeleton-based indexing for cost optimization (KET-RAG inspired).
  2: 
  3: This module implements cost-efficient indexing:
  4: - PageRank-based core chunk selection
  5: - Skeleton graph builder (full KG only for core chunks)
  6: - Keyword-chunk bipartite graph for non-core chunks
  7: - Lazy entity expansion on retrieval
  8: 
  9: Expected cost reduction: 5-10x fewer LLM extraction calls compared to full KG.
 10: """
 11: 
 12: from __future__ import annotations
 13: 
 14: import math
 15: from collections import defaultdict
 16: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 17: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 18: from uuid import UUID
 19: 
 20: from loguru import logger
 21: 
 22: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 23:     from khora.engines.skeleton.backends import TemporalChunk
 24: 
 25: 
 26: @dataclass
 27: class ChunkNode:
 28:     """Node in the chunk graph for PageRank calculation."""
 29: 
 30:     chunk_id: UUID
 31:     content: str
 32:     keywords: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
 33:     pagerank_score: float = 0.0
 34:     is_core: bool = False
 35: 
 36: 
 37: @dataclass
 38: class KeywordNode:
 39:     """Node representing a keyword in the bipartite graph."""
 40: 
 41:     keyword: str
 42:     chunk_ids: set[UUID] = field(default_factory=set)
 43:     idf_score: float = 0.0  # Inverse document frequency
 44: 
 45: 
 46: class SkeletonIndexer:
 47:     """Builds skeleton indexes for cost-efficient retrieval.
 48: 
 49:     The skeleton approach (inspired by KET-RAG) works as follows:
 50:     1. Extract keywords from all chunks (fast, no LLM)
 51:     2. Build keyword-chunk bipartite graph
 52:     3. Calculate PageRank to identify core chunks
 53:     4. Only run LLM extraction on core chunks (5-10% of total)
 54:     5. For non-core chunks, use keyword matching for retrieval
 55:     """
 56: 
 57:     def __init__(
 58:         self,
 59:         core_ratio: float = 0.1,
 60:         damping_factor: float = 0.85,
 61:         max_iterations: int = 100,
 62:         convergence_threshold: float = 1e-6,
 63:     ):
 64:         """Initialize the indexer.
 65: 
 66:         Args:
 67:             core_ratio: Fraction of chunks to mark as core (default: 10%)
 68:             damping_factor: PageRank damping factor
 69:             max_iterations: Max PageRank iterations
 70:             convergence_threshold: PageRank convergence threshold
 71:         """
 72:         self._core_ratio = core_ratio
 73:         self._damping_factor = damping_factor
 74:         self._max_iterations = max_iterations
 75:         self._convergence_threshold = convergence_threshold
 76: 
 77:         # Index structures
 78:         self._chunks: dict[UUID, ChunkNode] = {}
 79:         self._keywords: dict[str, KeywordNode] = {}
 80: 
 81:     def add_chunk(self, chunk: TemporalChunk) -> None:
 82:         """Add a chunk to the index.
 83: 
 84:         Args:
 85:             chunk: Temporal chunk to add
 86:         """
 87:         # Extract keywords using simple TF-based approach
 88:         keywords = self._extract_keywords(chunk.content)
 89: 
 90:         chunk_node = ChunkNode(
 91:             chunk_id=chunk.id,
 92:             content=chunk.content,
 93:             keywords=keywords,
 94:         )
 95:         self._chunks[chunk.id] = chunk_node
 96: 
 97:         # Update keyword-chunk links
 98:         for keyword in keywords:
 99:             if keyword not in self._keywords:
100:                 self._keywords[keyword] = KeywordNode(keyword=keyword)
101:             self._keywords[keyword].chunk_ids.add(chunk.id)
102: 
103:     def add_chunks_batch(self, chunks: list[TemporalChunk]) -> None:
104:         """Add multiple chunks to the index.
105: 
106:         Args:
107:             chunks: Temporal chunks to add
108:         """
109:         for chunk in chunks:
110:             self.add_chunk(chunk)
111: 
112:     def build_skeleton(self) -> list[UUID]:
113:         """Build the skeleton index and return core chunk IDs.
114: 
115:         Returns:
116:             List of core chunk IDs that should have full KG extraction
117:         """
118:         if not self._chunks:
119:             return []
120: 
121:         # Calculate IDF scores for keywords
122:         self._calculate_idf_scores()
123: 
124:         # Build chunk-to-chunk edges via shared keywords
125:         edges = self._build_chunk_edges()
126: 
127:         # Run PageRank
128:         self._calculate_pagerank(edges)
129: 
130:         # Select core chunks
131:         core_ids = self._select_core_chunks()
132: 
133:         logger.info(
134:             f"Skeleton built: {len(core_ids)}/{len(self._chunks)} core chunks "
135:             f"({len(core_ids) / len(self._chunks) * 100:.1f}%)"
136:         )
137: 
138:         return core_ids
139: 
140:     def get_core_chunks(self) -> list[UUID]:
141:         """Get list of core chunk IDs.
142: 
143:         Returns:
144:             List of chunk IDs marked as core
145:         """
146:         return [cid for cid, node in self._chunks.items() if node.is_core]
147: 
148:     def get_chunks_by_keyword(self, keyword: str) -> list[UUID]:
149:         """Get chunks containing a keyword.
150: 
151:         Args:
152:             keyword: Keyword to search
153: 
154:         Returns:
155:             List of chunk IDs containing the keyword
156:         """
157:         keyword_lower = keyword.lower()
158:         if keyword_lower in self._keywords:
159:             return list(self._keywords[keyword_lower].chunk_ids)
160:         return []
161: 
162:     def get_related_chunks(
163:         self,
164:         chunk_id: UUID,
165:         *,
166:         limit: int = 10,
167:     ) -> list[tuple[UUID, float]]:
168:         """Get chunks related to a given chunk via keyword overlap.
169: 
170:         Args:
171:             chunk_id: Source chunk
172:             limit: Maximum number of related chunks
173: 
174:         Returns:
175:             List of (chunk_id, relevance_score) tuples
176:         """
177:         if chunk_id not in self._chunks:
178:             return []
179: 
180:         source_node = self._chunks[chunk_id]
181:         if not source_node.keywords:
182:             return []
183: 
184:         # Calculate Jaccard-like similarity with IDF weighting
185:         scores: dict[UUID, float] = defaultdict(float)
186: 
187:         for keyword in source_node.keywords:
188:             if keyword not in self._keywords:
189:                 continue
190: 
191:             keyword_node = self._keywords[keyword]
192:             idf = keyword_node.idf_score
193: 
194:             for other_id in keyword_node.chunk_ids:
195:                 if other_id != chunk_id:
196:                     scores[other_id] += idf
197: 
198:         # Normalize and sort
199:         if not scores:
200:             return []
201: 
202:         max_score = max(scores.values())
203:         normalized = [(cid, score / max_score) for cid, score in scores.items()]
204:         normalized.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
205: 
206:         return normalized[:limit]
207: 
208:     def search_by_keywords(
209:         self,
210:         keywords: list[str],
211:         *,
212:         limit: int = 10,
213:     ) -> list[tuple[UUID, float]]:
214:         """Search chunks by multiple keywords.
215: 
216:         Args:
217:             keywords: Keywords to search
218:             limit: Maximum results
219: 
220:         Returns:
221:             List of (chunk_id, relevance_score) tuples
222:         """
223:         scores: dict[UUID, float] = defaultdict(float)
224: 
225:         for keyword in keywords:
226:             keyword_lower = keyword.lower()
227:             if keyword_lower not in self._keywords:
228:                 continue
229: 
230:             keyword_node = self._keywords[keyword_lower]
231:             idf = keyword_node.idf_score
232: 
233:             for chunk_id in keyword_node.chunk_ids:
234:                 scores[chunk_id] += idf
235: 
236:         if not scores:
237:             return []
238: 
239:         # Sort by score
240:         results = [(cid, score) for cid, score in scores.items()]
241:         results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
242: 
243:         return results[:limit]
244: 
245:     def get_pagerank_score(self, chunk_id: UUID) -> float:
246:         """Get PageRank score for a chunk.
247: 
248:         Args:
249:             chunk_id: Chunk ID
250: 
251:         Returns:
252:             PageRank score (0.0 if not found)
253:         """
254:         if chunk_id in self._chunks:
255:             return self._chunks[chunk_id].pagerank_score
256:         return 0.0
257: 
258:     def is_core_chunk(self, chunk_id: UUID) -> bool:
259:         """Check if a chunk is marked as core.
260: 
261:         Args:
262:             chunk_id: Chunk ID
263: 
264:         Returns:
265:             True if chunk is core
266:         """
267:         if chunk_id in self._chunks:
268:             return self._chunks[chunk_id].is_core
269:         return False
270: 
271:     def compute_adaptive_core_ratio(
272:         self,
273:         chunks: list[TemporalChunk] | None = None,
274:         base_ratio: float = 0.10,
275:     ) -> float:
276:         """Compute adaptive core ratio based on content heterogeneity.
277: 
278:         Dynamically adjusts the core chunk ratio based on how diverse the content is:
279:         - Homogeneous content (similar chunks): use base_ratio (0.10)
280:         - Heterogeneous content (diverse chunks): increase to 0.25-0.40
281: 
282:         Heterogeneity is measured by keyword diversity:
283:         - Unique-to-total keyword ratio across chunks
284:         - Low overlap between chunks indicates diverse content needing more core chunks
285: 
286:         This prevents under-extraction in diverse corpora where a fixed 10% might
287:         miss important semantic clusters, while maintaining efficiency for
288:         homogeneous content like chat logs or similar documents.
289: 
290:         Args:
291:             chunks: Optional list of chunks to analyze. If None, uses already-indexed chunks.
292:             base_ratio: Minimum ratio for homogeneous content (default: 0.10)
293: 
294:         Returns:
295:             Adaptive core ratio between base_ratio and 0.40
296:         """
297:         # Use indexed chunks if none provided
298:         if chunks is not None:
299:             # Build temporary index for analysis
300:             chunk_keywords: list[set[str]] = []
301:             for chunk in chunks:
302:                 keywords = self._extract_keywords(chunk.content)
303:                 chunk_keywords.append(keywords)
304:         else:
305:             # Use already indexed chunks
306:             chunk_keywords = [node.keywords for node in self._chunks.values()]
307: 
308:         if len(chunk_keywords) < 2:
309:             return base_ratio
310: 
311:         # Collect all keywords and count occurrences
312:         all_keywords: set[str] = set()
313:         keyword_chunk_count: dict[str, int] = {}
314: 
315:         for keywords in chunk_keywords:
316:             all_keywords.update(keywords)
317:             for kw in keywords:
318:                 keyword_chunk_count[kw] = keyword_chunk_count.get(kw, 0) + 1
319: 
320:         if not all_keywords:
321:             return base_ratio
322: 
323:         n_chunks = len(chunk_keywords)
324:         n_keywords = len(all_keywords)
325: 
326:         # Calculate heterogeneity score (0 to 1)
327:         # Two metrics combined:
328: 
329:         # 1. Unique keyword ratio: keywords appearing in only 1 chunk / total keywords
330:         # High ratio = diverse content, each chunk has unique concepts
331:         unique_keywords = sum(1 for count in keyword_chunk_count.values() if count == 1)
332:         unique_ratio = unique_keywords / n_keywords if n_keywords > 0 else 0
333: 
334:         # 2. Average pairwise Jaccard distance
335:         # High distance = low overlap between chunks = diverse content
336:         # Sample for efficiency on large chunk sets
337:         max_pairs = min(100, n_chunks * (n_chunks - 1) // 2)
338:         pair_distances: list[float] = []
339: 
340:         import random
341: 
342:         indices = list(range(n_chunks))
343:         pairs_checked = 0
344: 
345:         # Use deterministic sampling for reproducibility
346:         random.seed(42)
347:         random.shuffle(indices)
348: 
349:         for i in range(n_chunks):
350:             if pairs_checked >= max_pairs:
351:                 break
352:             for j in range(i + 1, n_chunks):
353:                 if pairs_checked >= max_pairs:
354:                     break
355:                 kw_i = chunk_keywords[indices[i]]
356:                 kw_j = chunk_keywords[indices[j]]
357:                 if kw_i or kw_j:
358:                     intersection = len(kw_i & kw_j)
359:                     union = len(kw_i | kw_j)
360:                     jaccard_similarity = intersection / union if union > 0 else 0
361:                     jaccard_distance = 1 - jaccard_similarity
362:                     pair_distances.append(jaccard_distance)
363:                     pairs_checked += 1
364: 
365:         avg_distance = sum(pair_distances) / len(pair_distances) if pair_distances else 0
366: 
367:         # Combine metrics: weighted average
368:         # unique_ratio is more stable, distance captures local diversity
369:         heterogeneity = 0.6 * unique_ratio + 0.4 * avg_distance
370: 
371:         # Map heterogeneity (0-1) to core ratio (base_ratio to 0.40)
372:         # Using a sigmoid-like curve to smooth the transition
373:         max_ratio = 0.40
374:         ratio_range = max_ratio - base_ratio
375: 
376:         # Smooth scaling: low heterogeneity stays near base, high approaches max
377:         # Using squared scaling for smoother transition
378:         adaptive_ratio = base_ratio + ratio_range * (heterogeneity**0.8)
379: 
380:         # Clamp to valid range
381:         adaptive_ratio = max(base_ratio, min(max_ratio, adaptive_ratio))
382: 
383:         logger.debug(
384:             f"Adaptive core ratio: {adaptive_ratio:.3f} "
385:             f"(heterogeneity={heterogeneity:.3f}, unique_ratio={unique_ratio:.3f}, "
386:             f"avg_distance={avg_distance:.3f})"
387:         )
388: 
389:         return adaptive_ratio
390: 
391:     def build_skeleton_adaptive(self, chunks: list[TemporalChunk] | None = None) -> list[UUID]:
392:         """Build skeleton with adaptive core ratio based on content heterogeneity.
393: 
394:         Combines compute_adaptive_core_ratio with build_skeleton for a single-call
395:         interface that automatically adjusts to content diversity.
396: 
397:         Args:
398:             chunks: Optional list of chunks to add before building. If provided,
399:                     these will be added and analyzed for heterogeneity.
400: 
401:         Returns:
402:             List of core chunk IDs that should have full KG extraction
403:         """
404:         if chunks:
405:             self.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
406: 
407:         # Compute adaptive ratio and update
408:         adaptive_ratio = self.compute_adaptive_core_ratio()
409:         original_ratio = self._core_ratio
410:         self._core_ratio = adaptive_ratio
411: 
412:         logger.info(f"Using adaptive core ratio: {adaptive_ratio:.3f} (original: {original_ratio:.3f})")
413: 
414:         return self.build_skeleton()
415: 
416:     # =========================================================================
417:     # Private methods
418:     # =========================================================================
419: 
420:     def _extract_keywords(self, content: str) -> set[str]:
421:         """Extract keywords from content using simple TF approach.
422: 
423:         Uses stopword filtering and basic normalization.
424:         """
425:         # Simple stopword list (expand as needed)
426:         stopwords = {
427:             "a",
428:             "an",
429:             "the",
430:             "and",
431:             "or",
432:             "but",
433:             "in",
434:             "on",
435:             "at",
436:             "to",
437:             "for",
438:             "of",
439:             "with",
440:             "by",
441:             "from",
442:             "as",
443:             "is",
444:             "was",
445:             "are",
446:             "were",
447:             "been",
448:             "be",
449:             "have",
450:             "has",
451:             "had",
452:             "do",
453:             "does",
454:             "did",
455:             "will",
456:             "would",
457:             "could",
458:             "should",
459:             "may",
460:             "might",
461:             "must",
462:             "that",
463:             "this",
464:             "these",
465:             "those",
466:             "it",
467:             "its",
468:             "he",
469:             "she",
470:             "they",
471:             "them",
472:             "his",
473:             "her",
474:             "their",
475:             "we",
476:             "our",
477:             "you",
478:             "your",
479:             "i",
480:             "me",
481:             "my",
482:             "what",
483:             "which",
484:             "who",
485:             "whom",
486:             "when",
487:             "where",
488:             "why",
489:             "how",
490:             "all",
491:             "each",
492:             "every",
493:             "both",
494:             "few",
495:             "more",
496:             "most",
497:             "other",
498:             "some",
499:             "such",
500:             "no",
501:             "not",
502:             "only",
503:             "own",
504:             "same",
505:             "so",
506:             "than",
507:             "too",
508:             "very",
509:             "just",
510:             "can",
511:         }
512: 
513:         # Tokenize and normalize
514:         import re
515: 
516:         words = re.findall(r"\b[a-zA-Z]{3,}\b", content.lower())
517:         keywords = {w for w in words if w not in stopwords}
518: 
519:         return keywords
520: 
521:     def _calculate_idf_scores(self) -> None:
522:         """Calculate IDF scores for all keywords."""
523:         n_docs = len(self._chunks)
524:         if n_docs == 0:
525:             return
526: 
527:         for keyword_node in self._keywords.values():
528:             df = len(keyword_node.chunk_ids)
529:             keyword_node.idf_score = math.log(n_docs / (1 + df)) + 1
530: 
531:     def _build_chunk_edges(self) -> dict[UUID, list[tuple[UUID, float]]]:
532:         """Build weighted edges between chunks via shared keywords.
533: 
534:         Returns:
535:             Dict mapping chunk_id -> list of (neighbor_id, weight)
536:         """
537:         edges: dict[UUID, list[tuple[UUID, float]]] = defaultdict(list)
538: 
539:         # For each keyword, connect all chunks that share it
540:         for keyword_node in self._keywords.values():
541:             chunk_list = list(keyword_node.chunk_ids)
542:             weight = keyword_node.idf_score
543: 
544:             for i, cid1 in enumerate(chunk_list):
545:                 for cid2 in chunk_list[i + 1 :]:
546:                     edges[cid1].append((cid2, weight))
547:                     edges[cid2].append((cid1, weight))
548: 
549:         return edges
550: 
551:     def _calculate_pagerank(
552:         self,
553:         edges: dict[UUID, list[tuple[UUID, float]]],
554:     ) -> None:
555:         """Calculate PageRank scores for all chunks."""
556:         if not self._chunks:
557:             return
558: 
559:         n = len(self._chunks)
560:         chunk_ids = list(self._chunks.keys())
561: 
562:         # Initialize scores
563:         scores = {cid: 1.0 / n for cid in chunk_ids}
564: 
565:         # Calculate out-degree (sum of weights)
566:         out_degree: dict[UUID, float] = {}
567:         for cid in chunk_ids:
568:             if cid in edges:
569:                 out_degree[cid] = sum(w for _, w in edges[cid])
570:             else:
571:                 out_degree[cid] = 0.0
572: 
573:         # Iterative PageRank
574:         d = self._damping_factor
575:         base = (1 - d) / n
576: 
577:         for iteration in range(self._max_iterations):
578:             new_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
579:             diff = 0.0
580: 
581:             for cid in chunk_ids:
582:                 # Sum contributions from neighbors
583:                 contrib = 0.0
584:                 if cid in edges:
585:                     for neighbor_id, weight in edges[cid]:
586:                         if out_degree[neighbor_id] > 0:
587:                             contrib += scores[neighbor_id] * weight / out_degree[neighbor_id]
588: 
589:                 new_score = base + d * contrib
590:                 diff += abs(new_score - scores[cid])
591:                 new_scores[cid] = new_score
592: 
593:             scores = new_scores
594: 
595:             if diff < self._convergence_threshold:
596:                 logger.debug(f"PageRank converged after {iteration + 1} iterations")
597:                 break
598: 
599:         # Store scores
600:         for cid, score in scores.items():
601:             self._chunks[cid].pagerank_score = score
602: 
603:     def _select_core_chunks(self) -> list[UUID]:
604:         """Select core chunks based on PageRank scores.
605: 
606:         Returns:
607:             List of core chunk IDs
608:         """
609:         if not self._chunks:
610:             return []
611: 
612:         # Sort by PageRank score
613:         sorted_chunks = sorted(
614:             self._chunks.items(),
615:             key=lambda x: x[1].pagerank_score,
616:             reverse=True,
617:         )
618: 
619:         # Select top N%
620:         n_core = max(1, int(len(sorted_chunks) * self._core_ratio))
621:         core_ids = []
622: 
623:         for i, (cid, node) in enumerate(sorted_chunks):
624:             if i < n_core:
625:                 node.is_core = True
626:                 core_ids.append(cid)
627:             else:
628:                 node.is_core = False
629: 
630:         return core_ids
631: 
632: 
633: class LazyEntityExpander:
634:     """Lazy entity expansion for non-core chunks.
635: 
636:     Instead of running LLM extraction on all chunks upfront, this class
637:     provides on-demand extraction when a non-core chunk is retrieved.
638:     """
639: 
640:     def __init__(
641:         self,
642:         skeleton_indexer: SkeletonIndexer,
643:         extraction_model: str = "gpt-4o-mini",
644:     ):
645:         """Initialize the expander.
646: 
647:         Args:
648:             skeleton_indexer: Skeleton indexer for core/non-core classification
649:             extraction_model: LLM model for entity extraction
650:         """
651:         self._skeleton = skeleton_indexer
652:         self._extraction_model = extraction_model
653:         self._expanded_chunks: set[UUID] = set()
654: 
655:     async def maybe_expand(
656:         self,
657:         chunk_id: UUID,
658:         chunk_content: str,
659:     ) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
660:         """Maybe expand a chunk with entity extraction.
661: 
662:         Only extracts if the chunk is not already expanded.
663: 
664:         Args:
665:             chunk_id: Chunk ID
666:             chunk_content: Chunk content
667: 
668:         Returns:
669:             Extraction result or None if already expanded/core
670:         """
671:         # Skip if already expanded
672:         if chunk_id in self._expanded_chunks:
673:             return None
674: 
675:         # Skip if core (should already have extraction)
676:         if self._skeleton.is_core_chunk(chunk_id):
677:             return None
678: 
679:         # Run extraction (simplified for now)
680:         # In production, this would call the full extraction pipeline
681:         logger.debug(f"Lazy expanding non-core chunk {chunk_id}")
682:         self._expanded_chunks.add(chunk_id)
683: 
684:         # Placeholder - return keywords as pseudo-entities
685:         keywords = self._skeleton._extract_keywords(chunk_content)
686:         return {
687:             "entities": [{"name": kw, "type": "KEYWORD"} for kw in list(keywords)[:10]],
688:             "relationships": [],
689:         }
690: 
691: 
692: __all__ = ["ChunkNode", "KeywordNode", "LazyEntityExpander", "SkeletonIndexer"]
````

## File: src/khora/engines/vectorcypher/__init__.py
````python
 1: """VectorCypher engine - hybrid vector+graph retrieval.
 2: 
 3: The VectorCypher engine combines:
 4: - Vector search (pgvector) for entry entity discovery
 5: - Cypher traversal (Neo4j) for relationship expansion
 6: - Smart query routing for optimal performance
 7: - Skeleton-based indexing for cost efficiency
 8: - Bi-temporal edges for temporal reasoning
 9: 
10: Usage:
11:     from khora.engines.vectorcypher import VectorCypherEngine
12: 
13:     engine = VectorCypherEngine(config)
14:     await engine.connect()
15: 
16:     # Store with temporal context
17:     await engine.remember("Meeting notes...", namespace_id, occurred_at=datetime(...))
18: 
19:     # Retrieve with hybrid search
20:     result = await engine.recall("What did we discuss?", namespace_id)
21: """
22: 
23: from .dual_nodes import ChunkNode, DualNodeManager, EntityChunkLink
24: from .engine import VectorCypherConfig, VectorCypherEngine

26:     FusedResult,
27:     apply_recency_boost,
28:     normalize_scores,
29:     reciprocal_rank_fusion,
30:     weighted_rrf,
31:     weighted_rrf_normalized,
32: )
33: from .retriever import RetrieverConfig, VectorCypherResult, VectorCypherRetriever
34: from .router import QueryComplexity, QueryComplexityRouter, RouterConfig, RoutingDecision
35: 
36: __all__ = [
37:     # Engine
38:     "VectorCypherConfig",
39:     "VectorCypherEngine",
40:     # Retriever
41:     "RetrieverConfig",
42:     "VectorCypherResult",
43:     "VectorCypherRetriever",
44:     # Router
45:     "QueryComplexity",
46:     "QueryComplexityRouter",
47:     "RouterConfig",
48:     "RoutingDecision",
49:     # Dual nodes
50:     "ChunkNode",
51:     "DualNodeManager",
52:     "EntityChunkLink",
53:     # Fusion
54:     "FusedResult",
55:     "apply_recency_boost",
56:     "normalize_scores",
57:     "reciprocal_rank_fusion",
58:     "weighted_rrf",
59:     "weighted_rrf_normalized",
60: ]
````

## File: src/khora/engines/vectorcypher/fusion.py
````python
  1: """RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) utilities for combining search results.
  2: 
  3: This module provides utilities for fusing vector and graph search results
  4: using Reciprocal Rank Fusion, a simple but effective method for combining
  5: ranked lists from different retrieval systems.
  6: """
  7: 
  8: from __future__ import annotations
  9: 
 10: from collections import defaultdict
 11: from dataclasses import dataclass
 12: from typing import Any, TypeVar
 13: from uuid import UUID
 14: 
 15: T = TypeVar("T")
 16: 
 17: 
 18: @dataclass
 19: class FusedResult:
 20:     """Result after RRF fusion."""
 21: 
 22:     item_id: UUID
 23:     item: Any
 24:     rrf_score: float
 25:     vector_rank: int | None = None
 26:     graph_rank: int | None = None
 27:     vector_score: float | None = None
 28:     graph_score: float | None = None
 29: 
 30: 
 31: def reciprocal_rank_fusion(
 32:     *result_lists: list[tuple[UUID, float, Any]],
 33:     k: int = 60,
 34: ) -> list[FusedResult]:
 35:     """Combine multiple ranked lists using Reciprocal Rank Fusion.
 36: 
 37:     RRF score = sum(1 / (k + rank_i)) for each list where item appears.
 38: 
 39:     Args:
 40:         *result_lists: Variable number of result lists, each containing
 41:                        tuples of (item_id, score, item_data)
 42:         k: Constant to prevent high-ranked items from dominating (default: 60)
 43: 
 44:     Returns:
 45:         List of FusedResult sorted by RRF score (highest first)
 46:     """
 47:     # Track RRF scores and source information
 48:     rrf_scores: dict[UUID, float] = defaultdict(float)
 49:     items: dict[UUID, Any] = {}
 50:     vector_ranks: dict[UUID, int] = {}
 51:     graph_ranks: dict[UUID, int] = {}
 52:     vector_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
 53:     graph_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
 54: 
 55:     for list_idx, results in enumerate(result_lists):
 56:         for rank, (item_id, score, item) in enumerate(results, start=1):
 57:             rrf_scores[item_id] += 1.0 / (k + rank)
 58:             items[item_id] = item
 59: 
 60:             # Track source-specific information
 61:             if list_idx == 0:  # Vector results
 62:                 vector_ranks[item_id] = rank
 63:                 vector_scores[item_id] = score
 64:             elif list_idx == 1:  # Graph results
 65:                 graph_ranks[item_id] = rank
 66:                 graph_scores[item_id] = score
 67: 
 68:     # Build fused results
 69:     fused = [
 70:         FusedResult(
 71:             item_id=item_id,
 72:             item=items[item_id],
 73:             rrf_score=rrf_score,
 74:             vector_rank=vector_ranks.get(item_id),
 75:             graph_rank=graph_ranks.get(item_id),
 76:             vector_score=vector_scores.get(item_id),
 77:             graph_score=graph_scores.get(item_id),
 78:         )
 79:         for item_id, rrf_score in rrf_scores.items()
 80:     ]
 81: 
 82:     # Sort by RRF score descending
 83:     fused.sort(key=lambda x: x.rrf_score, reverse=True)
 84: 
 85:     return fused
 86: 
 87: 
 88: def weighted_rrf(
 89:     vector_results: list[tuple[UUID, float, Any]],
 90:     graph_results: list[tuple[UUID, float, Any]],
 91:     *,
 92:     k: int = 60,
 93:     vector_weight: float = 0.6,
 94:     graph_weight: float = 0.4,
 95: ) -> list[FusedResult]:
 96:     """Weighted RRF fusion with configurable weights for each source.
 97: 
 98:     Extends RRF with weights to emphasize one source over another.
 99: 
100:     Args:
101:         vector_results: Results from vector search (item_id, score, item)
102:         graph_results: Results from graph traversal (item_id, score, item)
103:         k: RRF constant (default: 60)
104:         vector_weight: Weight for vector results (default: 0.6)
105:         graph_weight: Weight for graph results (default: 0.4)
106: 
107:     Returns:
108:         List of FusedResult sorted by weighted RRF score
109:     """
110:     # Track weighted RRF scores
111:     rrf_scores: dict[UUID, float] = defaultdict(float)
112:     items: dict[UUID, Any] = {}
113:     vector_ranks: dict[UUID, int] = {}
114:     graph_ranks: dict[UUID, int] = {}
115:     vector_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
116:     graph_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
117: 
118:     # Process vector results with weight
119:     for rank, (item_id, score, item) in enumerate(vector_results, start=1):
120:         rrf_scores[item_id] += vector_weight / (k + rank)
121:         items[item_id] = item
122:         vector_ranks[item_id] = rank
123:         vector_scores[item_id] = score
124: 
125:     # Process graph results with weight
126:     for rank, (item_id, score, item) in enumerate(graph_results, start=1):
127:         rrf_scores[item_id] += graph_weight / (k + rank)
128:         if item_id not in items:
129:             items[item_id] = item
130:         graph_ranks[item_id] = rank
131:         graph_scores[item_id] = score
132: 
133:     # Build fused results
134:     fused = [
135:         FusedResult(
136:             item_id=item_id,
137:             item=items[item_id],
138:             rrf_score=rrf_score,
139:             vector_rank=vector_ranks.get(item_id),
140:             graph_rank=graph_ranks.get(item_id),
141:             vector_score=vector_scores.get(item_id),
142:             graph_score=graph_scores.get(item_id),
143:         )
144:         for item_id, rrf_score in rrf_scores.items()
145:     ]
146: 
147:     # Sort by weighted RRF score descending
148:     fused.sort(key=lambda x: x.rrf_score, reverse=True)
149: 
150:     return fused
151: 
152: 
153: def normalize_scores(results: list[FusedResult]) -> list[FusedResult]:
154:     """Normalize RRF scores to [0, 1] range.
155: 
156:     Args:
157:         results: List of FusedResult to normalize
158: 
159:     Returns:
160:         Same list with normalized rrf_score values
161:     """
162:     if not results:
163:         return results
164: 
165:     max_score = max(r.rrf_score for r in results)
166:     min_score = min(r.rrf_score for r in results)
167: 
168:     if max_score == min_score:
169:         for r in results:
170:             r.rrf_score = 1.0
171:     else:
172:         for r in results:
173:             r.rrf_score = (r.rrf_score - min_score) / (max_score - min_score)
174: 
175:     return results
176: 
177: 
178: def _min_max_normalize(scores: list[float]) -> list[float]:
179:     """Min-max normalize a list of scores to [0, 1] range.
180: 
181:     Args:
182:         scores: List of scores to normalize
183: 
184:     Returns:
185:         Normalized scores in same order
186:     """
187:     if not scores:
188:         return scores
189: 
190:     min_score = min(scores)
191:     max_score = max(scores)
192: 
193:     if max_score == min_score:
194:         return [1.0] * len(scores)
195: 
196:     return [(s - min_score) / (max_score - min_score) for s in scores]
197: 
198: 
199: def weighted_rrf_normalized(
200:     vector_results: list[tuple[UUID, float, Any]],
201:     graph_results: list[tuple[UUID, float, Any]],
202:     *,
203:     k: int = 60,
204:     vector_weight: float = 0.6,
205:     graph_weight: float = 0.4,
206: ) -> list[FusedResult]:
207:     """Weighted RRF with score normalization before fusion.
208: 
209:     This improved fusion method:
210:     1. Normalizes vector and graph scores to [0, 1] before computing RRF
211:     2. Uses normalized scores as a tiebreaker for same-rank items
212:     3. Produces more balanced fusion when score distributions differ
213: 
214:     The normalization prevents one source from dominating when its scores
215:     are on a different scale (e.g., cosine similarity 0-1 vs graph scores 1-N).
216: 
217:     Args:
218:         vector_results: Results from vector search (item_id, score, item)
219:         graph_results: Results from graph traversal (item_id, score, item)
220:         k: RRF constant (default: 60)
221:         vector_weight: Weight for vector results (default: 0.6)
222:         graph_weight: Weight for graph results (default: 0.4)
223: 
224:     Returns:
225:         List of FusedResult sorted by weighted normalized RRF score
226:     """
227:     # Track scores and item data
228:     rrf_scores: dict[UUID, float] = defaultdict(float)
229:     score_contributions: dict[UUID, float] = defaultdict(float)  # Normalized score contributions
230:     items: dict[UUID, Any] = {}
231:     vector_ranks: dict[UUID, int] = {}
232:     graph_ranks: dict[UUID, int] = {}
233:     vector_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
234:     graph_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
235: 
236:     # Normalize vector scores
237:     if vector_results:
238:         raw_vector_scores = [score for _, score, _ in vector_results]
239:         normalized_vector_scores = _min_max_normalize(raw_vector_scores)
240: 
241:         for rank, ((item_id, score, item), norm_score) in enumerate(
242:             zip(vector_results, normalized_vector_scores), start=1
243:         ):
````
