````python
468: class TestAttributeNormalization:
469:     """Tests for attribute normalization helper functions."""
470: 
471:     def test_normalize_email(self) -> None:
472:         """Email normalization handles various formats."""
473:         assert _normalize_email("Alice@Example.COM") == "alice@example.com"
474:         assert _normalize_email("  bob@test.org  ") == "bob@test.org"
475:         assert _normalize_email(None) is None
476:         assert _normalize_email("") is None
477: 
478:     def test_normalize_domain(self) -> None:
479:         """Domain normalization strips protocol and www."""
480:         assert _normalize_domain("https://www.example.com/") == "example.com"
481:         assert _normalize_domain("http://example.com") == "example.com"
482:         assert _normalize_domain("www.example.com") == "example.com"
483:         assert _normalize_domain("example.com") == "example.com"
484:         assert _normalize_domain(None) is None
485:         assert _normalize_domain("") is None
486: 
487:     def test_parse_coordinates(self) -> None:
488:         """Coordinate parsing handles various formats."""
489:         assert _parse_coordinates("40.7128, -74.0060") == (40.7128, -74.0060)
490:         assert _parse_coordinates("40.7128,-74.0060") == (40.7128, -74.0060)
491:         assert _parse_coordinates("(40.7128, -74.0060)") == (40.7128, -74.0060)
492:         assert _parse_coordinates(None) is None
493:         assert _parse_coordinates("") is None
494:         assert _parse_coordinates("invalid") is None
495: 
496:     def test_coordinates_distance(self) -> None:
497:         """Coordinate distance calculation is reasonably accurate."""
498:         # NYC to LA is roughly 3940 km
499:         nyc = (40.7128, -74.0060)
500:         la = (34.0522, -118.2437)
501:         distance = _coordinates_distance(nyc, la)
502:         assert 3900 < distance < 4000
503: 
504:         # Same point should be 0
505:         assert _coordinates_distance(nyc, nyc) == 0.0
506: 
507: 
508: class TestPerTypeMergeThresholds:
509:     """Tests for per-type merge thresholds."""
510: 
511:     def test_default_thresholds_exist(self) -> None:
512:         """Default thresholds are defined for common types."""
513:         assert "PERSON" in DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS
514:         assert "ORGANIZATION" in DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS
515:         assert "LOCATION" in DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS
516:         assert "CONCEPT" in DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS
517: 
518:     def test_person_threshold_highest(self) -> None:
519:         """PERSON has highest threshold to avoid false merges."""
520:         assert DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS["PERSON"] >= DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS["CONCEPT"]
521:         assert DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS["PERSON"] >= DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS["LOCATION"]
522: 
523:     def test_resolver_uses_per_type_thresholds(self) -> None:
524:         """Resolver uses per-type thresholds."""
525:         storage = MagicMock()
526:         resolver = EntityResolver(storage, embedder=None)
527: 
528:         # Check threshold retrieval
529:         assert resolver._get_merge_threshold("PERSON") == DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS["PERSON"]
530:         assert resolver._get_merge_threshold("CONCEPT") == DEFAULT_MERGE_THRESHOLDS["CONCEPT"]
531:         assert resolver._get_merge_threshold("UNKNOWN") == 0.85  # Default
532: 
533:     def test_custom_thresholds(self) -> None:
534:         """Custom thresholds override defaults."""
535:         storage = MagicMock()
536:         custom = {"PERSON": 0.99, "CUSTOM_TYPE": 0.50}
537:         resolver = EntityResolver(storage, embedder=None, merge_thresholds=custom)
538: 
539:         assert resolver._get_merge_threshold("PERSON") == 0.99
540:         assert resolver._get_merge_threshold("CUSTOM_TYPE") == 0.50
541:         assert resolver._get_merge_threshold("UNKNOWN") == 0.85
542: 
543:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
544:     async def test_per_type_threshold_affects_merge_decision(self) -> None:
545:         """Per-type threshold affects should_merge decision."""
546:         ns_id = uuid4()
547:         # Create entity with fuzzy-matchable name
548:         existing = _make_entity("Alexander Hamilton", namespace_id=ns_id)
549: 
550:         storage = MagicMock()
551:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
552: 
553:         # With low threshold (CONCEPT), fuzzy match should merge
554:         resolver = EntityResolver(
555:             storage,
556:             merge_thresholds={"CONCEPT": 0.75},
557:             fuzzy_threshold=0.80,
558:         )
559:         result = await resolver.resolve("Alexandr Hamilton", "CONCEPT", ns_id)
560:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
561:         assert result.should_merge is True
562: 
563:         # With very high threshold (0.99), same fuzzy match should not merge
564:         # "Alexandr Hamilton" vs "Alexander Hamilton" has ~0.97 score
565:         resolver2 = EntityResolver(
566:             storage,
567:             merge_thresholds={"PERSON": 0.99},
568:             fuzzy_threshold=0.80,
569:         )
570:         result2 = await resolver2.resolve("Alexandr Hamilton", "PERSON", ns_id)
571:         assert result2.is_duplicate is True
572:         assert result2.should_merge is False  # Score (~0.97) too low for 0.99 threshold
573: 
574: 
575: class TestAttributeMatching:
576:     """Tests for attribute-level matching."""
577: 
578:     def _make_resolver(self, entities: list[Entity] | None = None) -> tuple[EntityResolver, MagicMock]:
579:         """Create resolver with mock storage."""
580:         storage = MagicMock()
581:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=entities or [])
582:         resolver = EntityResolver(storage, embedder=None, attribute_match=True)
583:         return resolver, storage
584: 
585:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
586:     async def test_person_email_match(self) -> None:
587:         """PERSON entities match on email."""
588:         ns_id = uuid4()
589:         existing = _make_entity(
590:             "Alice Smith",
591:             namespace_id=ns_id,
592:             attributes={"email": "alice@example.com"},
593:         )
594:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
595: 
596:         result = await resolver.resolve(
597:             "A. Smith",  # Different name
598:             "PERSON",
599:             ns_id,
600:             attributes={"email": "alice@example.com"},  # Same email
601:         )
602:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
603:         assert result.match_type == "attribute"
604:         assert result.match_score == 1.0
605: 
606:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
607:     async def test_person_different_email_no_match(self) -> None:
608:         """PERSON entities with different emails don't match well."""
609:         ns_id = uuid4()
610:         existing = _make_entity(
611:             "Alice Smith",
612:             namespace_id=ns_id,
613:             attributes={"email": "alice@example.com"},
614:         )
615:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
616: 
617:         result = await resolver.resolve(
618:             "Alice Smith",  # Same name
619:             "PERSON",
620:             ns_id,
621:             attributes={"email": "alice.smith@different.com"},  # Different email
622:         )
623:         # Different email for same name suggests different person
624:         # Exact name match should still work
625:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
626:         assert result.match_type == "exact"
627: 
628:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
629:     async def test_organization_domain_match(self) -> None:
630:         """ORGANIZATION entities match on domain."""
631:         ns_id = uuid4()
632:         existing = _make_entity(
633:             "Acme Corporation",
634:             EntityType.ORGANIZATION,
635:             namespace_id=ns_id,
636:             attributes={"website": "https://www.acme.com"},
637:         )
638:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
639: 
640:         result = await resolver.resolve(
641:             "ACME Corp",  # Different name variant
642:             "ORGANIZATION",
643:             ns_id,
644:             attributes={"domain": "acme.com"},  # Same domain
645:         )
646:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
647:         assert result.match_type == "attribute"
648:         assert result.match_score == 1.0
649: 
650:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
651:     async def test_location_coordinates_match(self) -> None:
652:         """LOCATION entities match on nearby coordinates."""
653:         ns_id = uuid4()
654:         existing = _make_entity(
655:             "Times Square",
656:             EntityType.LOCATION,
657:             namespace_id=ns_id,
658:             attributes={"coordinates": "40.7580, -73.9855"},
659:         )
660:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
661: 
662:         result = await resolver.resolve(
663:             "Times Sq",
664:             "LOCATION",
665:             ns_id,
666:             attributes={"coordinates": "40.7581, -73.9856"},  # Very close
667:         )
668:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
669:         assert result.match_type == "attribute"
670:         assert result.match_score == 1.0
671: 
672:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
673:     async def test_technology_vendor_match(self) -> None:
674:         """TECHNOLOGY entities match on vendor."""
675:         ns_id = uuid4()
676:         existing = _make_entity(
677:             "TensorFlow",
678:             EntityType.TECHNOLOGY,
679:             namespace_id=ns_id,
680:             attributes={"vendor": "Google", "type": "framework"},
681:         )
682:         resolver, _ = self._make_resolver([existing])
683: 
684:         result = await resolver.resolve(
685:             "Tensorflow",  # Different case
686:             "TECHNOLOGY",
687:             ns_id,
688:             attributes={"vendor": "Google", "type": "framework"},
689:         )
690:         # Exact name match should take precedence
691:         assert result.is_duplicate is True
692: 
693:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
694:     async def test_attribute_match_disabled(self) -> None:
695:         """Attribute matching can be disabled."""
696:         ns_id = uuid4()
697:         existing = _make_entity(
698:             "Different Name",
699:             namespace_id=ns_id,
700:             attributes={"email": "same@example.com"},
701:         )
702: 
703:         storage = MagicMock()
704:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
705: 
706:         resolver = EntityResolver(
707:             storage,
708:             attribute_match=False,
709:             exact_match=False,
710:             alias_match=False,
711:             fuzzy_match=False,
712:         )
713:         result = await resolver.resolve(
714:             "Another Name",
715:             "PERSON",
716:             ns_id,
717:             attributes={"email": "same@example.com"},
718:         )
719:         # Should not match without attribute matching
720:         assert result.is_duplicate is False
721: 
722: 
723: class TestResolutionMetrics:
724:     """Tests for resolution quality metrics."""
725: 
726:     def test_metrics_creation(self) -> None:
727:         """Metrics can be created and have correct defaults."""
728:         metrics = ResolutionMetrics()
729:         assert metrics.total_resolutions == 0
730:         assert metrics.exact_matches == 0
731:         assert metrics.new_entities == 0
732:         assert metrics.average_merge_confidence == 0.0
733: 
734:     def test_record_resolution(self) -> None:
735:         """Recording resolutions updates counters correctly."""
736:         metrics = ResolutionMetrics()
737: 
738:         metrics.record_resolution("PERSON", "exact", 1.0, True)
739:         assert metrics.total_resolutions == 1
740:         assert metrics.exact_matches == 1
741:         assert metrics.matches_by_type["PERSON"] == 1
742:         assert metrics.merges_by_type["PERSON"] == 1
743: 
744:         metrics.record_resolution("ORGANIZATION", "fuzzy", 0.88, True)
745:         assert metrics.total_resolutions == 2
746:         assert metrics.fuzzy_matches == 1
747:         assert metrics.low_confidence_merges == 1  # 0.88 < 0.9
748: 
749:     def test_record_new_entity(self) -> None:
750:         """Recording new entity (no match) updates correctly."""
751:         metrics = ResolutionMetrics()
752: 
753:         metrics.record_resolution("PERSON", "", 0.0, False)
754:         assert metrics.new_entities == 1
755:         assert metrics.total_resolutions == 1
756: 
757:     def test_average_merge_confidence(self) -> None:
758:         """Average merge confidence is calculated correctly."""
759:         metrics = ResolutionMetrics()
760: 
761:         metrics.record_resolution("PERSON", "exact", 1.0, True)
762:         metrics.record_resolution("PERSON", "fuzzy", 0.9, True)
763: 
764:         assert metrics.average_merge_confidence == 0.95
765: 
766:     def test_match_rate_by_type(self) -> None:
767:         """Match rate by type is calculated correctly."""
768:         metrics = ResolutionMetrics()
769: 
770:         # PERSON: 2 resolutions, 1 merge = 50%
771:         metrics.record_resolution("PERSON", "exact", 1.0, True)
772:         metrics.record_resolution("PERSON", "", 0.0, False)
773: 
774:         # ORG: 1 resolution, 1 merge = 100%
775:         metrics.record_resolution("ORGANIZATION", "alias", 0.95, True)
776: 
777:         rates = metrics.match_rate_by_type
778:         assert rates["PERSON"] == 0.5
779:         assert rates["ORGANIZATION"] == 1.0
780: 
781:     def test_resolver_tracks_metrics(self) -> None:
782:         """Resolver tracks metrics when enabled."""
783:         storage = MagicMock()
784:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
785: 
786:         resolver = EntityResolver(storage, embedder=None, track_metrics=True)
787:         assert resolver.metrics is not None
788:         assert isinstance(resolver.metrics, ResolutionMetrics)
789: 
790:     def test_resolver_no_metrics_by_default(self) -> None:
791:         """Resolver does not track metrics by default."""
792:         storage = MagicMock()
793:         resolver = EntityResolver(storage, embedder=None)
794:         assert resolver.metrics is None
795: 
796:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
797:     async def test_resolver_records_metrics(self) -> None:
798:         """Resolver records metrics on resolution."""
799:         ns_id = uuid4()
800:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
801: 
802:         storage = MagicMock()
803:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
804: 
805:         resolver = EntityResolver(storage, embedder=None, track_metrics=True)
806: 
807:         await resolver.resolve("Alice", "PERSON", ns_id)
808:         await resolver.resolve("Bob", "PERSON", ns_id)
809: 
810:         assert resolver.metrics is not None
811:         assert resolver.metrics.total_resolutions == 2
812:         assert resolver.metrics.exact_matches == 1
813:         assert resolver.metrics.new_entities == 1
814: 
815: 
816: class TestMergeProvenance:
817:     """Tests for merge provenance tracking."""
818: 
819:     def test_merge_source_creation(self) -> None:
820:         """MergeSource can be created and converted to dict."""
821:         source = MergeSource(
822:             entity_id="abc-123",
823:             score=0.95,
824:             match_type="fuzzy",
825:             source_tool="slack",
826:         )
827: 
828:         d = source.to_dict()
829:         assert d["entity_id"] == "abc-123"
830:         assert d["score"] == 0.95
831:         assert d["match_type"] == "fuzzy"
832:         assert d["source_tool"] == "slack"
833: 
834:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
835:     async def test_merge_tracks_provenance(self) -> None:
836:         """Merging entities tracks provenance in metadata."""
837:         ns_id = uuid4()
838:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
839: 
840:         storage = MagicMock()
841:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
842: 
843:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
844:             "Alice",
845:             "PERSON",
846:             ns_id,
847:             storage,
848:             source_tool="slack",
849:             track_provenance=True,
850:         )
851: 
852:         assert is_new is False
853:         assert "merge_sources" in entity.metadata
854:         sources = entity.metadata["merge_sources"]
855:         assert len(sources) == 1
856:         assert sources[0]["source_tool"] == "slack"
857:         assert sources[0]["match_type"] == "exact"
858:         assert sources[0]["score"] == 1.0
859: 
860:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
861:     async def test_merge_accumulates_provenance(self) -> None:
862:         """Multiple merges accumulate provenance."""
863:         ns_id = uuid4()
864:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
865:         existing.metadata["merge_sources"] = [
866:             {"entity_id": "prev-1", "score": 0.95, "match_type": "fuzzy", "source_tool": "gmail"}
867:         ]
868: 
869:         storage = MagicMock()
870:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
871: 
872:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
873:             "Alice",
874:             "PERSON",
875:             ns_id,
876:             storage,
877:             source_tool="slack",
878:             track_provenance=True,
879:         )
880: 
881:         assert is_new is False
882:         sources = entity.metadata["merge_sources"]
883:         assert len(sources) == 2
884:         assert sources[0]["source_tool"] == "gmail"
885:         assert sources[1]["source_tool"] == "slack"
886: 
887:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
888:     async def test_provenance_disabled(self) -> None:
889:         """Provenance tracking can be disabled."""
890:         ns_id = uuid4()
891:         existing = _make_entity("Alice", namespace_id=ns_id)
892: 
893:         storage = MagicMock()
894:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[existing])
895: 
896:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
897:             "Alice",
898:             "PERSON",
899:             ns_id,
900:             storage,
901:             source_tool="slack",
902:             track_provenance=False,
903:         )
904: 
905:         assert is_new is False
906:         assert "merge_sources" not in entity.metadata
907: 
908:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
909:     async def test_new_entity_has_source_tool(self) -> None:
910:         """New entities have source_tool set."""
911:         ns_id = uuid4()
912: 
913:         storage = MagicMock()
914:         storage.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
915: 
916:         entity, is_new = await resolve_and_merge_entity(
917:             "NewPerson",
918:             "PERSON",
919:             ns_id,
920:             storage,
921:             source_tool="confluence",
922:         )
923: 
924:         assert is_new is True
925:         assert entity.source_tool == "confluence"
````

````python
   1: """Unit tests for memory_lake.py — MemoryLake primary API."""

   3: from __future__ import annotations

   5: import warnings
   6: from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

   9: import pytest

  11: from khora.memory_lake import BatchResult, MemoryLake, RecallResult, RememberResult, Stats

  13: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  14: # Helpers

  17: 
  18: def _mock_config() -> MagicMock:
  19:     """Create a mock KhoraConfig with all required methods."""
  20:     mock_config = MagicMock()
  21:     mock_config.get_postgresql_url.return_value = "postgresql://test"
  22:     mock_config.get_graph_config.return_value = None
  23:     mock_config.get_vector_config.return_value = None
  24:     mock_config.get_neo4j_url.return_value = None
  25:     mock_config.get_neo4j_user.return_value = None
  26:     mock_config.get_neo4j_password.return_value = None
  27:     mock_config.get_neo4j_database.return_value = None
  28:     mock_config.storage.embedding_dimension = 1536
  29:     mock_config.llm.model = "gpt-4o-mini"
  30:     mock_config.llm.embedding_model = "text-embedding-3-small"
  31:     mock_config.llm.embedding_dimension = 1536
  32:     mock_config.llm.extraction_model = None
  33:     mock_config.llm.timeout = 30
  34:     mock_config.llm.max_retries = 3
  35:     mock_config.telemetry_database_url = None
  36:     mock_config.telemetry_service_name = "khora-test"
  37:     return mock_config
  38: 
  39: 
  40: def _mock_engine() -> MagicMock:
  41:     """Create a mock engine with all required methods."""
  42:     mock_eng = MagicMock()
  43: 
  44:     # Storage and query engine for deprecated property tests
  45:     mock_eng._storage = MagicMock()
  46:     mock_eng._query_engine = MagicMock()
  47:     mock_eng._embedder = MagicMock()
  48: 
  49:     # Default namespace ID
  50:     mock_eng._default_namespace_id = None
  51: 
  52:     # Lifecycle
  53:     mock_eng.connect = AsyncMock()
  54:     mock_eng.disconnect = AsyncMock()
  55:     mock_eng.health_check = AsyncMock(return_value={"status": "healthy"})
  56: 
  57:     # Core operations
  58:     mock_eng.remember = AsyncMock()
  59:     mock_eng.recall = AsyncMock()
  60:     mock_eng.forget = AsyncMock()
  61:     mock_eng.remember_batch = AsyncMock()
  62: 
  63:     # Namespace operations
  64:     mock_eng.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=uuid4())
  65:     mock_eng.create_namespace = AsyncMock()
  66:     mock_eng.get_namespace = AsyncMock()
  67:     mock_eng.ensure_namespace = AsyncMock()
  68: 
  69:     # Entity operations
  70:     mock_eng.get_entity = AsyncMock()
  71:     mock_eng.list_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
  72:     mock_eng.find_related_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
  73: 
  74:     # Document operations
  75:     mock_eng.get_document = AsyncMock()
  76:     mock_eng.list_documents = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
  77:     mock_eng.search_entities = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
  78: 
  79:     # Stats
  80:     mock_eng.stats = AsyncMock()
  81: 
  82:     return mock_eng
  83: 
  84: 
  85: def _make_lake(*, connected: bool = False) -> MemoryLake:
  86:     """Create a MemoryLake with mocked config, optionally pre-connected."""
  87:     with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config", return_value=_mock_config()):
  88:         lake = MemoryLake()
  89: 
  90:     if connected:
  91:         lake._connected = True
  92:         lake._engine = _mock_engine()
  93: 
  94:     return lake
  95:

  97: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  98: # RememberResult / RecallResult dataclass tests

 101: 
 102: class TestRememberResult:
 103:     """Tests for RememberResult dataclass."""
 104: 
 105:     def test_fields(self) -> None:
 106:         """All fields are accessible."""
 107:         r = RememberResult(
 108:             document_id=uuid4(),
 109:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
 110:             chunks_created=5,
 111:             entities_extracted=3,
 112:             relationships_created=2,
 113:         )
 114:         assert r.chunks_created == 5
 115:         assert r.entities_extracted == 3
 116:         assert r.relationships_created == 2
 117:         assert r.metadata == {}
 118: 
 119:     def test_custom_metadata(self) -> None:
 120:         """Custom metadata can be set."""
 121:         r = RememberResult(
 122:             document_id=uuid4(),
 123:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
 124:             chunks_created=0,
 125:             entities_extracted=0,
 126:             relationships_created=0,
 127:             metadata={"duplicate": True},
 128:         )
 129:         assert r.metadata["duplicate"] is True
 130: 
 131: 
 132: class TestRecallResult:
 133:     """Tests for RecallResult dataclass."""
 134: 
 135:     def test_fields(self) -> None:
 136:         """All fields are accessible."""
 137:         ns_id = uuid4()
 138:         r = RecallResult(
 139:             query="test query",
 140:             namespace_id=ns_id,
 141:             chunks=[("chunk1", 0.9)],
 142:             entities=[("entity1", 0.8)],
 143:             context_text="some text",
 144:         )
 145:         assert r.query == "test query"
 146:         assert r.namespace_id == ns_id

 148:         assert len(r.entities) == 1
 149:         assert r.context_text == "some text"

 151:     def test_default_metadata(self) -> None:
 152:         """Default metadata is empty dict."""

 155:             namespace_id=uuid4(),
 156:             chunks=[],
 157:             entities=[],
 158:             context_text="",

 163: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 164: # MemoryLake initialization

 166: 
 167: 
 168: class TestMemoryLakeInit:
 169:     """Tests for MemoryLake initialization."""
 170: 
 171:     def test_init_default(self) -> None:
 172:         """Default init loads config from env."""
 173:         lake = _make_lake()
 174:         assert lake._connected is False
 175:         assert lake._engine is None
 176: 
 177:     def test_init_with_config(self) -> None:
 178:         """Init with explicit config skips load_config."""
 179:         from khora.config import KhoraConfig
 180: 
 181:         # Create a real KhoraConfig (not a mock) to trigger the isinstance check
 182:         cfg = KhoraConfig(database_url="postgresql://test")
 183:         lake = MemoryLake(cfg)
 184: 
 185:         assert lake._config is cfg
 186:         assert lake._config.database_url == "postgresql://test"
 187: 
 188:     def test_init_with_storage_config(self) -> None:
 189:         """Init with explicit storage_config uses it directly."""
 190:         storage_cfg = MagicMock()
 191:         with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config", return_value=_mock_config()):
 192:             lake = MemoryLake(storage_config=storage_cfg)
 193:         assert lake._storage_config is storage_cfg
 194: 
 195:     def test_not_connected_properties_raise(self) -> None:
 196:         """Accessing storage/query_engine before connect raises."""
 197:         lake = _make_lake()
 198: 
 199:         with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="not connected"):
 200:             _ = lake.storage
 201: 
 202:         with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="not connected"):
 203:             _ = lake.query_engine
 204: 
 205:     def test_connected_properties_return(self) -> None:
 206:         """Accessing storage/query_engine after connect succeeds."""
 207:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 208:         # The deprecated properties access _storage and _query_engine from the engine
 209:         with warnings.catch_warnings():
 210:             warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)
 211:             assert lake.storage is lake._engine._storage
 212:             assert lake.query_engine is lake._engine._query_engine
 213: 
 214: 
 215: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 216: # connect / disconnect lifecycle
 217: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 218: 
 219: 
 220: class TestConnectDisconnect:
 221:     """Tests for connect() and disconnect() lifecycle."""
 222: 
 223:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 224:     async def test_connect(self) -> None:
 225:         """connect() creates engine and sets flag."""
 226:         lake = _make_lake()
 227: 
 228:         mock_engine = _mock_engine()
 229: 
 230:         with patch("khora.engines.create_engine", return_value=mock_engine):
 231:             await lake.connect()
 232: 
 233:         assert lake._connected is True
 234:         assert lake._engine is mock_engine
 235:         mock_engine.connect.assert_awaited_once()
 236: 
 237:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 238:     async def test_connect_idempotent(self) -> None:
 239:         """Calling connect() when already connected is a no-op."""
 240:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 241:         original_engine = lake._engine
 242: 
 243:         await lake.connect()
 244: 
 245:         assert lake._engine is original_engine
 246: 
 247:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 248:     async def test_disconnect(self) -> None:
 249:         """disconnect() tears down all components."""
 250:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 251: 
 252:         await lake.disconnect()
 253: 
 254:         assert lake._connected is False
 255:         assert lake._engine is None
 256: 
 257:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 258:     async def test_disconnect_idempotent(self) -> None:
 259:         """Calling disconnect() when not connected is a no-op."""
 260:         lake = _make_lake()
 261:         await lake.disconnect()  # Should not raise
 262:         assert lake._connected is False
 263: 
 264:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 265:     async def test_context_manager(self) -> None:
 266:         """async with MemoryLake() connects and disconnects."""
 267:         lake = _make_lake()
 268:         lake.connect = AsyncMock()
 269:         lake.disconnect = AsyncMock()
 270: 
 271:         async with lake as ctx:
 272:             assert ctx is lake
 273:             lake.connect.assert_awaited_once()
 274: 
 275:         lake.disconnect.assert_awaited_once()
 276:

 385: 
 386: 
 387: class TestRememberBatchLegacy:
 388:     """Tests for remember_batch_legacy() which returns list[RememberResult]."""
 389: 
 390:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 391:     async def test_empty_batch(self) -> None:
 392:         """Empty batch returns empty list."""
 393:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 394:         lake._engine.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=uuid4())
 395: 
 396:         with warnings.catch_warnings():
 397:             warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)
 398:             result = await lake.remember_batch_legacy([])
 399: 
 400:         assert result == []
 401: 
 402:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 403:     async def test_batch_returns_results(self) -> None:
 404:         """remember_batch_legacy() returns one RememberResult per document."""
 405:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 406:         ns_id = uuid4()
 407:         lake._engine.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=ns_id)
 408:         lake._config = _mock_config()
 409: 
 410:         doc_id_1 = str(uuid4())
 411:         doc_id_2 = str(uuid4())
 412:         ingest_result = {
 413:             "per_document_results": [
 414:                 {"document_id": doc_id_1, "chunks": 3, "entities": 1, "relationships": 0},
 415:                 {"document_id": doc_id_2, "chunks": 2, "entities": 0, "relationships": 0},
 416:             ],
 417:             "failed_documents": 0,
 418:         }
 419: 
 420:         with (
 421:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.ensure_trace_id"),
 422:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.clear_trace_id"),
 423:             patch("khora.pipelines.flows.ingest.ingest_documents", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=ingest_result),
 424:             warnings.catch_warnings(),
 425:         ):
 426:             warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)
 427:             results = await lake.remember_batch_legacy(
 428:                 [
 429:                     {"content": "Doc 1", "title": "First"},
 430:                     {"content": "Doc 2", "title": "Second"},
 431:                 ]
 432:             )
 433: 
 434:         assert len(results) == 2
 435:         assert results[0].document_id == UUID(doc_id_1)
 436:         assert results[0].chunks_created == 3
 437:         assert results[1].document_id == UUID(doc_id_2)
 438:         assert results[1].chunks_created == 2
 439: 
 440:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 441:     async def test_batch_with_failures(self) -> None:
 442:         """Failed documents get padded with error results."""
 443:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 444:         ns_id = uuid4()
 445:         lake._engine.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=ns_id)
 446:         lake._config = _mock_config()
 447: 
 448:         doc_id = str(uuid4())
 449:         ingest_result = {
 450:             "per_document_results": [
 451:                 {"document_id": doc_id, "chunks": 1, "entities": 0, "relationships": 0},
 452:             ],
 453:             "failed_documents": 1,
 454:         }
 455: 
 456:         with (
 457:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.ensure_trace_id"),
 458:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.clear_trace_id"),
 459:             patch("khora.pipelines.flows.ingest.ingest_documents", new_callable=AsyncMock, return_value=ingest_result),
 460:             warnings.catch_warnings(),
 461:         ):
 462:             warnings.simplefilter("ignore", DeprecationWarning)
 463:             results = await lake.remember_batch_legacy(
 464:                 [
 465:                     {"content": "Good doc"},
 466:                     {"content": "Bad doc"},
 467:                 ]
 468:             )
 469: 
 470:         assert len(results) == 2
 471:         assert results[1].metadata.get("failed") is True
 472:         assert results[1].chunks_created == 0
 473:

 479: 
 480: class TestRecall:
 481:     """Tests for recall()."""
 482: 
 483:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 484:     async def test_recall_delegates_to_engine(self) -> None:
 485:         """recall() delegates to engine.recall() and returns result."""
 486:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 487:         ns_id = uuid4()
 488:         lake._engine.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=ns_id)
 489: 
 490:         mock_result = RecallResult(
 491:             query="search query",
 492:             namespace_id=ns_id,
 493:             chunks=[("chunk", 0.9)],
 494:             entities=[("entity", 0.8)],
 495:             context_text="found content",
 496:             metadata={"mode": "HYBRID"},
 497:         )
 498:         lake._engine.recall = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_result)
 499: 
 500:         with (
 501:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.ensure_trace_id"),
 502:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.clear_trace_id"),
 503:         ):
 504:             result = await lake.recall("search query")
 505: 
 506:         assert isinstance(result, RecallResult)
 507:         assert result.query == "search query"
 508:         lake._engine.recall.assert_awaited_once()
 509: 
 510:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 511:     async def test_recall_passes_search_mode(self) -> None:
 512:         """recall() passes mode to engine."""
 513:         from khora.query.engine import SearchMode
 514: 
 515:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 516:         ns_id = uuid4()
 517:         lake._engine.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=ns_id)
 518: 
 519:         mock_result = RecallResult(
 520:             query="test",
 521:             namespace_id=ns_id,
 522:             chunks=[],
 523:             entities=[],
 524:             context_text="",
 525:         )
 526:         lake._engine.recall = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_result)
 527: 
 528:         with (
 529:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.ensure_trace_id"),
 530:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.clear_trace_id"),
 531:         ):
 532:             await lake.recall("test", mode=SearchMode.VECTOR)
 533: 
 534:         call_kwargs = lake._engine.recall.call_args
 535:         assert call_kwargs.kwargs.get("mode") == SearchMode.VECTOR
 536:

 543: class TestForget:

 546:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 547:     async def test_forget_delegates_to_engine(self) -> None:
 548:         """forget() delegates to engine.forget()."""
 549:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)

 552:         lake._engine.forget = AsyncMock(return_value=True)

 558:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 559:     async def test_forget_with_namespace(self) -> None:

 566: 
 567:         result = await lake.forget(doc_id, namespace=ns_id)
 568:         assert result is False
 569:         lake._engine.forget.assert_awaited_once_with(doc_id, ns_id)

 697: # New API: Simplified Constructor

 699: 
 700: 
 701: class TestSimplifiedConstructor:
 702:     """Tests for the simplified MemoryLake constructor."""
 703: 
 704:     def test_init_with_database_url_string(self) -> None:
 705:         """Init with database URL string creates config."""
 706:         with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config") as mock_load:
 707:             lake = MemoryLake("postgresql://localhost/mydb")
 708:             mock_load.assert_not_called()
 709: 
 710:         assert lake._config.database_url == "postgresql://localhost/mydb"
 711: 
 712:     def test_init_with_database_url_and_graph_url(self) -> None:
 713:         """Init with both database and graph URLs."""
 714:         with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config"):
 715:             lake = MemoryLake(
 716:                 "postgresql://localhost/mydb",
 717:                 graph_url="bolt://localhost:7687",
 718:             )
 719: 
 720:         assert lake._config.database_url == "postgresql://localhost/mydb"
 721:         assert lake._config.neo4j_url == "bolt://localhost:7687"
 722: 
 723:     def test_init_with_custom_embedding_model(self) -> None:
 724:         """Init with custom embedding model."""
 725:         with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config"):
 726:             lake = MemoryLake(
 727:                 "postgresql://localhost/mydb",
 728:                 embedding_model="text-embedding-3-large",
 729:             )
 730: 
 731:         assert lake._config.llm.embedding_model == "text-embedding-3-large"
 732: 
 733:     def test_init_with_khora_config(self) -> None:
 734:         """Init with full KhoraConfig object."""
 735:         from khora.config import KhoraConfig
 736: 
 737:         # Create a real KhoraConfig (not a mock) to trigger the isinstance check
 738:         cfg = KhoraConfig(database_url="postgresql://test")
 739:         lake = MemoryLake(cfg)
 740: 
 741:         assert lake._config is cfg
 742:         assert lake._config.database_url == "postgresql://test"
 743: 
 744:     def test_init_with_none_loads_from_env(self) -> None:
 745:         """Init with None loads config from env/file."""
 746:         with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config", return_value=_mock_config()) as mock_load:
 747:             lake = MemoryLake()
 748:             mock_load.assert_called_once()
 749: 
 750:         assert lake._config is not None
 751: 
 752:     def test_init_none_with_graph_override(self) -> None:
 753:         """Init with None but graph_url override."""
 754:         mock_cfg = _mock_config()
 755:         mock_cfg.neo4j_url = None
 756:         with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config", return_value=mock_cfg):
 757:             lake = MemoryLake(graph_url="bolt://custom:7687")
 758: 
 759:         assert lake._config.neo4j_url == "bolt://custom:7687"
 760: 
 761:     def test_init_with_engine_parameter(self) -> None:
 762:         """Init with explicit engine parameter."""
 763:         with patch("khora.memory_lake.load_config", return_value=_mock_config()):
 764:             lake = MemoryLake(engine="graphrag")
 765: 
 766:         assert lake._engine_name == "graphrag"

 774: class TestBatchResult:

 780:             total=10,
 781:             processed=8,
 782:             skipped=1,
 783:             failed=1,
 784:             chunks=50,
 785:             entities=20,
 786:             relationships=15,

 819: class TestDeprecationWarnings:
 820:     """Tests for deprecation warnings on storage and query_engine properties."""

 822:     def test_storage_property_warns(self) -> None:
 823:         """Accessing storage property emits DeprecationWarning."""
 824:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 825: 
 826:         with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:
 827:             warnings.simplefilter("always")
 828:             _ = lake.storage
 829: 
 830:         assert len(w) == 1
 831:         assert issubclass(w[0].category, DeprecationWarning)
 832:         assert "lake.storage is deprecated" in str(w[0].message)
 833: 
 834:     def test_query_engine_property_warns(self) -> None:
 835:         """Accessing query_engine property emits DeprecationWarning."""
 836:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 837: 
 838:         with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:
 839:             warnings.simplefilter("always")
 840:             _ = lake.query_engine
 841: 
 842:         assert len(w) == 1
 843:         assert issubclass(w[0].category, DeprecationWarning)
 844:         assert "lake.query_engine is deprecated" in str(w[0].message)

 947: 
 948: class TestEnhancedRememberBatch:
 949:     """Tests for enhanced remember_batch() with BatchResult."""
 950: 
 951:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 952:     async def test_empty_batch_returns_batch_result(self) -> None:
 953:         """Empty batch returns BatchResult with zeros."""
 954:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 955:         ns_id = uuid4()
 956:         lake._engine.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=ns_id)
 957:         lake._engine.remember_batch = AsyncMock(
 958:             return_value=BatchResult(
 959:                 total=0,
 960:                 processed=0,
 961:                 skipped=0,
 962:                 failed=0,
 963:                 chunks=0,
 964:                 entities=0,
 965:                 relationships=0,
 966:             )
 967:         )
 968: 
 969:         with (
 970:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.ensure_trace_id"),
 971:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.clear_trace_id"),
 972:         ):
 973:             result = await lake.remember_batch([])
 974: 
 975:         assert isinstance(result, BatchResult)
 976:         assert result.total == 0
 977:         assert result.processed == 0
 978: 
 979:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 980:     async def test_batch_returns_batch_result(self) -> None:
 981:         """remember_batch() returns BatchResult with aggregated stats."""
 982:         lake = _make_lake(connected=True)
 983:         ns_id = uuid4()
 984:         lake._engine.get_or_create_default_namespace = AsyncMock(return_value=ns_id)
 985:         lake._engine.remember_batch = AsyncMock(
 986:             return_value=BatchResult(
 987:                 total=3,
 988:                 processed=2,
 989:                 skipped=1,
 990:                 failed=0,
 991:                 chunks=10,
 992:                 entities=5,
 993:                 relationships=5,
 994:             )
 995:         )
 996: 
 997:         with (
 998:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.ensure_trace_id"),
 999:             patch("khora.telemetry.context.clear_trace_id"),
1000:         ):
1001:             result = await lake.remember_batch(
1002:                 [
1003:                     {"content": "Doc 1"},
1004:                     {"content": "Doc 2"},
1005:                     {"content": "Doc 3"},
1006:                 ]
1007:             )
1008: 
1009:         assert isinstance(result, BatchResult)
1010:         assert result.total == 3
1011:         assert result.processed == 2
1012:         assert result.skipped == 1
1013:         assert result.relationships == 5
1014:

1016: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1017: # Engine Registry Tests
1018: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1019: 
1020: 
1021: class TestEngineRegistry:
1022:     """Tests for engine registry functions."""
1023: 
1024:     def test_list_engines(self) -> None:
1025:         """list_engines returns available engines."""
1026:         from khora.engines import list_engines
1027: 
1028:         engines = list_engines()
1029:         assert "graphrag" in engines
1030: 
1031:     def test_register_engine(self) -> None:
1032:         """register_engine adds new engine to registry."""
1033:         from khora.engines import list_engines, register_engine
1034: 
1035:         register_engine("test_engine", "test.module", "TestEngine")
1036:         engines = list_engines()
1037:         assert "test_engine" in engines
1038: 
1039:     def test_create_engine_unknown_raises(self) -> None:
1040:         """create_engine raises for unknown engine."""
1041:         from khora.engines import create_engine
1042: 
1043:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Unknown engine"):
1044:             create_engine("nonexistent", _mock_config())
````

## File: tests/unit/test_pipelines_ingest.py
````python
  1: """Unit tests for pipelines/flows/ingest.py — Document ingestion.
  2: 
  3: Tests exercise checksum and timestamp logic directly, and test
  4: stage_document by reimplementing its logic without Prefect task
  5: wrappers to avoid server startup overhead.
  6: """
  7: 
  8: from __future__ import annotations
  9: 
 10: import hashlib
 11: from datetime import UTC, datetime
 12: from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock
 13: from uuid import uuid4
 14: 
 15: import pytest
 16: 
 17: from khora.pipelines.flows.ingest import _extract_source_timestamp
 18: 
 19: 
 20: def _compute_checksum(content: str) -> str:
 21:     """SHA-256 checksum — mirrors compute_checksum without Prefect."""
 22:     return hashlib.sha256(content.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
 23: 
 24: 
 25: class TestComputeChecksum:
 26:     """Tests for compute_checksum."""
 27: 
 28:     def test_deterministic(self) -> None:
 29:         """Same content produces same checksum."""
 30:         c1 = _compute_checksum("hello world")
 31:         c2 = _compute_checksum("hello world")
 32:         assert c1 == c2
 33: 
 34:     def test_different_content(self) -> None:
 35:         """Different content produces different checksum."""
 36:         c1 = _compute_checksum("hello")
 37:         c2 = _compute_checksum("world")
 38:         assert c1 != c2
 39: 
 40:     def test_sha256_format(self) -> None:
 41:         """Checksum is a 64-char hex string (SHA-256)."""
 42:         c = _compute_checksum("test")
 43:         assert len(c) == 64
 44:         assert all(ch in "0123456789abcdef" for ch in c)
 45: 
 46: 
 47: class TestExtractSourceTimestamp:
 48:     """Tests for _extract_source_timestamp."""
 49: 
 50:     def test_sent_at_iso(self) -> None:
 51:         """sent_at field in ISO format is parsed."""
 52:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp({"sent_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"})
 53:         assert ts is not None
 54:         assert ts.year == 2024
 55:         assert ts.month == 1
 56:         assert ts.day == 15
 57: 
 58:     def test_created_at(self) -> None:
 59:         """created_at field is parsed."""
 60:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp({"created_at": "2024-06-01T12:00:00+00:00"})
 61:         assert ts is not None
 62:         assert ts.year == 2024
 63: 
 64:     def test_date_only(self) -> None:
 65:         """Date-only format is parsed."""
 66:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp({"timestamp": "2024-03-15"})
 67:         assert ts is not None
 68:         assert ts.year == 2024
 69:         assert ts.month == 3
 70: 
 71:     def test_datetime_passthrough(self) -> None:
 72:         """datetime objects pass through directly."""
 73:         dt = datetime(2024, 5, 1, 12, 0, 0)
 74:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp({"sent_at": dt})
 75:         assert ts is dt
 76: 
 77:     def test_no_timestamp(self) -> None:
 78:         """No matching fields returns None."""
 79:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp({"title": "doc", "author": "me"})
 80:         assert ts is None
 81: 
 82:     def test_empty_metadata(self) -> None:
 83:         """Empty metadata returns None."""
 84:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp({})
 85:         assert ts is None
 86: 
 87:     def test_priority_order(self) -> None:
 88:         """sent_at has priority over created_at."""
 89:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp(
 90:             {
 91:                 "sent_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
 92:                 "created_at": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z",
 93:             }
 94:         )
 95:         assert ts is not None
 96:         assert ts.month == 1  # sent_at wins
 97: 
 98:     def test_invalid_format_skipped(self) -> None:
 99:         """Invalid format is skipped, next field tried."""
100:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp(
101:             {
102:                 "sent_at": "not-a-date",
103:                 "created_at": "2024-06-01T12:00:00+00:00",
104:             }
105:         )
106:         assert ts is not None
107:         assert ts.month == 6
108: 
109:     def test_falsy_values_skipped(self) -> None:
110:         """None and empty string values are skipped."""
111:         ts = _extract_source_timestamp({"sent_at": None, "created_at": ""})
112:         assert ts is None
113: 
114: 
115: class TestStageDocument:
116:     """Tests for stage_document logic without Prefect runtime."""
117: 
118:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
119:     async def test_new_document_created(self) -> None:
120:         """New document is created when no checksum match."""
121:         from khora.core.models import Document, DocumentMetadata
122: 
123:         ns_id = uuid4()
124:         storage = MagicMock()
125:         storage.get_document_by_checksum = AsyncMock(return_value=None)
126:         storage.create_document = AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda doc: doc)
127: 
128:         content = "hello world"
129:         checksum = _compute_checksum(content)
130: 
131:         metadata = DocumentMetadata(
132:             source="api",
133:             title="Test",
134:             checksum=checksum,
135:             size_bytes=len(content.encode("utf-8")),
136:             custom={},
137:         )
138:         doc = Document(namespace_id=ns_id, content=content, metadata=metadata, created_at=datetime.now(UTC))
139:         created = await storage.create_document(doc)
140: 
141:         assert created is not None
142:         storage.create_document.assert_awaited_once()
143: 
144:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
145:     async def test_duplicate_skipped(self) -> None:
146:         """Existing document (checksum match) returns None."""
147:         ns_id = uuid4()
148:         existing = MagicMock()
149:         existing.status = "completed"
150:         storage = MagicMock()
151:         storage.get_document_by_checksum = AsyncMock(return_value=existing)
152: 
153:         content = "hello world"
154:         checksum = _compute_checksum(content)
155: 
156:         result = await storage.get_document_by_checksum(ns_id, checksum)
157:         assert result is not None  # existing doc found, would skip
158: 
159:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
160:     async def test_source_timestamp_used(self) -> None:
161:         """Source timestamp from metadata is used for created_at."""
162:         from khora.core.models import Document, DocumentMetadata
163: 
164:         ns_id = uuid4()
165:         custom_metadata = {"sent_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
166:         source_timestamp = _extract_source_timestamp(custom_metadata)
167:         created_at = source_timestamp or datetime.now(UTC)
168: 
169:         content = "test content"
170:         checksum = _compute_checksum(content)
171: 
172:         metadata = DocumentMetadata(
173:             source="",
174:             title="",
175:             checksum=checksum,
176:             size_bytes=len(content.encode("utf-8")),
177:             custom=custom_metadata,
178:         )
179:         doc = Document(namespace_id=ns_id, content=content, metadata=metadata, created_at=created_at)
180: 
181:         assert doc.created_at.year == 2024
182:         assert doc.created_at.month == 1
183:

185: class TestStreamExtractAndEmbedEntities:
186:     """Tests for stream_extract_and_embed_entities."""
187: 
188:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
189:     async def test_empty_chunks_returns_empty(self) -> None:
190:         """Empty chunks list returns empty entities and relationships."""
191:         from khora.pipelines.flows.ingest import stream_extract_and_embed_entities
192: 
193:         embedder = MagicMock()
194:         embedder.embed_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=[])
195: 
196:         entities, relationships = await stream_extract_and_embed_entities(
197:             chunks=[],
198:             embedder=embedder,
199:         )
200: 
201:         assert entities == []
202:         assert relationships == []
203:         embedder.embed_batch.assert_not_called()
204: 
205:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
206:     async def test_entities_get_embeddings(self) -> None:
207:         """Extracted entities receive embeddings."""
208:         from unittest.mock import patch
209: 
210:         from khora.core.models import Chunk, ChunkMetadata
211:         from khora.pipelines.flows.ingest import stream_extract_and_embed_entities
212: 
213:         ns_id = uuid4()
214:         doc_id = uuid4()
215: 
216:         chunk = Chunk(
217:             id=uuid4(),
218:             namespace_id=ns_id,
219:             document_id=doc_id,
220:             content="Alice works at Acme Corp.",
221:             metadata=ChunkMetadata(),
222:             embedding=[],
223:         )
224: 
225:         # Mock embedder
226:         embedder = MagicMock()
227:         embedder.embed_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=[[0.1, 0.2, 0.3]])
228: 
229:         # Mock extractor - we need to patch the LLMEntityExtractor
230:         from khora.extraction.extractors.base import ExtractedEntity, ExtractionResult
231: 
232:         mock_result = ExtractionResult(
233:             entities=[
234:                 ExtractedEntity(
235:                     name="Alice",
236:                     entity_type="PERSON",
237:                     description="A person",
238:                     confidence=0.9,
239:                 )
240:             ],
241:             relationships=[],
242:         )
243: 
244:         class MockExtractor:
245:             def __init__(self, **kwargs):
246:                 pass
247: 
248:             async def extract_multi(self, texts, **kwargs):
249:                 return [mock_result] * len(texts)
250: 
251:         with patch(
252:             "khora.extraction.extractors.LLMEntityExtractor",
253:             MockExtractor,
254:         ):
255:             entities, relationships = await stream_extract_and_embed_entities(
256:                 chunks=[chunk],
257:                 embedder=embedder,
258:             )
259: 
260:         assert len(entities) == 1
261:         assert entities[0].name == "Alice"
262:         assert entities[0].embedding == [0.1, 0.2, 0.3]
263:         embedder.embed_batch.assert_called()
264: 
265:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
266:     async def test_relationships_extracted(self) -> None:
267:         """Relationships between entities are extracted."""
268:         from unittest.mock import patch
269: 
270:         from khora.core.models import Chunk, ChunkMetadata
271:         from khora.pipelines.flows.ingest import stream_extract_and_embed_entities
272: 
273:         ns_id = uuid4()
274:         doc_id = uuid4()
275: 
276:         chunk = Chunk(
277:             id=uuid4(),
278:             namespace_id=ns_id,
279:             document_id=doc_id,
280:             content="Alice works for Bob.",
281:             metadata=ChunkMetadata(),
282:             embedding=[],
283:         )
284: 
285:         embedder = MagicMock()
286:         embedder.embed_batch = AsyncMock(return_value=[[0.1], [0.2]])
287: 
288:         from khora.extraction.extractors.base import (
289:             ExtractedEntity,
290:             ExtractedRelationship,
291:             ExtractionResult,
292:         )
293: 
294:         mock_result = ExtractionResult(
295:             entities=[
296:                 ExtractedEntity(
297:                     name="Alice",
298:                     entity_type="PERSON",
299:                     description="Employee",
300:                     confidence=0.9,
301:                 ),
302:                 ExtractedEntity(
303:                     name="Bob",
304:                     entity_type="PERSON",
305:                     description="Manager",
306:                     confidence=0.9,
307:                 ),
308:             ],
309:             relationships=[
310:                 ExtractedRelationship(
311:                     source_entity="Alice",
312:                     target_entity="Bob",
313:                     relationship_type="WORKS_FOR",
314:                     description="Employment relationship",
315:                     confidence=0.85,
316:                 )
317:             ],
318:         )
319: 
320:         class MockExtractor:
321:             def __init__(self, **kwargs):
322:                 pass
323: 
324:             async def extract_multi(self, texts, **kwargs):
325:                 return [mock_result] * len(texts)
326: 
327:         with patch(
328:             "khora.extraction.extractors.LLMEntityExtractor",
329:             MockExtractor,
330:         ):
331:             entities, relationships = await stream_extract_and_embed_entities(
332:                 chunks=[chunk],
333:                 embedder=embedder,
334:             )
335: 
336:         assert len(entities) == 2
337:         assert len(relationships) == 1
338:         assert relationships[0].relationship_type == "WORKS_FOR"
339: 
340:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
341:     async def test_batch_embedding(self) -> None:
342:         """Entities are embedded in batches."""
343:         from unittest.mock import patch
344: 
345:         from khora.core.models import Chunk, ChunkMetadata
346:         from khora.pipelines.flows.ingest import stream_extract_and_embed_entities
347: 
348:         ns_id = uuid4()
349:         doc_id = uuid4()
350: 
351:         # Create multiple chunks
352:         chunks = [
353:             Chunk(
354:                 id=uuid4(),
355:                 namespace_id=ns_id,
356:                 document_id=doc_id,
357:                 content=f"Entity{i} is important.",
358:                 metadata=ChunkMetadata(),
359:                 embedding=[],
360:             )
361:             for i in range(5)
362:         ]
363: 
364:         embedder = MagicMock()
365:         # Return embeddings for each batch call
366:         embedder.embed_batch = AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda texts: [[0.1] * len(texts[0]) for _ in texts])
367: 
368:         from khora.extraction.extractors.base import ExtractedEntity, ExtractionResult
369: 
370:         def make_result(idx):
371:             return ExtractionResult(
372:                 entities=[
373:                     ExtractedEntity(
374:                         name=f"Entity{idx}",
375:                         entity_type="CONCEPT",
376:                         description="",
377:                         confidence=0.9,
378:                     )
379:                 ],
380:                 relationships=[],
381:             )
382: 
383:         class MockExtractor:
384:             def __init__(self, **kwargs):
385:                 pass
386: 
387:             async def extract_multi(self, texts, **kwargs):
388:                 return [make_result(i) for i in range(len(texts))]
389: 
390:         with patch(
391:             "khora.extraction.extractors.LLMEntityExtractor",
392:             MockExtractor,
393:         ):
394:             entities, _ = await stream_extract_and_embed_entities(
395:                 chunks=chunks,
396:                 embedder=embedder,
397:                 embedding_batch_size=2,  # Small batch size for testing
398:             )
399: 
400:         assert len(entities) == 5
401:         # embed_batch should have been called multiple times for batching
402:         assert embedder.embed_batch.call_count >= 1
````

````python
 26: class TestSearchMode:
 27:     """Tests for SearchMode enum."""

 29:     def test_modes_exist(self) -> None:

 32:         assert SearchMode.GRAPH is not None
 33:         assert SearchMode.HYBRID is not None
 34:         assert SearchMode.ALL is not None

 47: class TestQueryConfig:
 48:     """Tests for QueryConfig dataclass."""
 49: 
 50:     def test_defaults(self) -> None:
 51:         """Default config values."""
 52:         config = QueryConfig()
 53:         assert config.mode == SearchMode.HYBRID
 54:         assert config.max_chunks == 10
 55:         assert config.max_entities == 10
 56:         assert config.vector_weight == 0.5
 57:         assert config.graph_weight == 0.3
 58:         assert config.keyword_weight == 0.2
 59:         assert config.enable_query_understanding is True
 60:         assert config.enable_entity_linking is True

 62:     def test_custom_config(self) -> None:
 63:         """Custom config values."""

 65:             mode=SearchMode.VECTOR,
 66:             max_chunks=5,
 67:             min_chunk_similarity=0.1,
 68:             vector_weight=1.0,

 70:         assert config.mode == SearchMode.VECTOR
 71:         assert config.max_chunks == 5
 72:         assert config.min_chunk_similarity == 0.1
 73: 
 74:     def test_from_settings(self) -> None:
 75:         """from_settings creates config from QuerySettings."""
 76:         settings = MagicMock()
 77:         settings.default_mode = "all"
 78:         settings.min_chunk_similarity = 0.1
 79:         settings.min_entity_similarity = 0.2
 80:         settings.vector_weight = 0.6
 81:         settings.graph_weight = 0.2
 82:         settings.keyword_weight = 0.2
 83:         settings.apply_recency_bias = True
 84:         settings.recency_weight = 0.3
 85:         settings.recency_decay_days = 14.0
 86:         settings.enable_understanding = False
 87:         settings.understanding_expand_query = False
 88:         settings.understanding_extract_entities = True
 89:         settings.understanding_detect_temporal = True
 90:         settings.enable_entity_linking = True
 91:         settings.entity_linking_fuzzy_threshold = 0.5
 92:         settings.entity_linking_embedding_threshold = 0.3
 93:         settings.entity_linking_max_candidates = 3
 94:         settings.enable_reranking = False
 95:         settings.reranking_method = "llm"
 96:         settings.reranking_top_n = 30
 97:         settings.reranking_final_k = 5
 98:         settings.enable_keyword_search = True
 99:         settings.keyword_search_method = "bm25"
100:         settings.enable_hyde = True
101:         settings.hyde_num_hypotheticals = 2
102:         # Multi-stage settings
103:         settings.enable_multi_stage = True
104:         settings.stage1_recall_limit = 150
105:         settings.stage3_filter_limit = 40
106:         settings.stage4_rerank_limit = 40
107:         settings.enable_diversity = True
108:         settings.diversity_lambda = 0.7
109:

111:         assert config.mode == SearchMode.ALL
112:         assert config.vector_weight == 0.6
113:         assert config.apply_recency_bias is True
114:         assert config.enable_query_understanding is False
115:         assert config.reranking_method == "llm"
116:         assert config.enable_hyde is True
117:         # Multi-stage assertions

119:         assert config.stage1_recall_limit == 150
120:         assert config.stage3_filter_limit == 40
121:         assert config.stage4_rerank_limit == 40
122:         assert config.enable_diversity is True
123:         assert config.diversity_lambda == 0.7

165: class TestMultiStageConfig:

193:         assert config.diversity_lambda == 0.8

201: class TestSearchMethodStats:
202:     """Tests for SearchMethodStats."""

204:     def test_to_dict(self) -> None:
205:         """to_dict includes all fields."""
206:         stats = SearchMethodStats(
207:             chunk_count=5,
208:             entity_count=3,
209:             min_score=0.1,
210:             max_score=0.9,
211:             avg_score=0.5,
212:             latency_ms=100.0,

218:         assert d["scores"]["max"] == 0.9
219:         assert d["latency_ms"] == 100.0

233: 
234: class TestSearchMethodContribution:
235:     """Tests for SearchMethodContribution."""
236: 
237:     def test_compute_overlaps(self) -> None:
238:         """compute_overlaps correctly computes set operations."""
239:         contrib = SearchMethodContribution()
240:         contrib.vector.chunk_ids = ["a", "b", "c"]
241:         contrib.graph.chunk_ids = ["b", "c", "d"]
242:         contrib.keyword.chunk_ids = ["c", "e"]
243:         contrib.compute_overlaps()
244: 
245:         assert "a" in contrib.vector_only_chunks
246:         assert "d" in contrib.graph_only_chunks
247:         assert "e" in contrib.keyword_only_chunks
248:         assert "c" in contrib.all_methods_overlap
249:         assert "b" in contrib.vector_graph_overlap
250: 
251:     def test_compute_overlaps_empty(self) -> None:
252:         """compute_overlaps handles empty inputs."""
253:         contrib = SearchMethodContribution()
254:         contrib.compute_overlaps()
255:         assert contrib.all_methods_overlap == []
256:         assert contrib.vector_only_chunks == []
257: 
258:     def test_compute_entity_overlaps(self) -> None:
259:         """compute_overlaps computes entity set operations."""
260:         contrib = SearchMethodContribution()
261:         contrib.vector.entity_ids = ["e1", "e2"]
262:         contrib.graph.entity_ids = ["e2", "e3"]
263:         contrib.compute_overlaps()
264: 
265:         assert "e1" in contrib.vector_only_entities
266:         assert "e3" in contrib.graph_only_entities
267:         assert "e2" in contrib.vector_graph_entity_overlap
268: 
269:     def test_to_dict(self) -> None:
270:         """to_dict returns comprehensive statistics."""
271:         contrib = SearchMethodContribution()
272:         d = contrib.to_dict()
273:         assert "summary" in d
274:         assert "by_method" in d
275:         assert "chunk_overlap" in d
276:         assert "entity_overlap" in d
277: 
278:     def test_legacy_properties(self) -> None:
279:         """Legacy properties return chunk_ids."""
280:         contrib = SearchMethodContribution()
281:         contrib.vector.chunk_ids = ["a"]
282:         contrib.graph.chunk_ids = ["b"]
283:         contrib.keyword.chunk_ids = ["c"]
284:         assert contrib.vector_chunks == ["a"]
285:         assert contrib.graph_chunks == ["b"]
286:         assert contrib.keyword_chunks == ["c"]
287: 
288: 
289: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
290: # QueryResult
291: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
292: 
293: 
294: class TestQueryResult:
295:     """Tests for QueryResult dataclass."""
296: 
297:     def test_top_chunks(self) -> None:
298:         """top_chunks strips scores."""
299:         chunk1 = MagicMock()
300:         chunk2 = MagicMock()
301:         result = QueryResult(chunks=[(chunk1, 0.9), (chunk2, 0.5)])
302:         assert result.top_chunks == [chunk1, chunk2]
303: 
304:     def test_top_entities(self) -> None:
305:         """top_entities strips scores."""
306:         entity1 = MagicMock()
307:         result = QueryResult(entities=[(entity1, 0.8)])
308:         assert result.top_entities == [entity1]
309: 
310:     def test_get_context_text(self) -> None:
311:         """get_context_text concatenates chunk content."""
312:         chunk1 = MagicMock()
313:         chunk1.content = "first chunk"
314:         chunk2 = MagicMock()
315:         chunk2.content = "second chunk"
316:         result = QueryResult(chunks=[(chunk1, 0.9), (chunk2, 0.5)])
317:         text = result.get_context_text(max_chunks=2)
318:         assert "first chunk" in text
319:         assert "second chunk" in text
320:         assert "---" in text
321: 
322:     def test_get_context_text_max_chunks(self) -> None:
323:         """get_context_text respects max_chunks limit."""
324:         chunks = [(MagicMock(content=f"chunk{i}"), 0.5) for i in range(5)]
325:         result = QueryResult(chunks=chunks)
326:         text = result.get_context_text(max_chunks=2)
327:         assert "chunk0" in text
328:         assert "chunk1" in text
329:         assert "chunk4" not in text
330: 
331:     def test_get_context_text_empty(self) -> None:
332:         """get_context_text returns empty string for no chunks."""
333:         result = QueryResult()
334:         assert result.get_context_text() == ""
335: 
336:     def test_get_full_metadata(self) -> None:
337:         """get_full_metadata includes search contributions."""
338:         result = QueryResult(
339:             metadata={"query_id": "test"},
340:             search_contributions=SearchMethodContribution(),
341:         )
342:         meta = result.get_full_metadata()
343:         assert "query_id" in meta
344:         assert "search_methods" in meta
345: 
346:     def test_get_full_metadata_with_graph_and_temporal(self) -> None:
347:         """get_full_metadata includes graph and temporal info."""
348:         result = QueryResult(
349:             metadata={},
350:             graph_info=GraphTraversalInfo(entities_searched=["Alice"]),
351:             temporal_info=TemporalInfo(detected=True),
352:         )
353:         meta = result.get_full_metadata()
354:         assert "graph_traversal" in meta
355:         assert "temporal" in meta
356: 
357:     def test_get_full_metadata_without_extras(self) -> None:
358:         """get_full_metadata works without optional fields."""
359:         result = QueryResult(metadata={"foo": "bar"})
360:         meta = result.get_full_metadata()
361:         assert meta == {"foo": "bar"}
362: 
363: 
364: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
365: # GraphTraversalInfo
366: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
367: 
368: 
369: class TestGraphTraversalInfo:
370:     """Tests for GraphTraversalInfo."""
371: 
372:     def test_to_dict(self) -> None:
373:         """to_dict includes all fields."""
374:         info = GraphTraversalInfo(
375:             entities_searched=["Alice"],
376:             entities_linked=["Alice"],
377:             relationships_traversed=[("Alice", "WORKS_FOR", "Acme")],
378:             neighborhood_depth=2,
379:         )
380:         d = info.to_dict()
381:         assert d["entities_searched"] == ["Alice"]
382:         assert d["neighborhood_depth"] == 2
383:         assert len(d["relationships_traversed"]) == 1
384:         assert d["relationships_traversed"][0]["from"] == "Alice"
385: 
386:     def test_to_dict_truncates(self) -> None:
387:         """to_dict truncates long lists."""
388:         info = GraphTraversalInfo(
389:             entities_searched=[f"entity_{i}" for i in range(30)],
390:         )
391:         d = info.to_dict()
392:         assert len(d["entities_searched"]) == 20

396: # TemporalInfo
397: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
398: 
399: 
400: class TestTemporalInfo:
401:     """Tests for TemporalInfo in query engine."""
402: 
403:     def test_to_dict(self) -> None:
404:         """to_dict handles None times."""
405:         info = TemporalInfo(detected=True, filter_applied=False)
406:         d = info.to_dict()
407:         assert d["detected"] is True
408:         assert d["time_start"] is None
409: 
410:     def test_to_dict_with_times(self) -> None:
411:         """to_dict formats datetime values."""
412:         from datetime import datetime, timezone
413: 
414:         now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
415:         info = TemporalInfo(detected=True, filter_applied=True, time_start=now, time_end=now)
416:         d = info.to_dict()
417:         assert d["time_start"] == now.isoformat()
418:         assert d["time_end"] == now.isoformat()

421: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
422: # HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query
423: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
424: 
425: 
426: class TestIsSimpleQuery:
427:     """Tests for _is_simple_query static method."""
428: 
429:     def test_short_query_is_simple(self) -> None:
430:         """Short query without special patterns is simple."""
431:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("hello world") is True
432: 
433:     def test_long_query_is_not_simple(self) -> None:
434:         """Query with more than 8 words is not simple."""
435:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("this is a very long query with many many words") is False
436: 
437:     def test_temporal_reference_not_simple(self) -> None:
438:         """Query with temporal references is not simple."""
439:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("events yesterday") is False
440:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("last week meetings") is False
441:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("changes since Monday") is False
442: 
443:     def test_quoted_phrase_not_simple(self) -> None:
444:         """Query with quotes is not simple."""
445:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query('"exact phrase"') is False
446:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("'entity name'") is False
447: 
448:     def test_comparison_not_simple(self) -> None:
449:         """Query with comparison words is not simple."""
450:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("compare A vs B") is False
451:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("difference between X Y") is False
452: 
453:     def test_year_reference_not_simple(self) -> None:
454:         """Query with year is not simple."""
455:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("revenue in 2024") is False
456: 
457:     def test_month_reference_not_simple(self) -> None:
458:         """Query with month name is not simple."""
459:         assert HybridQueryEngine._is_simple_query("meetings in January") is False
460:

462: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
463: # HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost
464: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
465: 
466: 
467: class TestAttributeRelevanceBoost:
468:     """Tests for _attribute_relevance_boost static method."""
469: 
470:     def test_no_attributes(self) -> None:
471:         """Entity without attributes gets no boost."""
472:         entity = MagicMock(spec=[])  # No attributes attr
473:         assert HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, ["urgent"]) == 0.0
474: 
475:     def test_matching_attribute(self) -> None:
476:         """Entity with matching attribute gets boost."""
477:         entity = MagicMock()
478:         entity.attributes = {"priority": "urgent", "status": "open"}
479:         boost = HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, ["urgent"])
480:         assert boost == pytest.approx(0.1)
481: 
482:     def test_multiple_matches(self) -> None:
483:         """Multiple matches accumulate."""
484:         entity = MagicMock()
485:         entity.attributes = {"priority": "urgent", "assignee": "alice"}
486:         boost = HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, ["urgent", "alice"])
487:         assert boost == pytest.approx(0.2)
488: 
489:     def test_boost_capped_at_03(self) -> None:
490:         """Boost is capped at 0.3."""
491:         entity = MagicMock()
492:         entity.attributes = {f"attr_{i}": f"keyword{i}" for i in range(10)}
493:         boost = HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, [f"keyword{i}" for i in range(10)])
494:         assert boost == pytest.approx(0.3)
495: 
496:     def test_case_insensitive(self) -> None:
497:         """Matching is case-insensitive."""
498:         entity = MagicMock()
499:         entity.attributes = {"name": "Alice Smith"}
500:         boost = HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, ["alice"])
501:         assert boost > 0.0
502: 
503:     def test_empty_attributes_dict(self) -> None:
504:         """Empty attributes dict returns no boost."""
505:         entity = MagicMock()
506:         entity.attributes = {}
507:         assert HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, ["test"]) == 0.0
508: 
509:     def test_non_dict_attributes(self) -> None:
510:         """Non-dict attributes returns no boost."""
511:         entity = MagicMock()
512:         entity.attributes = "not a dict"
513:         assert HybridQueryEngine._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, ["test"]) == 0.0
514:
````
