````python
 90:                 metadata STRING,
 91:                 created_at STRING,
 92:                 updated_at STRING,
 93:                 PRIMARY KEY (id)
 94:             )
 95:             """
 96:         )
 97:         conn.execute(
 98:             """
 99:             CREATE NODE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Episode(
100:                 id STRING,
101:                 namespace_id STRING,
102:                 name STRING,
103:                 description STRING,
104:                 occurred_at STRING,
105:                 duration_seconds DOUBLE,
106:                 entity_ids STRING[],
107:                 source_document_ids STRING[],
108:                 source_chunk_ids STRING[],
109:                 metadata STRING,
110:                 created_at STRING,
111:                 updated_at STRING,
112:                 PRIMARY KEY (id)
113:             )
114:             """
115:         )
116:         conn.execute(
117:             """
118:             CREATE REL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS RELATES_TO(
119:                 FROM Entity TO Entity,
120:                 id STRING,
121:                 namespace_id STRING,
122:                 relationship_type STRING,
123:                 description STRING,
124:                 properties STRING,
125:                 source_document_ids STRING[],
126:                 source_chunk_ids STRING[],
127:                 valid_from STRING,
128:                 valid_until STRING,
129:                 confidence DOUBLE,
130:                 weight DOUBLE,
131:                 metadata STRING,
132:                 created_at STRING,
133:                 updated_at STRING
134:             )
135:             """
136:         )
137:         conn.execute(
138:             """
139:             CREATE REL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS INVOLVES(
140:                 FROM Episode TO Entity
141:             )
142:             """
143:         )
144: 
145:     async def disconnect(self) -> None:
146:         if self._conn is not None:
147:             logger.info("Closing Kùzu database...")
148:             self._conn = None
149:             self._db = None
150:             logger.info("Kùzu database closed")
151: 
152:     async def is_healthy(self) -> bool:
153:         if self._conn is None:
154:             return False
155:         try:
156:             await asyncio.to_thread(self._conn.execute, "RETURN 1")
157:             return True
158:         except Exception as e:
159:             logger.error(f"Kùzu health check failed: {e}")
160:             return False
161: 
162:     def _get_conn(self) -> Any:
163:         if self._conn is None:
164:             raise RuntimeError("Backend not connected. Call connect() first.")
165:         return self._conn
166: 
167:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
168:     # Helpers
169:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
170: 
171:     def _run_query(self, query: str, parameters: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> list[list[Any]]:
172:         """Execute a Cypher query synchronously and return all rows."""
173:         conn = self._get_conn()
174:         result = conn.execute(query, parameters=parameters or {})
175:         rows = []
176:         while result.has_next():
177:             rows.append(result.get_next())
178:         return rows
179: 
180:     async def _arun_query(self, query: str, parameters: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> list[list[Any]]:
181:         """Execute a Cypher query asynchronously."""
182:         return await asyncio.to_thread(self._run_query, query, parameters)
183: 
184:     def _row_to_entity(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> Entity:
185:         """Convert a result row dict to an Entity."""
186:         return Entity(
187:             id=parse_uuid(row["id"]),
188:             namespace_id=parse_uuid(row["namespace_id"]),
189:             name=row["name"],
190:             entity_type=(
191:                 EntityType(row["entity_type"]) if row["entity_type"] in EntityType.__members__ else row["entity_type"]
192:             ),
193:             description=row.get("description", ""),
194:             attributes=deserialize_dict(row.get("attributes")),
195:             source_document_ids=parse_uuid_list(row.get("source_document_ids")),
196:             source_chunk_ids=parse_uuid_list(row.get("source_chunk_ids")),
197:             mention_count=row.get("mention_count", 1),
198:             valid_from=parse_datetime(row.get("valid_from")),
199:             valid_until=parse_datetime(row.get("valid_until")),
200:             confidence=row.get("confidence", 1.0),
201:             metadata=deserialize_dict(row.get("metadata")),
202:             created_at=parse_datetime(row.get("created_at"), default=datetime.now()) or datetime.now(),
203:             updated_at=parse_datetime(row.get("updated_at"), default=datetime.now()) or datetime.now(),
204:         )
205: 
206:     def _row_to_relationship(self, row: dict[str, Any], source_id: str, target_id: str) -> Relationship:
207:         """Convert a result row dict to a Relationship."""
208:         rel_type = row.get("relationship_type", "CUSTOM")
209:         return Relationship(
210:             id=parse_uuid(row["id"]),
211:             namespace_id=parse_uuid(row["namespace_id"]),
212:             source_entity_id=parse_uuid(source_id),
213:             target_entity_id=parse_uuid(target_id),
214:             relationship_type=(RelationshipType(rel_type) if rel_type in RelationshipType.__members__ else rel_type),
215:             description=row.get("description", ""),
216:             properties=deserialize_dict(row.get("properties")),
217:             source_document_ids=parse_uuid_list(row.get("source_document_ids")),
218:             source_chunk_ids=parse_uuid_list(row.get("source_chunk_ids")),
219:             valid_from=parse_datetime(row.get("valid_from")),
220:             valid_until=parse_datetime(row.get("valid_until")),
221:             confidence=row.get("confidence", 1.0),
222:             weight=row.get("weight", 1.0),
223:             metadata=deserialize_dict(row.get("metadata")),
224:             created_at=parse_datetime(row.get("created_at"), default=datetime.now()) or datetime.now(),
225:             updated_at=parse_datetime(row.get("updated_at"), default=datetime.now()) or datetime.now(),
226:         )
227: 
228:     def _row_to_episode(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> Episode:
229:         """Convert a result row dict to an Episode."""
230:         return Episode(
231:             id=parse_uuid(row["id"]),
232:             namespace_id=parse_uuid(row["namespace_id"]),
233:             name=row["name"],
234:             description=row.get("description", ""),
235:             occurred_at=datetime.fromisoformat(row["occurred_at"]),
236:             duration_seconds=row.get("duration_seconds"),
237:             entity_ids=parse_uuid_list(row.get("entity_ids")),
238:             source_document_ids=parse_uuid_list(row.get("source_document_ids")),
239:             source_chunk_ids=parse_uuid_list(row.get("source_chunk_ids")),
240:             metadata=deserialize_dict(row.get("metadata")),
241:             created_at=parse_datetime(row.get("created_at"), default=datetime.now()) or datetime.now(),
242:             updated_at=parse_datetime(row.get("updated_at"), default=datetime.now()) or datetime.now(),
243:         )
244: 
245:     def _entity_params(self, entity: Entity) -> dict[str, Any]:
246:         """Build parameter dict for entity creation/update."""
247:         return {
248:             "id": str(entity.id),
249:             "namespace_id": str(entity.namespace_id),
250:             "name": entity.name,
251:             "entity_type": (
252:                 entity.entity_type.value if isinstance(entity.entity_type, EntityType) else entity.entity_type
253:             ),
254:             "description": entity.description,
255:             "attributes": serialize_dict(entity.attributes) or "{}",
256:             "source_document_ids": [str(d) for d in entity.source_document_ids],
257:             "source_chunk_ids": [str(c) for c in entity.source_chunk_ids],
258:             "mention_count": entity.mention_count,
259:             "valid_from": entity.valid_from.isoformat() if entity.valid_from else "",
260:             "valid_until": entity.valid_until.isoformat() if entity.valid_until else "",
261:             "confidence": entity.confidence,
262:             "metadata": serialize_dict(entity.metadata) or "{}",
263:             "created_at": entity.created_at.isoformat(),
264:             "updated_at": entity.updated_at.isoformat(),
265:         }
266: 
267:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
268:     # Entity operations
269:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
270: 
271:     async def create_entity(self, entity: Entity) -> Entity:
272:         params = self._entity_params(entity)
273:         await self._arun_query(
274:             """
275:             CREATE (e:Entity {
276:                 id: $id,
277:                 namespace_id: $namespace_id,
278:                 name: $name,
279:                 entity_type: $entity_type,
280:                 description: $description,
281:                 attributes: $attributes,
282:                 source_document_ids: $source_document_ids,
283:                 source_chunk_ids: $source_chunk_ids,
284:                 mention_count: $mention_count,
285:                 valid_from: $valid_from,
286:                 valid_until: $valid_until,
287:                 confidence: $confidence,
288:                 metadata: $metadata,
289:                 created_at: $created_at,
290:                 updated_at: $updated_at
291:             })
292:             """,
293:             parameters=params,
294:         )
295:         return entity
296: 
297:     async def get_entity(self, entity_id: UUID) -> Entity | None:
298:         rows = await self._arun_query(
299:             "MATCH (e:Entity {id: $id}) RETURN e.*",
300:             parameters={"id": str(entity_id)},
301:         )
302:         if not rows:
303:             return None
304:         # Kùzu returns columns as e.id, e.name, etc. — we need column names
305:         return await asyncio.to_thread(self._get_entity_by_id_sync, entity_id)
306: 
307:     def _get_entity_by_id_sync(self, entity_id: UUID) -> Entity | None:
308:         conn = self._get_conn()
309:         result = conn.execute(
310:             "MATCH (e:Entity {id: $id}) RETURN e.*",
311:             parameters={"id": str(entity_id)},
312:         )
313:         if not result.has_next():
314:             return None
315:         row = result.get_next()
316:         columns = result.get_column_names()
317:         row_dict = {col.replace("e.", ""): val for col, val in zip(columns, row)}
318:         return self._row_to_entity(row_dict)
319: 
320:     async def get_entity_by_name(self, namespace_id: UUID, name: str, entity_type: str) -> Entity | None:
321:         def _query() -> Entity | None:
322:             conn = self._get_conn()
323:             result = conn.execute(
324:                 """
325:                 MATCH (e:Entity {namespace_id: $ns, name: $name, entity_type: $et})
326:                 RETURN e.*
327:                 LIMIT 1
328:                 """,
329:                 parameters={"ns": str(namespace_id), "name": name, "et": entity_type},
330:             )
331:             if not result.has_next():
332:                 return None
333:             row = result.get_next()
334:             columns = result.get_column_names()
335:             row_dict = {col.replace("e.", ""): val for col, val in zip(columns, row)}
336:             return self._row_to_entity(row_dict)
337: 
338:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
339: 
340:     async def update_entity(self, entity: Entity) -> Entity:
341:         params = self._entity_params(entity)
342:         await self._arun_query(
343:             """
344:             MATCH (e:Entity {id: $id})
345:             SET e.name = $name,
346:                 e.description = $description,
347:                 e.attributes = $attributes,
348:                 e.source_document_ids = $source_document_ids,
349:                 e.source_chunk_ids = $source_chunk_ids,
350:                 e.mention_count = $mention_count,
351:                 e.valid_from = $valid_from,
352:                 e.valid_until = $valid_until,
353:                 e.confidence = $confidence,
354:                 e.metadata = $metadata,
355:                 e.updated_at = $updated_at
356:             """,
357:             parameters=params,
358:         )
359:         return entity
360: 
361:     async def delete_entity(self, entity_id: UUID) -> bool:
362:         def _delete() -> bool:
363:             conn = self._get_conn()
364:             # Delete relationships first, then entity
365:             conn.execute(
366:                 "MATCH (e:Entity {id: $id})-[r]->() DELETE r",
367:                 parameters={"id": str(entity_id)},
368:             )
369:             conn.execute(
370:                 "MATCH ()-[r]->(e:Entity {id: $id}) DELETE r",
371:                 parameters={"id": str(entity_id)},
372:             )
373:             conn.execute(
374:                 "MATCH (e:Entity {id: $id}) DELETE e",
375:                 parameters={"id": str(entity_id)},
376:             )
377:             return True
378: 
379:         try:
380:             return await asyncio.to_thread(_delete)
381:         except Exception:
382:             return False
383: 
384:     async def list_entities(
385:         self,
386:         namespace_id: UUID,
387:         *,
388:         entity_type: str | None = None,
389:         limit: int = 100,
390:         offset: int = 0,
391:     ) -> list[Entity]:
392:         def _query() -> list[Entity]:
393:             conn = self._get_conn()
394:             if entity_type:
395:                 result = conn.execute(
396:                     """
397:                     MATCH (e:Entity)
398:                     WHERE e.namespace_id = $ns AND e.entity_type = $et
399:                     RETURN e.*
400:                     ORDER BY e.name
401:                     SKIP $offset LIMIT $limit
402:                     """,
403:                     parameters={
404:                         "ns": str(namespace_id),
405:                         "et": entity_type,
406:                         "offset": offset,
407:                         "limit": limit,
408:                     },
409:                 )
410:             else:
411:                 result = conn.execute(
412:                     """
413:                     MATCH (e:Entity)
414:                     WHERE e.namespace_id = $ns
415:                     RETURN e.*
416:                     ORDER BY e.name
417:                     SKIP $offset LIMIT $limit
418:                     """,
419:                     parameters={"ns": str(namespace_id), "offset": offset, "limit": limit},
420:                 )
421:             entities = []
422:             columns = result.get_column_names()
423:             while result.has_next():
424:                 row = result.get_next()
425:                 row_dict = {col.replace("e.", ""): val for col, val in zip(columns, row)}
426:                 entities.append(self._row_to_entity(row_dict))
427:             return entities
428: 
429:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
430: 
431:     async def count_entities(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
432:         def _query() -> int:
433:             conn = self._get_conn()
434:             result = conn.execute(
435:                 "MATCH (e:Entity) WHERE e.namespace_id = $ns RETURN count(e)",
436:                 parameters={"ns": str(namespace_id)},
437:             )
438:             if result.has_next():
439:                 return result.get_next()[0]
440:             return 0
441: 
442:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
443: 
444:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
445:     # Relationship operations
446:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
447: 
448:     async def create_relationship(self, relationship: Relationship) -> Relationship:
449:         rel_type = (
450:             relationship.relationship_type.value
451:             if isinstance(relationship.relationship_type, RelationshipType)
452:             else relationship.relationship_type
453:         )
454:         params = {
455:             "source_id": str(relationship.source_entity_id),
456:             "target_id": str(relationship.target_entity_id),
457:             "id": str(relationship.id),
458:             "namespace_id": str(relationship.namespace_id),
459:             "relationship_type": rel_type,
460:             "description": relationship.description,
461:             "properties": serialize_dict(relationship.properties) or "{}",
462:             "source_document_ids": [str(d) for d in relationship.source_document_ids],
463:             "source_chunk_ids": [str(c) for c in relationship.source_chunk_ids],
464:             "valid_from": relationship.valid_from.isoformat() if relationship.valid_from else "",
465:             "valid_until": relationship.valid_until.isoformat() if relationship.valid_until else "",
466:             "confidence": relationship.confidence,
467:             "weight": relationship.weight,
468:             "metadata": serialize_dict(relationship.metadata) or "{}",
469:             "created_at": relationship.created_at.isoformat(),
470:             "updated_at": relationship.updated_at.isoformat(),
471:         }
472:         # Kùzu uses a single relationship table RELATES_TO for all relationship types
473:         await self._arun_query(
474:             """
475:             MATCH (source:Entity {id: $source_id}), (target:Entity {id: $target_id})
476:             CREATE (source)-[r:RELATES_TO {
477:                 id: $id,
478:                 namespace_id: $namespace_id,
479:                 relationship_type: $relationship_type,
480:                 description: $description,
481:                 properties: $properties,
482:                 source_document_ids: $source_document_ids,
483:                 source_chunk_ids: $source_chunk_ids,
484:                 valid_from: $valid_from,
485:                 valid_until: $valid_until,
486:                 confidence: $confidence,
487:                 weight: $weight,
488:                 metadata: $metadata,
489:                 created_at: $created_at,
490:                 updated_at: $updated_at
491:             }]->(target)
492:             """,
493:             parameters=params,
494:         )
495:         return relationship
496: 
497:     async def get_relationship(self, relationship_id: UUID) -> Relationship | None:
498:         def _query() -> Relationship | None:
499:             conn = self._get_conn()
500:             result = conn.execute(
501:                 """
502:                 MATCH (source:Entity)-[r:RELATES_TO {id: $id}]->(target:Entity)
503:                 RETURN r.*, source.id, target.id
504:                 """,
505:                 parameters={"id": str(relationship_id)},
506:             )
507:             if not result.has_next():
508:                 return None
509:             row = result.get_next()
510:             columns = result.get_column_names()
511:             row_dict = {}
512:             source_id = ""
513:             target_id = ""
514:             for col, val in zip(columns, row):
515:                 if col == "source.id":
516:                     source_id = val
517:                 elif col == "target.id":
518:                     target_id = val
519:                 else:
520:                     row_dict[col.replace("r.", "")] = val
521:             return self._row_to_relationship(row_dict, source_id, target_id)
522: 
523:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
524: 
525:     async def delete_relationship(self, relationship_id: UUID) -> bool:
526:         try:
527:             await self._arun_query(
528:                 """
529:                 MATCH ()-[r:RELATES_TO {id: $id}]->()
530:                 DELETE r
531:                 """,
532:                 parameters={"id": str(relationship_id)},
533:             )
534:             return True
535:         except Exception:
536:             return False
537: 
538:     async def get_entity_relationships(
539:         self,
540:         entity_id: UUID,
541:         *,
542:         direction: str = "both",
543:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
544:         limit: int = 100,
545:     ) -> list[Relationship]:
546:         def _query() -> list[Relationship]:
547:             conn = self._get_conn()
548:             eid = str(entity_id)
549: 
550:             if direction == "outgoing":
551:                 q = """
552:                 MATCH (e:Entity {id: $eid})-[r:RELATES_TO]->(other:Entity)
553:                 RETURN r.*, e.id AS source_id, other.id AS target_id
554:                 LIMIT $limit
555:                 """
556:             elif direction == "incoming":
557:                 q = """
558:                 MATCH (other:Entity)-[r:RELATES_TO]->(e:Entity {id: $eid})
559:                 RETURN r.*, other.id AS source_id, e.id AS target_id
560:                 LIMIT $limit
561:                 """
562:             else:
563:                 q = """
564:                 MATCH (e:Entity {id: $eid})-[r:RELATES_TO]-(other:Entity)
565:                 RETURN r.*, e.id AS source_id, other.id AS target_id
566:                 LIMIT $limit
567:                 """
568: 
569:             result = conn.execute(q, parameters={"eid": eid, "limit": limit})
570:             columns = result.get_column_names()
571:             rels = []
572:             while result.has_next():
573:                 row = result.get_next()
574:                 row_dict = {}
575:                 source_id = ""
576:                 target_id = ""
577:                 for col, val in zip(columns, row):
578:                     if col == "source_id":
579:                         source_id = val
580:                     elif col == "target_id":
581:                         target_id = val
582:                     else:
583:                         row_dict[col.replace("r.", "")] = val
584:                 if relationship_types and row_dict.get("relationship_type") not in relationship_types:
585:                     continue
586:                 rels.append(self._row_to_relationship(row_dict, source_id, target_id))
587:             return rels
588: 
589:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
590: 
591:     async def list_relationships(
592:         self,
593:         namespace_id: UUID,
594:         *,
595:         relationship_type: str | None = None,
596:         limit: int = 1000,
597:         offset: int = 0,
598:     ) -> list[Relationship]:
599:         def _query() -> list[Relationship]:
600:             conn = self._get_conn()
601:             params: dict[str, Any] = {"ns": str(namespace_id), "offset": offset, "limit": limit}
602: 
603:             if relationship_type:
604:                 q = """
605:                 MATCH (source:Entity)-[r:RELATES_TO]->(target:Entity)
606:                 WHERE r.namespace_id = $ns AND r.relationship_type = $rt
607:                 RETURN r.*, source.id AS source_id, target.id AS target_id
608:                 ORDER BY r.created_at DESC
609:                 SKIP $offset LIMIT $limit
610:                 """
611:                 params["rt"] = relationship_type
612:             else:
613:                 q = """
614:                 MATCH (source:Entity)-[r:RELATES_TO]->(target:Entity)
615:                 WHERE r.namespace_id = $ns
616:                 RETURN r.*, source.id AS source_id, target.id AS target_id
617:                 ORDER BY r.created_at DESC
618:                 SKIP $offset LIMIT $limit
619:                 """
620: 
621:             result = conn.execute(q, parameters=params)
622:             columns = result.get_column_names()
623:             rels = []
624:             while result.has_next():
625:                 row = result.get_next()
626:                 row_dict = {}
627:                 source_id = ""
628:                 target_id = ""
629:                 for col, val in zip(columns, row):
630:                     if col == "source_id":
631:                         source_id = val
632:                     elif col == "target_id":
633:                         target_id = val
634:                     else:
635:                         row_dict[col.replace("r.", "")] = val
636:                 rels.append(self._row_to_relationship(row_dict, source_id, target_id))
637:             return rels
638: 
639:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
640: 
641:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
642:     # Episode operations
643:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
644: 
645:     async def create_episode(self, episode: Episode) -> Episode:
646:         params = {
647:             "id": str(episode.id),
648:             "namespace_id": str(episode.namespace_id),
649:             "name": episode.name,
650:             "description": episode.description,
651:             "occurred_at": episode.occurred_at.isoformat(),
652:             "duration_seconds": episode.duration_seconds or 0.0,
653:             "entity_ids": [str(e) for e in episode.entity_ids],
654:             "source_document_ids": [str(d) for d in episode.source_document_ids],
655:             "source_chunk_ids": [str(c) for c in episode.source_chunk_ids],
656:             "metadata": serialize_dict(episode.metadata) or "{}",
657:             "created_at": episode.created_at.isoformat(),
658:             "updated_at": episode.updated_at.isoformat(),
659:         }
660: 
661:         def _create() -> None:
662:             conn = self._get_conn()
663:             conn.execute(
664:                 """
665:                 CREATE (ep:Episode {
666:                     id: $id,
667:                     namespace_id: $namespace_id,
668:                     name: $name,
669:                     description: $description,
670:                     occurred_at: $occurred_at,
671:                     duration_seconds: $duration_seconds,
672:                     entity_ids: $entity_ids,
673:                     source_document_ids: $source_document_ids,
674:                     source_chunk_ids: $source_chunk_ids,
675:                     metadata: $metadata,
676:                     created_at: $created_at,
677:                     updated_at: $updated_at
678:                 })
679:                 """,
680:                 parameters=params,
681:             )
682:             # Link to entities
683:             if episode.entity_ids:
684:                 conn.execute(
685:                     """
686:                     MATCH (ep:Episode {id: $ep_id}), (e:Entity)
687:                     WHERE e.id IN $entity_ids
688:                     CREATE (ep)-[:INVOLVES]->(e)
689:                     """,
690:                     parameters={
691:                         "ep_id": str(episode.id),
692:                         "entity_ids": [str(e) for e in episode.entity_ids],
693:                     },
694:                 )
695: 
696:         await asyncio.to_thread(_create)
697:         return episode
698: 
699:     async def get_episode(self, episode_id: UUID) -> Episode | None:
700:         def _query() -> Episode | None:
701:             conn = self._get_conn()
702:             result = conn.execute(
703:                 "MATCH (ep:Episode {id: $id}) RETURN ep.*",
704:                 parameters={"id": str(episode_id)},
705:             )
706:             if not result.has_next():
707:                 return None
708:             row = result.get_next()
709:             columns = result.get_column_names()
710:             row_dict = {col.replace("ep.", ""): val for col, val in zip(columns, row)}
711:             return self._row_to_episode(row_dict)
712: 
713:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
714: 
715:     async def list_episodes(
716:         self,
717:         namespace_id: UUID,
718:         *,
719:         start_time: datetime | None = None,
720:         end_time: datetime | None = None,
721:         limit: int = 100,
722:     ) -> list[Episode]:
723:         def _query() -> list[Episode]:
724:             conn = self._get_conn()
725:             conditions = ["ep.namespace_id = $ns"]
726:             params: dict[str, Any] = {"ns": str(namespace_id), "limit": limit}
727: 
728:             if start_time:
729:                 conditions.append("ep.occurred_at >= $start_time")
730:                 params["start_time"] = start_time.isoformat()
731:             if end_time:
732:                 conditions.append("ep.occurred_at <= $end_time")
733:                 params["end_time"] = end_time.isoformat()
734: 
735:             where = " AND ".join(conditions)
736:             q = f"""
737:             MATCH (ep:Episode)
738:             WHERE {where}
739:             RETURN ep.*
740:             ORDER BY ep.occurred_at DESC
741:             LIMIT $limit
742:             """
743: 
744:             result = conn.execute(q, parameters=params)
745:             columns = result.get_column_names()
746:             episodes = []
747:             while result.has_next():
748:                 row = result.get_next()
749:                 row_dict = {col.replace("ep.", ""): val for col, val in zip(columns, row)}
750:                 episodes.append(self._row_to_episode(row_dict))
751:             return episodes
752: 
753:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
754: 
755:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
756:     # Graph traversal
757:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
758: 
759:     async def find_paths(
760:         self,
761:         namespace_id: UUID,
762:         source_entity_id: UUID,
763:         target_entity_id: UUID,
764:         *,
765:         max_depth: int = 3,
766:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
767:     ) -> list[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
768:         def _query() -> list[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
769:             conn = self._get_conn()
770:             # Kùzu supports variable-length paths
771:             q = f"""
772:             MATCH path = (source:Entity {{id: $source_id}})-[r:RELATES_TO*1..{max_depth}]-(target:Entity {{id: $target_id}})
773:             WHERE source.namespace_id = $ns AND target.namespace_id = $ns
774:             RETURN nodes(path), rels(path)
775:             LIMIT 10
776:             """
777:             result = conn.execute(
778:                 q,
779:                 parameters={
780:                     "source_id": str(source_entity_id),
781:                     "target_id": str(target_entity_id),
782:                     "ns": str(namespace_id),
783:                 },
784:             )
785: 
786:             paths = []
787:             while result.has_next():
788:                 row = result.get_next()
789:                 nodes_data = row[0] if row[0] else []
790:                 rels_data = row[1] if len(row) > 1 and row[1] else []
791: 
792:                 path_elements: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
793:                 for node in nodes_data:
794:                     data = dict(node) if hasattr(node, "items") else {"_raw": str(node)}
795:                     path_elements.append({"type": "node", "data": data})
796:                 for rel in rels_data:
797:                     data = dict(rel) if hasattr(rel, "items") else {"_raw": str(rel)}
798:                     if relationship_types and data.get("relationship_type") not in relationship_types:
799:                         continue
800:                     path_elements.append({"type": "relationship", "data": data})
801:                 paths.append(path_elements)
802: 
803:             return paths
804: 
805:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
806: 
807:     async def get_neighborhood(
808:         self,
809:         entity_id: UUID,
810:         *,
811:         depth: int = 1,
812:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
813:         limit: int = 50,
814:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
815:         def _query() -> dict[str, Any]:
816:             conn = self._get_conn()
817:             q = f"""
818:             MATCH (center:Entity {{id: $eid}})-[r:RELATES_TO*1..{depth}]-(other:Entity)
819:             RETURN DISTINCT other.*, r
820:             LIMIT $limit
821:             """
822:             result = conn.execute(q, parameters={"eid": str(entity_id), "limit": limit})
823:             columns = result.get_column_names()
824: 
825:             nodes: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
826:             relationships: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
827:             seen_ids: set[str] = set()
828: 
829:             while result.has_next():
830:                 row = result.get_next()
831:                 row_dict = {}
832:                 for col, val in zip(columns, row):
833:                     if col == "r":
834:                         # Variable-length path returns list of relationships
835:                         if isinstance(val, list):
836:                             for rel in val:
837:                                 rel_data = dict(rel) if hasattr(rel, "items") else {"_raw": str(rel)}
838:                                 if relationship_types and rel_data.get("relationship_type") not in relationship_types:
839:                                     continue
840:                                 relationships.append(rel_data)
841:                         elif val is not None:
842:                             rel_data = dict(val) if hasattr(val, "items") else {"_raw": str(val)}
843:                             relationships.append(rel_data)
844:                     else:
845:                         row_dict[col.replace("other.", "")] = val
846: 
847:                 node_id = row_dict.get("id", "")
848:                 if node_id and node_id not in seen_ids:
849:                     seen_ids.add(node_id)
850:                     nodes.append(row_dict)
851: 
852:             return {"entities": nodes, "relationships": relationships}
853: 
854:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
855: 
856:     async def search_entities_by_attribute(
857:         self,
858:         namespace_id: UUID,
859:         attribute_name: str,
860:         attribute_value: Any,
861:         *,
862:         limit: int = 100,
863:     ) -> list[Entity]:
864:         """Search entities by attribute value.
865: 
866:         Since Kùzu stores attributes as a JSON string, we search within
867:         the serialized string. For exact matching, deserialize and check.
868:         """
869: 
870:         def _query() -> list[Entity]:
871:             conn = self._get_conn()
872:             # Kùzu doesn't support JSON extraction natively — search in serialized string
873:             search_str = f'"{attribute_name}": ' if isinstance(attribute_value, str) else f'"{attribute_name}":'
874:             result = conn.execute(
875:                 """
876:                 MATCH (e:Entity)
877:                 WHERE e.namespace_id = $ns AND contains(e.attributes, $search)
878:                 RETURN e.*
879:                 LIMIT $limit
880:                 """,
881:                 parameters={"ns": str(namespace_id), "search": search_str, "limit": limit},
882:             )
883:             columns = result.get_column_names()
884:             entities = []
885:             while result.has_next():
886:                 row = result.get_next()
887:                 row_dict = {col.replace("e.", ""): val for col, val in zip(columns, row)}
888:                 entity = self._row_to_entity(row_dict)
889:                 # Post-filter: check actual attribute value
890:                 if entity.attributes.get(attribute_name) == attribute_value:
891:                     entities.append(entity)
892:             return entities
893: 
894:         return await asyncio.to_thread(_query)
````

## File: src/khora/engines/vectorcypher/engine.py
````python
   1: """VectorCypher engine - hybrid vector+graph retrieval with temporal support.
   2: 
   3: This engine implements the VectorCypher retrieval paradigm inspired by Graph RAG 2026:
   4: - Vector search to find entry entities (pgvector)
   5: - Cypher traversal to expand relationships (Neo4j)
   6: - Query routing to optimize simple vs complex queries
   7: - HippoRAG 2 dual-node architecture (Entity + Chunk nodes)
   8: - Skeleton-based construction (KET-RAG) - full KG extraction for top 40% of chunks
   9: - Bi-temporal edges (Graphiti-style) - occurred_at vs ingested_at with invalidation
  10: 
  11: Target: Sub-300ms P95 for simple queries, sub-800ms for complex multi-hop queries.
  12: """
  13: 
  14: from __future__ import annotations
  15: 
  16: import asyncio
  17: import hashlib
  18: from collections.abc import Callable
  19: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  20: from datetime import UTC, datetime
  21: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
  22: from uuid import UUID
  23: 
  24: from loguru import logger
  25: from neo4j import AsyncGraphDatabase
  26: 
  27: from khora.config import KhoraConfig, LiteLLMConfig
  28: from khora.core.models import Chunk, Document, DocumentMetadata, Entity, MemoryNamespace, Organization, Workspace
  29: from khora.engines.skeleton.backends import TemporalChunk, TemporalFilter, create_temporal_store
  30: from khora.engines.skeleton.skeleton import SkeletonIndexer
  31: from khora.extraction.embedders import LiteLLMEmbedder
  32: from khora.memory_lake import BatchResult, RecallResult, RememberResult, Stats
  33: from khora.query import SearchMode
  34: from khora.storage import StorageConfig, create_storage_coordinator
  35: 
  36: from .dual_nodes import DualNodeManager, EntityChunkLink
  37: from .retriever import RetrieverConfig, VectorCypherRetriever
  38: from .router import QueryComplexityRouter, RouterConfig
  39: 
  40: if TYPE_CHECKING:
  41:     from neo4j import AsyncDriver
  42: 
  43:     from khora.extraction.skills import ExpertiseConfig
  44:     from khora.storage import StorageCoordinator
  45: 
  46: 
  47: @dataclass
  48: class ExtractionQualityMetrics:
  49:     """Track extraction quality for monitoring."""
  50: 
  51:     total_chunks: int = 0
  52:     chunks_with_entities: int = 0
  53:     total_entities: int = 0
  54:     total_relationships: int = 0
  55:     avg_entities_per_chunk: float = 0.0
  56:     avg_confidence: float = 0.0
  57:     entity_type_distribution: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
  58: 
  59:     def compute_averages(self) -> None:
  60:         """Compute average metrics from totals."""
  61:         if self.total_chunks > 0:
  62:             self.avg_entities_per_chunk = self.total_entities / self.total_chunks
  63: 
  64: 
  65: @dataclass
  66: class VectorCypherConfig:
  67:     """VectorCypher-specific configuration."""
  68: 
  69:     # Routing
  70:     routing_enabled: bool = True
  71:     routing_use_llm: bool = False
  72: 
  73:     # Skeleton indexing
  74:     skeleton_core_ratio: float = 0.40  # 40% get full KG extraction (increased for +10-20% recall)
  75: 
  76:     # Graph traversal
  77:     graph_default_depth: int = 2
  78:     graph_max_depth: int = 4
  79:     graph_max_entry_entities: int = 10
  80: 
  81:     # Fusion
  82:     fusion_rrf_k: int = 60
  83:     fusion_vector_weight: float = 0.6
  84:     fusion_graph_weight: float = 0.4
  85: 
  86:     # Temporal
  87:     temporal_recency_weight: float = 0.2
  88:     temporal_recency_decay_days: int = 30
  89: 
  90: 
  91: class VectorCypherEngine:
  92:     """VectorCypher engine - hybrid vector+graph retrieval with temporal support.
  93: 
  94:     Key features:
  95:     - Dual retrieval: Vector similarity (pgvector) + graph traversal (Neo4j)
  96:     - Smart routing: Route queries to optimal path (simple vs complex)
  97:     - Skeleton indexing: Full KG extraction only for core 40% chunks
  98:     - Bi-temporal model: Track occurred_at vs ingested_at
  99:     - RRF fusion: Combine vector and graph scores
 100: 
 101:     Requirements:
 102:     - PostgreSQL with pgvector extension
 103:     - Neo4j (required, not optional like in SkeletonEngine)
 104: 
 105:     Usage:
 106:         engine = VectorCypherEngine(config)
 107:         await engine.connect()
 108: 
 109:         # Store with temporal context
 110:         result = await engine.remember(
 111:             "Meeting with John about Q1 planning",
 112:             namespace_id,
 113:             occurred_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15),
 114:         )
 115: 
 116:         # Retrieve with hybrid search
 117:         result = await engine.recall(
 118:             "What did we discuss with John about planning?",
 119:             namespace_id,
 120:             graph_depth=2,
 121:         )
 122:     """
 123: 
 124:     def __init__(
 125:         self,
 126:         config: KhoraConfig,
 127:         *,
 128:         storage_config: StorageConfig | None = None,
 129:         vectorcypher_config: VectorCypherConfig | None = None,
 130:     ) -> None:
 131:         """Initialize the VectorCypher engine.
 132: 
 133:         Args:
 134:             config: KhoraConfig instance
 135:             storage_config: Storage configuration (deprecated, derived from config)
 136:             vectorcypher_config: VectorCypher-specific configuration
 137:         """
 138:         self._config = config
 139:         self._vc_config = vectorcypher_config or VectorCypherConfig()
 140: 
 141:         # Build storage config
 142:         if storage_config:
 143:             self._storage_config = storage_config
 144:         else:
 145:             postgresql_url = config.get_postgresql_url()
 146:             graph_config = config.get_graph_config()
 147: 
 148:             storage_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
 149:                 "postgresql_url": postgresql_url,
 150:                 "pgvector_url": postgresql_url,
 151:                 "pgvector_embedding_dimension": config.storage.embedding_dimension,
 152:                 "graph_config": graph_config,
 153:                 "vector_config": config.get_vector_config(),
 154:             }
 155:             if graph_config is not None:
 156:                 storage_kwargs["neo4j_url"] = config.get_neo4j_url()
 157:                 storage_kwargs["neo4j_user"] = config.get_neo4j_user()
 158:                 storage_kwargs["neo4j_password"] = config.get_neo4j_password()
 159:                 storage_kwargs["neo4j_database"] = config.get_neo4j_database()
 160: 
 161:             self._storage_config = StorageConfig(**storage_kwargs)
 162: 
 163:         # Component instances (initialized on connect)
 164:         self._storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None
 165:         self._temporal_store = None
 166:         self._neo4j_driver: AsyncDriver | None = None
 167:         self._embedder: LiteLLMEmbedder | None = None
 168:         self._retriever: VectorCypherRetriever | None = None
 169:         self._dual_nodes: DualNodeManager | None = None
 170:         self._router: QueryComplexityRouter | None = None
 171:         self._connected = False
 172: 
 173:         # Default namespace cache
 174:         self._default_namespace_id: UUID | None = None
 175: 
 176:     async def connect(self) -> None:
 177:         """Connect to all storage backends."""
 178:         if self._connected:
 179:             return
 180: 
 181:         logger.info("Connecting VectorCypher engine...")
 182: 
 183:         # Create and connect relational storage
 184:         self._storage = create_storage_coordinator(self._storage_config)
 185:         await self._storage.connect()
 186: 
 187:         # Create and connect temporal vector store (pgvector)
 188:         self._temporal_store = create_temporal_store("pgvector", self._config)
 189:         await self._temporal_store.connect()
 190: 
 191:         # Connect to Neo4j (required for VectorCypher)
 192:         neo4j_url = self._config.get_neo4j_url()
 193:         if not neo4j_url:
 194:             raise ValueError(
 195:                 "Neo4j URL is required for VectorCypher engine. Set GENESIS_NEO4J_URL or configure graph_config."
 196:             )
 197: 
 198:         self._neo4j_driver = AsyncGraphDatabase.driver(
 199:             neo4j_url,
 200:             auth=(self._config.get_neo4j_user(), self._config.get_neo4j_password()),
 201:             max_connection_pool_size=50,
 202:         )
 203:         await self._neo4j_driver.verify_connectivity()
 204: 
 205:         # Create embedder
 206:         llm_config = LiteLLMConfig(
 207:             model=self._config.llm.model,
 208:             embedding_model=self._config.llm.embedding_model,
 209:             embedding_dimension=self._config.llm.embedding_dimension,
 210:             timeout=self._config.llm.timeout,
 211:             max_retries=self._config.llm.max_retries,
 212:         )
 213:         self._embedder = LiteLLMEmbedder.from_config(llm_config)
 214: 
 215:         # Initialize dual node manager
 216:         neo4j_database = self._config.get_neo4j_database() or "neo4j"
 217:         self._dual_nodes = DualNodeManager(self._neo4j_driver, neo4j_database)
 218:         await self._dual_nodes.ensure_indexes()
 219: 
 220:         # Initialize router
 221:         router_config = RouterConfig(
 222:             enabled=self._vc_config.routing_enabled,
 223:             use_llm=self._vc_config.routing_use_llm,
 224:             moderate_depth=1,
 225:             complex_depth=self._vc_config.graph_default_depth,
 226:         )
 227:         self._router = QueryComplexityRouter(router_config)
 228: 
 229:         # Initialize retriever
 230:         retriever_config = RetrieverConfig(
 231:             default_depth=self._vc_config.graph_default_depth,
 232:             max_depth=self._vc_config.graph_max_depth,
 233:             max_entry_entities=self._vc_config.graph_max_entry_entities,
 234:             rrf_k=self._vc_config.fusion_rrf_k,
 235:             vector_weight=self._vc_config.fusion_vector_weight,
 236:             graph_weight=self._vc_config.fusion_graph_weight,
 237:             recency_weight=self._vc_config.temporal_recency_weight,
 238:             recency_decay_days=self._vc_config.temporal_recency_decay_days,
 239:         )
 240:         self._retriever = VectorCypherRetriever(
 241:             vector_store=self._temporal_store,
 242:             neo4j_driver=self._neo4j_driver,
 243:             embedder=self._embedder,
 244:             database=neo4j_database,
 245:             config=retriever_config,
 246:         )
 247: 
 248:         # Initialize telemetry
 249:         from khora.telemetry import init_telemetry
 250:         from khora.telemetry.config import TelemetryConfig
 251: 
 252:         telemetry_cfg = TelemetryConfig(
 253:             database_url=self._config.telemetry_database_url,
 254:             service_name=self._config.telemetry_service_name,
 255:         )
 256:         await init_telemetry(telemetry_cfg)
 257: 
 258:         self._connected = True
 259:         logger.info("VectorCypher engine connected")
 260: 
 261:     async def disconnect(self) -> None:
 262:         """Disconnect from all storage backends."""
 263:         if not self._connected:
 264:             return
 265: 
 266:         logger.info("Disconnecting VectorCypher engine...")
 267: 
 268:         # Shutdown telemetry
 269:         from khora.telemetry import shutdown_telemetry
 270: 
 271:         await shutdown_telemetry()
 272: 
 273:         if self._neo4j_driver:
 274:             await self._neo4j_driver.close()
 275:             self._neo4j_driver = None
 276: 
 277:         if self._temporal_store:
 278:             await self._temporal_store.disconnect()
 279:             self._temporal_store = None
 280: 
 281:         if self._storage:
 282:             await self._storage.disconnect()
 283:             self._storage = None
 284: 
 285:         self._embedder = None
 286:         self._retriever = None
 287:         self._dual_nodes = None
 288:         self._router = None
 289:         self._connected = False
 290: 
 291:         logger.info("VectorCypher engine disconnected")
 292: 
 293:     def _get_storage(self) -> StorageCoordinator:
 294:         if self._storage is None:
 295:             raise RuntimeError("VectorCypher engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
 296:         return self._storage
 297: 
 298:     def _get_temporal_store(self):
 299:         if self._temporal_store is None:
 300:             raise RuntimeError("VectorCypher engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
 301:         return self._temporal_store
 302: 
 303:     def _get_embedder(self) -> LiteLLMEmbedder:
 304:         if self._embedder is None:
 305:             raise RuntimeError("VectorCypher engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
 306:         return self._embedder
 307: 
 308:     def _get_retriever(self) -> VectorCypherRetriever:
 309:         if self._retriever is None:
 310:             raise RuntimeError("VectorCypher engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
 311:         return self._retriever
 312: 
 313:     def _get_dual_nodes(self) -> DualNodeManager:
 314:         if self._dual_nodes is None:
 315:             raise RuntimeError("VectorCypher engine not connected. Call connect() first.")
 316:         return self._dual_nodes
 317: 
 318:     # =========================================================================
 319:     # Core API: remember, recall, forget
 320:     # =========================================================================
 321: 
 322:     async def remember(
 323:         self,
 324:         content: str,
 325:         namespace_id: UUID,
 326:         *,
 327:         title: str = "",
 328:         source: str = "",
 329:         metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 330:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
 331:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig | str | None = None,
 332:         extraction_model: str | None = None,
 333:         occurred_at: datetime | None = None,
 334:     ) -> RememberResult:
 335:         """Store content in the memory engine.
 336: 
 337:         Args:
 338:             content: Content to store
 339:             namespace_id: Namespace to store in
 340:             title: Document title
 341:             source: Document source
 342:             metadata: Additional metadata
 343:             skill_name: Extraction skill to use
 344:             expertise: ExpertiseConfig, expertise name string, or file path
 345:             extraction_model: LLM model for entity extraction (default: config model)
 346:             occurred_at: When this content/event occurred (default: now)
 347: 
 348:         Returns:
 349:             RememberResult with document_id and counts
 350:         """
 351:         checksum = hashlib.sha256(content.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
 352:         storage = self._get_storage()
 353: 
 354:         # Check for duplicate
 355:         existing = await storage.get_document_by_checksum(namespace_id, checksum)
 356:         if existing:
 357:             logger.debug(f"Document already exists (checksum={checksum[:8]}..., status={existing.status})")
 358:             return RememberResult(
 359:                 document_id=existing.id,
 360:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
 361:                 chunks_created=existing.chunk_count,
 362:                 entities_extracted=existing.entity_count,
 363:                 relationships_created=0,
 364:                 metadata={"duplicate": True, "status": str(existing.status)},
 365:             )
 366: 
 367:         # Create document
 368:         doc_metadata = DocumentMetadata(
 369:             title=title,
 370:             source=source,
 371:             source_type="api",
 372:             checksum=checksum,
 373:             size_bytes=len(content.encode("utf-8")),
 374:             custom=metadata or {},
 375:         )
 376:         document = Document(
 377:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 378:             content=content,
 379:             metadata=doc_metadata,
 380:         )
 381:         document = await storage.create_document(document)
 382: 
 383:         # Process document
 384:         chunks_created, entities_extracted, relationships_created = await self._process_document(
 385:             document,
 386:             skill_name=skill_name,
 387:             expertise=expertise,
 388:             extraction_model=extraction_model,
 389:             occurred_at=occurred_at or datetime.now(UTC),
 390:         )
 391: 
 392:         return RememberResult(
 393:             document_id=document.id,
 394:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 395:             chunks_created=chunks_created,
 396:             entities_extracted=entities_extracted,
 397:             relationships_created=relationships_created,
 398:         )
 399: 
 400:     async def _process_document(
 401:         self,
 402:         document: Document,
 403:         *,
 404:         skill_name: str,
 405:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig | str | None = None,
 406:         extraction_model: str | None = None,
 407:         occurred_at: datetime,
 408:     ) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
 409:         """Process a document into chunks with skeleton-based entity extraction.
 410: 
 411:         The VectorCypher pipeline:
 412:         1. Chunk the document
 413:         2. Embed all chunks
 414:         3. Run skeleton indexing to identify core chunks (25%)
 415:         4. Extract entities only from core chunks
 416:         5. Store chunks in pgvector and create Chunk nodes in Neo4j
 417:         6. Link entities to chunks via MENTIONED_IN
 418:         """
 419:         from khora.pipelines.chunking import create_chunker
 420:         from khora.pipelines.chunking.config import ChunkerConfig
 421: 
 422:         storage = self._get_storage()
 423:         embedder = self._get_embedder()
 424:         temporal_store = self._get_temporal_store()
 425:         dual_nodes = self._get_dual_nodes()
 426: 
 427:         # Create chunker
 428:         chunker_config = ChunkerConfig(
 429:             strategy=self._config.pipeline.chunking_strategy,
 430:             chunk_size=self._config.pipeline.chunk_size,
 431:             chunk_overlap=self._config.pipeline.chunk_overlap,
 432:         )
 433:         chunker = create_chunker(chunker_config)
 434: 
 435:         # Chunk the document
 436:         raw_chunks = await asyncio.to_thread(chunker.chunk, document.content)
 437: 
 438:         if not raw_chunks:
 439:             document.mark_completed(0, 0)
 440:             await storage.update_document(document)
 441:             return 0, 0, 0
 442: 
 443:         # Embed chunks in batch
 444:         chunk_texts = [c.content for c in raw_chunks]
 445:         embeddings = await embedder.embed_batch(chunk_texts)
 446: 
 447:         # Extract metadata
 448:         doc_metadata = document.metadata.custom if document.metadata else {}
 449: 
 450:         # Create temporal chunks
 451:         temporal_chunks = []
 452:         for i, (raw_chunk, embedding) in enumerate(zip(raw_chunks, embeddings)):
 453:             temporal_chunk = TemporalChunk(
 454:                 id=None,
 455:                 namespace_id=document.namespace_id,
 456:                 document_id=document.id,
 457:                 content=raw_chunk.content,
 458:                 embedding=embedding,
 459:                 occurred_at=occurred_at,
 460:                 created_at=datetime.now(UTC),
 461:                 source_system=doc_metadata.get("source_system"),
 462:                 author=doc_metadata.get("author"),
 463:                 channel=doc_metadata.get("channel"),
 464:                 tags=doc_metadata.get("tags", []),
 465:                 confidence=1.0,
 466:                 metadata={
 467:                     "chunk_index": i,
 468:                     "start_char": raw_chunk.start_char if hasattr(raw_chunk, "start_char") else 0,
 469:                     "end_char": raw_chunk.end_char if hasattr(raw_chunk, "end_char") else len(raw_chunk.content),
 470:                 },
 471:             )
 472:             temporal_chunks.append(temporal_chunk)
 473: 
 474:         # Store in pgvector
 475:         stored_chunks = await temporal_store.create_chunks_batch(temporal_chunks)
 476: 
 477:         # Update temporal_chunks with assigned IDs
 478:         for i, stored in enumerate(stored_chunks):
 479:             temporal_chunks[i].id = stored.id
 480: 
 481:         # Create Chunk nodes in Neo4j
 482:         await dual_nodes.create_chunk_nodes_batch(temporal_chunks, document.namespace_id)
 483: 
 484:         # Skeleton-based entity extraction (for core chunks only)
 485:         entities_extracted = 0
 486:         relationships_created = 0
 487: 
 488:         if self._config.pipeline.extract_entities:
 489:             entities_extracted, relationships_created = await self._run_skeleton_extraction(
 490:                 temporal_chunks,
 491:                 document.namespace_id,
 492:                 skill_name=skill_name,
 493:                 expertise=expertise,
 494:                 extraction_model=extraction_model,
 495:             )
 496: 
 497:         # Update document status
 498:         document.mark_completed(len(stored_chunks), entities_extracted)
 499:         await storage.update_document(document)
 500: 
 501:         logger.debug(
 502:             f"Processed document {document.id}: {len(stored_chunks)} chunks, "
 503:             f"{entities_extracted} entities, {relationships_created} relationships"
 504:         )
 505: 
 506:         return len(stored_chunks), entities_extracted, relationships_created
 507: 
 508:     async def _run_skeleton_extraction(
 509:         self,
 510:         chunks: list[TemporalChunk],
 511:         namespace_id: UUID,
 512:         *,
 513:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
 514:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig | str | None = None,
 515:         extraction_model: str | None = None,
 516:     ) -> tuple[int, int]:
 517:         """Run skeleton-based entity extraction on core chunks only.
 518: 
 519:         Uses the skeleton indexer to identify the top core chunks (by PageRank),
 520:         then runs full LLM extraction on those chunks and stores the results
 521:         in both Neo4j (entities, relationships, MENTIONED_IN links) and pgvector.
 522: 
 523:         Args:
 524:             chunks: All chunks from the document
 525:             namespace_id: Namespace ID
 526:             skill_name: Extraction skill to use
 527:             expertise: ExpertiseConfig, expertise name string, or file path
 528:             extraction_model: LLM model for extraction (default: config model)
 529: 
 530:         Returns:
 531:             Tuple of (entities_extracted, relationships_created)
 532:         """
 533:         from khora.pipelines.tasks.extract import extract_entities
 534: 
 535:         if not chunks:
 536:             return 0, 0
 537: 
 538:         # Build skeleton index
 539:         skeleton = SkeletonIndexer(core_ratio=self._vc_config.skeleton_core_ratio)
 540:         skeleton.add_chunks_batch(chunks)
 541:         core_ids = await asyncio.to_thread(skeleton.build_skeleton)
 542: 
 543:         logger.debug(f"Skeleton indexing: {len(core_ids)}/{len(chunks)} core chunks")
 544: 
 545:         if not core_ids:
 546:             return 0, 0
 547: 
 548:         # Get core chunks
 549:         core_temporal_chunks = [c for c in chunks if c.id in core_ids]
 550: 
 551:         # Convert TemporalChunk -> Chunk for extract_entities()
 552:         chunk_objects = []
 553:         for tc in core_temporal_chunks:
 554:             chunk_objects.append(
 555:                 Chunk(
 556:                     id=tc.id,
 557:                     namespace_id=tc.namespace_id,
 558:                     document_id=tc.document_id,
 559:                     content=tc.content,
 560:                     created_at=tc.created_at or tc.occurred_at,
 561:                 )
 562:             )
 563: 
 564:         # Run LLM extraction on core chunks
 565:         model = extraction_model or self._config.llm.model
 566:         entities, relationships = await extract_entities(
 567:             chunk_objects,
 568:             skill_name=skill_name,
 569:             expertise=expertise,
 570:             model=model,
 571:             max_concurrent=self._config.llm.max_concurrent_llm_calls,
 572:         )
 573: 
 574:         if not entities:
 575:             return 0, 0
 576: 
 577:         storage = self._get_storage()
 578:         dual_nodes = self._get_dual_nodes()
 579: 
 580:         # Store entities in Neo4j + pgvector
 581:         await storage.upsert_entities_batch(namespace_id, entities)
 582: 
 583:         # Store relationships in Neo4j
 584:         relationships_created = 0
 585:         if relationships:
 586:             relationships_created = await storage.create_relationships_batch(relationships)
 587: 
 588:         # Build entity-chunk links from source_chunk_ids
 589:         entity_chunk_links: list[EntityChunkLink] = []
 590:         for entity in entities:
 591:             for chunk_id in entity.source_chunk_ids:
 592:                 entity_chunk_links.append(
 593:                     EntityChunkLink(
 594:                         entity_id=entity.id,
 595:                         chunk_id=chunk_id,
 596:                     )
 597:                 )
 598: 
 599:         # Create MENTIONED_IN edges in Neo4j
 600:         if entity_chunk_links:
 601:             await dual_nodes.link_entities_to_chunks_batch(entity_chunk_links)
 602: 
 603:         logger.debug(
 604:             f"Skeleton extraction: {len(entities)} entities, "
 605:             f"{relationships_created} relationships from {len(core_temporal_chunks)} core chunks"
 606:         )
 607: 
 608:         return len(entities), relationships_created
 609: 
 610:     def _validate_recall_results(
 611:         self,
 612:         chunks: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], float]],
 613:         query: str,
 614:         *,
 615:         min_content_length: int = 10,
 616:     ) -> list[tuple[dict[str, Any], float]]:
 617:         """Validate and filter retrieval results.
 618: 
 619:         Removes duplicates, filters out empty content, ensures scores are normalized,
 620:         and logs quality warnings.
 621: 
 622:         Args:
 623:             chunks: List of (chunk_dict, score) tuples
 624:             query: Original query text for logging context
 625:             min_content_length: Minimum content length to accept
 626: 
 627:         Returns:
 628:             Validated and filtered list of (chunk_dict, score) tuples
 629:         """
 630:         validated: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], float]] = []
 631:         seen_ids: set[str] = set()
 632:         empty_count = 0
 633:         duplicate_count = 0
 634: 
 635:         for chunk_dict, score in chunks:
 636:             if not isinstance(chunk_dict, dict):
 637:                 logger.warning(f"Skipping non-dict chunk result: {type(chunk_dict)}")
 638:                 continue
 639: 
 640:             chunk_id = chunk_dict.get("id", "")
 641: 
 642:             # Skip duplicates
 643:             if chunk_id and chunk_id in seen_ids:
 644:                 duplicate_count += 1
 645:                 continue
 646:             if chunk_id:
 647:                 seen_ids.add(chunk_id)
 648: 
 649:             # Skip empty content
 650:             content = chunk_dict.get("content", "")
 651:             if not content or len(content.strip()) < min_content_length:
 652:                 empty_count += 1
 653:                 continue
 654: 
 655:             # Normalize score to [0, 1]
 656:             normalized_score = min(max(score, 0.0), 1.0)
 657: 
 658:             validated.append((chunk_dict, normalized_score))
 659: 
 660:         # Log quality warnings
 661:         if duplicate_count > 0:
 662:             logger.debug(f"Recall validation: removed {duplicate_count} duplicate chunks for query: {query[:50]}...")
 663:         if empty_count > 0:
 664:             logger.warning(f"Recall validation: filtered {empty_count} empty/short chunks for query: {query[:50]}...")
 665: 
 666:         return validated
 667: 
 668:     async def recall(
 669:         self,
 670:         query: str,
 671:         namespace_id: UUID,
 672:         *,
 673:         limit: int = 10,
 674:         mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.HYBRID,
 675:         min_similarity: float = 0.0,
 676:         agentic: bool = False,
 677:         raw: bool = False,
 678:         # VectorCypher-specific parameters
 679:         temporal_filter: TemporalFilter | None = None,
 680:         graph_depth: int | None = None,
 681:         hybrid_alpha: float | None = None,
 682:     ) -> RecallResult:
 683:         """Recall memories relevant to a query using VectorCypher.
 684: 
 685:         Args:
 686:             query: Query text
 687:             namespace_id: Namespace to search
 688:             limit: Maximum number of results
 689:             mode: Search mode (VECTOR, GRAPH, HYBRID)
 690:             min_similarity: Minimum similarity threshold
 691:             agentic: Whether to use agentic mode
 692:             raw: Disable all LLM features
 693:             temporal_filter: Temporal constraints
 694:             graph_depth: Override graph traversal depth
 695:             hybrid_alpha: Blend factor (0=graph, 1=vector)
 696: 
 697:         Returns:
 698:             RecallResult with chunks, entities, and context
 699:         """
 700:         retriever = self._get_retriever()
 701: 
 702:         # Use VectorCypher retriever
 703:         result = await retriever.retrieve(
 704:             query=query,
 705:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 706:             temporal_filter=temporal_filter,
 707:             graph_depth=graph_depth,
 708:             limit=limit,
 709:         )
 710: 
 711:         # Validate and filter retrieval results
 712:         validated_chunks = self._validate_recall_results(result.chunks, query)
 713: 
 714:         # Build context text from validated chunks
 715:         context_parts = []
 716:         for chunk_dict, score in validated_chunks:
 717:             if isinstance(chunk_dict, dict):
 718:                 context_parts.append(chunk_dict.get("content", ""))
 719: 
 720:         context_text = "\n\n---\n\n".join(context_parts[:limit])
 721: 
 722:         return RecallResult(
 723:             query=query,
 724:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 725:             chunks=validated_chunks,
 726:             entities=result.entities,
 727:             context_text=context_text,
 728:             metadata={
 729:                 "engine": "vectorcypher",
 730:                 "routing": result.routing_decision.complexity.value,
 731:                 "use_graph": result.routing_decision.use_graph,
 732:                 "graph_depth": result.routing_decision.graph_depth,
 733:                 "raw_chunk_count": len(result.chunks),
 734:                 "validated_chunk_count": len(validated_chunks),
 735:                 **result.metadata,
 736:             },
 737:         )
 738: 
 739:     async def forget(self, document_id: UUID, namespace_id: UUID | None) -> bool:
 740:         """Remove a memory from the engine."""
 741:         storage = self._get_storage()
 742:         temporal_store = self._get_temporal_store()
 743:         dual_nodes = self._get_dual_nodes()
 744: 
 745:         # Verify namespace if provided
 746:         if namespace_id:
 747:             document = await storage.get_document(document_id)
 748:             if document and document.namespace_id != namespace_id:
 749:                 logger.warning(f"Document {document_id} not in namespace {namespace_id}")
 750:                 return False
 751: 
 752:         ns_id = namespace_id
 753:         if not ns_id:
 754:             document = await storage.get_document(document_id)
 755:             if document:
 756:                 ns_id = document.namespace_id
 757:             else:
 758:                 return False
 759: 
 760:         # Delete from Neo4j (Chunk nodes and relationships)
 761:         await dual_nodes.delete_chunks_by_document(document_id, ns_id)
 762: 
 763:         # Delete from pgvector
 764:         await temporal_store.delete_chunks_by_document(document_id, ns_id)
 765: 
 766:         # Delete from relational storage
 767:         return await storage.delete_document(document_id)
 768: 
 769:     async def remember_batch(
 770:         self,
 771:         documents: list[dict[str, Any]],
 772:         namespace_id: UUID,
 773:         *,
 774:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
 775:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig | str | None = None,
 776:         extraction_model: str | None = None,
 777:         max_concurrent: int = 10,
 778:         deduplicate: bool = True,
 779:         infer_relationships: bool = True,
 780:         on_progress: Callable[[int, int], None] | None = None,
 781:     ) -> BatchResult:
 782:         """Store multiple documents with automatic optimization.
 783: 
 784:         Args:
 785:             documents: List of document dicts with 'content', 'title', 'source', 'metadata'
 786:             namespace_id: Namespace to store documents in
 787:             skill_name: Extraction skill to use
 788:             max_concurrent: Maximum concurrent document processing
 789:             deduplicate: Whether to skip duplicate documents
 790:             infer_relationships: Whether to infer relationships
 791:             on_progress: Callback for progress updates
 792: 
 793:         Returns:
 794:             BatchResult with processing statistics
 795:         """
 796:         if not documents:
 797:             return BatchResult(
 798:                 total=0,
 799:                 processed=0,
 800:                 skipped=0,
 801:                 failed=0,
 802:                 chunks=0,
 803:                 entities=0,
 804:                 relationships=0,
 805:             )
 806: 
 807:         storage = self._get_storage()
 808:         total = len(documents)
 809: 
 810:         # Track results
 811:         results: dict[str, int] = {
 812:             "processed": 0,
 813:             "skipped": 0,
 814:             "failed": 0,
 815:             "chunks": 0,
 816:             "entities": 0,
 817:             "relationships": 0,
 818:         }
 819:         results_lock = asyncio.Lock()
 820:         progress_count = 0
 821:         progress_lock = asyncio.Lock()
 822: 
 823:         # Compute checksums
 824:         doc_checksums: list[str] = []
 825:         for doc_data in documents:
 826:             content = doc_data.get("content", "")
 827:             checksum = hashlib.sha256(content.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
 828:             doc_checksums.append(checksum)
 829: 
 830:         # Batch lookup existing documents
 831:         existing_docs: dict[str, Any] = {}
 832:         if deduplicate:
 833:             existing_docs = await storage.get_documents_by_checksums(namespace_id, doc_checksums)
 834: 
 835:         # Track in-flight checksums for intra-batch deduplication
 836:         checksums_in_flight: set[str] = set()
 837:         checksums_lock = asyncio.Lock()
 838: 
 839:         semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrent)
 840: 
 841:         async def process_document(doc_data: dict[str, Any], checksum: str) -> None:
 842:             nonlocal progress_count
 843: 
 844:             # Check for intra-batch duplicate
 845:             async with checksums_lock:
 846:                 if checksum in checksums_in_flight:
 847:                     async with results_lock:
 848:                         results["skipped"] += 1
 849:                     return
 850:                 checksums_in_flight.add(checksum)
 851: 
 852:             # Check if already exists
 853:             if deduplicate and checksum in existing_docs:
 854:                 async with results_lock:
 855:                     results["skipped"] += 1
 856:                 if on_progress:
 857:                     async with progress_lock:
 858:                         progress_count += 1
 859:                         on_progress(progress_count, total)
 860:                 return
 861: 
 862:             async with semaphore:
 863:                 try:
 864:                     doc_metadata = doc_data.get("metadata", {})
 865:                     occurred_at = None
 866:                     if "occurred_at" in doc_metadata:
 867:                         occurred_at = self._parse_datetime(doc_metadata["occurred_at"])
 868: 
 869:                     result = await self.remember(
 870:                         doc_data.get("content", ""),
 871:                         namespace_id,
 872:                         title=doc_data.get("title", ""),
 873:                         source=doc_data.get("source", ""),
 874:                         metadata=doc_metadata,
 875:                         skill_name=skill_name,
 876:                         expertise=expertise,
 877:                         extraction_model=extraction_model,
 878:                         occurred_at=occurred_at,
 879:                     )
 880: 
 881:                     async with results_lock:
 882:                         if result.metadata.get("duplicate"):
 883:                             results["skipped"] += 1
 884:                         else:
 885:                             results["processed"] += 1
 886:                             results["chunks"] += result.chunks_created
 887:                             results["entities"] += result.entities_extracted
 888:                             results["relationships"] += result.relationships_created
 889: 
 890:                 except Exception as e:
 891:                     logger.error(f"Failed to process document: {e}")
 892:                     async with results_lock:
 893:                         results["failed"] += 1
 894: 
 895:             if on_progress:
 896:                 async with progress_lock:
 897:                     progress_count += 1
 898:                     on_progress(progress_count, total)
 899: 
 900:         await asyncio.gather(*[process_document(doc, checksum) for doc, checksum in zip(documents, doc_checksums)])
 901: 
 902:         return BatchResult(
 903:             total=total,
 904:             processed=results["processed"],
 905:             skipped=results["skipped"],
 906:             failed=results["failed"],
 907:             chunks=results["chunks"],
 908:             entities=results["entities"],
 909:             relationships=results["relationships"],
 910:         )
 911: 
 912:     def _parse_datetime(self, value: Any) -> datetime:
 913:         """Parse a datetime value from various formats."""
 914:         if isinstance(value, datetime):
 915:             if value.tzinfo is None:
 916:                 return value.replace(tzinfo=UTC)
 917:             return value
 918:         if isinstance(value, str):
 919:             try:
 920:                 return datetime.strptime(value, "%Y-%m-%d").replace(tzinfo=UTC)
 921:             except ValueError:
 922:                 pass
 923:             try:
 924:                 dt = datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
 925:                 if dt.tzinfo is None:
 926:                     dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=UTC)
 927:                 return dt
 928:             except ValueError:
 929:                 pass
 930:         raise ValueError(f"Cannot parse datetime: {value}")
 931: 
 932:     # =========================================================================
 933:     # Namespace Management
 934:     # =========================================================================
 935: 
 936:     async def get_or_create_default_namespace(self) -> UUID:
 937:         """Get or create a default namespace for simple usage."""
 938:         if self._default_namespace_id:
 939:             return self._default_namespace_id
 940: 
 941:         storage = self._get_storage()
 942: 
 943:         default_org = await storage.get_organization_by_slug("default")
 944:         if not default_org:
 945:             default_org = await storage.create_organization(Organization(name="Default", slug="default"))
 946: 
 947:         workspaces = await storage.list_workspaces(default_org.id)
 948:         if workspaces:
 949:             default_workspace = workspaces[0]
 950:         else:
 951:             default_workspace = await storage.create_workspace(
 952:                 Workspace(
 953:                     organization_id=default_org.id,
 954:                     name="Default",
 955:                     slug="default",
 956:                 )
 957:             )
 958: 
 959:         namespaces = await storage.list_namespaces(default_workspace.id)
 960:         if namespaces:
 961:             default_namespace = namespaces[0]
 962:         else:
 963:             default_namespace = await storage.create_namespace(
 964:                 MemoryNamespace(
 965:                     workspace_id=default_workspace.id,
 966:                     name="Default",
 967:                     slug="default",
 968:                 )
 969:             )
 970: 
 971:         self._default_namespace_id = default_namespace.id
 972:         return self._default_namespace_id
 973: 
 974:     async def create_namespace(
 975:         self,
 976:         name: str,
 977:         workspace_id: UUID,
 978:         *,
 979:         description: str = "",
 980:         config_overrides: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 981:     ) -> MemoryNamespace:
 982:         """Create a new memory namespace."""
 983:         namespace = MemoryNamespace(
 984:             workspace_id=workspace_id,
 985:             name=name,
 986:             description=description,
 987:             config_overrides=config_overrides or {},
 988:         )
 989:         return await self._get_storage().create_namespace(namespace)
 990: 
 991:     async def get_namespace(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> MemoryNamespace | None:
 992:         """Get a namespace by ID."""
 993:         return await self._get_storage().get_namespace(namespace_id)
 994: 
 995:     async def ensure_namespace(
 996:         self,
 997:         name: str,
 998:         *,
 999:         description: str = "",
1000:     ) -> UUID:
1001:         """Get or create a namespace by name."""
1002:         storage = self._get_storage()
1003: 
1004:         await self.get_or_create_default_namespace()
1005: 
1006:         default_org = await storage.get_organization_by_slug("default")
1007:         if not default_org:
1008:             raise RuntimeError("Default organization not found")
1009: 
1010:         workspaces = await storage.list_workspaces(default_org.id)
1011:         if not workspaces:
1012:             raise RuntimeError("Default workspace not found")
1013: 
1014:         default_workspace = workspaces[0]
1015: 
1016:         slug = name.lower().replace(" ", "-")
1017:         existing_ns = await storage.get_namespace_by_slug(default_workspace.id, slug)
1018:         if existing_ns:
1019:             return existing_ns.id
1020: 
1021:         new_ns = await storage.create_namespace(
1022:             MemoryNamespace(
1023:                 workspace_id=default_workspace.id,
````
