1024:                 name=name,
1025:                 slug=slug,
1026:                 description=description,
1027:             )
1028:         )
1029:         return new_ns.id
1030: 
1031:     # =========================================================================
1032:     # Entity Operations
1033:     # =========================================================================
1034: 
1035:     async def get_entity(self, entity_id: UUID) -> Entity | None:
1036:         """Get an entity by ID."""
1037:         return await self._get_storage().get_entity(entity_id)
1038: 
1039:     async def list_entities(
1040:         self,
1041:         namespace_id: UUID,
1042:         *,
1043:         entity_type: str | None = None,
1044:         limit: int = 100,
1045:     ) -> list[Entity]:
1046:         """List entities in a namespace."""
1047:         return await self._get_storage().list_entities(namespace_id, entity_type=entity_type, limit=limit)
1048: 
1049:     async def find_related_entities(
1050:         self,
1051:         entity_id: UUID,
1052:         namespace_id: UUID,
1053:         *,
1054:         max_depth: int = 2,
1055:         limit: int = 20,
1056:     ) -> list[tuple[Entity, float]]:
1057:         """Find entities related to a given entity via graph traversal."""
1058:         dual_nodes = self._get_dual_nodes()
1059: 
1060:         neighborhoods = await dual_nodes.get_entity_neighborhoods(
1061:             entity_ids=[entity_id],
1062:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1063:             depth=max_depth,
1064:             limit_per_entity=limit,
1065:         )
1066: 
1067:         results: list[tuple[Entity, float]] = []
1068:         entity_infos = neighborhoods.get(str(entity_id), [])
1069: 
1070:         for info in entity_infos[:limit]:
1071:             entity = await self._get_storage().get_entity(UUID(info["id"]))
1072:             if entity:
1073:                 score = 1.0 / (1 + info.get("distance", 1))
1074:                 results.append((entity, score))
1075: 
1076:         return results
1077: 
1078:     async def search_entities(
1079:         self,
1080:         query: str,
1081:         namespace_id: UUID,
1082:         *,
1083:         limit: int = 10,
1084:     ) -> list[Entity]:
1085:         """Search entities by query text using embedding similarity."""
1086:         embedder = self._get_embedder()
1087:         query_embedding = await embedder.embed(query)
1088: 
1089:         # Search via storage coordinator
1090:         return await self._get_storage().search_entities_by_embedding(
1091:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1092:             embedding=query_embedding,
1093:             limit=limit,
1094:         )
1095: 
1096:     # =========================================================================
1097:     # Document Operations
1098:     # =========================================================================
1099: 
1100:     async def get_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> Document | None:
1101:         """Get a document by ID."""
1102:         return await self._get_storage().get_document(document_id)
1103: 
1104:     async def list_documents(
1105:         self,
1106:         namespace_id: UUID,
1107:         *,
1108:         limit: int = 100,
1109:     ) -> list[Document]:
1110:         """List documents in a namespace."""
1111:         return await self._get_storage().list_documents(namespace_id, limit=limit)
1112: 
1113:     async def stats(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> Stats:
1114:         """Get document/chunk/entity/relationship counts for a namespace."""
1115:         storage = self._get_storage()
1116:         dual_nodes = self._get_dual_nodes()
1117: 
1118:         try:
1119:             doc_count = await storage.count_documents(namespace_id)
1120:         except (AttributeError, NotImplementedError):
1121:             documents = await storage.list_documents(namespace_id, limit=0)
1122:             doc_count = len(documents) if documents else 0
1123: 
1124:         # Get chunk count from Neo4j
1125:         chunk_count = await dual_nodes.count_chunks(namespace_id)
1126: 
1127:         try:
1128:             entity_count = await storage.count_entities(namespace_id)
1129:         except (AttributeError, NotImplementedError):
1130:             entity_count = 0
1131: 
1132:         try:
1133:             relationship_count = await storage.count_relationships(namespace_id)
1134:         except (AttributeError, NotImplementedError):
1135:             relationship_count = 0
1136: 
1137:         return Stats(
1138:             documents=doc_count,
1139:             chunks=chunk_count,
1140:             entities=entity_count,
1141:             relationships=relationship_count,
1142:         )
1143: 
1144:     async def health_check(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
1145:         """Check health of all components."""
1146:         if not self._connected:
1147:             return {"status": "disconnected"}
1148: 
1149:         storage_health = await self._get_storage().health_check()
1150:         temporal_health = await self._get_temporal_store().health_check()
1151: 
1152:         # Check Neo4j
1153:         neo4j_healthy = False
1154:         if self._neo4j_driver:
1155:             try:
1156:                 await self._neo4j_driver.verify_connectivity()
1157:                 neo4j_healthy = True
1158:             except Exception:
1159:                 pass
1160: 
1161:         all_healthy = storage_health.is_healthy and temporal_health.get("status") == "healthy" and neo4j_healthy
1162: 
1163:         return {
1164:             "status": "healthy" if all_healthy else "degraded",
1165:             "storage": storage_health.summary,
1166:             "temporal_store": temporal_health,
1167:             "neo4j": "healthy" if neo4j_healthy else "unhealthy",
1168:             "engine": "vectorcypher",
1169:         }
1170: 
1171: 
1172: __all__ = ["ExtractionQualityMetrics", "VectorCypherConfig", "VectorCypherEngine"]
````

## File: src/khora/query/linking.py
````python
  1: """Entity linking module for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Links entity mentions from queries to existing entities in the knowledge graph.
  4: Uses multiple strategies:
  5: - Exact name matching
  6: - Fuzzy string matching
  7: - Embedding similarity
  8: """
  9: 
 10: from __future__ import annotations
 11: 
 12: import asyncio
 13: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 14: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 15: from uuid import UUID
 16: 
 17: from loguru import logger
 18: 
 19: from khora._accel import sequence_match_ratio
 20: from khora.core.models.entity import entity_type_str
 21: 
 22: from .understanding import EntityMention
 23: 
 24: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 25:     from khora.core.models import Entity
 26:     from khora.extraction.embedders import Embedder
 27:     from khora.storage import StorageCoordinator
 28: 
 29: 
 30: @dataclass
 31: class LinkedEntity:
 32:     """An entity mention linked to a stored entity."""
 33: 
 34:     mention: EntityMention
 35:     entity: Entity | None = None
 36:     match_method: str = ""  # exact, fuzzy, embedding
 37:     match_score: float = 0.0
 38:     candidates: list[tuple[Entity, float]] = field(default_factory=list)
 39: 
 40:     @property
 41:     def is_linked(self) -> bool:
 42:         """Check if the mention was successfully linked."""
 43:         return self.entity is not None
 44: 
 45: 
 46: @dataclass
 47: class LinkingResult:
 48:     """Result of entity linking."""
 49: 
 50:     linked_entities: list[LinkedEntity] = field(default_factory=list)
 51:     unlinked_count: int = 0
 52:     total_mentions: int = 0
 53:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 54: 
 55:     @property
 56:     def linked_count(self) -> int:
 57:         """Get count of successfully linked entities."""
 58:         return sum(1 for le in self.linked_entities if le.is_linked)
 59: 
 60:     @property
 61:     def success_rate(self) -> float:
 62:         """Get linking success rate."""
 63:         if self.total_mentions == 0:
 64:             return 0.0
 65:         return self.linked_count / self.total_mentions
 66: 
 67:     def get_linked_entity_ids(self) -> list[UUID]:
 68:         """Get IDs of all linked entities."""
 69:         return [le.entity.id for le in self.linked_entities if le.entity is not None]
 70: 
 71: 
 72: class EntityLinker:
 73:     """Links query entity mentions to stored entities.
 74: 
 75:     Uses multiple strategies to find matches:
 76:     1. Exact name match (fastest, highest precision)
 77:     2. Fuzzy string matching (handles typos, variations)
 78:     3. Embedding similarity (semantic matching)
 79:     """
 80: 
 81:     def __init__(
 82:         self,
 83:         storage: StorageCoordinator,
 84:         embedder: Embedder | None = None,
 85:         *,
 86:         exact_match: bool = True,
 87:         fuzzy_match: bool = True,
 88:         embedding_match: bool = True,
 89:         fuzzy_threshold: float = 0.8,
 90:         embedding_threshold: float = 0.7,
 91:         max_candidates: int = 5,
 92:     ) -> None:
 93:         """Initialize the entity linker.
 94: 
 95:         Args:
 96:             storage: Storage coordinator for entity access
 97:             embedder: Embedder for semantic matching (optional)
 98:             exact_match: Enable exact name matching
 99:             fuzzy_match: Enable fuzzy string matching
100:             embedding_match: Enable embedding-based matching
101:             fuzzy_threshold: Minimum fuzzy match ratio (0-1)
102:             embedding_threshold: Minimum embedding similarity (0-1)
103:             max_candidates: Maximum candidates to return per mention
104:         """
105:         self._storage = storage
106:         self._embedder = embedder
107:         self._exact_match = exact_match
108:         self._fuzzy_match = fuzzy_match
109:         self._embedding_match = embedding_match
110:         self._fuzzy_threshold = fuzzy_threshold
111:         self._embedding_threshold = embedding_threshold
112:         self._max_candidates = max_candidates
113: 
114:     async def link(
115:         self,
116:         mentions: list[EntityMention],
117:         namespace_id: UUID,
118:     ) -> LinkingResult:
119:         """Link entity mentions to stored entities.
120: 
121:         Pre-computes embeddings for all mentions in a single batch call,
122:         then runs linking in parallel using the cached embeddings.
123: 
124:         Args:
125:             mentions: List of entity mentions to link
126:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
127: 
128:         Returns:
129:             LinkingResult with linked and unlinked entities
130:         """
131:         if not mentions:
132:             return LinkingResult(total_mentions=0)
133: 
134:         # Pre-compute all mention embeddings in a single batch call
135:         # This avoids N individual embed() calls (each ~418ms) in favor of
136:         # one batch call (~500ms total for typical mention counts).
137:         mention_embeddings: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
138:         if self._embedding_match and self._embedder:
139:             mention_texts = [f"{m.entity_type}: {m.name}" for m in mentions]
140:             try:
141:                 embeddings = await self._embedder.embed_batch(mention_texts)
142:                 for text, embedding in zip(mention_texts, embeddings):
143:                     mention_embeddings[text] = embedding
144:             except Exception as e:
145:                 logger.warning(f"Batch mention embedding failed, falling back to per-mention: {e}")
146: 
147:         # Parallelize linking across all mentions
148:         linked_entities = await asyncio.gather(
149:             *[self._link_single(m, namespace_id, mention_embeddings) for m in mentions]
150:         )
151:         unlinked_count = sum(1 for le in linked_entities if not le.is_linked)
152: 
153:         result = LinkingResult(
154:             linked_entities=list(linked_entities),
155:             unlinked_count=unlinked_count,
156:             total_mentions=len(mentions),
157:         )
158: 
159:         # Record telemetry for entity linking
160:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
161: 
162:         exact_count = sum(1 for le in linked_entities if le.match_method == "exact")
163:         fuzzy_count = sum(1 for le in linked_entities if le.match_method == "fuzzy")
164:         embedding_count = sum(1 for le in linked_entities if le.match_method == "embedding")
165:         get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
166:             pipeline="query",
167:             stage="entity_linking",
168:             input_count=len(mentions),
169:             output_count=result.linked_count,
170:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
171:             metadata={"exact": exact_count, "fuzzy": fuzzy_count, "embedding": embedding_count},
172:         )
173: 
174:         return result
175: 
176:     async def _link_single(
177:         self,
178:         mention: EntityMention,
179:         namespace_id: UUID,
180:         precomputed_embeddings: dict[str, list[float]] | None = None,
181:     ) -> LinkedEntity:
182:         """Link a single entity mention.
183: 
184:         Args:
185:             mention: Entity mention to link
186:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
187:             precomputed_embeddings: Pre-computed embeddings keyed by mention text
188: 
189:         Returns:
190:             LinkedEntity with match result
191:         """
192:         candidates: list[tuple[Entity, float, str]] = []  # (entity, score, method)
193: 
194:         # 1. Try exact match first (fastest)
195:         if self._exact_match:
196:             exact = await self._exact_name_match(mention, namespace_id)
197:             if exact:
198:                 return LinkedEntity(
199:                     mention=mention,
200:                     entity=exact,
201:                     match_method="exact",
202:                     match_score=1.0,
203:                 )
204: 
205:         # 2+3. Run fuzzy and embedding matching in parallel
206:         tasks: dict[str, Any] = {}
207:         if self._fuzzy_match:
208:             tasks["fuzzy"] = self._fuzzy_name_match(mention, namespace_id)
209:         if self._embedding_match and self._embedder:
210:             tasks["embedding"] = self._embedding_match_entities(mention, namespace_id, precomputed_embeddings)
211: 
212:         if tasks:
213:             results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks.values(), return_exceptions=True)
214:             for method, result in zip(tasks.keys(), results):
215:                 if isinstance(result, Exception):
216:                     logger.debug(f"{method} match failed: {result}")
217:                     continue
218:                 candidates.extend([(e, s, method) for e, s in result])
219: 
220:         # Select best match
221:         if candidates:
222:             # Sort by score descending
223:             candidates.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
224:             best_entity, best_score, best_method = candidates[0]
225: 
226:             # Return with candidates for context
227:             return LinkedEntity(
228:                 mention=mention,
229:                 entity=best_entity,
230:                 match_method=best_method,
231:                 match_score=best_score,
232:                 candidates=[(e, s) for e, s, _ in candidates[: self._max_candidates]],
233:             )
234: 
235:         # No matches found
236:         return LinkedEntity(
237:             mention=mention,
238:             entity=None,
239:             match_method="",
240:             match_score=0.0,
241:         )
242: 
243:     async def _exact_name_match(
244:         self,
245:         mention: EntityMention,
246:         namespace_id: UUID,
247:     ) -> Entity | None:
248:         """Try exact name match.
249: 
250:         Args:
251:             mention: Entity mention
252:             namespace_id: Namespace to search
253: 
254:         Returns:
255:             Matched entity or None
256:         """
257:         try:
258:             # Try exact match with specified type
259:             entity = await self._storage.get_entity_by_name(
260:                 namespace_id,
261:                 mention.name,
262:                 mention.entity_type,
263:             )
264:             if entity:
265:                 return entity
266: 
267:             # Try case-insensitive match by listing and filtering
268:             entities = await self._storage.list_entities(
269:                 namespace_id,
270:                 entity_type=mention.entity_type,
271:                 limit=1000,
272:             )
273:             name_lower = mention.name.lower()
274:             for entity in entities:
275:                 if entity.name.lower() == name_lower:
276:                     return entity
277: 
278:         except Exception as e:
279:             logger.debug(f"Exact match failed: {e}")
280: 
281:         return None
282: 
283:     async def _fuzzy_name_match(
284:         self,
285:         mention: EntityMention,
286:         namespace_id: UUID,
287:     ) -> list[tuple[Entity, float]]:
288:         """Find entities with fuzzy name matching.
289: 
290:         Args:
291:             mention: Entity mention
292:             namespace_id: Namespace to search
293: 
294:         Returns:
295:             List of (entity, score) tuples
296:         """
297:         matches = []
298: 
299:         try:
300:             # Get all entities — search broadly to catch cross-type matches
301:             # that we can then penalize appropriately
302:             entities = await self._storage.list_entities(
303:                 namespace_id,
304:                 entity_type=mention.entity_type,
305:                 limit=1000,
306:             )
307: 
308:             mention_name_lower = mention.name.lower()
309: 
310:             for entity in entities:
311:                 # Calculate fuzzy similarity
312:                 ratio = sequence_match_ratio(
313:                     mention_name_lower,
314:                     entity.name.lower(),
315:                 )
316: 
317:                 if ratio >= self._fuzzy_threshold:
318:                     # Apply type penalty so wrong-type matches score lower
319:                     penalty = self._type_penalty(mention.entity_type, entity_type_str(entity.entity_type))
320:                     matches.append((entity, ratio * penalty))
321: 
322:             # Also try partial matching (for handling first/last name, abbreviations)
323:             for entity in entities:
324:                 if entity in [m[0] for m in matches]:
325:                     continue
326: 
327:                 # Check if mention is contained in entity name or vice versa
328:                 entity_name_lower = entity.name.lower()
329:                 penalty = self._type_penalty(mention.entity_type, entity_type_str(entity.entity_type))
330:                 if mention_name_lower in entity_name_lower:
331:                     ratio = len(mention_name_lower) / len(entity_name_lower)
332:                     if ratio >= 0.5:  # At least 50% match
333:                         matches.append((entity, ratio * self._fuzzy_threshold * penalty))
334:                 elif entity_name_lower in mention_name_lower:
335:                     ratio = len(entity_name_lower) / len(mention_name_lower)
336:                     if ratio >= 0.5:
337:                         matches.append((entity, ratio * self._fuzzy_threshold * penalty))
338: 
339:         except Exception as e:
340:             logger.debug(f"Fuzzy match failed: {e}")
341: 
342:         # Sort by score and limit
343:         matches.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
344:         return matches[: self._max_candidates]
345: 
346:     async def _embedding_match_entities(
347:         self,
348:         mention: EntityMention,
349:         namespace_id: UUID,
350:         precomputed_embeddings: dict[str, list[float]] | None = None,
351:     ) -> list[tuple[Entity, float]]:
352:         """Find entities using embedding similarity.
353: 
354:         Args:
355:             mention: Entity mention
356:             namespace_id: Namespace to search
357:             precomputed_embeddings: Pre-computed embeddings keyed by mention text
358: 
359:         Returns:
360:             List of (entity, score) tuples
361:         """
362:         if not self._embedder:
363:             return []
364: 
365:         matches = []
366: 
367:         try:
368:             # Use pre-computed embedding if available, otherwise compute on the fly
369:             mention_text = f"{mention.entity_type}: {mention.name}"
370:             if precomputed_embeddings and mention_text in precomputed_embeddings:
371:                 embedding = precomputed_embeddings[mention_text]
372:             else:
373:                 embedding = await self._embedder.embed(mention_text)
374: 
375:             # Search for similar entities
376:             results = await self._storage.search_similar_entities(
377:                 namespace_id,
378:                 embedding,
379:                 limit=self._max_candidates * 2,
380:                 min_similarity=self._embedding_threshold,
381:             )
382: 
383:             # Fetch full entities
384:             for entity_id, score in results:
385:                 entity = await self._storage.get_entity(entity_id)
386:                 if entity:
387:                     # Only include entities of compatible types
388:                     if self._types_compatible(mention.entity_type, entity_type_str(entity.entity_type)):
389:                         # Apply type penalty so exact type matches rank higher
390:                         penalty = self._type_penalty(mention.entity_type, entity_type_str(entity.entity_type))
391:                         matches.append((entity, score * penalty))
392: 
393:         except Exception as e:
394:             logger.debug(f"Embedding match failed: {e}")
395: 
396:         return matches[: self._max_candidates]
397: 
398:     def _types_compatible(self, mention_type: str, entity_type: str) -> bool:
399:         """Check if entity types are compatible for linking.
400: 
401:         Args:
402:             mention_type: Type from query mention
403:             entity_type: Type from stored entity
404: 
405:         Returns:
406:             True if types are compatible
407:         """
408:         # Exact match
409:         if mention_type.upper() == entity_type.upper():
410:             return True
411: 
412:         # CONCEPT is compatible with most types
413:         if mention_type.upper() == "CONCEPT" or entity_type.upper() == "CONCEPT":
414:             return True
415: 
416:         # CUSTOM is a wildcard
417:         if mention_type.upper() == "CUSTOM" or entity_type.upper() == "CUSTOM":
418:             return True
419: 
420:         return False
421: 
422:     def _type_penalty(self, mention_type: str | None, entity_type: str) -> float:
423:         """Compute a score penalty based on entity type compatibility.
424: 
425:         When the query understanding provides an entity type hint,
426:         penalize matches of the wrong type. For example, a query about
427:         "the company Linear" should penalize matching a PERSON named "Linear".
428: 
429:         Args:
430:             mention_type: Expected type from query mention (None = no hint)
431:             entity_type: Type of the candidate entity
432: 
433:         Returns:
434:             Multiplier between 0.0 and 1.0 (1.0 = no penalty)
435:         """
436:         if not mention_type:
437:             return 1.0  # No type info, no penalty
438: 
439:         mention_upper = mention_type.upper()
440:         entity_upper = entity_type.upper()
441: 
442:         # Exact type match — no penalty
443:         if mention_upper == entity_upper:
444:             return 1.0
445: 
446:         # Wildcards — minimal penalty
447:         if mention_upper in ("CONCEPT", "CUSTOM") or entity_upper in ("CONCEPT", "CUSTOM"):
448:             return 0.9
449: 
450:         # Wrong type — heavy penalty
451:         return 0.3
452: 
453: 
454: async def link_query_entities(
455:     mentions: list[EntityMention],
456:     namespace_id: UUID,
457:     storage: StorageCoordinator,
458:     embedder: Embedder | None = None,
459:     *,
460:     fuzzy_threshold: float = 0.8,
461:     embedding_threshold: float = 0.7,
462:     max_candidates: int = 5,
463: ) -> LinkingResult:
464:     """Convenience function to link query entities.
465: 
466:     Args:
467:         mentions: Entity mentions from query understanding
468:         namespace_id: Namespace to search in
469:         storage: Storage coordinator
470:         embedder: Optional embedder for semantic matching
471:         fuzzy_threshold: Fuzzy match threshold
472:         embedding_threshold: Embedding similarity threshold
473:         max_candidates: Max candidates per mention
474: 
475:     Returns:
476:         LinkingResult with linked entities
477:     """
478:     linker = EntityLinker(
479:         storage,
480:         embedder,
481:         fuzzy_threshold=fuzzy_threshold,
482:         embedding_threshold=embedding_threshold,
483:         max_candidates=max_candidates,
484:     )
485:     return await linker.link(mentions, namespace_id)
````

## File: README.md
````markdown
  1: # Khora
  2: 
  3: > *"Khora is the receptacle, the space, the matrix in which all things come to be."*
  4: > *— Plato, Timaeus*
  5: 
  6: In Plato's cosmology, **Khora** (χώρα) is the primordial receptacle—neither being nor non-being, but the space that receives all forms and gives them place. It is the nurse of becoming, the womb of the cosmos where the eternal Forms find material expression. Khora does not impose form; it receives, holds, and makes manifestation possible.
  7: 
  8: This project embodies that philosophy: **Khora is a memory lake**—a receptacle for knowledge that receives information from disparate sources, holds it in structured form, and enables its retrieval through multiple paths of inquiry. Just as Plato's Khora mediates between the intelligible and sensible worlds, this Memory Lake bridges raw data and meaningful knowledge through semantic extraction, graph relationships, and temporal context.
  9: 
 10: ---
 11: 
 12: ## Overview
 13: 
 14: Khora is a **Memory Lake** system that combines three storage paradigms:
 15: 
 16: - **Knowledge Graph** (Neo4j) — Entities and their relationships
 17: - **Vector Database** (pgvector) — Semantic embeddings for similarity search
 18: - **Relational Database** (PostgreSQL) — Documents, events, and metadata
 19: 
 20: It supports **multi-tenancy** with hierarchical isolation (Organization → Workspace → Namespace), **event sourcing** for complete audit trails, and **hybrid search** combining vector similarity, graph traversal, and keyword matching.
 21: 
 22: ### Key Features
 23: 
 24: - **Library-First Design**: Use as a Python library or deploy as a FastAPI service
 25: - **Pluggable Engines**: Choose GraphRAG (knowledge graphs) or Skeleton (temporal-first)
 26: - **Hybrid Search**: Vector + graph + keyword search with Reciprocal Rank Fusion
 27: - **Multi-Tenancy**: Shared mode with ACLs or complete tenant isolation
 28: - **Event Sourcing**: Immutable event log for temporal queries and audit trails
 29: - **LiteLLM Integration**: Unified access to OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and other providers
 30: - **Prefect Pipelines**: Orchestrated ingestion with checksum-based change detection
 31: - **Semantic Extraction**: LLM-powered entity and relationship extraction
 32: 
 33: ---
 34: 
 35: ## Pluggable Engines
 36: 
 37: Khora supports three engines with different strengths:
 38: 
 39: | Engine | Focus | Best For | LLM Cost |
 40: |--------|-------|----------|----------|
 41: | **GraphRAG** | Knowledge graphs | Knowledge bases, entity exploration | Higher |
 42: | **VectorCypher** | Hybrid retrieval | Multi-hop queries, complex relationships | Medium |
 43: | **Skeleton Construction** | Temporal events | Chat logs, events, cost-sensitive apps | 5-10x lower |
 44: 
 45: ### GraphRAG Engine (Default)
 46: 
 47: Full knowledge graph construction with entity and relationship extraction:
 48: 
 49: ```python
 50: from khora import MemoryLake
 51: 
 52: async with MemoryLake("postgresql://...", engine="graphrag") as lake:
 53:     # Extracts entities and relationships from all content
 54:     result = await lake.remember("Einstein developed relativity in 1905.")
 55:     print(f"Extracted {result.entities_extracted} entities")
 56: 
 57:     # Graph-based retrieval
 58:     entities = await lake.list_entities(entity_type="PERSON")
 59:     related = await lake.find_related_entities(entity_id, max_depth=2)
 60: ```
 61: 
 62: **Requirements:** PostgreSQL + pgvector + Neo4j/Kuzu/Memgraph
 63: 
 64: ### Skeleton Construction Engine (Temporal-First)
 65: 
 66: Cost-optimized engine with bi-temporal model and skeleton indexing:
 67: 
 68: ```python
 69: from khora import MemoryLake
 70: 
 71: async with MemoryLake("postgresql://...", engine="skeleton") as lake:
 72:     # Store with temporal metadata
 73:     result = await lake.remember(
 74:         "Team standup notes",
 75:         metadata={
 76:             "occurred_at": "2024-01-15T09:00:00Z",
 77:             "author": "alice@company.com",
 78:             "channel": "engineering"
 79:         }
 80:     )
 81: 
 82:     # Temporal and structured filtering
 83:     results = await lake.recall(
 84:         "What decisions were made?",
 85:         temporal_filter={
 86:             "occurred_after": "2024-01-01",
 87:             "author": "alice@company.com"
 88:         },
 89:         hybrid_alpha=0.7  # 70% vector, 30% BM25
 90:     )
 91: ```
 92: 
 93: **Requirements:** PostgreSQL + pgvector only (Neo4j optional)
 94: 
 95: See [Engine Comparison](docs/engines/engine-comparison.md) for detailed guidance.
 96: 
 97: ---
 98: 
 99: ## Documentation
100: 
101: Comprehensive documentation is available in the [`docs/`](docs/) directory:
102: 
103: | Topic | Description |
104: |-------|-------------|
105: | **Engines** | |
106: | [Skeleton Construction Engine](docs/engines/skeleton-engine.md) | Temporal-first engine documentation |
107: | [VectorCypher Engine](docs/engines/vectorcypher-engine.md) | Hybrid vector+graph engine documentation |
108: | [Engine Comparison](docs/engines/engine-comparison.md) | GraphRAG vs Skeleton vs VectorCypher comparison |
109: | [Temporal Model](docs/engines/temporal-model.md) | Bi-temporal design deep dive |
110: | [Skeleton Indexing](docs/engines/skeleton-indexing.md) | Cost optimization via PageRank |
111: | [Hybrid Search](docs/engines/hybrid-search.md) | Vector + BM25 fusion |
112: | **Architecture** | |
113: | [Overview](docs/architecture/overview.md) | System design, components, data flow |
114: | [Storage Backends](docs/architecture/storage-backends.md) | PostgreSQL, pgvector, Neo4j configuration |
115: | [Multi-Tenancy](docs/architecture/multi-tenancy.md) | Organization → Workspace → Namespace hierarchy |
116: | [Event Sourcing](docs/architecture/event-sourcing.md) | Immutable event log, audit trails |
117: | **Data Models** | |
118: | [Overview](docs/data-models/overview.md) | Model relationships and purposes |
119: | [Documents & Chunks](docs/data-models/documents-chunks.md) | Content storage and chunking |
120: | [Knowledge Graph](docs/data-models/knowledge-graph.md) | Entities, relationships, episodes |
121: | [Events](docs/data-models/events.md) | MemoryEvent types and usage |
122: | **Extraction Pipeline** | |
123: | [Overview](docs/extraction/overview.md) | Pipeline components and flow |
124: | [Ingestion Pipeline](docs/extraction/ingestion-pipeline.md) | Two-phase ingestion with Prefect |
125: | [Chunkers](docs/extraction/chunkers.md) | Fixed, semantic, recursive chunking |
126: | [Embedders](docs/extraction/embedders.md) | LiteLLM-based embedding generation |
127: | [Extractors](docs/extraction/extractors.md) | LLM entity and relationship extraction |
128: | [Expertise System](docs/extraction/expertise-system.md) | Domain-specific extraction configuration |
129: | [Semantic Expansion](docs/extraction/semantic-expansion.md) | Entity unification and relationship inference |
130: | **Query Engine** | |
131: | [Overview](docs/query-engine/overview.md) | HybridQueryEngine architecture |
132: | [Search Modes](docs/query-engine/search-modes.md) | Vector, graph, keyword, hybrid search |
133: | [Query Understanding](docs/query-engine/query-understanding.md) | LLM-based query analysis |
134: | [Fusion](docs/query-engine/fusion.md) | Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) |
135: | [Temporal Queries](docs/query-engine/temporal-queries.md) | Time filtering and recency bias |
136: | [Agentic Search](docs/query-engine/agentic-search.md) | Multi-step exploration |
137: | **Planning** | |
138: | [Roadmap](docs/roadmap.md) | Future improvements and features |
139: | **References** | |
140: | [References](docs/REFERENCES.md) | Research papers and inspirations |
141: 
142: ---
143: 
144: ## Installation
145: 
146: ### Prerequisites
147: 
148: - Python 3.13+
149: - [uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv) for package management
150: - PostgreSQL with pgvector extension
151: - Neo4j (optional, for graph features)
152: 
153: ### Quick Install
154: 
155: ```bash
156: # Clone and install
157: git clone https://github.com/DeytaHQ/khora.git
158: cd khora
159: uv sync --all-extras
160: 
161: # Install pre-commit hooks
162: uv run prek install
163: ```
164: 
165: ### Start Development Databases
166: 
167: ```bash
168: # Start PostgreSQL and Neo4j via Docker
169: make dev
170: 
171: # Run database migrations
172: uv run alembic upgrade head
173: ```
174: 
175: ---
176: 
177: ## Usage
178: 
179: ### As a Library
180: 
181: The primary interface is the `MemoryLake` class:
182: 
183: ```python
184: from khora import MemoryLake, SearchMode
185: 
186: async def main():
187:     # Simplest - reads KHORA_DATABASE_URL from environment
188:     async with MemoryLake() as lake:
189:         # Store a memory
190:         result = await lake.remember(
191:             "Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity in 1905.",
192:             title="Einstein Biography",
193:             source="wikipedia",
194:         )
195:         print(f"Stored document: {result.document_id}")
196:         print(f"Extracted {result.entities_extracted} entities")
197: 
198:         # Recall relevant memories
199:         memories = await lake.recall(
200:             "Who developed relativity?",
201:             limit=5,
202:             mode=SearchMode.HYBRID,  # vector + graph + keyword
203:         )
204:         print(f"Found {len(memories.chunks)} relevant chunks")
205:         print(f"Context: {memories.context_text}")
206: 
207:         # Explore entity relationships
208:         entities = await lake.list_entities(entity_type="PERSON")
209:         for entity in entities:
210:             related = await lake.find_related_entities(entity.id, max_depth=2)
211:             print(f"{entity.name} is related to {len(related)} entities")
212: 
213:         # Forget a memory
214:         await lake.forget(result.document_id)
215: 
216: import asyncio
217: asyncio.run(main())
218: ```
219: 
220: ### Simplified Constructor
221: 
222: The `MemoryLake` constructor supports multiple initialization patterns:
223: 
224: ```python
225: from khora import MemoryLake, KhoraConfig
226: 
227: # 1. From environment variables (KHORA_DATABASE_URL)
228: lake = MemoryLake()
229: 
230: # 2. Explicit database URL
231: lake = MemoryLake("postgresql://localhost/mydb")
232: 
233: # 3. With graph backend
234: lake = MemoryLake(
235:     "postgresql://localhost/mydb",
236:     graph_url="bolt://localhost:7687",
237: )
238: 
239: # 4. Custom embedding model
240: lake = MemoryLake(
241:     "postgresql://localhost/mydb",
242:     embedding_model="text-embedding-3-large",
243: )
244: 
245: # 5. Full configuration object (for advanced use)
246: config = KhoraConfig(
247:     database_url="postgresql://localhost/mydb",
248:     neo4j_url="bolt://localhost:7687",
249: )
250: lake = MemoryLake(config)
251: ```
252: 
253: ### Batch Ingestion
254: 
255: For efficient bulk document ingestion:
256: 
257: ```python
258: from khora import MemoryLake
259: 
260: async with MemoryLake(database_url) as lake:
261:     # Batch ingestion with automatic optimization
262:     result = await lake.remember_batch(
263:         [
264:             {"content": "Document 1 text...", "title": "Doc 1"},
265:             {"content": "Document 2 text...", "title": "Doc 2"},
266:             {"content": "Document 3 text...", "title": "Doc 3"},
267:         ],
268:         deduplicate=True,           # Cross-document entity deduplication
269:         infer_relationships=True,   # Relationship inference after ingestion
270:         on_progress=lambda done, total: print(f"Progress: {done}/{total}"),
271:     )
272: 
273:     print(f"Processed: {result.processed}/{result.total} documents")
274:     print(f"Chunks: {result.chunks}, Entities: {result.entities}")
275:     print(f"Relationships: {result.relationships}")
276: ```
277: 
278: ### Raw Search (No LLM Features)
279: 
280: For benchmarks or simple searches without LLM overhead:
281: 
282: ```python
283: # Skip query understanding, entity linking, reranking, HyDE
284: results = await lake.recall(
285:     "search query",
286:     mode=SearchMode.ALL,
287:     raw=True,  # Disables all LLM features
288: )
289: ```
290: 
291: ### Search Modes
292: 
293: ```python
294: from khora import MemoryLake, SearchMode
295: 
296: async with MemoryLake() as lake:
297:     # Vector-only search (semantic similarity)
298:     results = await lake.recall("quantum physics", mode=SearchMode.VECTOR)
299: 
300:     # Graph-only search (entity relationships)
301:     results = await lake.recall("Einstein collaborators", mode=SearchMode.GRAPH)
302: 
303:     # Hybrid search (combines all sources with RRF)
304:     results = await lake.recall("relativity theory", mode=SearchMode.HYBRID)
305: 
306:     # All sources (returns results from each separately)
307:     results = await lake.recall("physics discoveries", mode=SearchMode.ALL)
308: ```
309: 
310: ### Multi-Tenancy
311: 
312: ```python
313: from khora import MemoryLake
314: 
315: async with MemoryLake() as lake:
316:     # Simple: Get or create a namespace by name
317:     namespace_id = await lake.ensure_namespace("physics", description="Physics research")
318: 
319:     # Store memories in specific namespace
320:     await lake.remember(
321:         "Important research findings...",
322:         namespace=namespace_id,
323:     )
324: 
325:     # Query within namespace (isolated from other namespaces)
326:     results = await lake.recall("findings", namespace=namespace_id)
327: 
328:     # Get namespace statistics
329:     stats = await lake.stats(namespace=namespace_id)
330:     print(f"Documents: {stats.documents}, Entities: {stats.entities}")
331: ```
332: 
333: ### As a Service
334: 
335: ```bash
336: # Start the API server
337: uv run khora serve --reload
338: 
339: # Or with Docker
340: docker compose up
341: ```
342: 
343: #### API Endpoints
344: 
345: **Memory Operations:**
346: ```bash
347: # Store a memory
348: curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/memory/remember \
349:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
350:   -d '{
351:     "content": "Einstein developed relativity in 1905.",
352:     "title": "Physics History",
353:     "skill_name": "general_entities"
354:   }'
355: 
356: # Recall memories
357: curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/memory/recall \
358:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
359:   -d '{
360:     "query": "Who developed relativity?",
361:     "limit": 10,
362:     "mode": "hybrid"
363:   }'
364: 
365: # Get a document
366: curl http://localhost:8100/memory/documents/{document_id}
367: 
368: # List entities
369: curl "http://localhost:8100/memory/entities?entity_type=PERSON&limit=50"
370: 
371: # Get related entities
372: curl "http://localhost:8100/memory/entities/{entity_id}/related?max_depth=2"
373: 
374: # Forget a memory
375: curl -X DELETE http://localhost:8100/memory/forget \
376:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
377:   -d '{"document_id": "uuid-here"}'
378: ```
379: 
380: **Namespace Management:**
381: ```bash
382: # Create organization
383: curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/namespaces/organizations \
384:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
385:   -d '{"name": "Acme Corp", "slug": "acme"}'
386: 
387: # Create workspace
388: curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/namespaces/workspaces \
389:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
390:   -d '{"organization_id": "org-uuid", "name": "Research"}'
391: 
392: # Create namespace
393: curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/namespaces/ \
394:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
395:   -d '{"workspace_id": "ws-uuid", "name": "Physics"}'
396: ```
397: 
398: **Sync & Pipelines:**
399: ```bash
400: # Ingest documents
401: curl -X POST http://localhost:8100/sync/ingest \
402:   -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
403:   -d '{
404:     "namespace_id": "ns-uuid",
405:     "documents": [{"content": "Document text..."}],
406:     "skill_name": "general_entities"
407:   }'
408: 
409: # List available pipelines
410: curl http://localhost:8100/sync/pipelines
411: ```
412: 
413: **Health Checks:**
414: ```bash
415: curl http://localhost:8100/status        # Service status
416: curl http://localhost:8100/health        # Health check
417: curl http://localhost:8100/health/ready  # Readiness probe
418: curl http://localhost:8100/health/live   # Liveness probe
419: ```
420: 
421: ---
422: 
423: ## Architecture
424: 
425: ```
426: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
427: │                              MemoryLake API                                  │
428: │                         (Library + FastAPI Service)                          │
429: ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
430: │                                                                              │
431: │  ┌──────────────┐   ┌──────────────┐   ┌──────────────┐   ┌──────────────┐ │
432: │  │    Query     │   │  Pipelines   │   │     ACL      │   │   Config     │ │
433: │  │   Engine     │   │  (Prefect)   │   │   Enforcer   │   │   Resolver   │ │
434: │  └──────┬───────┘   └──────┬───────┘   └──────┬───────┘   └──────┬───────┘ │
435: │         │                  │                  │                  │          │
436: ├─────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────┴──────────┤
437: │                          Storage Coordinator                                 │
438: ├─────────┬───────────────────┬───────────────────┬───────────────────────────┤
439: │         │                   │                   │                            │
440: │  ┌──────┴──────┐     ┌──────┴──────┐     ┌──────┴──────┐     ┌────────────┐ │
441: │  │ PostgreSQL  │     │  pgvector   │     │   Neo4j    │     │  LiteLLM   │ │
442: │  │  (Events,   │     │ (Embeddings)│     │  (Graph)   │     │  (Models)  │ │
443: │  │ Documents)  │     │             │     │            │     │            │ │
444: │  └─────────────┘     └─────────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘ │
445: │                                                                              │
446: └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
447: ```
448: 
449: ### Core Components
450: 
451: | Component | Purpose |
452: |-----------|---------|
453: | `MemoryLake` | Primary API for remember/recall/forget operations |
454: | `StorageCoordinator` | Orchestrates all storage backends |
455: | `HybridQueryEngine` | Combines vector, graph, and keyword search |
456: | `PipelineManager` | Manages Prefect ingestion flows |
457: | `ACLEnforcer` | Cross-layer permission enforcement |
458: 
459: ### Storage Backends
460: 
461: | Backend | Technology | Purpose |
462: |---------|------------|---------|
463: | Relational | PostgreSQL | Documents, events, permissions, metadata |
464: | Vector | pgvector | Embeddings for semantic similarity search |
465: | Graph | Neo4j | Entity nodes and relationship edges |
466: | Event Store | PostgreSQL | Immutable event log for sourcing |
467: 
468: ### Data Flow
469: 
470: 1. **Ingestion** (Three-Phase Pipeline)
471:    - Phase 1: Stage documents, compute checksums, detect duplicates
472:    - Phase 2: Chunk text, then generate embeddings and extract entities concurrently
473:    - Phase 3 (optional): Cross-document entity unification and relationship inference
474: 
475: 2. **Query** (Hybrid Search)
476:    - Execute vector, graph, and keyword searches in parallel
477:    - Apply Reciprocal Rank Fusion to combine results
478:    - Filter by ACL and temporal context
479: 
480: 3. **Event Sourcing**
481:    - All changes recorded as immutable events
482:    - Enables temporal queries ("state as of date X")
483:    - Complete audit trail for compliance
484: 
485: ---
486: 
487: ## Configuration
488: 
489: ### Environment Variables
490: 
491: | Variable | Description | Default |
492: |----------|-------------|---------|
493: | `KHORA_DATABASE_URL` | PostgreSQL connection URL | Required |
494: | `KHORA_NEO4J_URL` | Neo4j connection URL | `bolt://localhost:7687` |
495: | `KHORA_NEO4J_USER` | Neo4j username | `neo4j` |
496: | `KHORA_NEO4J_PASSWORD` | Neo4j password | Required for Neo4j |
497: | `KHORA_DEBUG` | Enable debug mode | `false` |
498: | `KHORA_API_HOST` | API server host | `127.0.0.1` |
499: | `KHORA_API_PORT` | API server port | `8100` |
500: | `KHORA_AUTH_ENABLED` | Enable authentication | `true` |
501: | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI API key (for embeddings) | - |
502: | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic API key (for extraction) | - |
503: 
504: ### LiteLLM Configuration
505: 
506: Khora uses LiteLLM for unified model access. Configure in `examples/config/litellm/`:
507: 
508: ```yaml
509: # examples/config/litellm/openai.yaml
510: model: "gpt-4o-mini"
511: api_key_env: "OPENAI_API_KEY"
512: temperature: 0.7
513: max_tokens: 8192
514: embedding_model: "text-embedding-3-small"
515: ```
516: 
517: ```yaml
518: # examples/config/litellm/claude.yaml
519: model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
520: api_key_env: "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"
521: temperature: 0.7
522: max_tokens: 8192
523: 
524: # Router with fallbacks
525: model_list:
526:   - model_name: claude-sonnet-4
527:     litellm_params:
528:       model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
529:       api_key: os.environ/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
530:   - model_name: claude-sonnet-4
531:     litellm_params:
532:       model: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022
533:       api_key: os.environ/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
534: ```
535: 
536: ### Extraction Skills
537: 
538: Configure entity extraction in your code:
539: 
540: ```python
541: from khora.extraction.skills import ExtractionSkill
542: 
543: skill = ExtractionSkill(
544:     name="custom_entities",
545:     description="Extract domain-specific entities",
546:     entity_types=["COMPANY", "PRODUCT", "TECHNOLOGY"],
547:     relationship_types=["DEVELOPS", "COMPETES_WITH", "USES"],
548: )
549: 
550: await lake.remember(content, skill_name="custom_entities")
551: ```
552: 
553: ---
554: 
555: ## Project Structure
556: 
557: ```
558: khora/
559: ├── src/khora/
560: │   ├── __init__.py              # Package exports
561: │   ├── memory_lake.py           # Primary MemoryLake class
562: │   ├── api/                     # FastAPI application
563: │   │   ├── app.py               # App factory with lifespan
564: │   │   ├── deps.py              # Dependency injection
565: │   │   └── routes/              # API endpoints
566: │   │       ├── memory.py        # Remember/recall/forget
567: │   │       ├── namespaces.py    # Multi-tenancy management
568: │   │       ├── sync.py          # Ingestion pipelines
569: │   │       └── status.py        # Health checks
570: │   ├── acl/                     # Access control
571: │   │   ├── checker.py           # Permission checking
572: │   │   └── enforcer.py          # Cross-layer enforcement
573: │   ├── cli/                     # Command-line interface
574: │   ├── config/                  # Configuration
575: │   │   ├── schema.py            # Pydantic settings
576: │   │   ├── llm.py               # LiteLLM configuration
577: │   │   └── resolver.py          # Hierarchical config
578: │   ├── core/models/             # Domain models
579: │   │   ├── document.py          # Document, Chunk
580: │   │   ├── entity.py            # Entity, Relationship
581: │   │   ├── event.py             # MemoryEvent (sourcing)
582: │   │   └── tenancy.py           # Org, Workspace, Namespace
583: │   ├── db/                      # Database layer
584: │   │   ├── models.py            # SQLAlchemy ORM
585: │   │   └── session.py           # Async session management
586: │   ├── extraction/              # Content processing
587: │   │   ├── chunkers/            # Text chunking strategies
588: │   │   ├── embedders/           # Embedding generation
589: │   │   ├── extractors/          # Entity extraction
590: │   │   └── skills/              # Extraction configurations
591: │   ├── pipelines/               # Prefect workflows
592: │   │   ├── flows/               # Ingestion and sync flows
593: │   │   ├── tasks/               # Individual pipeline tasks
594: │   │   ├── manager.py           # Pipeline orchestration
595: │   │   └── registry.py          # Pipeline registration
596: │   ├── query/                   # Search engine
597: │   │   ├── engine.py            # HybridQueryEngine
598: │   │   ├── fusion.py            # Reciprocal Rank Fusion
599: │   │   └── temporal.py          # Time-based queries
600: │   └── storage/                 # Storage backends
601: │       ├── backends/            # PostgreSQL, pgvector, Neo4j
602: │       ├── coordinator.py       # Backend orchestration
603: │       ├── event_store.py       # Event sourcing
604: │       └── factory.py           # Storage initialization
605: ├── tests/                       # Test suite
606: ├── alembic/                     # Database migrations
607: ├── examples/config/             # Example configurations
608: ├── docker-compose.yml           # Development services
609: └── pyproject.toml               # Project configuration
610: ```
611: 
612: ---
613: 
614: ## Development
615: 
616: ### Commands
617: 
618: ```bash
619: # Start development server
620: uv run khora serve --reload --no-auth
621: 
622: # Run tests with coverage
623: make test
624: 
625: # Format code
626: make format
627: 
628: # Run linting
629: make lint
630: 
631: # Run all pre-commit hooks
632: make prek
633: 
634: # Start development databases
635: make dev
636: 
637: # Stop development databases
638: make down
639: ```
640: 
641: ### Database Migrations
642: 
643: ```bash
644: # Run all migrations
645: uv run alembic upgrade head
646: 
647: # Create a new migration
648: uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m "Add new table"
649: 
650: # Rollback one migration
651: uv run alembic downgrade -1
652: ```
653: 
654: ### Testing
655: 
656: ```bash
657: # Run all tests
658: make test
659: 
660: # Run specific test file
661: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_api.py -v
662: 
663: # Run with markers
664: uv run pytest -m unit        # Unit tests only
665: uv run pytest -m integration # Integration tests
666: uv run pytest -m e2e         # End-to-end tests
667: ```
668: 
669: ---
670: 
671: ## API Reference
672: 
673: ### MemoryLake Class
674: 
675: #### Constructor
676: 
677: ```python
678: class MemoryLake:
679:     def __init__(
680:         self,
681:         database_url: str | KhoraConfig | None = None,
682:         *,
683:         graph_url: str | None = None,
684:         embedding_model: str = "text-embedding-3-small",
685:     ):
686:         """Initialize the Memory Lake.
687: 
688:         Args:
689:             database_url: PostgreSQL URL, KhoraConfig, or None (reads from env)
690:             graph_url: Optional Neo4j URL (bolt://user:pass@host:port)
691:             embedding_model: Embedding model to use
692:         """
693: ```
694: 
695: #### Core Methods
696: 
697: ```python
698:     async def remember(
699:         self,
700:         content: str,
701:         *,
702:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
703:         title: str = "",
704:         source: str = "",
705:         metadata: dict = {},
706:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
707:     ) -> RememberResult:
708:         """Store content in the memory lake."""
709: 
710:     async def remember_batch(
711:         self,
712:         documents: list[dict],
713:         *,
714:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
715:         skill_name: str = "general_entities",
716:         max_concurrent: int = 5,
717:         deduplicate: bool = True,
718:         infer_relationships: bool = True,
719:         on_progress: Callable[[int, int], None] | None = None,
720:     ) -> BatchResult:
721:         """Store multiple documents with automatic optimization."""
722: 
723:     async def recall(
724:         self,
725:         query: str,
726:         *,
727:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
728:         limit: int = 10,
729:         mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.HYBRID,
730:         min_similarity: float = 0.0,
731:         agentic: bool = False,
732:         raw: bool = False,  # Skip all LLM features
733:     ) -> RecallResult:
734:         """Recall memories relevant to a query."""
735: 
736:     async def forget(
737:         self,
738:         document_id: UUID,
739:         *,
740:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
741:     ) -> bool:
742:         """Remove a memory from the lake."""
743: ```
744: 
745: #### Convenience Methods
746: 
747: ```python
748:     async def ensure_namespace(
749:         self,
750:         name: str,
751:         *,
752:         description: str = "",
753:     ) -> UUID:
754:         """Get or create a namespace by name."""
755: 
756:     async def get_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> Document | None:
757:         """Get a document by ID."""
758: 
759:     async def list_documents(
760:         self,
761:         *,
762:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
763:         limit: int = 100,
764:     ) -> list[Document]:
765:         """List documents in a namespace."""
766: 
767:     async def search_entities(
768:         self,
769:         query: str,
770:         *,
771:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
772:         limit: int = 10,
773:     ) -> list[Entity]:
774:         """Search entities by query text using embedding similarity."""
775: 
776:     async def stats(
777:         self,
778:         *,
779:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
780:     ) -> Stats:
781:         """Get document/chunk/entity/relationship counts."""
782: 
783:     async def list_entities(
784:         self,
785:         *,
786:         namespace: str | UUID | None = None,
787:         entity_type: str | None = None,
788:         limit: int = 100,
789:     ) -> list[Entity]:
790:         """List entities in a namespace."""
791: 
792:     async def find_related_entities(
793:         self,
794:         entity_id: UUID,
795:         *,
796:         max_depth: int = 2,
797:         limit: int = 20,
798:     ) -> list[tuple[Entity, float]]:
799:         """Find entities related to a given entity."""
800: ```
801: 
802: ### Data Classes
803: 
804: ```python
805: @dataclass
806: class RememberResult:
807:     """Result of a remember() operation."""
808:     document_id: UUID
809:     namespace_id: UUID
810:     chunks_created: int
811:     entities_extracted: int
812:     relationships_created: int
813:     metadata: dict[str, Any]
814: 
815: @dataclass
816: class RecallResult:
817:     """Result of a recall() operation."""
818:     query: str
819:     namespace_id: UUID
820:     chunks: list[tuple[Chunk, float]]
821:     entities: list[tuple[Entity, float]]
822:     context_text: str
823:     metadata: dict[str, Any]
824: 
825: @dataclass
826: class BatchResult:
827:     """Result of remember_batch() operation."""
828:     total: int        # Total documents submitted
829:     processed: int    # Successfully processed
830:     skipped: int      # Skipped (duplicates)
831:     failed: int       # Failed to process
832:     chunks: int       # Total chunks created
833:     entities: int     # Total entities extracted
834:     relationships: int # Total relationships created
835: 
836: @dataclass
837: class Stats:
838:     """Namespace statistics from stats()."""
839:     documents: int
840:     chunks: int
841:     entities: int
842:     relationships: int
843: ```
844: 
845: ### Search Modes
846: 
847: | Mode | Description |
848: |------|-------------|
849: | `VECTOR` | Semantic similarity search using embeddings |
850: | `GRAPH` | Entity and relationship traversal |
851: | `HYBRID` | Combined vector + graph + keyword with RRF fusion |
852: | `ALL` | All sources (vector, graph, keyword) |
853: 
854: ### Entity Types
855: 
856: | Type | Description |
857: |------|-------------|
858: | `PERSON` | Individual people |
859: | `ORGANIZATION` | Companies, institutions |
860: | `LOCATION` | Places, addresses |
861: | `CONCEPT` | Abstract ideas, theories |
862: | `EVENT` | Occurrences, incidents |
863: | `TECHNOLOGY` | Tools, platforms, languages |
864: | `PRODUCT` | Goods, services |
865: | `DOCUMENT` | Referenced documents |
866: | `OTHER` | Uncategorized entities |
867: 
868: ### Deprecation Notices
869: 
870: The following properties emit `DeprecationWarning` and will be removed in a future version:
871: 
872: | Deprecated | Replacement |
873: |------------|-------------|
874: | `lake.storage` | Use `lake.get_document()`, `lake.list_documents()`, `lake.stats()` |
875: | `lake.query_engine` | Use `lake.recall()` with `raw=True` for unprocessed search |
876:

````

## File: src/khora/pipelines/tasks/extract.py
````python
  1: """Entity extraction task."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
  6: 
  7: if TYPE_CHECKING:
  8:     from khora.core.models import Chunk, Entity, Relationship
  9:     from khora.extraction.skills import ExpertiseConfig
 10: 
 11: 
 12: async def extract_entities(
 13:     chunks: list[Chunk],
 14:     *,
 15:     skill_name: str = "general_entities",
 16:     expertise: ExpertiseConfig | str | None = None,
 17:     model: str = "gpt-4o-mini",
 18:     max_concurrent: int = 10,
 19:     context: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
 20:     timeout: int = 120,
 21:     max_retries: int = 3,
 22:     retry_wait: float = 2.0,
 23: ) -> tuple[list[Entity], list[Relationship]]:
 24:     """Extract entities and relationships from chunks.
 25: 
 26:     Uses batch extraction for parallel processing of multiple chunks.
 27:     Supports both legacy skills and new expertise configurations.
 28: 
 29:     Args:
 30:         chunks: Chunks to extract from
 31:         skill_name: Extraction skill to use (legacy, ignored if expertise provided)
 32:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig, expertise name string, or file path
 33:         model: LLM model for extraction
 34:         max_concurrent: Maximum concurrent extractions
 35:         context: Optional context dict for prompt template rendering
 36:         timeout: Request timeout in seconds
 37:         max_retries: Maximum retries on failure
 38:         retry_wait: Base wait time for exponential backoff between retries
 39: 
 40:     Returns:
 41:         Tuple of (entities, relationships)
 42:     """
 43:     from khora.core.models import Entity, Relationship
 44:     from khora.core.models.entity import EntityType, RelationshipType
 45:     from khora.extraction.extractors import LLMEntityExtractor
 46:     from khora.extraction.skills import ExpertiseConfig
 47:     from khora.extraction.skills.registry import get_default_registry
 48: 
 49:     if not chunks:
 50:         return [], []
 51: 
 52:     # Resolve expertise configuration
 53:     resolved_expertise: ExpertiseConfig | None = None
 54:     if expertise is not None:
 55:         if isinstance(expertise, ExpertiseConfig):
 56:             resolved_expertise = expertise
 57:         elif isinstance(expertise, str):
 58:             # Load from string (file path or builtin name)
 59:             from khora.extraction.skills import load_expertise
 60: 
 61:             try:
 62:                 resolved_expertise = load_expertise(expertise)
 63:             except Exception:
 64:                 # Fall back to registry lookup
 65:                 registry = get_default_registry()
 66:                 resolved_expertise = registry.get_expertise(expertise)
 67: 
 68:     # Get legacy skill for backward compatibility
 69:     registry = get_default_registry()
 70:     skill = registry.get_or_default(skill_name)
 71: 
 72:     # If expertise provided, use its confidence thresholds
 73:     if resolved_expertise:
 74:         min_entity_confidence = resolved_expertise.confidence.min_entity
 75:         min_relationship_confidence = resolved_expertise.confidence.min_relationship
 76:     else:
 77:         min_entity_confidence = skill.min_entity_confidence
 78:         min_relationship_confidence = skill.min_relationship_confidence
 79: 
 80:     # Create extractor with concurrency limit and timeout settings
 81:     extractor = LLMEntityExtractor(
 82:         model=model,
 83:         max_concurrent=max_concurrent,
 84:         timeout=timeout,
 85:         max_retries=max_retries,
 86:         retry_wait=retry_wait,
 87:     )
 88: 
 89:     # Extract from all chunks using adaptive token-budget-based batching
 90:     # Groups chunks into batches that fit within the model's input token budget,
 91:     # reducing API round-trips by up to 5x while avoiding context overflow
 92:     texts = [chunk.content for chunk in chunks]
 93: 
 94:     # Use adaptive batching based on token budget (auto-calculated from max_tokens)
 95:     # batch_size=5 is the max texts per batch; actual batching respects token limits
 96:     if resolved_expertise:
 97:         results = await extractor.extract_multi(
 98:             texts,
 99:             expertise=resolved_expertise,
100:             context=context,
101:             batch_size=5,
102:             max_input_tokens=None,  # Auto-calculate from model
103:         )
104:     else:
105:         results = await extractor.extract_multi(
106:             texts,
107:             entity_types=skill.entity_types,
108:             batch_size=5,
109:             max_input_tokens=None,  # Auto-calculate from model
110:         )
111: 
112:     # Process results
113:     all_entities: dict[str, Entity] = {}  # name -> entity (for dedup)
114:     all_relationships: list[Relationship] = []
115: 
116:     for chunk, result in zip(chunks, results):
117:         # Process entities
118:         for extracted in result.entities:
119:             if extracted.confidence < min_entity_confidence:
120:                 continue
121: 
122:             # Deduplicate by name
123:             key = f"{extracted.name}:{extracted.entity_type}"
124:             if key in all_entities:
125:                 # Merge into existing
126:                 existing = all_entities[key]
127:                 existing.mention_count += 1
128:                 if chunk.document_id not in existing.source_document_ids:
129:                     existing.source_document_ids.append(chunk.document_id)
130:                 if chunk.id not in existing.source_chunk_ids:
131:                     existing.source_chunk_ids.append(chunk.id)
132:                 # Update valid_from to earliest timestamp
133:                 if existing.valid_from and chunk.created_at < existing.valid_from:
134:                     existing.valid_from = chunk.created_at
135:             else:
136:                 # Create new entity — preserve original type string from LLM
137:                 try:
138:                     entity_type: EntityType | str = EntityType(extracted.entity_type)
139:                 except ValueError:
140:                     entity_type = extracted.entity_type or "CONCEPT"
141: 
142:                 entity = Entity(
143:                     namespace_id=chunk.namespace_id,
144:                     name=extracted.name,
145:                     entity_type=entity_type,
146:                     description=extracted.description,
147:                     attributes=extracted.attributes,
148:                     source_document_ids=[chunk.document_id],
149:                     source_chunk_ids=[chunk.id],
150:                     confidence=extracted.confidence,
151:                     valid_from=chunk.created_at,  # Inherit source timestamp
152:                 )
153:                 all_entities[key] = entity
154: 
155:         # Process relationships
156:         for extracted_rel in result.relationships:
157:             if extracted_rel.confidence < min_relationship_confidence:
158:                 continue
159: 
160:             # Preserve original type string from LLM
161:             try:
162:                 rel_type: RelationshipType | str = RelationshipType(extracted_rel.relationship_type)
163:             except ValueError:
164:                 rel_type = extracted_rel.relationship_type or "RELATES_TO"
165: 
166:             # Find source and target entities
167:             source_key = next(
168:                 (k for k in all_entities if k.startswith(f"{extracted_rel.source_entity}:")),
169:                 None,
170:             )
171:             target_key = next(
172:                 (k for k in all_entities if k.startswith(f"{extracted_rel.target_entity}:")),
173:                 None,
174:             )
175: 
176:             if source_key and target_key:
177:                 relationship = Relationship(
178:                     namespace_id=chunk.namespace_id,
179:                     source_entity_id=all_entities[source_key].id,
180:                     target_entity_id=all_entities[target_key].id,
181:                     relationship_type=rel_type,
182:                     description=extracted_rel.description,
183:                     properties=extracted_rel.properties,
184:                     source_document_ids=[chunk.document_id],
185:                     source_chunk_ids=[chunk.id],
186:                     confidence=extracted_rel.confidence,
187:                     valid_from=chunk.created_at,  # Inherit source timestamp
188:                 )
189:                 all_relationships.append(relationship)
190: 
191:     return list(all_entities.values()), all_relationships
````

## File: src/khora/telemetry/collector.py
````python
  1: """TelemetryCollector -- async buffered writer for telemetry events."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: import asyncio
  6: from collections import deque
  7: from typing import Any
  8: 
  9: from loguru import logger
 10: from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncEngine
 11: 
 12: from .models import LLMEvent, PipelineEvent, StorageEvent
 13: from .tables import SCHEMA_VERSION, llm_events, metadata, pipeline_events, storage_events
 14: 
 15: 
 16: class TelemetryCollector:
 17:     """Buffers telemetry events and periodically flushes them to PostgreSQL.
 18: 
 19:     All ``record_*`` methods are **synchronous** -- they append to an in-memory
 20:     deque so callers never block on I/O.  A background ``asyncio.Task`` drains
 21:     the buffer every *flush_interval* seconds or when the buffer exceeds
 22:     *flush_threshold* events.
 23:     """
 24: 
 25:     def __init__(
 26:         self,
 27:         engine: AsyncEngine,
 28:         service_name: str = "khora",
 29:         flush_interval: float = 5.0,
 30:         flush_threshold: int = 100,
 31:     ) -> None:
 32:         self._engine = engine
 33:         self._service_name = service_name
 34:         self._flush_interval = flush_interval
 35:         self._flush_threshold = flush_threshold
 36:         self._buffer: deque[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]] = deque()
 37:         self._flush_task: asyncio.Task[None] | None = None
 38: 
 39:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 40:     # Lifecycle
 41:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
 42: 
 43:     async def start(self) -> None:
 44:         """Create tables (if missing) and start the background flush loop.
 45: 
 46:         On startup, checks if the schema version has changed.  If so,
 47:         drops the old tables and recreates them with the new schema.
 48:         """
 49:         try:
 50:             await self._migrate_schema()
 51:             logger.info("Telemetry tables ensured")
 52:         except Exception as exc:
 53:             logger.warning(f"Telemetry table creation failed (non-fatal): {exc}")
 54: 
 55:         self._flush_task = asyncio.create_task(self._flush_loop(), name="telemetry-flush")
 56: 
 57:     async def _migrate_schema(self) -> None:
 58:         """Detect schema version and recreate tables if needed.
 59: 
 60:         Uses separate connections for probing and DDL to avoid aborted
 61:         transaction issues with asyncpg.
 62:         """
 63:         import sqlalchemy as sa
 64: 
 65:         # Step 1: probe whether the current schema is up-to-date
 66:         needs_recreate = False
 67:         async with self._engine.connect() as conn:
 68:             try:
 69:                 result = await conn.execute(sa.text("SELECT trace_id FROM llm_events LIMIT 0"))
 70:                 result.close()
 71:             except Exception:
 72:                 needs_recreate = True
 73:             finally:
 74:                 await conn.rollback()
 75: 
 76:         # Step 2: drop old tables if they exist but are outdated
 77:         if needs_recreate:
 78:             try:
 79:                 async with self._engine.begin() as conn:
 80:                     await conn.execute(sa.text("SELECT 1 FROM llm_events LIMIT 0"))
 81:                     # Old table exists without trace_id — drop all
 82:                     logger.info(f"Telemetry schema v{SCHEMA_VERSION}: dropping old tables for migration")
 83:                     await conn.run_sync(metadata.drop_all)
 84:             except Exception:
 85:                 # Tables don't exist at all — fine, create_all will handle it
 86:                 pass
 87: 
 88:         # Step 3: create tables (no-op if already up-to-date)
 89:         async with self._engine.begin() as conn:
 90:             await conn.run_sync(metadata.create_all)
 91: 
 92:     async def shutdown(self) -> None:
 93:         """Cancel the flush loop, do a final flush, and dispose the engine."""
 94:         if self._flush_task and not self._flush_task.done():
 95:             self._flush_task.cancel()
 96:             try:
 97:                 await self._flush_task
 98:             except asyncio.CancelledError:
 99:                 pass
100: 
101:         # Final drain
102:         await self._flush()
103: 
104:         await self._engine.dispose()
105:         logger.info("Telemetry collector shut down")
106: 
107:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
108:     # Record helpers (sync -- safe to call from anywhere)
109:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
110: 
111:     def _inject_trace_context(self, kwargs: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
112:         """Auto-populate trace_id and parent_event_id from context vars."""
113:         from .context import get_parent_event_id, get_trace_id
114: 
115:         if "trace_id" not in kwargs or kwargs["trace_id"] is None:
116:             kwargs["trace_id"] = get_trace_id()
117:         if "parent_event_id" not in kwargs or kwargs["parent_event_id"] is None:
118:             kwargs["parent_event_id"] = get_parent_event_id()
119:         return kwargs
120: 
121:     def record_llm_call(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
122:         kwargs = self._inject_trace_context(kwargs)
123:         event = LLMEvent(service_name=self._service_name, **kwargs)
124:         self._buffer.append(("llm", event.model_dump()))
125: 
126:     def record_storage_op(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
127:         kwargs = self._inject_trace_context(kwargs)
128:         event = StorageEvent(service_name=self._service_name, **kwargs)
129:         self._buffer.append(("storage", event.model_dump()))
130: 
131:     def record_pipeline_stage(self, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
132:         kwargs = self._inject_trace_context(kwargs)
133:         event = PipelineEvent(service_name=self._service_name, **kwargs)
134:         self._buffer.append(("pipeline", event.model_dump()))
135: 
136:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
137:     # Internal flush machinery
138:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------
139: 
140:     async def _flush_loop(self) -> None:
141:         """Background loop that flushes periodically or when threshold hit."""
142:         try:
143:             while True:
144:                 await asyncio.sleep(self._flush_interval)
145:                 if self._buffer:
146:                     await self._flush()
147:         except asyncio.CancelledError:
148:             return
149: 
150:     async def _flush(self) -> None:
151:         """Batch-insert all buffered events.  Errors are logged, never raised."""
152:         if not self._buffer:
153:             return
154: 
155:         # Drain the buffer into local lists
156:         llm_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
157:         storage_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
158:         pipeline_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
159: 
160:         while self._buffer:
161:             kind, data = self._buffer.popleft()
162:             row = dict(data)
163:             meta_value = row.pop("metadata", None)
164:             row["metadata"] = meta_value
165:             if kind == "llm":
166:                 llm_rows.append(row)
167:             elif kind == "storage":
168:                 storage_rows.append(row)
169:             elif kind == "pipeline":
170:                 pipeline_rows.append(row)
171: 
172:         total = len(llm_rows) + len(storage_rows) + len(pipeline_rows)
173:         try:
174:             async with self._engine.begin() as conn:
175:                 if llm_rows:
176:                     await conn.execute(llm_events.insert(), llm_rows)
177:                 if storage_rows:
178:                     await conn.execute(storage_events.insert(), storage_rows)
179:                 if pipeline_rows:
180:                     await conn.execute(pipeline_events.insert(), pipeline_rows)
181:             logger.debug(f"Telemetry flushed {total} events")
182:         except Exception as exc:
183:             # Truncate error to avoid dumping huge SQL with embedding vectors
184:             err_str = str(exc)
185:             if len(err_str) > 300:
186:                 err_str = err_str[:300] + "..."
187:             logger.warning(f"Telemetry flush failed ({total} events dropped): {err_str}")
````

## File: src/khora/config/schema.py
````python
  1: """Pydantic configuration models for Khora."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from dataclasses import dataclass
  6: from pathlib import Path
  7: from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
  8: from urllib.parse import urlparse
  9: 
 10: import yaml
 11: from pydantic import BaseModel, Discriminator, Field, Tag, model_validator
 12: from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
 13: 
 14: 
 15: @dataclass
 16: class ParsedNeo4jUrl:
 17:     """Parsed Neo4j URL components."""
 18: 
 19:     url: str  # URL without credentials (bolt://host:port)
 20:     user: str
 21:     password: str
 22:     database: str
 23: 
 24:     @classmethod
 25:     def parse(cls, url: str, default_user: str = "neo4j", default_database: str = "neo4j") -> ParsedNeo4jUrl:
 26:         """Parse a Neo4j URL with optional embedded credentials.
 27: 
 28:         Supports formats:
 29:         - bolt://host:port
 30:         - bolt://user:password@host:port
 31:         - bolt://user:password@host:port/database
 32:         """
 33:         parsed = urlparse(url)
 34: 
 35:         # Extract user and password from URL
 36:         user = parsed.username or default_user
 37:         password = parsed.password or ""
 38: 
 39:         # Extract database from path (e.g., /mydb -> mydb)
 40:         database = parsed.path.lstrip("/") if parsed.path and parsed.path != "/" else default_database
 41: 
 42:         # Reconstruct URL without credentials
 43:         host_port = parsed.hostname or "localhost"
 44:         if parsed.port:
 45:             host_port = f"{host_port}:{parsed.port}"
 46:         clean_url = f"{parsed.scheme}://{host_port}"
 47: 
 48:         return cls(url=clean_url, user=user, password=password, database=database)
 49: 
 50: 
 51: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 52: # Graph backend configs (discriminated union on "backend" field)
 53: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 54: 
 55: 
 56: class Neo4jConfig(BaseModel):
 57:     """Neo4j graph backend configuration."""
 58: 
 59:     backend: Literal["neo4j"] = "neo4j"
 60:     url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="Neo4j connection URL (bolt:// or neo4j://)")
 61:     user: str = Field(default="neo4j", description="Neo4j username")
 62:     password: str = Field(default="", description="Neo4j password")
 63:     database: str = Field(default="neo4j", description="Neo4j database name")
 64: 
 65: 
 66: class KuzuConfig(BaseModel):
 67:     """Kùzu embedded graph backend configuration."""
 68: 
 69:     backend: Literal["kuzu"] = "kuzu"
 70:     database_path: str = Field(default="./kuzu_db", description="Path to Kùzu database directory")
 71:     read_only: bool = Field(default=False, description="Open database in read-only mode")
 72: 
 73: 
 74: class MemgraphConfig(BaseModel):
 75:     """Memgraph graph backend configuration."""
 76: 
 77:     backend: Literal["memgraph"] = "memgraph"
 78:     url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="Memgraph connection URL (bolt://)")
 79:     user: str = Field(default="memgraph", description="Memgraph username")
 80:     password: str = Field(default="", description="Memgraph password")
 81: 
 82: 
 83: class ArcadeDBGraphConfig(BaseModel):
 84:     """ArcadeDB graph backend configuration."""
 85: 
 86:     backend: Literal["arcadedb"] = "arcadedb"
 87:     url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="ArcadeDB HTTP URL")
 88:     database: str = Field(default="khora", description="ArcadeDB database name")
 89:     user: str = Field(default="root", description="ArcadeDB username")
 90:     password: str = Field(default="", description="ArcadeDB password")
 91:     query_language: str = Field(default="cypher", description="Query language: cypher or gremlin")
 92: 
 93: 
 94: def _graph_discriminator(v: Any) -> str:
 95:     if isinstance(v, dict):
 96:         return v.get("backend", "neo4j")
 97:     return getattr(v, "backend", "neo4j")
 98: 
 99: 
100: GraphConfig = Annotated[
101:     Annotated[Neo4jConfig, Tag("neo4j")]
102:     | Annotated[KuzuConfig, Tag("kuzu")]
103:     | Annotated[MemgraphConfig, Tag("memgraph")]
104:     | Annotated[ArcadeDBGraphConfig, Tag("arcadedb")],
105:     Discriminator(_graph_discriminator),
106: ]
107: 
108:
