````python
109: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110: # Vector backend configs (discriminated union on "backend" field)
111: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
112: 
113: 
114: class PgVectorConfig(BaseModel):
115:     """pgvector vector backend configuration."""
116: 
117:     backend: Literal["pgvector"] = "pgvector"
118:     url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="pgvector connection URL")
119:     embedding_dimension: int = Field(default=1536, description="Embedding vector dimension")
120: 
121: 
122: class ArcadeDBVectorConfig(BaseModel):
123:     """ArcadeDB vector backend configuration."""
124: 
125:     backend: Literal["arcadedb"] = "arcadedb"
126:     url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="ArcadeDB HTTP URL")
127:     database: str = Field(default="khora", description="ArcadeDB database name")
128:     user: str = Field(default="root", description="ArcadeDB username")
129:     password: str = Field(default="", description="ArcadeDB password")
130:     embedding_dimension: int = Field(default=1536, description="Embedding vector dimension")
131: 
132: 
133: def _vector_discriminator(v: Any) -> str:
134:     if isinstance(v, dict):
135:         return v.get("backend", "pgvector")
136:     return getattr(v, "backend", "pgvector")
137: 
138: 
139: VectorConfig = Annotated[
140:     Annotated[PgVectorConfig, Tag("pgvector")] | Annotated[ArcadeDBVectorConfig, Tag("arcadedb")],
141:     Discriminator(_vector_discriminator),
142: ]
143: 
144: 
145: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146: # Storage settings
147: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
148: 
149: 
150: class StorageSettings(BaseModel):
151:     """Storage backend configuration.
152: 
153:     Supports both the new discriminated-union graph/vector configs and
154:     the legacy flat fields (neo4j_url, pgvector_url, etc.) for backwards
155:     compatibility.
156:     """
157: 
158:     # PostgreSQL (relational)
159:     postgresql_url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="PostgreSQL connection URL")
160: 
161:     # New-style backend configs
162:     graph: GraphConfig | None = Field(default=None, description="Graph backend configuration (optional)")
163:     vector: VectorConfig = Field(default_factory=PgVectorConfig, description="Vector backend configuration")
164: 
165:     # Legacy flat fields — kept for backwards compatibility
166:     pgvector_url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="[deprecated] pgvector connection URL")
167:     embedding_dimension: int = Field(default=1536, description="[deprecated] Embedding vector dimension")
168:     neo4j_url: str | None = Field(default=None, description="[deprecated] Neo4j connection URL")
169:     neo4j_user: str = Field(default="neo4j", description="[deprecated] Neo4j username")
170:     neo4j_password: str = Field(default="", description="[deprecated] Neo4j password")
171:     neo4j_database: str = Field(default="neo4j", description="[deprecated] Neo4j database name")
172: 
173:     @model_validator(mode="before")
174:     @classmethod
175:     def _migrate_legacy_fields(cls, data: Any) -> Any:
176:         """Migrate legacy flat fields into the new graph/vector config objects."""
177:         if not isinstance(data, dict):
178:             return data
179: 
180:         # Only migrate if the new-style configs are not explicitly provided
181:         if "graph" not in data:
182:             neo4j_url = data.get("neo4j_url")
183:             neo4j_user = data.get("neo4j_user", "neo4j")
184:             neo4j_password = data.get("neo4j_password", "")
185:             neo4j_database = data.get("neo4j_database", "neo4j")
186:             if neo4j_url:
187:                 data["graph"] = {
188:                     "backend": "neo4j",
189:                     "url": neo4j_url,
190:                     "user": neo4j_user,
191:                     "password": neo4j_password,
192:                     "database": neo4j_database,
193:                 }
194: 
195:         if "vector" not in data:
196:             pgvector_url = data.get("pgvector_url")
197:             embedding_dim = data.get("embedding_dimension", 1536)
198:             if pgvector_url:
199:                 data["vector"] = {
200:                     "backend": "pgvector",
201:                     "url": pgvector_url,
202:                     "embedding_dimension": embedding_dim,
203:                 }
204: 
205:         return data
206: 
207: 
208: class LLMSettings(BaseModel):
209:     """LLM configuration settings."""
210: 
211:     model: str = Field(default="gpt-4o-mini", description="Primary LLM model")
212:     api_key_env: str = Field(default="OPENAI_API_KEY", description="Environment variable for API key")
213:     temperature: float = Field(default=0.7, description="Sampling temperature")
214:     max_tokens: int = Field(default=2000, description="Maximum tokens to generate")
215:     timeout: int = Field(default=30, description="Request timeout in seconds")
216:     max_retries: int = Field(default=3, description="Maximum retries on failure")
217:     max_concurrent_llm_calls: int = Field(default=10, description="Maximum concurrent LLM calls")
218: 
219:     # Embedding settings
220:     embedding_model: str = Field(default="text-embedding-3-small", description="Embedding model")
221:     embedding_dimension: int = Field(default=1536, description="Embedding dimension")
222: 
223:     # Extraction model (defaults to primary model if not set)
224:     extraction_model: str | None = Field(
225:         default=None,
226:         description="Model for entity extraction (defaults to primary model). "
227:         "Smaller/faster models like claude-3-5-haiku or gemini-2.0-flash work well for extraction.",
228:     )
229: 
230:     # Router configuration
231:     config_file: str | None = Field(default=None, description="Path to LiteLLM config YAML")
232:     model_list: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = Field(default=None, description="Model list for router")
233:     router_settings: dict[str, Any] | None = Field(default=None, description="Router settings")
234: 
235: 
236: class PipelineSettings(BaseModel):
237:     """Pipeline configuration settings."""
238: 
239:     # Chunking settings
240:     chunking_strategy: str = Field(default="semantic", description="Chunking strategy: fixed, semantic, recursive")
241:     chunk_size: int = Field(default=512, description="Target chunk size in tokens")
242:     chunk_overlap: int = Field(default=50, description="Overlap between chunks in tokens")
243: 
244:     # Conversation chunking settings
245:     conversation_time_gap_minutes: int = Field(default=15, description="Time gap (minutes) to split conversations")
246:     conversation_max_group_size: int = Field(default=50, description="Max messages per conversation chunk")
247:     conversation_min_group_size: int = Field(default=2, description="Min messages per chunk (merges below this)")
248:     conversation_semantic_threshold: float | None = Field(
249:         default=None, description="Optional cosine similarity threshold for semantic splitting"
250:     )
251: 
252:     # Extraction settings
253:     extract_entities: bool = Field(default=True, description="Extract entities from documents")
254:     entity_types: list[str] = Field(
255:         default=["PERSON", "ORGANIZATION", "CONCEPT", "LOCATION"],
256:         description="Entity types to extract",
257:     )
258: 
259: 
260: class TenancySettings(BaseModel):
261:     """Multi-tenancy configuration settings."""
262: 
263:     default_mode: str = Field(default="shared", description="Default tenancy mode: shared or isolated")
264:     enforce_namespace: bool = Field(default=True, description="Enforce namespace isolation")
265: 
266: 
267: class QuerySettings(BaseModel):
268:     """Query pipeline configuration.
269: 
270:     All fields are flat to allow single-underscore env vars, e.g.:
271:     KHORA_QUERY__DEFAULT_MODE, KHORA_QUERY__ENABLE_RERANKING, etc.
272:     """
273: 
274:     # Basic search settings
275:     default_mode: str = Field(default="hybrid", description="Default search mode: vector, graph, hybrid, all")
276:     min_chunk_similarity: float = Field(default=0.05, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Minimum chunk similarity threshold")
277:     min_entity_similarity: float = Field(
278:         default=0.05, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Minimum entity similarity threshold"
279:     )
280: 
281:     # Fusion weights
282:     vector_weight: float = Field(default=0.5, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Weight for vector search in fusion")
283:     graph_weight: float = Field(default=0.3, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Weight for graph search in fusion")
284:     keyword_weight: float = Field(default=0.2, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Weight for keyword search in fusion")
285: 
286:     # Temporal settings
287:     apply_recency_bias: bool = Field(default=False, description="Apply recency bias to results")
288:     recency_weight: float = Field(default=0.2, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Weight of recency in scoring")
289:     recency_decay_days: float = Field(default=30.0, ge=1.0, description="Days for recency score to decay by half")
290: 
291:     # Query understanding
292:     enable_understanding: bool = Field(default=True, description="Enable LLM-based query understanding")
293:     understanding_expand_query: bool = Field(default=True, description="Generate query expansions/reformulations")
294:     understanding_extract_entities: bool = Field(default=True, description="Extract entity mentions from query")
295:     understanding_detect_temporal: bool = Field(default=True, description="Detect temporal references in query")
296:     understanding_model: str | None = Field(
297:         default=None, description="Model to use for query understanding (defaults to main LLM)"
298:     )
299: 
300:     # Entity linking
301:     enable_entity_linking: bool = Field(default=True, description="Enable entity linking")
302:     entity_linking_exact_match: bool = Field(default=True, description="Use exact name matching")
303:     entity_linking_fuzzy_match: bool = Field(default=True, description="Use fuzzy name matching")
304:     entity_linking_embedding_match: bool = Field(default=True, description="Use embedding similarity matching")
305:     entity_linking_fuzzy_threshold: float = Field(default=0.6, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Minimum fuzzy match ratio")
306:     entity_linking_embedding_threshold: float = Field(
307:         default=0.4, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Minimum embedding similarity"
308:     )
309:     entity_linking_max_candidates: int = Field(default=5, ge=1, description="Maximum entity candidates per mention")
310: 
311:     # Reranking
312:     enable_reranking: bool = Field(default=True, description="Enable result reranking")
313:     reranking_method: str = Field(default="cross_encoder", description="Reranking method: cross_encoder, llm")
314:     reranking_model: str | None = Field(default=None, description="Model for reranking (cross-encoder model or LLM)")
315:     reranking_top_n: int = Field(default=50, ge=1, description="Number of candidates to rerank")
316:     reranking_final_k: int = Field(default=10, ge=1, description="Number of results after reranking")
317: 
318:     # Keyword search
319:     enable_keyword_search: bool = Field(default=True, description="Enable keyword search")
320:     keyword_search_method: str = Field(default="fulltext", description="Keyword search method: bm25, fulltext")
321:     keyword_search_use_stemming: bool = Field(default=True, description="Apply stemming to search terms")
322:     keyword_search_use_stopwords: bool = Field(default=True, description="Remove stopwords from search")
323:     keyword_search_language: str = Field(default="english", description="Language for stemming and stopwords")
324: 
325:     # HyDE
326:     enable_hyde: bool = Field(default=False, description="Enable HyDE query expansion")
327:     hyde_num_hypotheticals: int = Field(
328:         default=1, ge=1, le=5, description="Number of hypothetical documents to generate"
329:     )
330: 
331:     # Multi-stage ranking pipeline
332:     enable_multi_stage: bool = Field(
333:         default=True, description="Enable multi-stage ranking pipeline for improved quality"
334:     )
335:     stage1_recall_limit: int = Field(
336:         default=200, ge=50, le=500, description="Number of candidates to retrieve in Stage 1 (broad recall)"
337:     )
338:     stage3_filter_limit: int = Field(
339:         default=50, ge=20, le=200, description="Number of candidates after Stage 3 filtering"
340:     )
341:     stage4_rerank_limit: int = Field(
342:         default=50, ge=10, le=100, description="Number of candidates to send to neural reranking in Stage 4"
343:     )
344:     enable_diversity: bool = Field(default=False, description="Enable MMR-style diversity selection in Stage 5")
345:     diversity_lambda: float = Field(
346:         default=0.5, ge=0.0, le=1.0, description="Diversity vs relevance tradeoff (0=pure diversity, 1=pure relevance)"
347:     )
348: 
349: 
350: class KhoraConfig(BaseSettings):
351:     """Main application configuration."""
352: 
353:     model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
354:         env_prefix="KHORA_",
355:         env_nested_delimiter="__",
356:         case_sensitive=False,
357:     )
358: 
359:     # Application settings
360:     app_name: str = Field(
361:         default="khora",
362:         description="Application name",
363:     )
364:     environment: str = Field(
365:         default="development",
366:         description="Environment: development, staging, or production",
367:     )
368:     debug: bool = Field(
369:         default=False,
370:         description="Enable debug mode",
371:     )
372: 
373:     # Authentication settings
374:     auth_enabled: bool = Field(
375:         default=True,
376:         description="Enable authentication (set to False for local development)",
377:     )
378: 
379:     # API settings
380:     api_host: str = Field(
381:         default="127.0.0.1",
382:         description="API server host",
383:     )
384:     api_port: int = Field(
385:         default=8000,
386:         description="API server port",
387:     )
388: 
389:     # Database for Khora internal state (shortcuts for storage.* URLs)
390:     # These can be set via KHORA_DATABASE_URL and KHORA_NEO4J_URL environment variables
391:     # Programmatic values take priority over environment variables
392:     database_url: str | None = Field(
393:         default=None,
394:         description="PostgreSQL URL for Khora database (shortcut for storage.postgresql_url)",
395:     )
396:     neo4j_url: str | None = Field(
397:         default=None,
398:         description="Neo4j URL for graph storage (shortcut for storage.neo4j_url)",
399:     )
400: 
401:     # LLM extraction model shortcut (single-underscore env var: KHORA_LLM_EXTRACTION_MODEL)
402:     # Propagated to llm.extraction_model — see model_validator below.
403:     llm_extraction_model: str | None = Field(
404:         default=None,
405:         description="Model for entity extraction (shortcut for llm.extraction_model)",
406:     )
407: 
408:     # Storage configuration
409:     storage: StorageSettings = Field(default_factory=StorageSettings)
410: 
411:     # LLM configuration
412:     llm: LLMSettings = Field(default_factory=LLMSettings)
413: 
414:     # Pipeline configuration
415:     pipelines: PipelineSettings = Field(default_factory=PipelineSettings)
416: 
417:     # Tenancy configuration
418:     tenancy: TenancySettings = Field(default_factory=TenancySettings)
419: 
420:     # Query pipeline configuration
421:     query: QuerySettings = Field(default_factory=QuerySettings)
422: 
423:     # Telemetry
424:     telemetry_database_url: str | None = Field(
425:         default=None,
426:         description="PostgreSQL URL for telemetry database (set KHORA_TELEMETRY_DATABASE_URL to enable)",
427:     )
428:     telemetry_service_name: str = Field(
429:         default="khora",
430:         description="Service name tag for telemetry events",
431:     )
432: 
433:     @model_validator(mode="after")
434:     def _propagate_shortcuts(self) -> KhoraConfig:
435:         """Propagate top-level shortcut fields into nested configs."""
436:         if self.llm_extraction_model and not self.llm.extraction_model:
437:             self.llm.extraction_model = self.llm_extraction_model
438:         return self
439: 
440:     @classmethod
441:     def from_yaml(cls, path: str | Path) -> KhoraConfig:
442:         """Load configuration from a YAML file.
443: 
444:         Args:
445:             path: Path to the YAML configuration file
446: 
447:         Returns:
448:             KhoraConfig instance
449:         """
450:         path = Path(path)
451:         with path.open() as f:
452:             data = yaml.safe_load(f)
453:         return cls.model_validate(data or {})
454: 
455:     def get_postgresql_url(self) -> str | None:
456:         """Get PostgreSQL URL from config."""
457:         return self.storage.postgresql_url or self.database_url
458: 
459:     def _get_raw_neo4j_url(self) -> str | None:
460:         """Get raw Neo4j URL (may contain credentials)."""
461:         # Check new-style graph config first
462:         graph = self.storage.graph
463:         if isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig) and graph.url:
464:             return graph.url
465:         # Fall back to legacy fields
466:         return self.storage.neo4j_url or self.neo4j_url
467: 
468:     def _parse_neo4j_url(self) -> ParsedNeo4jUrl | None:
469:         """Parse Neo4j URL and extract components."""
470:         raw_url = self._get_raw_neo4j_url()
471:         if not raw_url:
472:             return None
473:         # Use graph config defaults if available
474:         graph = self.storage.graph
475:         default_user = graph.user if isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig) else self.storage.neo4j_user
476:         default_db = graph.database if isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig) else self.storage.neo4j_database
477:         return ParsedNeo4jUrl.parse(raw_url, default_user=default_user, default_database=default_db)
478: 
479:     def get_neo4j_url(self) -> str | None:
480:         """Get Neo4j URL without credentials (for driver connection).
481: 
482:         Parses URL like bolt://user:pass@host:port and returns bolt://host:port
483:         """
484:         parsed = self._parse_neo4j_url()
485:         return parsed.url if parsed else None
486: 
487:     def get_neo4j_user(self) -> str:
488:         """Get Neo4j username from URL or config."""
489:         parsed = self._parse_neo4j_url()
490:         if parsed:
491:             return parsed.user
492:         graph = self.storage.graph
493:         if isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig):
494:             return graph.user
495:         return self.storage.neo4j_user
496: 
497:     def get_neo4j_password(self) -> str:
498:         """Get Neo4j password from URL or config."""
499:         parsed = self._parse_neo4j_url()
500:         if parsed:
501:             return parsed.password
502:         graph = self.storage.graph
503:         if isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig):
504:             return graph.password
505:         return self.storage.neo4j_password
506: 
507:     def get_neo4j_database(self) -> str:
508:         """Get Neo4j database from URL or config."""
509:         parsed = self._parse_neo4j_url()
510:         if parsed:
511:             return parsed.database
512:         graph = self.storage.graph
513:         if isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig):
514:             return graph.database
515:         return self.storage.neo4j_database
516: 
517:     def get_graph_config(self) -> GraphConfig | None:
518:         """Get the graph backend configuration.
519: 
520:         Returns None if no graph backend is configured, allowing graph-free operation.
521:         If using legacy config, builds a Neo4jConfig from the parsed URL.
522:         """
523:         graph = self.storage.graph
524:         # If it's already set from new-style config with a URL, return as-is
525:         if isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig) and graph.url:
526:             return graph
527:         # If it's a non-Neo4j backend (Kuzu, Memgraph, etc.), return as-is
528:         if graph is not None and not isinstance(graph, Neo4jConfig):
529:             return graph
530:         # Check legacy neo4j_url (covers both graph=None and Neo4jConfig without URL)
531:         neo4j_url = self.get_neo4j_url()
532:         if neo4j_url:
533:             return Neo4jConfig(
534:                 url=neo4j_url,
535:                 user=self.get_neo4j_user(),
536:                 password=self.get_neo4j_password(),
537:                 database=self.get_neo4j_database(),
538:             )
539:         # No URL configured - graph backend is disabled
540:         return None
541: 
542:     def get_vector_config(self) -> VectorConfig:
543:         """Get the vector backend configuration.
544: 
545:         If using legacy config, builds a PgVectorConfig from the flat fields.
546:         """
547:         vector = self.storage.vector
548:         if isinstance(vector, PgVectorConfig) and not vector.url:
549:             # Populate from legacy fields
550:             url = self.storage.pgvector_url or self.get_postgresql_url()
551:             return PgVectorConfig(
552:                 url=url,
553:                 embedding_dimension=self.storage.embedding_dimension,
554:             )
555:         return vector
````

## File: src/khora/extraction/embedders/litellm.py
````python
  1: """LiteLLM-based embedder for unified embedding generation."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: import asyncio
  6: from collections import OrderedDict
  7: from hashlib import sha256
  8: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
  9: 
 10: from loguru import logger
 11: from tenacity import AsyncRetrying, before_sleep_log, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential
 12: 
 13: from .base import Embedder
 14: 
 15: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 16:     from khora.config import LiteLLMConfig
 17: 
 18: 
 19: class LiteLLMEmbedder(Embedder):
 20:     """LiteLLM-based embedder for text embeddings.
 21: 
 22:     Uses LiteLLM to generate embeddings from various providers
 23:     (OpenAI, Cohere, etc.) through a unified interface.
 24: 
 25:     Includes an in-memory embedding cache to avoid re-embedding
 26:     identical texts (e.g. entity mentions that recur across queries).
 27: 
 28:     Cache Behavior:
 29:         The cache persists across multiple embed_batch() calls within
 30:         the embedder's lifetime. This enables cross-document embedding
 31:         deduplication when processing document batches - if chunk text
 32:         appears in multiple documents, it's only embedded once. For
 33:         optimal batch processing, reuse the same embedder instance
 34:         across all documents in a session.
 35:     """
 36: 
 37:     def __init__(
 38:         self,
 39:         model: str = "text-embedding-3-small",
 40:         dimension: int = 1536,
 41:         *,
 42:         timeout: int = 30,
 43:         max_retries: int = 3,
 44:         batch_size: int = 100,
 45:         cache_max_size: int = 10000,
 46:         embed_concurrency: int = 10,
 47:         retry_wait: float = 1.0,
 48:     ) -> None:
 49:         """Initialize the LiteLLM embedder.
 50: 
 51:         Args:
 52:             model: Embedding model name
 53:             dimension: Embedding vector dimension
 54:             timeout: Request timeout in seconds
 55:             max_retries: Maximum retries on failure
 56:             batch_size: Maximum batch size for embed_batch
 57:             cache_max_size: Maximum cached embeddings (0 to disable)
 58:             embed_concurrency: Maximum concurrent embedding sub-batch API calls
 59:             retry_wait: Base wait time (seconds) for exponential backoff between retries
 60:         """
 61:         self._model = model
 62:         self._dimension = dimension
 63:         self._timeout = timeout
 64:         self._max_retries = max_retries
 65:         self._batch_size = batch_size
 66:         self._embed_concurrency = embed_concurrency
 67:         self._retry_wait = retry_wait
 68:         self._cache: OrderedDict[str, list[float]] = OrderedDict()
 69:         self._cache_max_size = cache_max_size
 70:         self._cache_hits = 0
 71:         self._cache_misses = 0
 72: 
 73:     def _cache_key(self, text: str) -> str:
 74:         """Generate a cache key for a text."""
 75:         return sha256(f"{self._model}:{text}".encode()).hexdigest()
 76: 
 77:     def _cache_get(self, text: str, *, key: str | None = None) -> list[float] | None:
 78:         """Look up a cached embedding."""
 79:         if not self._cache_max_size:
 80:             return None
 81:         key = key or self._cache_key(text)
 82:         if key in self._cache:
 83:             self._cache.move_to_end(key)
 84:             self._cache_hits += 1
 85:             return self._cache[key]
 86:         self._cache_misses += 1
 87:         return None
 88: 
 89:     def _cache_put(self, text: str, embedding: list[float], *, key: str | None = None) -> None:
 90:         """Store an embedding in the cache."""
 91:         if not self._cache_max_size:
 92:             return
 93:         key = key or self._cache_key(text)
 94:         self._cache[key] = embedding
 95:         self._cache.move_to_end(key)
 96:         while len(self._cache) > self._cache_max_size:
 97:             self._cache.popitem(last=False)
 98: 
 99:     @property
100:     def cache_stats(self) -> dict[str, int]:
101:         """Return cache hit/miss statistics."""
102:         return {
103:             "size": len(self._cache),
104:             "hits": self._cache_hits,
105:             "misses": self._cache_misses,
106:         }
107: 
108:     @classmethod
109:     def from_config(cls, config: LiteLLMConfig) -> LiteLLMEmbedder:
110:         """Create embedder from LiteLLM configuration.
111: 
112:         Args:
113:             config: LiteLLMConfig instance
114: 
115:         Returns:
116:             Configured LiteLLMEmbedder
117:         """
118:         return cls(
119:             model=config.embedding_model,
120:             dimension=config.embedding_dimension,
121:             timeout=config.timeout,
122:             max_retries=config.max_retries,
123:             retry_wait=config.retry_wait,
124:             embed_concurrency=config.embed_concurrency,
125:         )
126: 
127:     @property
128:     def model_name(self) -> str:
129:         """Get the model name."""
130:         return self._model
131: 
132:     @property
133:     def dimension(self) -> int:
134:         """Get the embedding dimension."""
135:         return self._dimension
136: 
137:     async def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
138:         """Generate embedding for a single text.
139: 
140:         Args:
141:             text: Text to embed
142: 
143:         Returns:
144:             Embedding vector
145:         """
146:         cached = self._cache_get(text)
147:         if cached is not None:
148:             return cached
149:         embeddings = await self.embed_batch([text])
150:         return embeddings[0]
151: 
152:     async def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
153:         """Generate embeddings for multiple texts.
154: 
155:         Uses an in-memory cache to skip API calls for previously seen texts.
156: 
157:         Args:
158:             texts: List of texts to embed
159: 
160:         Returns:
161:             List of embedding vectors
162:         """
163:         if not texts:
164:             return []
165: 
166:         try:
167:             import litellm  # noqa: F401
168:         except ImportError:
169:             raise RuntimeError("litellm package not installed. Run: pip install litellm")
170: 
171:         # Separate cached vs uncached texts; compute cache keys once
172:         results: list[list[float] | None] = [None] * len(texts)
173:         uncached_indices: list[int] = []
174:         uncached_texts: list[str] = []
175:         uncached_keys: list[str] = []
176: 
177:         for i, text in enumerate(texts):
178:             key = self._cache_key(text)
179:             cached = self._cache_get(text, key=key)
180:             if cached is not None:
181:                 results[i] = cached
182:             else:
183:                 uncached_indices.append(i)
184:                 uncached_texts.append(text)
185:                 uncached_keys.append(key)
186: 
187:         # Record embedding cache statistics
188:         cache_hits = len(texts) - len(uncached_texts)
189:         if cache_hits > 0:
190:             from khora.telemetry import get_collector
191: 
192:             get_collector().record_llm_call(
193:                 operation="embedding",
194:                 model=self._model,
195:                 cache_hit=True,
196:                 batch_size=cache_hits,
197:                 latency_ms=0.0,
198:             )
199: 
200:         # Fetch uncached embeddings with deduplication
201:         if uncached_texts:
202:             # Deduplicate: same text appearing multiple times only needs one API call
203:             unique_text_map: dict[str, int] = {}  # key -> first occurrence index in unique list
204:             unique_texts: list[str] = []
205:             dedup_indices: list[int] = []  # maps uncached position -> unique_texts position
206: 
207:             for key, text in zip(uncached_keys, uncached_texts):
208:                 if key not in unique_text_map:
209:                     unique_text_map[key] = len(unique_texts)
210:                     unique_texts.append(text)
211:                 dedup_indices.append(unique_text_map[key])
212: 
213:             if len(unique_texts) > self._batch_size:
214:                 sub_batches = [
215:                     unique_texts[i : i + self._batch_size] for i in range(0, len(unique_texts), self._batch_size)
216:                 ]
217:                 sem = asyncio.Semaphore(self._embed_concurrency)
218: 
219:                 async def _embed_sub(batch: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
220:                     async with sem:
221:                         return await self._embed_batch_internal(batch)
222: 
223:                 sub_results = await asyncio.gather(*[_embed_sub(b) for b in sub_batches])
224:                 unique_embeddings: list[list[float]] = [emb for result in sub_results for emb in result]
225:             else:
226:                 unique_embeddings = await self._embed_batch_internal(unique_texts)
227: 
228:             # Map deduplicated results back to original positions and populate cache
229:             for i, (idx, key) in enumerate(zip(uncached_indices, uncached_keys)):
230:                 embedding = unique_embeddings[dedup_indices[i]]
231:                 results[idx] = embedding
232:                 self._cache_put(texts[idx], embedding, key=key)
233: 
234:         return results  # type: ignore[return-value]
235: 
236:     async def _embed_batch_internal(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
237:         """Internal batch embedding without chunking."""
238:         import time as _time
239: 
240:         import litellm
241: 
242:         # Sanitize inputs: replace None/empty strings with a placeholder to avoid
243:         # OpenAI '$.input' is invalid errors
244:         sanitized = [t if t and t.strip() else " " for t in texts]
245: 
246:         async for attempt in AsyncRetrying(
247:             stop=stop_after_attempt(self._max_retries),
248:             wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=self._retry_wait, min=self._retry_wait, max=10),
249:             before_sleep=before_sleep_log(logger, "WARNING"),
250:             reraise=True,
251:         ):
252:             with attempt:
253:                 _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
254:                 response = await litellm.aembedding(
255:                     model=self._model,
256:                     input=sanitized,
257:                     timeout=self._timeout,
258:                 )
259:                 _latency = (_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000
260: 
261:                 # Record telemetry
262:                 from khora.telemetry import get_collector
263: 
264:                 usage = getattr(response, "usage", None)
265:                 get_collector().record_llm_call(
266:                     operation="embedding",
267:                     model=self._model,
268:                     prompt_tokens=getattr(usage, "prompt_tokens", 0) or 0,
269:                     total_tokens=getattr(usage, "total_tokens", 0) or 0,
270:                     latency_ms=_latency,
271:                     batch_size=len(texts),
272:                     cache_hit=False,
273:                 )
274: 
275:                 return [item["embedding"] for item in response.data]
````

## File: src/khora/query/engine.py
````python
   1: """Hybrid query engine for Khora Memory Lake.
   2: 
   3: Combines vector search, graph traversal, and keyword search
   4: with configurable fusion weights. Now enhanced with:
   5: - LLM-based query understanding
   6: - Entity linking
   7: - BM25 keyword search
   8: - Neural reranking
   9: """
  10: 
  11: from __future__ import annotations
  12: 
  13: import asyncio
  14: 
  15: # Regex for simple-query detection heuristic
  16: import re
  17: import time
  18: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  19: from enum import Enum, auto
  20: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
  21: from uuid import UUID
  22: 
  23: from loguru import logger
  24: 
  25: from .fusion import reciprocal_rank_fusion
  26: from .keyword import KeywordSearcher, normalize_bm25_score
  27: from .linking import EntityLinker, LinkingResult
  28: from .metrics import SearchMetrics
  29: from .reranking import RerankCandidate, create_reranker
  30: from .temporal import TemporalFilter, TemporalQuery
  31: from .understanding import QueryUnderstanding, UnderstandingResult
  32: 
  33: _TEMPORAL_PATTERN = re.compile(
  34:     r"\b(yesterday|today|last\s+\w+|this\s+\w+|ago|since|before|after|"
  35:     r"recent(?:ly)?|q[1-4]|20\d{2}|january|february|march|april|may|june|"
  36:     r"july|august|september|october|november|december)\b",
  37:     re.IGNORECASE,
  38: )
  39: 
  40: if TYPE_CHECKING:
  41:     from khora.acl import ACLContext
  42:     from khora.config.llm import LiteLLMConfig
  43:     from khora.core.models import Chunk, Entity
  44:     from khora.extraction.embedders import Embedder
  45:     from khora.storage import StorageCoordinator
  46: 
  47: 
  48: class SearchMode(Enum):
  49:     """Search mode for the query engine."""
  50: 
  51:     VECTOR = auto()  # Vector similarity only
  52:     GRAPH = auto()  # Graph traversal only
  53:     HYBRID = auto()  # Combine vector and graph
  54:     ALL = auto()  # Vector, graph, and keyword
  55: 
  56: 
  57: @dataclass
  58: class SearchMethodStats:
  59:     """Statistics for a single search method."""
  60: 
  61:     chunk_count: int = 0
  62:     entity_count: int = 0
  63:     chunk_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
  64:     entity_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
  65: 
  66:     # Score statistics
  67:     min_score: float = 0.0
  68:     max_score: float = 0.0
  69:     avg_score: float = 0.0
  70: 
  71:     # Timing (in milliseconds)
  72:     latency_ms: float = 0.0
  73: 
  74:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
  75:         """Convert to dictionary."""
  76:         return {
  77:             "chunks": {
  78:                 "count": self.chunk_count,
  79:                 "ids": self.chunk_ids,  # All IDs for proper attribution
  80:             },
  81:             "entities": {
  82:                 "count": self.entity_count,
  83:                 "ids": self.entity_ids,  # All IDs for proper attribution
  84:             },
  85:             "scores": {
  86:                 "min": round(self.min_score, 4),
  87:                 "max": round(self.max_score, 4),
  88:                 "avg": round(self.avg_score, 4),
  89:             },
  90:             "latency_ms": round(self.latency_ms, 2),
  91:         }
  92: 
  93: 
  94: @dataclass
  95: class SearchMethodContribution:
  96:     """Tracks which search methods contributed to results with detailed statistics."""
  97: 
  98:     # Per-method statistics
  99:     vector: SearchMethodStats = field(default_factory=SearchMethodStats)
 100:     graph: SearchMethodStats = field(default_factory=SearchMethodStats)
 101:     keyword: SearchMethodStats = field(default_factory=SearchMethodStats)
 102: 
 103:     # Overlap analysis
 104:     vector_only_chunks: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Chunks found ONLY by vector
 105:     graph_only_chunks: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Chunks found ONLY by graph
 106:     keyword_only_chunks: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Chunks found ONLY by keyword
 107:     vector_graph_overlap: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Chunks found by both vector AND graph
 108:     vector_keyword_overlap: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Chunks found by both vector AND keyword
 109:     graph_keyword_overlap: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Chunks found by both graph AND keyword
 110:     all_methods_overlap: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Chunks found by ALL methods
 111: 
 112:     # Entity overlap
 113:     vector_only_entities: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 114:     graph_only_entities: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 115:     vector_graph_entity_overlap: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 116: 
 117:     # Total timing
 118:     total_search_latency_ms: float = 0.0
 119:     fusion_latency_ms: float = 0.0
 120: 
 121:     def compute_overlaps(self) -> None:
 122:         """Compute overlap statistics from per-method chunk/entity lists."""
 123:         vector_set = set(self.vector.chunk_ids)
 124:         graph_set = set(self.graph.chunk_ids)
 125:         keyword_set = set(self.keyword.chunk_ids)
 126: 
 127:         # Chunk overlaps
 128:         self.vector_graph_overlap = list(vector_set & graph_set)
 129:         self.vector_keyword_overlap = list(vector_set & keyword_set)
 130:         self.graph_keyword_overlap = list(graph_set & keyword_set)
 131:         self.all_methods_overlap = list(vector_set & graph_set & keyword_set)
 132: 
 133:         # Exclusive chunks
 134:         self.vector_only_chunks = list(vector_set - graph_set - keyword_set)
 135:         self.graph_only_chunks = list(graph_set - vector_set - keyword_set)
 136:         self.keyword_only_chunks = list(keyword_set - vector_set - graph_set)
 137: 
 138:         # Entity overlaps
 139:         vector_entities = set(self.vector.entity_ids)
 140:         graph_entities = set(self.graph.entity_ids)
 141:         self.vector_graph_entity_overlap = list(vector_entities & graph_entities)
 142:         self.vector_only_entities = list(vector_entities - graph_entities)
 143:         self.graph_only_entities = list(graph_entities - vector_entities)
 144: 
 145:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
 146:         """Convert to dictionary with comprehensive statistics."""
 147:         total_unique_chunks = len(set(self.vector.chunk_ids) | set(self.graph.chunk_ids) | set(self.keyword.chunk_ids))
 148:         total_unique_entities = len(set(self.vector.entity_ids) | set(self.graph.entity_ids))
 149: 
 150:         return {
 151:             "summary": {
 152:                 "total_unique_chunks": total_unique_chunks,
 153:                 "total_unique_entities": total_unique_entities,
 154:                 "total_search_latency_ms": round(self.total_search_latency_ms, 2),
 155:                 "fusion_latency_ms": round(self.fusion_latency_ms, 2),
 156:             },
 157:             "by_method": {
 158:                 "vector": self.vector.to_dict(),
 159:                 "graph": self.graph.to_dict(),
 160:                 "keyword": self.keyword.to_dict(),
 161:             },
 162:             "chunk_overlap": {
 163:                 "vector_only": {"count": len(self.vector_only_chunks), "ids": self.vector_only_chunks},
 164:                 "graph_only": {"count": len(self.graph_only_chunks), "ids": self.graph_only_chunks},
 165:                 "keyword_only": {"count": len(self.keyword_only_chunks), "ids": self.keyword_only_chunks},
 166:                 "vector_and_graph": {"count": len(self.vector_graph_overlap), "ids": self.vector_graph_overlap},
 167:                 "vector_and_keyword": {
 168:                     "count": len(self.vector_keyword_overlap),
 169:                     "ids": self.vector_keyword_overlap,
 170:                 },
 171:                 "graph_and_keyword": {"count": len(self.graph_keyword_overlap), "ids": self.graph_keyword_overlap},
 172:                 "all_three_methods": {"count": len(self.all_methods_overlap), "ids": self.all_methods_overlap},
 173:             },
 174:             "entity_overlap": {
 175:                 "vector_only": {"count": len(self.vector_only_entities), "ids": self.vector_only_entities},
 176:                 "graph_only": {"count": len(self.graph_only_entities), "ids": self.graph_only_entities},
 177:                 "vector_and_graph": {
 178:                     "count": len(self.vector_graph_entity_overlap),
 179:                     "ids": self.vector_graph_entity_overlap,
 180:                 },
 181:             },
 182:         }
 183: 
 184:     # Legacy property for backwards compatibility
 185:     @property
 186:     def vector_chunks(self) -> list[str]:
 187:         return self.vector.chunk_ids
 188: 
 189:     @property
 190:     def graph_chunks(self) -> list[str]:
 191:         return self.graph.chunk_ids
 192: 
 193:     @property
 194:     def keyword_chunks(self) -> list[str]:
 195:         return self.keyword.chunk_ids
 196: 
 197: 
 198: @dataclass
 199: class GraphTraversalInfo:
 200:     """Information about graph elements triggered during search."""
 201: 
 202:     entities_searched: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Entity names searched
 203:     entities_linked: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Entities linked from query
 204:     relationships_traversed: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = field(default_factory=list)  # (from, rel, to)
 205:     neighborhood_depth: int = 0
 206: 
 207:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
 208:         """Convert to dictionary."""
 209:         return {
 210:             "entities_searched": self.entities_searched[:20],
 211:             "entities_linked": self.entities_linked[:10],
 212:             "relationships_traversed": [
 213:                 {"from": f, "relationship": r, "to": t} for f, r, t in self.relationships_traversed[:20]
 214:             ],
 215:             "neighborhood_depth": self.neighborhood_depth,
 216:         }
 217: 
 218: 
 219: @dataclass
 220: class TemporalInfo:
 221:     """Information about temporal filtering applied."""
 222: 
 223:     detected: bool = False
 224:     filter_applied: bool = False
 225:     time_start: Any = None  # datetime or None
 226:     time_end: Any = None  # datetime or None
 227:     reference_text: str = ""  # Original temporal reference like "last 7 days"
 228: 
 229:     def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
 230:         """Convert to dictionary."""
 231:         return {
 232:             "detected": self.detected,
 233:             "filter_applied": self.filter_applied,
 234:             "time_start": self.time_start.isoformat() if self.time_start else None,
 235:             "time_end": self.time_end.isoformat() if self.time_end else None,
 236:             "reference_text": self.reference_text,
 237:         }
 238: 
 239: 
 240: @dataclass
 241: class QueryResult:
 242:     """Result from a query with enhanced metadata."""
 243: 
 244:     chunks: list[tuple[Chunk, float]] = field(default_factory=list)
 245:     entities: list[tuple[Entity, float]] = field(default_factory=list)
 246:     graph_context: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 247:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 248: 
 249:     # Enhanced tracking
 250:     search_contributions: SearchMethodContribution | None = None
 251:     graph_info: GraphTraversalInfo | None = None
 252:     temporal_info: TemporalInfo | None = None
 253: 
 254:     @property
 255:     def top_chunks(self) -> list[Chunk]:
 256:         """Get top chunks without scores."""
 257:         return [chunk for chunk, _ in self.chunks]
 258: 
 259:     @property
 260:     def top_entities(self) -> list[Entity]:
 261:         """Get top entities without scores."""
 262:         return [entity for entity, _ in self.entities]
 263: 
 264:     def get_context_text(self, max_chunks: int = 5) -> str:
 265:         """Get concatenated text from top chunks for LLM context."""
 266:         texts = []
 267:         for chunk, score in self.chunks[:max_chunks]:
 268:             texts.append(chunk.content)
 269:         return "\n\n---\n\n".join(texts)
 270: 
 271:     def get_full_metadata(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
 272:         """Get complete metadata including search method contributions."""
 273:         result = dict(self.metadata)
 274:         if self.search_contributions:
 275:             result["search_methods"] = self.search_contributions.to_dict()
 276:         if self.graph_info:
 277:             result["graph_traversal"] = self.graph_info.to_dict()
 278:         if self.temporal_info:
 279:             result["temporal"] = self.temporal_info.to_dict()
 280:         return result
 281: 
 282: 
 283: @dataclass
 284: class QueryConfig:
 285:     """Configuration for query execution."""
 286: 
 287:     # Search mode
 288:     mode: SearchMode = SearchMode.HYBRID
 289: 
 290:     # Result limits
 291:     max_chunks: int = 10
 292:     max_entities: int = 10
 293:     max_graph_depth: int = 2
 294: 
 295:     # Similarity thresholds
 296:     min_chunk_similarity: float = 0.05
 297:     min_entity_similarity: float = 0.05
 298: 
 299:     # Fusion weights
 300:     vector_weight: float = 0.5
 301:     graph_weight: float = 0.3
 302:     keyword_weight: float = 0.2
 303: 
 304:     # RRF parameter
 305:     rrf_k: int = 60
 306: 
 307:     # Temporal settings
 308:     apply_recency_bias: bool = False
 309:     recency_weight: float = 0.2
 310:     recency_decay_days: float = 30.0
 311: 
 312:     # Query understanding settings
 313:     enable_query_understanding: bool = True
 314:     enable_query_expansion: bool = True
 315:     enable_entity_extraction: bool = True
 316:     enable_temporal_detection: bool = True
 317: 
 318:     # Entity linking settings
 319:     enable_entity_linking: bool = True
 320:     entity_linking_fuzzy_threshold: float = 0.6
 321:     entity_linking_embedding_threshold: float = 0.4
 322:     entity_linking_max_candidates: int = 5
 323: 
 324:     # Reranking settings
 325:     enable_reranking: bool = True
 326:     reranking_method: str = "cross_encoder"
 327:     reranking_top_n: int = 50
 328:     reranking_final_k: int = 10
 329: 
 330:     # Keyword search settings
 331:     enable_keyword_search: bool = True
 332:     keyword_search_method: str = "fulltext"
 333: 
 334:     # HyDE settings
 335:     enable_hyde: bool = False
 336:     hyde_num_hypotheticals: int = 1
 337: 
 338:     # Multi-stage ranking pipeline settings
 339:     enable_multi_stage: bool = True
 340:     stage1_recall_limit: int = 200
 341:     stage3_filter_limit: int = 50
 342:     stage4_rerank_limit: int = 50
 343:     enable_diversity: bool = False
 344:     diversity_lambda: float = 0.5
 345: 
 346:     @classmethod
 347:     def from_settings(cls, settings: Any) -> QueryConfig:
 348:         """Create QueryConfig from QuerySettings.
 349: 
 350:         Args:
 351:             settings: QuerySettings from KhoraConfig
 352: 
 353:         Returns:
 354:             QueryConfig instance
 355:         """
 356:         mode_map = {
 357:             "vector": SearchMode.VECTOR,
 358:             "graph": SearchMode.GRAPH,
 359:             "hybrid": SearchMode.HYBRID,
 360:             "all": SearchMode.ALL,
 361:         }
 362: 
 363:         return cls(
 364:             mode=mode_map.get(settings.default_mode.lower(), SearchMode.HYBRID),
 365:             min_chunk_similarity=settings.min_chunk_similarity,
 366:             min_entity_similarity=settings.min_entity_similarity,
 367:             vector_weight=settings.vector_weight,
 368:             graph_weight=settings.graph_weight,
 369:             keyword_weight=settings.keyword_weight,
 370:             apply_recency_bias=settings.apply_recency_bias,
 371:             recency_weight=settings.recency_weight,
 372:             recency_decay_days=settings.recency_decay_days,
 373:             # Query understanding
 374:             enable_query_understanding=settings.enable_understanding,
 375:             enable_query_expansion=settings.understanding_expand_query,
 376:             enable_entity_extraction=settings.understanding_extract_entities,
 377:             enable_temporal_detection=settings.understanding_detect_temporal,
 378:             # Entity linking
 379:             enable_entity_linking=settings.enable_entity_linking,
 380:             entity_linking_fuzzy_threshold=settings.entity_linking_fuzzy_threshold,
 381:             entity_linking_embedding_threshold=settings.entity_linking_embedding_threshold,
 382:             entity_linking_max_candidates=settings.entity_linking_max_candidates,
 383:             # Reranking
 384:             enable_reranking=settings.enable_reranking,
 385:             reranking_method=settings.reranking_method,
 386:             reranking_top_n=settings.reranking_top_n,
 387:             reranking_final_k=settings.reranking_final_k,
 388:             # Keyword search
 389:             enable_keyword_search=settings.enable_keyword_search,
 390:             keyword_search_method=settings.keyword_search_method,
 391:             # HyDE
 392:             enable_hyde=settings.enable_hyde,
 393:             hyde_num_hypotheticals=settings.hyde_num_hypotheticals,
 394:             # Multi-stage ranking pipeline
 395:             enable_multi_stage=settings.enable_multi_stage,
 396:             stage1_recall_limit=settings.stage1_recall_limit,
 397:             stage3_filter_limit=settings.stage3_filter_limit,
 398:             stage4_rerank_limit=settings.stage4_rerank_limit,
 399:             enable_diversity=settings.enable_diversity,
 400:             diversity_lambda=settings.diversity_lambda,
 401:         )
 402: 
 403: 
 404: class HybridQueryEngine:
 405:     """Hybrid query engine combining multiple search methods.
 406: 
 407:     Supports:
 408:     - Vector similarity search on chunks and entities
 409:     - Graph traversal for related entities
 410:     - BM25 keyword search
 411:     - Reciprocal Rank Fusion for combining results
 412:     - Temporal filtering and recency bias
 413:     - LLM-based query understanding
 414:     - Entity linking
 415:     - Neural reranking
 416:     """
 417: 
 418:     def __init__(
 419:         self,
 420:         storage: StorageCoordinator,
 421:         embedder: Embedder | None = None,
 422:         config: QueryConfig | None = None,
 423:         llm_config: LiteLLMConfig | None = None,
 424:     ) -> None:
 425:         """Initialize the query engine.
 426: 
 427:         Args:
 428:             storage: StorageCoordinator for data access
 429:             embedder: Embedder for query embedding
 430:             config: Query configuration
 431:             llm_config: LLM configuration for understanding/reranking
 432:         """
 433:         self._storage = storage
 434:         self._embedder = embedder
 435:         self._config = config or QueryConfig()
 436:         self._llm_config = llm_config
 437: 
 438:         # Initialize query understanding
 439:         self._query_understanding = QueryUnderstanding(llm_config=llm_config)
 440: 
 441:         # Entity linker (created per-query with embedder)
 442:         self._entity_linker: EntityLinker | None = None
 443: 
 444:         # HyDE expander
 445:         self._hyde_expander: HyDEExpander | None = None
 446:         if self._config.enable_hyde and self._embedder:
 447:             from .hyde import HyDEExpander
 448: 
 449:             self._hyde_expander = HyDEExpander(
 450:                 self._embedder,
 451:                 llm_config=llm_config,
 452:                 num_hypotheticals=self._config.hyde_num_hypotheticals,
 453:             )
 454: 
 455:         # Query cache
 456:         from .cache import QueryCache
 457: 
 458:         self._cache = QueryCache()
 459: 
 460:         # Understanding cache (keyed by normalized query + namespace)
 461:         self._understanding_cache = QueryCache(max_size=500, ttl_seconds=120)
 462: 
 463:         # Keyword searcher (built per namespace)
 464:         self._keyword_searchers: dict[str, KeywordSearcher] = {}
 465: 
 466:         # Cached rerankers (keyed by method name) so model is loaded once
 467:         self._rerankers: dict[str, Any] = {}
 468: 
 469:     async def query(
 470:         self,
 471:         query_text: str,
 472:         namespace_id: UUID,
 473:         *,
 474:         config: QueryConfig | None = None,
 475:         temporal_filter: TemporalFilter | None = None,
 476:         context: ACLContext | None = None,
 477:         agentic: bool = False,
 478:         _lightweight_understanding: bool | None = None,
 479:     ) -> QueryResult:
 480:         """Execute a hybrid query with optional enhanced pipeline.
 481: 
 482:         The query pipeline:
 483:         1. Query Understanding (optional) - Extract intent, entities, temporal refs
 484:         2. Entity Linking (optional) - Link mentions to stored entities
 485:         3. Multi-source Search - Vector, graph, keyword (BM25)
 486:         4. RRF Fusion - Combine results
 487:         5. Temporal Filtering - Apply time constraints
 488:         6. Reranking (optional) - Neural re-ranking
 489:         7. Final Limiting - Return top results
 490: 
 491:         Args:
 492:             query_text: Query text
 493:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
 494:             config: Optional query config override
 495:             temporal_filter: Optional temporal filter
 496:             context: Optional ACL context for permission filtering
 497:             agentic: If True, use agentic two-step exploration
 498: 
 499:         Returns:
 500:             QueryResult with matched chunks and entities
 501:         """
 502:         # Agentic mode - use two-step exploration agent
 503:         if agentic:
 504:             from .agentic import AgenticSearchAgent
 505: 
 506:             agent = AgenticSearchAgent(self, self._llm_config)
 507:             agentic_result = await agent.search(query_text, namespace_id, config)
 508: 
 509:             # Convert to QueryResult
 510:             return QueryResult(
 511:                 chunks=[(c, s) for c, s, _ in agentic_result.chunks],
 512:                 entities=agentic_result.entities,
 513:                 metadata={
 514:                     "agentic": True,
 515:                     "summary": agentic_result.summary,
 516:                     "trace": agentic_result.trace.to_dict() if agentic_result.trace else None,
 517:                     **agentic_result.metadata,
 518:                 },
 519:             )
 520: 
 521:         cfg = config or self._config
 522: 
 523:         # Check cache
 524:         cached = await self._cache.get(query_text, namespace_id, cfg.mode.name)
 525:         if cached is not None:
 526:             logger.debug(f"Cache hit for query: {query_text[:50]}...")
 527:             from khora.telemetry import get_collector as _get_tc
 528: 
 529:             _get_tc().record_pipeline_stage(
 530:                 pipeline="query",
 531:                 stage="cache_lookup",
 532:                 latency_ms=0.0,
 533:                 output_count=len(cached.chunks),
 534:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
 535:                 metadata={"hit": True},
 536:             )
 537:             return cached
 538: 
 539:         logger.debug(f"Executing query: {query_text[:50]}... (mode={cfg.mode.name})")
 540: 
 541:         from uuid import uuid4 as _uuid4
 542: 
 543:         from khora.telemetry.instrument import pipeline_stage
 544: 
 545:         _run_id = _uuid4()
 546: 
 547:         # Initialize metrics
 548:         metrics = SearchMetrics()
 549:         metrics.total_timer.start()
 550:         metrics.features = {
 551:             "query_understanding": cfg.enable_query_understanding,
 552:             "entity_linking": cfg.enable_entity_linking,
 553:             "reranking": cfg.enable_reranking,
 554:             "hyde": cfg.enable_hyde,
 555:             "keyword_method": cfg.keyword_search_method,
 556:         }
 557: 
 558:         # Initialize tracking objects
 559:         search_contributions = SearchMethodContribution()
 560:         graph_info = GraphTraversalInfo()
 561:         temporal_info = TemporalInfo()
 562: 
 563:         # Initialize metadata
 564:         metadata: dict[str, Any] = {
 565:             "query": query_text,
 566:             "mode": cfg.mode.name,
 567:             "namespace_id": str(namespace_id),
 568:         }
 569: 
 570:         # Step 1: Query Understanding
 571:         # Check cache FIRST (fastest path), then heuristic, then LLM call
 572:         metrics.understanding_timer.start()
 573:         understanding: UnderstandingResult | None = None
 574:         if cfg.enable_query_understanding:
 575:             # Check understanding cache before running heuristic (cache lookup is faster)
 576:             cached_understanding = await self._understanding_cache.get(query_text, namespace_id, "understanding")
 577:             if cached_understanding is not None:
 578:                 understanding = cached_understanding
 579:                 logger.debug(f"Understanding cache hit for: {query_text[:50]}...")
 580:             elif not self._is_simple_query(query_text):
 581:                 # Only call LLM for complex queries that aren't cached
 582:                 try:
 583:                     # Use lightweight prompt unless caller explicitly opted out
 584:                     use_lightweight = _lightweight_understanding if _lightweight_understanding is not None else True
 585:                     async with pipeline_stage("query", "understanding", _run_id, namespace_id=namespace_id):
 586:                         understanding = await self._query_understanding.understand(
 587:                             query_text,
 588:                             expand_query=cfg.enable_query_expansion,
 589:                             extract_entities=cfg.enable_entity_extraction,
 590:                             detect_temporal=cfg.enable_temporal_detection,
 591:                             lightweight=use_lightweight,
 592:                         )
 593:                     # Cache the understanding result
 594:                     await self._understanding_cache.set(query_text, namespace_id, "understanding", understanding)
 595:                 except Exception as e:
 596:                     logger.warning(f"Query understanding failed: {e}")
 597: 
 598:             # Apply understanding results (works for both cached and fresh)
 599:             if understanding is not None:
 600:                 metadata["understanding"] = {
 601:                     "intent": understanding.intent.name,
 602:                     "answer_type": understanding.answer_type.name,
 603:                     "entities": [e.name for e in understanding.entities],
 604:                     "entity_aliases": {e.name: e.aliases for e in understanding.entities if e.aliases},
 605:                     "relationships": [
 606:                         {"from": r.from_entity, "type": r.relationship_type, "to": r.to_entity}
 607:                         for r in understanding.relationships
 608:                     ],
 609:                     "temporal": understanding.has_temporal,
 610:                     "expanded_queries": understanding.expanded_queries,
 611:                     "keywords": understanding.keywords,
 612:                     "source_priority": {
 613:                         "slack": understanding.source_priority.slack,
 614:                         "linear": understanding.source_priority.linear,
 615:                         "notion": understanding.source_priority.notion,
 616:                         "attio": understanding.source_priority.attio,
 617:                         "gong": understanding.source_priority.gong,
 618:                         "github": understanding.source_priority.github,
 619:                         "bamboohr": understanding.source_priority.bamboohr,
 620:                     },
 621:                     "search_strategy": {
 622:                         "vector_weight": understanding.search_strategy.vector_weight,
 623:                         "graph_weight": understanding.search_strategy.graph_weight,
 624:                         "keyword_weight": understanding.search_strategy.keyword_weight,
 625:                         "reasoning": understanding.search_strategy.strategy_reasoning,
 626:                     },
 627:                     "complexity_score": understanding.complexity_score,
 628:                     "requires_multi_step": understanding.requires_multi_step,
 629:                     "follow_up_queries": [fq.query for fq in understanding.follow_up_queries],
 630:                     "reasoning": understanding.reasoning,
 631:                 }
 632: 
 633:                 # Apply LLM-recommended search strategy weights
 634:                 if understanding.search_strategy:
 635:                     cfg.vector_weight = understanding.search_strategy.vector_weight
 636:                     cfg.graph_weight = understanding.search_strategy.graph_weight
 637:                     cfg.keyword_weight = understanding.search_strategy.keyword_weight
 638:                     cfg.max_graph_depth = understanding.search_strategy.graph_depth
 639: 
 640:                 logger.debug(
 641:                     f"Query understanding: intent={understanding.intent.name}, "
 642:                     f"entities={len(understanding.entities)}, complexity={understanding.complexity_score:.2f}"
 643:                 )
 644: 
 645:                 # Extract temporal information and create filter if detected
 646:                 if understanding.has_temporal and understanding.temporal_references:
 647:                     temporal_info.detected = True
 648:                     for temp_ref in understanding.temporal_references:
 649:                         temporal_info.reference_text = temp_ref.text
 650:                         if temp_ref.start_date:
 651:                             temporal_info.time_start = temp_ref.start_date
 652:                         if temp_ref.end_date:
 653:                             temporal_info.time_end = temp_ref.end_date
 654: 
 655:                     # Create temporal filter if not already provided
 656:                     if not temporal_filter and (temporal_info.time_start or temporal_info.time_end):
 657:                         temporal_filter = TemporalFilter(
 658:                             start_date=temporal_info.time_start,
 659:                             end_date=temporal_info.time_end,
 660:                         )
 661:                         temporal_info.filter_applied = True
 662:                         logger.debug(f"Temporal filter applied: {temporal_info.time_start} to {temporal_info.time_end}")
 663: 
 664:         metrics.understanding_timer.stop()
 665: 
 666:         # Step 2: Entity Linking
 667:         metrics.linking_timer.start()
 668:         linking_result: LinkingResult | None = None
 669:         linked_entity_ids: list[UUID] = []
 670:         if cfg.enable_entity_linking and understanding and understanding.entities:
 671:             try:
 672:                 async with pipeline_stage("query", "entity_linking", _run_id, namespace_id=namespace_id):
 673:                     linker = EntityLinker(
 674:                         self._storage,
 675:                         self._embedder,
 676:                         fuzzy_threshold=cfg.entity_linking_fuzzy_threshold,
 677:                         embedding_threshold=cfg.entity_linking_embedding_threshold,
 678:                         max_candidates=cfg.entity_linking_max_candidates,
 679:                     )
 680:                     linking_result = await linker.link(understanding.entities, namespace_id)
 681:                 linked_entity_ids = linking_result.get_linked_entity_ids()
 682: 
 683:                 # Track linked entity names for graph info
 684:                 for linked in linking_result.linked_entities:
 685:                     if linked.entity:
 686:                         graph_info.entities_linked.append(linked.entity.name)
 687: 
 688:                 metadata["entity_linking"] = {
 689:                     "total_mentions": linking_result.total_mentions,
 690:                     "linked_count": linking_result.linked_count,
 691:                     "success_rate": linking_result.success_rate,
 692:                     "linked_entities": graph_info.entities_linked,
 693:                 }
 694:                 logger.debug(f"Entity linking: {linking_result.linked_count}/{linking_result.total_mentions} linked")
 695:             except Exception as e:
 696:                 logger.warning(f"Entity linking failed: {e}")
 697: 
 698:         metrics.linking_timer.stop()
 699: 
 700:         # Get query embedding for the main query
 701:         query_embedding = None
 702:         if self._embedder and cfg.mode in (SearchMode.VECTOR, SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL):
 703:             query_embedding = await self._embedder.embed(query_text)
 704: 
 705:             # Apply HyDE expansion
 706:             if query_embedding is not None and self._hyde_expander:
 707:                 query_embedding = await self._hyde_expander.expand_query_embedding(query_text, query_embedding)
 708:                 metadata["hyde_applied"] = True
 709: 
 710:         # Step 3-7: Execute search pipeline (multi-stage or legacy)
 711:         if cfg.enable_multi_stage:
 712:             # Use multi-stage ranking pipeline for improved quality
 713:             metrics.multi_stage_enabled = True
 714:             metadata["multi_stage_enabled"] = True
 715: 
 716:             fused_chunks, fused_entities, graph_context, search_contributions = await self._multi_stage_search(
 717:                 query_text=query_text,
 718:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
 719:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
 720:                 config=cfg,
 721:                 understanding=understanding,
 722:                 linked_entity_ids=linked_entity_ids,
 723:                 temporal_filter=temporal_filter,
 724:                 metrics=metrics,
 725:                 graph_info=graph_info,
 726:             )
 727: 
 728:             # Populate per-source counts into metrics from contributions
 729:             metrics.vector_chunk_count = search_contributions.vector.chunk_count
 730:             metrics.graph_chunk_count = search_contributions.graph.chunk_count
 731:             metrics.keyword_chunk_count = search_contributions.keyword.chunk_count
 732:             metrics.vector_entity_count = search_contributions.vector.entity_count
 733:             metrics.graph_entity_count = search_contributions.graph.entity_count
 734:             metrics.fused_chunk_count = metrics.stage2_normalized_count
 735:             metrics.fused_entity_count = len(fused_entities)
 736: 
 737:             # Boost linked entities
 738:             if linked_entity_ids and fused_entities:
 739:                 boosted_entities = []
 740:                 for entity, score in fused_entities:
 741:                     if entity.id in linked_entity_ids:
 742:                         boosted_entities.append((entity, score * 1.5))  # 50% boost
 743:                     else:
 744:                         boosted_entities.append((entity, score))
 745:                 fused_entities = sorted(boosted_entities, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
 746: 
 747:             # Compute overlap statistics
 748:             search_contributions.compute_overlaps()
 749: 
 750:         else:
 751:             # Legacy single-stage pipeline
 752:             metadata["multi_stage_enabled"] = False
 753: 
 754:             # Determine queries to search (original + expansions)
 755:             queries_to_search = [query_text]
 756:             if understanding and cfg.enable_query_expansion:
 757:                 queries_to_search.extend(understanding.expanded_queries[:2])  # Limit expansions
 758: 
 759:             # Step 3: Execute searches
 760:             all_chunk_results: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]] = {}
 761:             all_entity_results: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]] = {}
 762:             graph_context: dict[str, Any] = {}
 763: 
 764:             metrics.search_timer.start()
 765:             search_start_time = time.perf_counter()
 766: 
 767:             for i, q in enumerate(queries_to_search):
 768:                 suffix = "" if i == 0 else f"_exp{i}"
 769: 
 770:                 # Get query embedding for expanded queries
 771:                 current_embedding = query_embedding if i == 0 else None
 772:                 if self._embedder and i > 0 and cfg.mode in (SearchMode.VECTOR, SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL):
 773:                     current_embedding = await self._embedder.embed(q)
 774: 
 775:                 # Execute searches in parallel based on mode
 776:                 tasks = []
 777:                 task_types = []  # Track which task is which for timing
 778: 
 779:                 if cfg.mode in (SearchMode.VECTOR, SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL) and current_embedding is not None:
 780:                     tasks.append(
 781:                         self._timed_search(self._vector_search(namespace_id, current_embedding, cfg), "vector")
 782:                     )
 783:                     task_types.append("vector")
 784: 
 785:                 if cfg.mode in (SearchMode.GRAPH, SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL):
 786:                     tasks.append(
 787:                         self._timed_search(
 788:                             self._graph_search(namespace_id, q, current_embedding, cfg, linked_entity_ids), "graph"
 789:                         )
 790:                     )
 791:                     task_types.append("graph")
 792: 
 793:                 if cfg.mode in (SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL) and cfg.enable_keyword_search:
 794:                     keywords = understanding.keywords if understanding else None
 795:                     if cfg.keyword_search_method == "fulltext":
 796:                         tasks.append(self._timed_search(self._keyword_search_fulltext(namespace_id, q, cfg), "keyword"))
 797:                     else:
 798:                         tasks.append(
 799:                             self._timed_search(self._keyword_search_bm25(namespace_id, q, cfg, keywords), "keyword")
 800:                         )
 801:                     task_types.append("keyword")
 802: 
 803:                 # Execute in parallel
 804:                 results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
 805: 
 806:                 # Process results and track contributions with detailed stats
 807:                 for j, result in enumerate(results):
 808:                     if isinstance(result, Exception):
 809:                         logger.error(f"Search {j} failed: {result}")
 810:                         continue
 811: 
 812:                     if isinstance(result, dict):
 813:                         source_type = result.get("source", f"search_{j}")
 814:                         latency_ms = result.get("latency_ms", 0.0)
 815: 
 816:                         if "chunks" in result:
 817:                             source = source_type + suffix
 818:                             all_chunk_results[source] = result["chunks"]
 819: 
 820:                             # Track contributions by search method with detailed stats
 821:                             chunk_ids = [str(c.id) for c, _ in result["chunks"]]
 822:                             scores = [s for _, s in result["chunks"]]
 823: 
 824:                             if source_type == "vector":
 825:                                 search_contributions.vector.chunk_count += len(result["chunks"])
 826:                                 search_contributions.vector.chunk_ids.extend(chunk_ids)
 827:                                 search_contributions.vector.latency_ms = latency_ms
 828:                                 if scores:
 829:                                     search_contributions.vector.min_score = min(scores)
 830:                                     search_contributions.vector.max_score = max(scores)
 831:                                     search_contributions.vector.avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)
 832:                             elif source_type == "graph":
 833:                                 search_contributions.graph.chunk_count += len(result["chunks"])
 834:                                 search_contributions.graph.chunk_ids.extend(chunk_ids)
 835:                                 search_contributions.graph.latency_ms = latency_ms
 836:                                 if scores:
 837:                                     search_contributions.graph.min_score = min(scores)
 838:                                     search_contributions.graph.max_score = max(scores)
 839:                                     search_contributions.graph.avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)
 840:                             elif source_type == "keyword":
 841:                                 search_contributions.keyword.chunk_count += len(result["chunks"])
 842:                                 search_contributions.keyword.chunk_ids.extend(chunk_ids)
 843:                                 search_contributions.keyword.latency_ms = latency_ms
 844:                                 if scores:
 845:                                     search_contributions.keyword.min_score = min(scores)
 846:                                     search_contributions.keyword.max_score = max(scores)
 847:                                     search_contributions.keyword.avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)
 848: 
 849:                         if "entities" in result:
 850:                             source = source_type + suffix
 851:                             all_entity_results[source] = result["entities"]
 852: 
 853:                             # Track entity stats
 854:                             entity_ids = [str(e.id) for e, _ in result["entities"]]
 855:                             if source_type == "vector":
 856:                                 search_contributions.vector.entity_count += len(result["entities"])
 857:                                 search_contributions.vector.entity_ids.extend(entity_ids)
 858:                             elif source_type == "graph":
 859:                                 search_contributions.graph.entity_count += len(result["entities"])
 860:                                 search_contributions.graph.entity_ids.extend(entity_ids)
 861:                                 # Also track entity names for graph info
 862:                                 for entity, _ in result["entities"]:
 863:                                     graph_info.entities_searched.append(entity.name)
 864: 
 865:                         if "graph_context" in result:
 866:                             graph_context.update(result["graph_context"])
 867: 
 868:                             # Track relationships from graph context
 869:                             if "relationships" in result.get("graph_context", {}):
 870:                                 for rel in result["graph_context"]["relationships"]:
 871:                                     if isinstance(rel, dict):
 872:                                         graph_info.relationships_traversed.append(
 873:                                             (rel.get("from", ""), rel.get("type", ""), rel.get("to", ""))
 874:                                         )
 875: 
 876:             # Record total search time
 877:             search_contributions.total_search_latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - search_start_time) * 1000
 878:             metrics.search_timer.stop()
 879: 
 880:             # Record search stage telemetry
 881:             from khora.telemetry import get_collector as _get_collector
 882: 
 883:             _get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
 884:                 pipeline="query",
 885:                 stage="search",
 886:                 run_id=_run_id,
 887:                 latency_ms=search_contributions.total_search_latency_ms,
 888:                 output_count=sum(len(v) for v in all_chunk_results.values()),
 889:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
 890:             )
 891: 
 892:             # Populate per-source counts into metrics
 893:             metrics.vector_chunk_count = search_contributions.vector.chunk_count
 894:             metrics.graph_chunk_count = search_contributions.graph.chunk_count
 895:             metrics.keyword_chunk_count = search_contributions.keyword.chunk_count
 896:             metrics.vector_entity_count = search_contributions.vector.entity_count
 897:             metrics.graph_entity_count = search_contributions.graph.entity_count
 898: 
 899:             # Step 4: Apply RRF fusion
 900:             metrics.fusion_timer.start()
 901:             fusion_start_time = time.perf_counter()
 902: 
 903:             fused_chunks = []
 904:             if all_chunk_results:
 905:                 weights = {
 906:                     "vector": cfg.vector_weight,
 907:                     "graph": cfg.graph_weight,
 908:                     "keyword": cfg.keyword_weight,
 909:                 }
 910:                 # Add weights for expanded query results
 911:                 for key in all_chunk_results:
 912:                     if "_exp" in key:
 913:                         base_source = key.split("_exp")[0]
 914:                         weights[key] = weights.get(base_source, cfg.vector_weight) * 0.7  # Discount expansions
 915: 
 916:                 fused_chunks = reciprocal_rank_fusion(
 917:                     all_chunk_results,
 918:                     k=cfg.rrf_k,
 919:                     weights=weights,
 920:                     id_extractor=lambda c: str(c.id),
 921:                 )
 922: 
 923:             fused_entities = []
 924:             if all_entity_results:
 925:                 weights = {
 926:                     "vector": cfg.vector_weight,
 927:                     "graph": cfg.graph_weight,
 928:                 }
 929:                 fused_entities = reciprocal_rank_fusion(
 930:                     all_entity_results,
 931:                     k=cfg.rrf_k,
 932:                     weights=weights,
 933:                     id_extractor=lambda e: str(e.id),
 934:                 )
 935:
````
