````python
 936:             search_contributions.fusion_latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - fusion_start_time) * 1000
 937:             metrics.fusion_timer.stop()
 938: 
 939:             _get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
 940:                 pipeline="query",
 941:                 stage="fusion",
 942:                 run_id=_run_id,
 943:                 latency_ms=search_contributions.fusion_latency_ms,
 944:                 input_count=sum(len(v) for v in all_chunk_results.values()),
 945:                 output_count=len(fused_chunks) + len(fused_entities),
 946:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
 947:             )
 948:             metrics.fused_chunk_count = len(fused_chunks)
 949:             metrics.fused_entity_count = len(fused_entities)
 950: 
 951:             # Zero-result fallback: retry with no threshold and keyword search
 952:             if not fused_chunks:
 953:                 logger.info("Zero results after fusion — attempting fallback search")
 954:                 fallback_chunks: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]] = {}
 955: 
 956:                 # Fallback 1: vector search with no similarity threshold
 957:                 if query_embedding is not None:
 958:                     try:
 959:                         fb_vector = await self._vector_search(
 960:                             namespace_id,
 961:                             query_embedding,
 962:                             QueryConfig(
 963:                                 max_chunks=cfg.max_chunks,
 964:                                 max_entities=cfg.max_entities,
 965:                                 min_chunk_similarity=0.0,
 966:                                 min_entity_similarity=0.0,
 967:                             ),
 968:                         )
 969:                         if fb_vector["chunks"]:
 970:                             fallback_chunks["vector"] = fb_vector["chunks"]
 971:                             if fb_vector.get("entities"):
 972:                                 all_entity_results["vector_fallback"] = fb_vector["entities"]
 973:                     except Exception as e:
 974:                         logger.warning(f"Fallback vector search failed: {e}")
 975: 
 976:                 # Fallback 2: keyword search (if not already run)
 977:                 if not search_contributions.keyword.chunk_count:
 978:                     try:
 979:                         fb_keyword = await self._keyword_search_fulltext(namespace_id, query_text, cfg)
 980:                         if fb_keyword["chunks"]:
 981:                             fallback_chunks["keyword"] = fb_keyword["chunks"]
 982:                     except Exception as e:
 983:                         logger.warning(f"Fallback keyword search failed: {e}")
 984: 
 985:                 if fallback_chunks:
 986:                     fused_chunks = reciprocal_rank_fusion(
 987:                         fallback_chunks,
 988:                         k=cfg.rrf_k,
 989:                         weights={"vector": cfg.vector_weight, "keyword": cfg.keyword_weight},
 990:                         id_extractor=lambda c: str(c.id),
 991:                     )
 992:                     metrics.fused_chunk_count = len(fused_chunks)
 993:                     logger.info(f"Fallback recovered {len(fused_chunks)} chunks")
 994: 
 995:             # Apply source priority boosting from query understanding
 996:             if understanding and understanding.source_priority:
 997:                 fused_chunks = self._apply_source_priority(fused_chunks, understanding)
 998:                 fused_entities = self._apply_source_priority_entities(fused_entities, understanding)
 999: 
1000:             # Apply attribute-aware scoring boost for entities
1001:             if understanding and understanding.keywords and fused_entities:
1002:                 fused_entities = [
1003:                     (entity, score + self._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, understanding.keywords))
1004:                     for entity, score in fused_entities
1005:                 ]
1006:                 fused_entities.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
1007: 
1008:             # Boost linked entities
1009:             if linked_entity_ids:
1010:                 boosted_entities = []
1011:                 for entity, score in fused_entities:
1012:                     if entity.id in linked_entity_ids:
1013:                         boosted_entities.append((entity, score * 1.5))  # 50% boost
1014:                     else:
1015:                         boosted_entities.append((entity, score))
1016:                 fused_entities = sorted(boosted_entities, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
1017: 
1018:             # Step 5: Apply temporal filter
1019:             if temporal_filter:
1020:                 fused_chunks = [(c, s) for c, s in fused_chunks if temporal_filter.matches(c.created_at)]
1021: 
1022:             # Apply recency bias
1023:             if cfg.apply_recency_bias:
1024:                 temporal_query = TemporalQuery(query_text).with_recency_bias(
1025:                     cfg.recency_weight,
1026:                     cfg.recency_decay_days,
1027:                 )
1028:                 fused_chunks = [(c, s * temporal_query.calculate_recency_score(c.created_at)) for c, s in fused_chunks]
1029:                 fused_chunks.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
1030: 
1031:             # Step 6: Reranking (optional, skip for small result sets)
1032:             metrics.reranking_timer.start()
1033:             if cfg.enable_reranking and len(fused_chunks) >= 5:
1034:                 try:
1035:                     if cfg.reranking_method not in self._rerankers:
1036:                         self._rerankers[cfg.reranking_method] = create_reranker(
1037:                             method=cfg.reranking_method,
1038:                             llm_config=self._llm_config,
1039:                         )
1040:                     reranker = self._rerankers[cfg.reranking_method]
1041:                     candidates = [
1042:                         RerankCandidate(
1043:                             item=chunk,
1044:                             original_score=score,
1045:                             content=chunk.content,
1046:                             metadata=chunk.metadata,
1047:                         )
1048:                         for chunk, score in fused_chunks[: cfg.reranking_top_n]
1049:                     ]
1050:                     async with pipeline_stage(
1051:                         "query",
1052:                         "reranking",
1053:                         _run_id,
1054:                         namespace_id=namespace_id,
1055:                         input_count=len(candidates),
1056:                         extra_metadata={"method": cfg.reranking_method},
1057:                     ) as _rerank_ctx:
1058:                         reranked = await reranker.rerank(query_text, candidates, top_k=cfg.reranking_final_k)
1059:                         _rerank_ctx["output_count"] = len(reranked)
1060:                     fused_chunks = [(r.item, r.final_score) for r in reranked]
1061:                     metadata["reranking"] = {"method": cfg.reranking_method, "reranked_count": len(fused_chunks)}
1062:                     logger.debug(f"Reranked {len(candidates)} candidates to {len(fused_chunks)} results")
1063:                 except Exception as e:
1064:                     logger.warning(f"Reranking failed: {e}")
1065: 
1066:             metrics.reranking_timer.stop()
1067: 
1068:             # Step 7: Limit results
1069:             fused_chunks = fused_chunks[: cfg.max_chunks]
1070:             fused_entities = fused_entities[: cfg.max_entities]
1071: 
1072:             # Compute overlap statistics
1073:             search_contributions.compute_overlaps()
1074: 
1075:         # Update graph info with depth used
1076:         graph_info.neighborhood_depth = cfg.max_graph_depth
1077: 
1078:         # Finalize metrics
1079:         metrics.final_chunk_count = len(fused_chunks)
1080:         metrics.final_entity_count = len(fused_entities)
1081:         metrics.set_chunk_scores([s for _, s in fused_chunks])
1082:         metrics.total_timer.stop()
1083:         metrics.log()
1084: 
1085:         # Add search method info to metadata
1086:         metadata["search_methods"] = search_contributions.to_dict()
1087:         metadata["graph_traversal"] = graph_info.to_dict()
1088:         metadata["temporal"] = temporal_info.to_dict()
1089:         metadata["metrics"] = metrics.to_dict()
1090: 
1091:         result = QueryResult(
1092:             chunks=fused_chunks,
1093:             entities=fused_entities,
1094:             graph_context=graph_context,
1095:             metadata=metadata,
1096:             search_contributions=search_contributions,
1097:             graph_info=graph_info,
1098:             temporal_info=temporal_info,
1099:         )
1100: 
1101:         # Cache the result
1102:         await self._cache.set(query_text, namespace_id, cfg.mode.name, result)
1103: 
1104:         return result
1105: 
1106:     async def _timed_search(
1107:         self,
1108:         search_coro: Any,
1109:         source_type: str,
1110:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1111:         """Wrap a search coroutine with timing instrumentation.
1112: 
1113:         Args:
1114:             search_coro: The search coroutine to execute
1115:             source_type: The type of search (vector, graph, keyword)
1116: 
1117:         Returns:
1118:             Search result dict with latency_ms added
1119:         """
1120:         start_time = time.perf_counter()
1121:         result = await search_coro
1122:         latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - start_time) * 1000
1123: 
1124:         if isinstance(result, dict):
1125:             result["latency_ms"] = latency_ms
1126:         return result
1127: 
1128:     async def _vector_search(
1129:         self,
1130:         namespace_id: UUID,
1131:         query_embedding: list[float],
1132:         config: QueryConfig,
1133:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1134:         """Perform vector similarity search."""
1135:         # Search chunks
1136:         chunk_results = await self._storage.search_similar_chunks(
1137:             namespace_id,
1138:             query_embedding,
1139:             limit=config.max_chunks * 2,  # Get extra for fusion
1140:             min_similarity=config.min_chunk_similarity,
1141:         )
1142: 
1143:         # Search entities
1144:         entity_ids_scores = await self._storage.search_similar_entities(
1145:             namespace_id,
1146:             query_embedding,
1147:             limit=config.max_entities * 2,
1148:             min_similarity=config.min_entity_similarity,
1149:         )
1150: 
1151:         # Fetch full entities in batch (optimization: single query instead of N queries)
1152:         entity_ids = [eid for eid, _ in entity_ids_scores]
1153:         entities_map = await self._storage.get_entities_batch(entity_ids)
1154:         entities = [(entities_map[eid], score) for eid, score in entity_ids_scores if eid in entities_map]
1155: 
1156:         return {
1157:             "source": "vector",
1158:             "chunks": chunk_results,
1159:             "entities": entities,
1160:         }
1161: 
1162:     async def _graph_search(
1163:         self,
1164:         namespace_id: UUID,
1165:         query_text: str,
1166:         query_embedding: list[float] | None,
1167:         config: QueryConfig,
1168:         linked_entity_ids: list[UUID] | None = None,
1169:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1170:         """Perform graph-based search.
1171: 
1172:         Args:
1173:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
1174:             query_text: Query text
1175:             query_embedding: Query embedding (optional)
1176:             config: Query configuration
1177:             linked_entity_ids: Entity IDs from entity linking (optional)
1178: 
1179:         Returns:
1180:             Dict with chunks, entities, and graph context
1181:         """
1182:         entities = []
1183:         graph_context = {}
1184:         seen_entity_ids: set[UUID] = set()
1185: 
1186:         # Collect all entity IDs we need to fetch
1187:         all_entity_ids_to_fetch: list[UUID] = []
1188:         linked_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
1189:         similar_scores: dict[UUID, float] = {}
1190: 
1191:         # Linked entities (high priority)
1192:         if linked_entity_ids:
1193:             for entity_id in linked_entity_ids[:5]:
1194:                 if entity_id not in seen_entity_ids:
1195:                     all_entity_ids_to_fetch.append(entity_id)
1196:                     linked_scores[entity_id] = 1.0  # High confidence from linking
1197:                     seen_entity_ids.add(entity_id)
1198: 
1199:         # Similar entities via embedding
1200:         if query_embedding is not None:
1201:             entity_ids_scores = await self._storage.search_similar_entities(
1202:                 namespace_id,
1203:                 query_embedding,
1204:                 limit=5,
1205:                 min_similarity=config.min_entity_similarity,
1206:             )
1207: 
1208:             for entity_id, score in entity_ids_scores[:3]:
1209:                 if entity_id not in seen_entity_ids:
1210:                     all_entity_ids_to_fetch.append(entity_id)
1211:                     similar_scores[entity_id] = score
1212:                     seen_entity_ids.add(entity_id)
1213: 
1214:             # Fallback: if no entities found, retry with no threshold
1215:             if not all_entity_ids_to_fetch and config.min_entity_similarity > 0:
1216:                 entity_ids_scores = await self._storage.search_similar_entities(
1217:                     namespace_id,
1218:                     query_embedding,
1219:                     limit=3,
1220:                     min_similarity=0.0,
1221:                 )
1222:                 for entity_id, score in entity_ids_scores[:3]:
1223:                     if entity_id not in seen_entity_ids:
1224:                         all_entity_ids_to_fetch.append(entity_id)
1225:                         similar_scores[entity_id] = score
1226:                         seen_entity_ids.add(entity_id)
1227: 
1228:         # Batch fetch all entities and neighborhoods in parallel
1229:         if all_entity_ids_to_fetch:
1230:             # Fetch entities and neighborhoods in parallel
1231:             entities_map, neighborhoods = await asyncio.gather(
1232:                 self._storage.get_entities_batch(all_entity_ids_to_fetch),
1233:                 self._storage.get_neighborhoods_batch(
1234:                     all_entity_ids_to_fetch,
1235:                     depth=config.max_graph_depth,
1236:                     limit_per_entity=20,
1237:                 ),
1238:             )
1239: 
1240:             # Process results maintaining priority order
1241:             for entity_id in all_entity_ids_to_fetch:
1242:                 if entity_id in entities_map:
1243:                     entity = entities_map[entity_id]
1244:                     score = linked_scores.get(entity_id) or similar_scores.get(entity_id, 0.5)
1245:                     entities.append((entity, score))
1246: 
1247:                     # Add neighborhood to context
1248:                     if entity_id in neighborhoods:
1249:                         graph_context[str(entity_id)] = neighborhoods[entity_id]
1250: 
1251:         # Get related chunks through entities - batch fetch to avoid N+1
1252:         chunks = []
1253: 
1254:         # Collect all unique chunk IDs with their associated entity/score info
1255:         chunk_ids_to_fetch: list[UUID] = []
1256:         chunk_id_to_info: dict[UUID, tuple[Any, float]] = {}
1257:         for entity, score in entities:
1258:             for chunk_id in entity.source_chunk_ids[:5]:
1259:                 if chunk_id not in chunk_id_to_info:
1260:                     chunk_ids_to_fetch.append(chunk_id)
1261:                     chunk_id_to_info[chunk_id] = (entity, score)
1262: 
1263:         # Batch fetch all chunks in a single query
1264:         if chunk_ids_to_fetch:
1265:             chunks_map = await self._storage.get_chunks_batch(chunk_ids_to_fetch)
1266:             for chunk_id, chunk in chunks_map.items():
1267:                 entity, score = chunk_id_to_info[chunk_id]
1268:                 # Score based on entity score and mention count
1269:                 chunk_score = score * (1 + 0.1 * min(entity.mention_count, 10))
1270:                 chunks.append((chunk, chunk_score))
1271: 
1272:         return {
1273:             "source": "graph",
1274:             "chunks": chunks,
1275:             "entities": entities,
1276:             "graph_context": graph_context,
1277:         }
1278: 
1279:     async def _keyword_search(
1280:         self,
1281:         namespace_id: UUID,
1282:         query_text: str,
1283:         config: QueryConfig,
1284:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1285:         """Perform keyword-based search (legacy, returns empty).
1286: 
1287:         Use _keyword_search_bm25 for actual BM25-based search.
1288:         """
1289:         return {
1290:             "source": "keyword",
1291:             "chunks": [],
1292:             "entities": [],
1293:         }
1294: 
1295:     async def _keyword_search_bm25(
1296:         self,
1297:         namespace_id: UUID,
1298:         query_text: str,
1299:         config: QueryConfig,
1300:         keywords: list[str] | None = None,
1301:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1302:         """Perform BM25-based keyword search.
1303: 
1304:         Args:
1305:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
1306:             query_text: Query text
1307:             config: Query configuration
1308:             keywords: Optional pre-extracted keywords from query understanding
1309: 
1310:         Returns:
1311:             Dict with chunks and entities
1312:         """
1313:         ns_key = str(namespace_id)
1314: 
1315:         # Build or get keyword index for this namespace
1316:         if ns_key not in self._keyword_searchers:
1317:             try:
1318:                 # Fetch all chunks for the namespace (up to a limit)
1319:                 chunks = await self._storage.list_chunks(
1320:                     namespace_id,
1321:                     limit=10000,  # Reasonable limit for in-memory index
1322:                 )
1323:                 if chunks:
1324:                     searcher = KeywordSearcher(
1325:                         use_stemming=True,
1326:                         remove_stopwords=True,
1327:                     )
1328:                     searcher.index_chunks(chunks)
1329:                     self._keyword_searchers[ns_key] = searcher
1330:                     logger.debug(f"Built BM25 index with {len(chunks)} chunks")
1331:                 else:
1332:                     logger.debug("No chunks to index for keyword search")
1333:                     return {"source": "keyword", "chunks": [], "entities": []}
1334:             except Exception as e:
1335:                 logger.warning(f"Failed to build keyword index: {e}")
1336:                 return {"source": "keyword", "chunks": [], "entities": []}
1337: 
1338:         searcher = self._keyword_searchers.get(ns_key)
1339:         if not searcher:
1340:             return {"source": "keyword", "chunks": [], "entities": []}
1341: 
1342:         try:
1343:             # Use keywords if available, otherwise use query text
1344:             if keywords:
1345:                 results = searcher.search_with_keywords(
1346:                     keywords,
1347:                     limit=config.max_chunks * 2,
1348:                     min_score=0.1,
1349:                 )
1350:             else:
1351:                 results = searcher.search(
1352:                     query_text,
1353:                     limit=config.max_chunks * 2,
1354:                     min_score=0.1,
1355:                 )
1356: 
1357:             # Normalize BM25 scores to 0-1 range
1358:             normalized_results = [(chunk, normalize_bm25_score(score)) for chunk, score in results]
1359: 
1360:             return {
1361:                 "source": "keyword",
1362:                 "chunks": normalized_results,
1363:                 "entities": [],  # Keyword search doesn't directly find entities
1364:             }
1365:         except Exception as e:
1366:             logger.warning(f"Keyword search failed: {e}")
1367:             return {"source": "keyword", "chunks": [], "entities": []}
1368: 
1369:     async def _keyword_search_fulltext(
1370:         self,
1371:         namespace_id: UUID,
1372:         query_text: str,
1373:         config: QueryConfig,
1374:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1375:         """Perform PostgreSQL full-text search using tsvector/tsquery.
1376: 
1377:         Unlike BM25, this runs entirely in PostgreSQL using the GIN-indexed
1378:         content_tsv column, with no chunk count limit.
1379:         """
1380:         try:
1381:             from khora.telemetry import get_collector as _get_tc
1382: 
1383:             _kw_start = time.perf_counter()
1384:             results = await self._storage.search_fulltext_chunks(
1385:                 namespace_id,
1386:                 query_text,
1387:                 limit=config.max_chunks * 2,
1388:             )
1389:             _get_tc().record_pipeline_stage(
1390:                 pipeline="query",
1391:                 stage="keyword_search",
1392:                 latency_ms=(time.perf_counter() - _kw_start) * 1000,
1393:                 output_count=len(results),
1394:                 namespace_id=namespace_id,
1395:                 metadata={"method": "fulltext"},
1396:             )
1397: 
1398:             # Normalize ts_rank scores to 0-1 range
1399:             if results:
1400:                 max_score = max(s for _, s in results) or 1.0
1401:                 normalized = [(chunk, score / max_score) for chunk, score in results]
1402:             else:
1403:                 normalized = []
1404: 
1405:             return {
1406:                 "source": "keyword",
1407:                 "chunks": normalized,
1408:                 "entities": [],
1409:             }
1410:         except Exception as e:
1411:             logger.warning(f"Full-text search failed: {e}")
1412:             return {"source": "keyword", "chunks": [], "entities": []}
1413: 
1414:     def _apply_source_priority(
1415:         self,
1416:         results: list[tuple[Any, float]],
1417:         understanding: UnderstandingResult,
1418:     ) -> list[tuple[Any, float]]:
1419:         """Boost or demote chunk results based on source priority weights.
1420: 
1421:         Reads source_tool from chunk -> document metadata and applies
1422:         a multiplicative boost based on the query understanding's source_priority.
1423: 
1424:         Args:
1425:             results: List of (chunk, score) tuples
1426:             understanding: Query understanding with source_priority
1427: 
1428:         Returns:
1429:             Re-sorted list of (chunk, score) tuples
1430:         """
1431:         sp = understanding.source_priority
1432:         source_filters = set(understanding.source_filters)
1433: 
1434:         boosted = []
1435:         for chunk, score in results:
1436:             source_tool = ""
1437:             if hasattr(chunk, "metadata") and hasattr(chunk.metadata, "custom"):
1438:                 source_tool = chunk.metadata.custom.get("source_tool", "")
1439: 
1440:             if not source_tool:
1441:                 boosted.append((chunk, score))
1442:                 continue
1443: 
1444:             # Filter out results from excluded sources
1445:             if source_tool in source_filters:
1446:                 continue
1447: 
1448:             weight = getattr(sp, source_tool, 1.0) if hasattr(sp, source_tool) else 1.0
1449:             # Apply as multiplicative boost — never fully zero out
1450:             adjusted_score = score * (0.5 + 0.5 * weight)
1451:             boosted.append((chunk, adjusted_score))
1452: 
1453:         boosted.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
1454:         return boosted
1455: 
1456:     def _apply_source_priority_entities(
1457:         self,
1458:         results: list[tuple[Any, float]],
1459:         understanding: UnderstandingResult,
1460:     ) -> list[tuple[Any, float]]:
1461:         """Boost or demote entity results based on source priority weights.
1462: 
1463:         Args:
1464:             results: List of (entity, score) tuples
1465:             understanding: Query understanding with source_priority
1466: 
1467:         Returns:
1468:             Re-sorted list of (entity, score) tuples
1469:         """
1470:         sp = understanding.source_priority
1471:         source_filters = set(understanding.source_filters)
1472: 
1473:         boosted = []
1474:         for entity, score in results:
1475:             source_tool = getattr(entity, "source_tool", "")
1476: 
1477:             if not source_tool:
1478:                 boosted.append((entity, score))
1479:                 continue
1480: 
1481:             if source_tool in source_filters:
1482:                 continue
1483: 
1484:             weight = getattr(sp, source_tool, 1.0) if hasattr(sp, source_tool) else 1.0
1485:             adjusted_score = score * (0.5 + 0.5 * weight)
1486:             boosted.append((entity, adjusted_score))
1487: 
1488:         boosted.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
1489:         return boosted
1490: 
1491:     @staticmethod
1492:     def _is_simple_query(query_text: str) -> bool:
1493:         """Check if a query is simple enough to skip LLM understanding.
1494: 
1495:         Simple queries are short, lack temporal references, and don't contain
1496:         complex entity mentions. Skipping understanding saves ~14s per query.
1497: 
1498:         Args:
1499:             query_text: The query text
1500: 
1501:         Returns:
1502:             True if the query should skip understanding
1503:         """
1504:         words = query_text.split()
1505:         if len(words) > 8:
1506:             return False
1507: 
1508:         # Has temporal references
1509:         if _TEMPORAL_PATTERN.search(query_text):
1510:             return False
1511: 
1512:         # Contains quoted phrases (specific entity searches)
1513:         if '"' in query_text or "'" in query_text:
1514:             return False
1515: 
1516:         # Contains comparison words
1517:         comparison_words = {"compare", "versus", "vs", "difference", "between", "similar"}
1518:         if comparison_words & {w.lower().strip("?.,!") for w in words}:
1519:             return False
1520: 
1521:         return True
1522: 
1523:     @staticmethod
1524:     def _attribute_relevance_boost(entity: Any, keywords: list[str]) -> float:
1525:         """Score boost based on entity attribute value matches with query keywords.
1526: 
1527:         When a query like "urgent tickets assigned to Alice" matches entities
1528:         with priority: "urgent" and assignee: "Alice" in their attributes,
1529:         those entities get a relevance boost.
1530: 
1531:         Args:
1532:             entity: Entity with attributes dict
1533:             keywords: Keywords from query understanding
1534: 
1535:         Returns:
1536:             Additional score boost (0.0 to 0.3, capped)
1537:         """
1538:         attributes = getattr(entity, "attributes", None)
1539:         if not attributes or not isinstance(attributes, dict):
1540:             return 0.0
1541: 
1542:         boost = 0.0
1543:         for value in attributes.values():
1544:             value_str = str(value).lower()
1545:             for keyword in keywords:
1546:                 if keyword.lower() in value_str:
1547:                     boost += 0.1
1548:         return min(boost, 0.3)  # Cap at 0.3
1549: 
1550:     # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
1551:     # Multi-Stage Ranking Pipeline
1552:     # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
1553: 
1554:     async def _multi_stage_search(
1555:         self,
1556:         query_text: str,
1557:         namespace_id: UUID,
1558:         query_embedding: list[float] | None,
1559:         config: QueryConfig,
1560:         understanding: Any | None,
1561:         linked_entity_ids: list[UUID],
1562:         temporal_filter: TemporalFilter | None,
1563:         metrics: SearchMetrics,
1564:         graph_info: GraphTraversalInfo,
1565:     ) -> tuple[list[tuple[Any, float]], list[tuple[Any, float]], dict[str, Any], SearchMethodContribution]:
1566:         """Multi-stage ranking pipeline for improved search quality.
1567: 
1568:         Pipeline stages:
1569:         1. Broad Recall - Fast retrieval of 100-200 candidates from all sources
1570:         2. Score Normalization & Fusion - Normalize scores to [0,1] and apply weighted RRF
1571:         3. Lightweight Filtering - Apply temporal filters and source priority, reduce to top 50
1572:         4. Neural Reranking - Cross-encoder reranking on top candidates (expensive)
1573:         5. Diversity & Final Selection - Optional MMR-style diversity, return top 10
1574: 
1575:         Args:
1576:             query_text: Query text
1577:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
1578:             query_embedding: Query embedding (optional)
1579:             config: Query configuration
1580:             understanding: Query understanding result (optional)
1581:             linked_entity_ids: Entity IDs from entity linking
1582:             temporal_filter: Optional temporal filter
1583:             metrics: SearchMetrics for tracking
1584:             graph_info: GraphTraversalInfo for tracking
1585: 
1586:         Returns:
1587:             Tuple of (chunks, entities, graph_context, search_contributions)
1588:         """
1589:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector as _get_collector
1590: 
1591:         search_contributions = SearchMethodContribution()
1592:         graph_context: dict[str, Any] = {}
1593: 
1594:         # Stage 1: Broad Recall
1595:         metrics.stage1_recall_timer.start()
1596:         stage1_chunks, stage1_entities, stage1_graph_context, stage1_contributions = await self._stage1_recall(
1597:             query_text=query_text,
1598:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1599:             query_embedding=query_embedding,
1600:             config=config,
1601:             understanding=understanding,
1602:             linked_entity_ids=linked_entity_ids,
1603:             graph_info=graph_info,
1604:         )
1605:         metrics.stage1_recall_timer.stop()
1606:         metrics.stage1_candidate_count = sum(len(v) for v in stage1_chunks.values())
1607:         graph_context.update(stage1_graph_context)
1608:         search_contributions = stage1_contributions
1609: 
1610:         _get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
1611:             pipeline="query",
1612:             stage="multi_stage_1_recall",
1613:             latency_ms=metrics.stage1_recall_timer.elapsed_ms,
1614:             output_count=metrics.stage1_candidate_count,
1615:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1616:         )
1617: 
1618:         logger.debug(
1619:             f"Stage 1 (Recall): {metrics.stage1_candidate_count} chunks, "
1620:             f"{sum(len(v) for v in stage1_entities.values())} entities"
1621:         )
1622: 
1623:         if not stage1_chunks:
1624:             fused_entities = []
1625:             if stage1_entities:
1626:                 weights = {"vector": config.vector_weight, "graph": config.graph_weight}
1627:                 fused_entities = reciprocal_rank_fusion(
1628:                     stage1_entities,
1629:                     k=config.rrf_k,
1630:                     weights=weights,
1631:                     id_extractor=lambda e: str(e.id),
1632:                 )
1633:             return [], fused_entities, graph_context, search_contributions
1634: 
1635:         # Stage 2: Score Normalization & Fusion
1636:         metrics.stage2_normalize_timer.start()
1637:         stage2_chunks, stage2_entities = self._stage2_normalize_fuse(
1638:             stage1_chunks=stage1_chunks,
1639:             stage1_entities=stage1_entities,
1640:             config=config,
1641:         )
1642:         metrics.stage2_normalize_timer.stop()
1643:         metrics.stage2_normalized_count = len(stage2_chunks)
1644: 
1645:         _get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
1646:             pipeline="query",
1647:             stage="multi_stage_2_normalize",
1648:             latency_ms=metrics.stage2_normalize_timer.elapsed_ms,
1649:             input_count=metrics.stage1_candidate_count,
1650:             output_count=len(stage2_chunks),
1651:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1652:         )
1653: 
1654:         logger.debug(f"Stage 2 (Normalize/Fuse): {len(stage2_chunks)} chunks")
1655: 
1656:         # Stage 3: Lightweight Filtering
1657:         metrics.stage3_filter_timer.start()
1658:         stage3_chunks, stage3_entities = self._stage3_filter(
1659:             chunks=stage2_chunks,
1660:             entities=stage2_entities,
1661:             config=config,
1662:             understanding=understanding,
1663:             temporal_filter=temporal_filter,
1664:         )
1665:         metrics.stage3_filter_timer.stop()
1666:         metrics.stage3_filtered_count = len(stage3_chunks)
1667: 
1668:         _get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
1669:             pipeline="query",
1670:             stage="multi_stage_3_filter",
1671:             latency_ms=metrics.stage3_filter_timer.elapsed_ms,
1672:             input_count=len(stage2_chunks),
1673:             output_count=len(stage3_chunks),
1674:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1675:         )
1676: 
1677:         logger.debug(f"Stage 3 (Filter): {len(stage3_chunks)} chunks")
1678: 
1679:         if not stage3_chunks:
1680:             return [], stage3_entities, graph_context, search_contributions
1681: 
1682:         # Stage 4: Neural Reranking
1683:         metrics.stage4_rerank_timer.start()
1684:         stage4_chunks = await self._stage4_rerank(
1685:             chunks=stage3_chunks,
1686:             query_text=query_text,
1687:             config=config,
1688:         )
1689:         metrics.stage4_rerank_timer.stop()
1690:         metrics.stage4_reranked_count = len(stage4_chunks)
1691: 
1692:         _get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
1693:             pipeline="query",
1694:             stage="multi_stage_4_rerank",
1695:             latency_ms=metrics.stage4_rerank_timer.elapsed_ms,
1696:             input_count=len(stage3_chunks),
1697:             output_count=len(stage4_chunks),
1698:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1699:             metadata={"method": config.reranking_method if config.enable_reranking else "none"},
1700:         )
1701: 
1702:         logger.debug(f"Stage 4 (Rerank): {len(stage4_chunks)} chunks")
1703: 
1704:         # Stage 5: Diversity & Final Selection
1705:         metrics.stage5_diversity_timer.start()
1706:         stage5_chunks, stage5_entities = self._stage5_diversity(
1707:             chunks=stage4_chunks,
1708:             entities=stage3_entities,
1709:             query_embedding=query_embedding,
1710:             config=config,
1711:         )
1712:         metrics.stage5_diversity_timer.stop()
1713:         metrics.stage5_final_count = len(stage5_chunks)
1714: 
1715:         _get_collector().record_pipeline_stage(
1716:             pipeline="query",
1717:             stage="multi_stage_5_diversity",
1718:             latency_ms=metrics.stage5_diversity_timer.elapsed_ms,
1719:             input_count=len(stage4_chunks),
1720:             output_count=len(stage5_chunks),
1721:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
1722:             metadata={"diversity_enabled": config.enable_diversity},
1723:         )
1724: 
1725:         logger.debug(f"Stage 5 (Diversity/Final): {len(stage5_chunks)} chunks")
1726: 
1727:         return stage5_chunks, stage5_entities, graph_context, search_contributions
1728: 
1729:     async def _stage1_recall(
1730:         self,
1731:         query_text: str,
1732:         namespace_id: UUID,
1733:         query_embedding: list[float] | None,
1734:         config: QueryConfig,
1735:         understanding: Any | None,
1736:         linked_entity_ids: list[UUID],
1737:         graph_info: GraphTraversalInfo,
1738:     ) -> tuple[
1739:         dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]],
1740:         dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]],
1741:         dict[str, Any],
1742:         SearchMethodContribution,
1743:     ]:
1744:         """Stage 1: Broad recall from all search sources.
1745: 
1746:         Retrieves a large candidate set (100-200) using fast search methods.
1747:         Uses higher limits than final output to ensure we don't miss relevant results.
1748: 
1749:         Returns:
1750:             Tuple of (chunk_results_by_source, entity_results_by_source, graph_context, contributions)
1751:         """
1752:         search_contributions = SearchMethodContribution()
1753:         all_chunk_results: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]] = {}
1754:         all_entity_results: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]] = {}
1755:         graph_context: dict[str, Any] = {}
1756: 
1757:         # Calculate per-source limits based on stage1_recall_limit
1758:         # Distribute recall limit across sources with vector getting the most
1759:         recall_limit = config.stage1_recall_limit
1760:         vector_limit = int(recall_limit * 0.5)  # 50% from vector
1761:         graph_limit = int(recall_limit * 0.3)  # 30% from graph
1762:         keyword_limit = int(recall_limit * 0.3)  # 30% from keyword (overlap allowed)
1763: 
1764:         # Determine queries to search (original + expansions)
1765:         queries_to_search = [query_text]
1766:         if understanding and config.enable_query_expansion:
1767:             queries_to_search.extend(understanding.expanded_queries[:2])
1768: 
1769:         search_start_time = time.perf_counter()
1770: 
1771:         for i, q in enumerate(queries_to_search):
1772:             suffix = "" if i == 0 else f"_exp{i}"
1773: 
1774:             # Get query embedding for this query variant
1775:             current_embedding = query_embedding if i == 0 else None
1776:             if self._embedder and i > 0 and config.mode in (SearchMode.VECTOR, SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL):
1777:                 current_embedding = await self._embedder.embed(q)
1778: 
1779:             # Execute searches in parallel
1780:             tasks = []
1781:             task_types = []
1782: 
1783:             if config.mode in (SearchMode.VECTOR, SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL) and current_embedding is not None:
1784:                 # Create a temporary config with higher limits for broad recall
1785:                 recall_cfg = QueryConfig(
1786:                     max_chunks=vector_limit,
1787:                     max_entities=config.max_entities * 2,
1788:                     min_chunk_similarity=config.min_chunk_similarity * 0.5,  # Lower threshold for recall
1789:                     min_entity_similarity=config.min_entity_similarity * 0.5,
1790:                 )
1791:                 tasks.append(
1792:                     self._timed_search(self._vector_search(namespace_id, current_embedding, recall_cfg), "vector")
1793:                 )
1794:                 task_types.append("vector")
1795: 
1796:             if config.mode in (SearchMode.GRAPH, SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL):
1797:                 recall_cfg = QueryConfig(
1798:                     max_chunks=graph_limit,
1799:                     max_entities=config.max_entities * 2,
1800:                     max_graph_depth=config.max_graph_depth,
1801:                     min_chunk_similarity=config.min_chunk_similarity * 0.5,
1802:                     min_entity_similarity=config.min_entity_similarity * 0.5,
1803:                 )
1804:                 tasks.append(
1805:                     self._timed_search(
1806:                         self._graph_search(namespace_id, q, current_embedding, recall_cfg, linked_entity_ids),
1807:                         "graph",
1808:                     )
1809:                 )
1810:                 task_types.append("graph")
1811: 
1812:             if config.mode in (SearchMode.HYBRID, SearchMode.ALL) and config.enable_keyword_search:
1813:                 keywords = understanding.keywords if understanding else None
1814:                 if config.keyword_search_method == "fulltext":
1815:                     # Create config with higher limit for recall
1816:                     recall_cfg = QueryConfig(max_chunks=keyword_limit)
1817:                     tasks.append(
1818:                         self._timed_search(self._keyword_search_fulltext(namespace_id, q, recall_cfg), "keyword")
1819:                     )
1820:                 else:
1821:                     recall_cfg = QueryConfig(max_chunks=keyword_limit)
1822:                     tasks.append(
1823:                         self._timed_search(self._keyword_search_bm25(namespace_id, q, recall_cfg, keywords), "keyword")
1824:                     )
1825:                 task_types.append("keyword")
1826: 
1827:             # Execute in parallel
1828:             results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
1829: 
1830:             # Process results
1831:             for j, result in enumerate(results):
1832:                 if isinstance(result, Exception):
1833:                     logger.error(f"Stage 1 search {j} failed: {result}")
1834:                     continue
1835: 
1836:                 if isinstance(result, dict):
1837:                     source_type = result.get("source", f"search_{j}")
1838:                     latency_ms = result.get("latency_ms", 0.0)
1839: 
1840:                     if "chunks" in result:
1841:                         source = source_type + suffix
1842:                         all_chunk_results[source] = result["chunks"]
1843: 
1844:                         chunk_ids = [str(c.id) for c, _ in result["chunks"]]
1845:                         scores = [s for _, s in result["chunks"]]
1846: 
1847:                         if source_type == "vector":
1848:                             search_contributions.vector.chunk_count += len(result["chunks"])
1849:                             search_contributions.vector.chunk_ids.extend(chunk_ids)
1850:                             search_contributions.vector.latency_ms = latency_ms
1851:                             if scores:
1852:                                 search_contributions.vector.min_score = min(scores)
1853:                                 search_contributions.vector.max_score = max(scores)
1854:                                 search_contributions.vector.avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)
1855:                         elif source_type == "graph":
1856:                             search_contributions.graph.chunk_count += len(result["chunks"])
1857:                             search_contributions.graph.chunk_ids.extend(chunk_ids)
1858:                             search_contributions.graph.latency_ms = latency_ms
1859:                             if scores:
1860:                                 search_contributions.graph.min_score = min(scores)
1861:                                 search_contributions.graph.max_score = max(scores)
1862:                                 search_contributions.graph.avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)
1863:                         elif source_type == "keyword":
1864:                             search_contributions.keyword.chunk_count += len(result["chunks"])
1865:                             search_contributions.keyword.chunk_ids.extend(chunk_ids)
1866:                             search_contributions.keyword.latency_ms = latency_ms
1867:                             if scores:
1868:                                 search_contributions.keyword.min_score = min(scores)
1869:                                 search_contributions.keyword.max_score = max(scores)
1870:                                 search_contributions.keyword.avg_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)
1871: 
1872:                     if "entities" in result:
1873:                         source = source_type + suffix
1874:                         all_entity_results[source] = result["entities"]
1875: 
1876:                         entity_ids = [str(e.id) for e, _ in result["entities"]]
1877:                         if source_type == "vector":
1878:                             search_contributions.vector.entity_count += len(result["entities"])
1879:                             search_contributions.vector.entity_ids.extend(entity_ids)
1880:                         elif source_type == "graph":
1881:                             search_contributions.graph.entity_count += len(result["entities"])
1882:                             search_contributions.graph.entity_ids.extend(entity_ids)
1883:                             for entity, _ in result["entities"]:
1884:                                 graph_info.entities_searched.append(entity.name)
1885: 
1886:                     if "graph_context" in result:
1887:                         graph_context.update(result["graph_context"])
1888:                         if "relationships" in result.get("graph_context", {}):
1889:                             for rel in result["graph_context"]["relationships"]:
1890:                                 if isinstance(rel, dict):
1891:                                     graph_info.relationships_traversed.append(
1892:                                         (rel.get("from", ""), rel.get("type", ""), rel.get("to", ""))
1893:                                     )
1894: 
1895:         search_contributions.total_search_latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - search_start_time) * 1000
1896: 
1897:         return all_chunk_results, all_entity_results, graph_context, search_contributions
1898: 
1899:     def _stage2_normalize_fuse(
1900:         self,
1901:         stage1_chunks: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]],
1902:         stage1_entities: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]],
1903:         config: QueryConfig,
1904:     ) -> tuple[list[tuple[Any, float]], list[tuple[Any, float]]]:
1905:         """Stage 2: Score normalization and fusion.
1906: 
1907:         Normalizes all scores to [0,1] range and applies weighted RRF fusion.
1908:         This ensures fair comparison across different search methods.
1909: 
1910:         Returns:
1911:             Tuple of (fused_chunks, fused_entities)
1912:         """
1913:         # Normalize scores within each source to [0,1]
1914:         normalized_chunks: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]] = {}
1915:         for source, results in stage1_chunks.items():
1916:             if not results:
1917:                 continue
1918:             scores = [s for _, s in results]
1919:             max_score = max(scores) if scores else 1.0
1920:             min_score = min(scores) if scores else 0.0
1921:             score_range = max_score - min_score
1922: 
1923:             if score_range > 0:
1924:                 normalized_chunks[source] = [(chunk, (score - min_score) / score_range) for chunk, score in results]
1925:             else:
1926:                 # All same score - use 0.5
1927:                 normalized_chunks[source] = [(chunk, 0.5) for chunk, _ in results]
1928: 
1929:         normalized_entities: dict[str, list[tuple[Any, float]]] = {}
1930:         for source, results in stage1_entities.items():
1931:             if not results:
1932:                 continue
1933:             scores = [s for _, s in results]
1934:             max_score = max(scores) if scores else 1.0
1935:             min_score = min(scores) if scores else 0.0
1936:             score_range = max_score - min_score
1937: 
1938:             if score_range > 0:
1939:                 normalized_entities[source] = [(entity, (score - min_score) / score_range) for entity, score in results]
1940:             else:
1941:                 normalized_entities[source] = [(entity, 0.5) for entity, _ in results]
1942: 
1943:         # Apply weighted RRF fusion
1944:         fused_chunks = []
1945:         if normalized_chunks:
1946:             weights = {
1947:                 "vector": config.vector_weight,
1948:                 "graph": config.graph_weight,
1949:                 "keyword": config.keyword_weight,
1950:             }
1951:             # Add weights for expanded query results
1952:             for key in normalized_chunks:
1953:                 if "_exp" in key:
1954:                     base_source = key.split("_exp")[0]
1955:                     weights[key] = weights.get(base_source, config.vector_weight) * 0.7
1956: 
1957:             fused_chunks = reciprocal_rank_fusion(
1958:                 normalized_chunks,
1959:                 k=config.rrf_k,
1960:                 weights=weights,
1961:                 id_extractor=lambda c: str(c.id),
1962:             )
1963: 
1964:         fused_entities = []
1965:         if normalized_entities:
1966:             weights = {
1967:                 "vector": config.vector_weight,
1968:                 "graph": config.graph_weight,
1969:             }
1970:             fused_entities = reciprocal_rank_fusion(
1971:                 normalized_entities,
1972:                 k=config.rrf_k,
1973:                 weights=weights,
1974:                 id_extractor=lambda e: str(e.id),
1975:             )
1976: 
1977:         return fused_chunks, fused_entities
1978: 
1979:     def _stage3_filter(
1980:         self,
1981:         chunks: list[tuple[Any, float]],
1982:         entities: list[tuple[Any, float]],
1983:         config: QueryConfig,
1984:         understanding: Any | None,
1985:         temporal_filter: TemporalFilter | None,
1986:     ) -> tuple[list[tuple[Any, float]], list[tuple[Any, float]]]:
1987:         """Stage 3: Lightweight filtering.
1988: 
1989:         Applies temporal filters and source priority boosting to reduce
1990:         the candidate set before expensive reranking.
1991: 
1992:         Returns:
1993:             Tuple of (filtered_chunks, filtered_entities)
1994:         """
1995:         filtered_chunks = chunks
1996: 
1997:         # Apply temporal filter
1998:         if temporal_filter:
1999:             filtered_chunks = [(c, s) for c, s in filtered_chunks if temporal_filter.matches(c.created_at)]
2000: 
2001:         # Apply recency bias
2002:         if config.apply_recency_bias:
2003:             temporal_query = TemporalQuery(query="").with_recency_bias(
2004:                 config.recency_weight,
2005:                 config.recency_decay_days,
2006:             )
2007:             filtered_chunks = [
2008:                 (c, s * temporal_query.calculate_recency_score(c.created_at)) for c, s in filtered_chunks
2009:             ]
2010:             filtered_chunks.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
2011: 
2012:         # Apply source priority boosting
2013:         if understanding and understanding.source_priority:
2014:             filtered_chunks = self._apply_source_priority(filtered_chunks, understanding)
2015:             entities = self._apply_source_priority_entities(entities, understanding)
2016: 
2017:         # Apply attribute-aware scoring boost for entities
2018:         if understanding and understanding.keywords and entities:
2019:             entities = [
2020:                 (entity, score + self._attribute_relevance_boost(entity, understanding.keywords))
2021:                 for entity, score in entities
2022:             ]
2023:             entities.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
2024: 
2025:         # Limit to stage3_filter_limit
2026:         filtered_chunks = filtered_chunks[: config.stage3_filter_limit]
2027:         entities = entities[: config.max_entities]
2028: 
2029:         return filtered_chunks, entities
2030: 
2031:     async def _stage4_rerank(
2032:         self,
2033:         chunks: list[tuple[Any, float]],
2034:         query_text: str,
2035:         config: QueryConfig,
2036:     ) -> list[tuple[Any, float]]:
2037:         """Stage 4: Neural reranking.
2038: 
2039:         Applies cross-encoder or LLM-based reranking to the filtered candidates.
2040:         This is the expensive step, only applied to top candidates from Stage 3.
2041: 
2042:         Returns:
2043:             Reranked chunks with updated scores
2044:         """
2045:         if not config.enable_reranking or len(chunks) < 3:
2046:             return chunks
2047: 
2048:         try:
2049:             # Get or create reranker
2050:             if config.reranking_method not in self._rerankers:
2051:                 self._rerankers[config.reranking_method] = create_reranker(
2052:                     method=config.reranking_method,
2053:                     llm_config=self._llm_config,
2054:                 )
2055:             reranker = self._rerankers[config.reranking_method]
2056: 
2057:             # Limit candidates to stage4_rerank_limit
2058:             candidates_to_rerank = chunks[: config.stage4_rerank_limit]
2059: 
2060:             candidates = [
2061:                 RerankCandidate(
2062:                     item=chunk,
2063:                     original_score=score,
2064:                     content=chunk.content,
2065:                     metadata=chunk.metadata,
2066:                 )
2067:                 for chunk, score in candidates_to_rerank
2068:             ]
2069: 
2070:             # Rerank - use max_chunks as final limit
2071:             reranked = await reranker.rerank(
2072:                 query_text,
2073:                 candidates,
2074:                 top_k=config.max_chunks * 2,  # Keep extra for diversity stage
2075:             )
2076: 
2077:             return [(r.item, r.final_score) for r in reranked]
2078: 
2079:         except Exception as e:
2080:             logger.warning(f"Stage 4 reranking failed: {e}")
2081:             return chunks
2082: 
2083:     def _stage5_diversity(
2084:         self,
2085:         chunks: list[tuple[Any, float]],
2086:         entities: list[tuple[Any, float]],
2087:         query_embedding: list[float] | None,
2088:         config: QueryConfig,
2089:     ) -> tuple[list[tuple[Any, float]], list[tuple[Any, float]]]:
2090:         """Stage 5: Diversity selection and final limiting.
2091: 
2092:         Optionally applies MMR-style diversity to ensure varied results.
2093:         Then applies final limit to return requested number of results.
2094: 
2095:         Returns:
2096:             Tuple of (final_chunks, final_entities)
2097:         """
2098:         final_chunks = chunks
2099: 
2100:         if config.enable_diversity and query_embedding is not None and len(chunks) > config.max_chunks:
2101:             # Apply MMR-style diversity selection
2102:             final_chunks = self._mmr_diversity_select(
2103:                 chunks=chunks,
2104:                 query_embedding=query_embedding,
2105:                 k=config.max_chunks,
2106:                 lambda_param=config.diversity_lambda,
2107:             )
2108:         else:
2109:             # Just limit to max_chunks
2110:             final_chunks = chunks[: config.max_chunks]
2111: 
2112:         final_entities = entities[: config.max_entities]
2113: 
2114:         return final_chunks, final_entities
2115: 
2116:     def _mmr_diversity_select(
2117:         self,
2118:         chunks: list[tuple[Any, float]],
2119:         query_embedding: list[float],
2120:         k: int,
2121:         lambda_param: float = 0.5,
2122:     ) -> list[tuple[Any, float]]:
2123:         """Maximal Marginal Relevance selection for diversity.
2124: 
2125:         Balances relevance to query with diversity among selected results.
2126: 
2127:         Args:
2128:             chunks: Candidate chunks with scores
2129:             query_embedding: Query embedding for relevance
2130:             k: Number of results to select
2131:             lambda_param: Tradeoff between relevance (1.0) and diversity (0.0)
2132: 
2133:         Returns:
2134:             Selected chunks with adjusted scores
2135:         """
2136:         import numpy as np
2137: 
2138:         if len(chunks) <= k:
2139:             return chunks
2140: 
2141:         # Extract embeddings from chunks (if available)
2142:         chunk_embeddings: list[list[float] | None] = []
2143:         for chunk, _ in chunks:
2144:             embedding = getattr(chunk, "embedding", None)
2145:             if embedding is None and hasattr(chunk, "metadata"):
2146:                 embedding = getattr(chunk.metadata, "embedding", None)
2147:             chunk_embeddings.append(embedding)
2148: 
2149:         # If no embeddings available, fall back to score-based selection
2150:         if all(e is None for e in chunk_embeddings):
2151:             return chunks[:k]
2152: 
2153:         # Convert to numpy arrays
2154:         query_vec = np.array(query_embedding)
2155:         selected: list[tuple[Any, float]] = []
2156:         selected_indices: set[int] = set()
2157:         remaining_indices = set(range(len(chunks)))
2158: 
2159:         def cosine_similarity(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray) -> float:
2160:             """Compute cosine similarity between two vectors."""
2161:             norm_a = np.linalg.norm(a)
2162:             norm_b = np.linalg.norm(b)
2163:             if norm_a == 0 or norm_b == 0:
2164:                 return 0.0
2165:             return float(np.dot(a, b) / (norm_a * norm_b))
2166: 
2167:         while len(selected) < k and remaining_indices:
2168:             best_idx = -1
2169:             best_mmr_score = float("-inf")
2170: 
2171:             for idx in remaining_indices:
2172:                 chunk, relevance_score = chunks[idx]
2173:                 embedding = chunk_embeddings[idx]
2174: 
2175:                 if embedding is None:
2176:                     # Use original score as relevance
2177:                     query_sim = relevance_score
2178:                 else:
2179:                     query_sim = cosine_similarity(query_vec, np.array(embedding))
2180: 
2181:                 # Calculate max similarity to already selected
2182:                 max_sim_to_selected = 0.0
2183:                 if selected_indices:
2184:                     for sel_idx in selected_indices:
2185:                         sel_embedding = chunk_embeddings[sel_idx]
2186:                         if embedding is not None and sel_embedding is not None:
2187:                             sim = cosine_similarity(np.array(embedding), np.array(sel_embedding))
2188:                             max_sim_to_selected = max(max_sim_to_selected, sim)
2189: 
2190:                 # MMR score: lambda * relevance - (1 - lambda) * max_similarity
2191:                 mmr_score = lambda_param * query_sim - (1 - lambda_param) * max_sim_to_selected
2192: 
2193:                 if mmr_score > best_mmr_score:
2194:                     best_mmr_score = mmr_score
2195:                     best_idx = idx
2196: 
2197:             if best_idx >= 0:
2198:                 selected.append(chunks[best_idx])
2199:                 selected_indices.add(best_idx)
2200:                 remaining_indices.remove(best_idx)
2201:             else:
2202:                 break
2203: 
2204:         return selected
2205: 
2206:     async def find_related_entities(
2207:         self,
2208:         entity_id: UUID,
2209:         namespace_id: UUID,
2210:         *,
2211:         max_depth: int = 2,
2212:         limit: int = 20,
2213:     ) -> list[tuple[Entity, float]]:
2214:         """Find entities related to a given entity through the graph.
2215: 
2216:         Uses batch entity fetching to avoid N+1 queries for better performance.
2217: 
2218:         Args:
2219:             entity_id: Starting entity
2220:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
2221:             max_depth: Maximum relationship depth
2222:             limit: Maximum entities to return
2223: 
2224:         Returns:
2225:             List of (entity, relevance_score) tuples
2226:         """
2227:         neighborhood = await self._storage.get_neighborhood(
2228:             entity_id,
2229:             depth=max_depth,
2230:             limit=limit,
2231:         )
2232: 
2233:         entity_nodes = neighborhood.get("entities", [])
2234:         if not entity_nodes:
2235:             return []
2236: 
2237:         # Collect all entity IDs for batch fetch
2238:         entity_ids = [UUID(node["id"]) for node in entity_nodes]
2239: 
2240:         # Batch fetch all entities in a single query (avoids N+1)
2241:         entities_map = await self._storage.get_entities_batch(entity_ids)
2242: 
2243:         # Score based on path length (shorter = higher score)
2244:         # This is simplified - full impl would consider actual path lengths
2245:         base_score = 1.0 / (1 + len(neighborhood.get("relationships", [])))
2246: 
2247:         entities = []
2248:         for node in entity_nodes:
2249:             eid = UUID(node["id"])
2250:             if eid in entities_map:
2251:                 entities.append((entities_map[eid], base_score))
2252: 
2253:         return entities
2254: 
2255:     async def temporal_query(
2256:         self,
2257:         query: TemporalQuery,
2258:         namespace_id: UUID,
2259:         *,
2260:         config: QueryConfig | None = None,
2261:     ) -> QueryResult:
2262:         """Execute a query with temporal context.
2263: 
2264:         Args:
2265:             query: TemporalQuery with filters and settings
2266:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
2267:             config: Optional query config override
2268: 
2269:         Returns:
2270:             QueryResult with temporal filtering applied
2271:         """
2272:         cfg = config or QueryConfig()
2273: 
2274:         # Apply temporal settings to config
2275:         if query.recency_weight > 0:
2276:             cfg.apply_recency_bias = True
2277:             cfg.recency_weight = query.recency_weight
2278:             cfg.recency_decay_days = query.decay_days
2279: 
2280:         # Get context filter
2281:         temporal_filter = None
2282:         if query.filters:
2283:             temporal_filter = query.filters[0]  # Use first filter for now
2284:         elif query.context_window_days:
2285:             temporal_filter = query.get_context_filter()
2286: 
2287:         return await self.query(
2288:             query.query,
2289:             namespace_id,
2290:             config=cfg,
2291:             temporal_filter=temporal_filter,
2292:         )
2293: 
2294:     async def warm_cache(
2295:         self,
2296:         namespace_id: UUID,
2297:         queries: list[str] | None = None,
2298:         *,
2299:         config: QueryConfig | None = None,
2300:         include_entity_based: bool = True,
2301:         max_entity_queries: int = 20,
2302:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2303:         """Pre-warm the query cache with common query patterns.
2304: 
2305:         This is useful for reducing latency on frequently-used queries or
2306:         for pre-loading cache after namespace updates.
2307: 
2308:         Args:
2309:             namespace_id: Namespace to warm cache for
2310:             queries: Explicit list of queries to execute
2311:             config: Optional query config
2312:             include_entity_based: Generate queries based on top entities
2313:             max_entity_queries: Max entity-based queries to generate
2314: 
2315:         Returns:
2316:             Summary of cache warming results
2317:         """
2318:         cfg = config or self._config
2319:         warmed_queries: list[str] = []
2320:         errors: list[str] = []
2321: 
2322:         # Collect all queries to warm
2323:         all_queries = list(queries or [])
2324: 
2325:         # Generate entity-based queries if requested
2326:         if include_entity_based:
2327:             try:
2328:                 # Get top entities by mention count
2329:                 entities = await self._storage.list_entities(
2330:                     namespace_id,
2331:                     limit=max_entity_queries * 2,
2332:                 )
2333:                 # Sort by mention count and take top N
2334:                 entities.sort(key=lambda e: e.mention_count, reverse=True)
2335:                 for entity in entities[:max_entity_queries]:
2336:                     # Generate natural queries about entities
2337:                     all_queries.append(entity.name)
2338:                     if entity.description:
2339:                         all_queries.append(f"What is {entity.name}?")
2340:             except Exception as e:
2341:                 logger.warning(f"Failed to fetch entities for cache warming: {e}")
2342: 
2343:         # Execute queries to populate cache
2344:         for query_text in all_queries:
2345:             try:
2346:                 # Execute query with understanding disabled for speed
2347:                 await self.query(
2348:                     query_text,
2349:                     namespace_id,
2350:                     config=QueryConfig(
2351:                         mode=cfg.mode,
2352:                         max_chunks=cfg.max_chunks,
2353:                         max_entities=cfg.max_entities,
2354:                         enable_query_understanding=False,
2355:                         enable_reranking=False,
2356:                     ),
2357:                 )
2358:                 warmed_queries.append(query_text)
2359:             except Exception as e:
2360:                 errors.append(f"{query_text}: {e}")
2361:                 logger.debug(f"Cache warming failed for '{query_text}': {e}")
2362: 
2363:         logger.info(f"Cache warming complete: {len(warmed_queries)} queries cached, {len(errors)} errors")
2364: 
2365:         return {
2366:             "namespace_id": str(namespace_id),
2367:             "queries_warmed": len(warmed_queries),
2368:             "errors": len(errors),
2369:             "cache_stats": self._cache.stats,
2370:         }
2371: 
2372:     async def warm_keyword_index(
2373:         self,
2374:         namespace_id: UUID,
2375:         max_chunks: int = 10000,
2376:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2377:         """Pre-build the BM25 keyword index for a namespace.
2378: 
2379:         This is useful for reducing first-query latency when keyword
2380:         search is enabled.
2381: 
2382:         Args:
2383:             namespace_id: Namespace to build index for
2384:             max_chunks: Maximum chunks to index
2385: 
2386:         Returns:
2387:             Summary of indexing results
2388:         """
2389:         ns_key = str(namespace_id)
2390: 
2391:         if ns_key in self._keyword_searchers:
2392:             return {
2393:                 "namespace_id": ns_key,
2394:                 "status": "already_indexed",
2395:                 "chunk_count": (
2396:                     len(self._keyword_searchers[ns_key]._chunks)
2397:                     if hasattr(self._keyword_searchers[ns_key], "_chunks")
2398:                     else 0
2399:                 ),
2400:             }
2401: 
2402:         try:
2403:             chunks = await self._storage.list_chunks(namespace_id, limit=max_chunks)
2404:             if chunks:
2405:                 searcher = KeywordSearcher(use_stemming=True, remove_stopwords=True)
2406:                 searcher.index_chunks(chunks)
2407:                 self._keyword_searchers[ns_key] = searcher
2408:                 logger.info(f"Pre-built BM25 index with {len(chunks)} chunks for {namespace_id}")
2409:                 return {
2410:                     "namespace_id": ns_key,
2411:                     "status": "indexed",
2412:                     "chunk_count": len(chunks),
2413:                 }
2414:             return {
2415:                 "namespace_id": ns_key,
2416:                 "status": "no_chunks",
2417:                 "chunk_count": 0,
2418:             }
2419:         except Exception as e:
2420:             logger.warning(f"Failed to pre-build keyword index: {e}")
2421:             return {
2422:                 "namespace_id": ns_key,
2423:                 "status": "error",
2424:                 "error": str(e),
2425:             }
````

## File: src/khora/storage/backends/neo4j.py
````python
   1: """Neo4j backend for knowledge graph storage.
   2: 
   3: Handles storage and traversal of entities, relationships, and episodes
   4: in Neo4j graph database.
   5: """
   6: 
   7: from __future__ import annotations
   8: 
   9: import re as _re
  10: from datetime import datetime
  11: from typing import Any
  12: from uuid import UUID
  13: 
  14: from loguru import logger
  15: from neo4j import AsyncDriver, AsyncGraphDatabase, AsyncManagedTransaction
  16: 
  17: from khora.core.models import Entity, Episode, Relationship
  18: from khora.core.models.entity import EntityType, RelationshipType
  19: from khora.storage.backends.mixins import (
  20:     GraphBackendBase,
  21: )
  22: from khora.storage.backends.mixins import deserialize_dict as _deserialize_dict
  23: from khora.storage.backends.mixins import element_to_dict as _element_to_dict
  24: from khora.storage.backends.mixins import serialize_dict as _serialize_dict
  25: 
  26: # Neo4j relationship labels must be valid identifiers: letters, digits, underscores.
  27: # LLM-generated types like "at-risk" or "works for" need sanitizing.
  28: _NEO4J_LABEL_RE = _re.compile(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_]")
  29: 
  30: 
  31: def _sanitize_neo4j_label(label: str) -> str:
  32:     """Sanitize a string for use as a Neo4j relationship type label."""
  33:     sanitized = _NEO4J_LABEL_RE.sub("_", label.strip())
  34:     return sanitized.upper() if sanitized else "RELATES_TO"
  35: 
  36: 
  37: class Neo4jBackend(GraphBackendBase):
  38:     """Neo4j backend for knowledge graph operations.
  39: 
  40:     Stores entities as nodes and relationships as edges in Neo4j,
  41:     enabling efficient graph traversal and pattern matching.
  42:     """
  43: 
  44:     def __init__(
  45:         self,
  46:         url: str,
  47:         *,
  48:         user: str = "neo4j",
  49:         password: str = "",
  50:         database: str = "neo4j",
  51:         max_connection_pool_size: int = 50,
  52:     ) -> None:
  53:         """Initialize the Neo4j backend.
  54: 
  55:         Args:
  56:             url: Neo4j connection URL (bolt:// or neo4j://)
  57:             user: Database user
  58:             password: Database password
  59:             database: Database name
  60:             max_connection_pool_size: Maximum connection pool size
  61:         """
  62:         self._url = url
  63:         self._user = user
  64:         self._password = password
  65:         self._database = database
  66:         self._max_connection_pool_size = max_connection_pool_size
  67:         self._driver: AsyncDriver | None = None
  68: 
  69:     @classmethod
  70:     def from_config(cls, config: Any) -> Neo4jBackend:
  71:         """Create a Neo4jBackend from a Neo4jConfig object."""
  72:         return cls(
  73:             url=config.url or "",
  74:             user=config.user,
  75:             password=config.password,
  76:             database=config.database,
  77:         )
  78: 
  79:     async def connect(self) -> None:
  80:         """Establish connection to Neo4j."""
  81:         if self._driver is not None:
  82:             return
  83: 
  84:         logger.info(f"Connecting to Neo4j at {self._url}...")
  85:         self._driver = AsyncGraphDatabase.driver(
  86:             self._url,
  87:             auth=(self._user, self._password),
  88:             max_connection_pool_size=self._max_connection_pool_size,
  89:         )
  90:         # Verify connectivity
  91:         await self._driver.verify_connectivity()
  92: 
  93:         # Create indexes for performance
  94:         await self._create_indexes()
  95:         logger.info("Connected to Neo4j")
  96: 
  97:     async def disconnect(self) -> None:
  98:         """Close Neo4j connections."""
  99:         if self._driver is not None:
 100:             logger.info("Disconnecting from Neo4j...")
 101:             await self._driver.close()
 102:             self._driver = None
 103:             logger.info("Disconnected from Neo4j")
 104: 
 105:     async def is_healthy(self) -> bool:
 106:         """Check if the backend is healthy and connected."""
 107:         if self._driver is None:
 108:             return False
 109:         try:
 110:             await self._driver.verify_connectivity()
 111:             return True
 112:         except Exception as e:
 113:             logger.error(f"Neo4j health check failed: {e}")
 114:             return False
 115: 
 116:     async def _create_indexes(self) -> None:
 117:         """Create indexes for common queries."""
 118:         if self._driver is None:
 119:             return
 120: 
 121:         indexes = [
 122:             # Entity indexes
 123:             "CREATE INDEX entity_id IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.id)",
 124:             "CREATE INDEX entity_namespace IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.namespace_id)",
 125:             "CREATE INDEX entity_name IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.name)",
 126:             "CREATE INDEX entity_type IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.entity_type)",
 127:             # Composite index for MERGE pattern (namespace_id, name, entity_type)
 128:             "CREATE INDEX entity_ns_name_type IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.namespace_id, e.name, e.entity_type)",
 129:             # Composite: namespace + type (for list queries filtering by type without name)
 130:             "CREATE INDEX entity_ns_type IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.namespace_id, e.entity_type)",
 131:             # Entity source_tool (for source-aware queries)
 132:             "CREATE INDEX entity_source_tool IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.source_tool)",
 133:             # Entity confidence (for threshold filtering: min_entity_confidence)
 134:             "CREATE INDEX entity_confidence IF NOT EXISTS FOR (e:Entity) ON (e.confidence)",
 135:             # Episode indexes
 136:             "CREATE INDEX episode_id IF NOT EXISTS FOR (ep:Episode) ON (ep.id)",
 137:             "CREATE INDEX episode_namespace IF NOT EXISTS FOR (ep:Episode) ON (ep.namespace_id)",
 138:             "CREATE INDEX episode_occurred_at IF NOT EXISTS FOR (ep:Episode) ON (ep.occurred_at)",
 139:         ]
 140: 
 141:         # Relationship property indexes require Neo4j ≥5.7 or Enterprise Edition
 142:         rel_indexes = [
 143:             "CREATE INDEX rel_namespace IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:RELATES_TO]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 144:             # namespace_id on high-volume relationship types
 145:             "CREATE INDEX rel_collaborates_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:COLLABORATES_WITH]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 146:             "CREATE INDEX rel_associated_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 147:             "CREATE INDEX rel_depends_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:DEPENDS_ON]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 148:             "CREATE INDEX rel_owns_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:OWNS]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 149:             "CREATE INDEX rel_works_for_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:WORKS_FOR]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 150:             "CREATE INDEX rel_implements_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:IMPLEMENTS]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 151:             "CREATE INDEX rel_part_of_ns IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:PART_OF]-() ON (r.namespace_id)",
 152:             # confidence on highest-volume relationship types
 153:             "CREATE INDEX rel_collaborates_conf IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:COLLABORATES_WITH]-() ON (r.confidence)",
 154:             "CREATE INDEX rel_associated_conf IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-() ON (r.confidence)",
 155:             "CREATE INDEX rel_depends_conf IF NOT EXISTS FOR ()-[r:DEPENDS_ON]-() ON (r.confidence)",
 156:         ]
 157: 
 158:         async with self._driver.session(database=self._database) as session:
 159:             for index in indexes:
 160:                 try:
 161:                     await session.run(index)
 162:                 except Exception as e:
 163:                     logger.debug(f"Index creation: {e}")
 164: 
 165:             for index in rel_indexes:
 166:                 try:
 167:                     await session.run(index)
 168:                 except Exception as e:
 169:                     logger.warning(f"Relationship index creation skipped (may require Neo4j ≥5.7 or Enterprise): {e}")
 170: 
 171:     def _get_driver(self) -> AsyncDriver:
 172:         """Get the Neo4j driver."""
 173:         if self._driver is None:
 174:             raise RuntimeError("Backend not connected. Call connect() first.")
 175:         return self._driver
 176: 
 177:     # =========================================================================
 178:     # Entity operations
 179:     # =========================================================================
 180: 
 181:     async def create_entity(self, entity: Entity) -> Entity:
 182:         """Create an entity node in the graph."""
 183:         driver = self._get_driver()
 184: 
 185:         async def _create(tx: AsyncManagedTransaction) -> None:
 186:             query = """
 187:             CREATE (e:Entity {
 188:                 id: $id,
 189:                 namespace_id: $namespace_id,
 190:                 name: $name,
 191:                 entity_type: $entity_type,
 192:                 description: $description,
 193:                 attributes: $attributes,
 194:                 source_document_ids: $source_document_ids,
 195:                 source_chunk_ids: $source_chunk_ids,
 196:                 mention_count: $mention_count,
 197:                 valid_from: $valid_from,
 198:                 valid_until: $valid_until,
 199:                 confidence: $confidence,
 200:                 metadata: $metadata,
 201:                 created_at: $created_at,
 202:                 updated_at: $updated_at
 203:             })
 204:             """
 205:             await tx.run(
 206:                 query,
 207:                 id=str(entity.id),
 208:                 namespace_id=str(entity.namespace_id),
 209:                 name=entity.name,
 210:                 entity_type=(
 211:                     entity.entity_type.value if isinstance(entity.entity_type, EntityType) else entity.entity_type
 212:                 ),
 213:                 description=entity.description,
 214:                 attributes=_serialize_dict(entity.attributes),
 215:                 source_document_ids=[str(d) for d in entity.source_document_ids],
 216:                 source_chunk_ids=[str(c) for c in entity.source_chunk_ids],
 217:                 mention_count=entity.mention_count,
 218:                 valid_from=entity.valid_from.isoformat() if entity.valid_from else None,
 219:                 valid_until=entity.valid_until.isoformat() if entity.valid_until else None,
 220:                 confidence=entity.confidence,
 221:                 metadata=_serialize_dict(entity.metadata),
 222:                 created_at=entity.created_at.isoformat(),
 223:                 updated_at=entity.updated_at.isoformat(),
 224:             )
 225: 
 226:         async with driver.session(database=self._database) as session:
````
