````python
223:         self._expertise = expertise
224:         self._min_confidence = min_confidence
225:         self._max_inferences_per_rule = max_inferences_per_rule
226:         self._rule_engine = RuleEngine(expertise)
227:         self._rel_index: RelationshipTypeIndex | None = None
228: 
229:     def infer(
230:         self,
231:         entities: list[Entity],
232:         relationships: list[Relationship],
233:         *,
234:         depth: int = 1,
235:     ) -> list[InferredRelationship]:
236:         """Infer new relationships from existing graph.
237: 
238:         Uses RelationshipTypeIndex for O(1) lookups by relationship type,
239:         significantly improving performance for graphs with many relationships.
240: 
241:         Args:
242:             entities: Existing entities
243:             relationships: Existing relationships
244:             depth: Number of inference passes (for transitive inference)
245: 
246:         Returns:
247:             List of inferred relationships
248:         """
249:         if not self._expertise or not self._expertise.inference_rules:
250:             logger.debug("No expertise or inference rules configured, skipping inference")
251:             return []
252: 
253:         # Build relationship type index for O(1) lookups
254:         self._rel_index = RelationshipTypeIndex()
255:         self._rel_index.build(relationships)
256:         logger.debug(f"Built relationship index: {self._rel_index.stats()}")
257: 
258:         # Diagnostic logging
259:         entity_types = Counter(
260:             e.entity_type.value if hasattr(e.entity_type, "value") else str(e.entity_type) for e in entities
261:         )
262:         logger.debug(f"Inference input: {len(entities)} entities, types: {dict(entity_types)}")
263: 
264:         rel_types = Counter(
265:             r.relationship_type.value if hasattr(r.relationship_type, "value") else str(r.relationship_type)
266:             for r in relationships
267:         )
268:         logger.debug(f"Inference input: {len(relationships)} relationships, types: {dict(rel_types)}")
269: 
270:         # Detailed pattern diagnostics (debug-only to avoid overhead)
271:         if logger._core.min_level <= 10:  # DEBUG level
272:             entity_type_lookup = {
273:                 e.id: (e.entity_type.value if hasattr(e.entity_type, "value") else str(e.entity_type)) for e in entities
274:             }
275:             rel_type_patterns: dict[str, Counter] = {}
276:             for r in relationships:
277:                 rt = r.relationship_type.value if hasattr(r.relationship_type, "value") else str(r.relationship_type)
278:                 source_type = entity_type_lookup.get(r.source_entity_id, "UNKNOWN")
279:                 target_type = entity_type_lookup.get(r.target_entity_id, "UNKNOWN")
280:                 if rt not in rel_type_patterns:
281:                     rel_type_patterns[rt] = Counter()
282:                 rel_type_patterns[rt][f"{source_type}->{target_type}"] += 1
283: 
284:             for rel_type, patterns in rel_type_patterns.items():
285:                 logger.debug(f"  {rel_type} patterns: {dict(patterns.most_common(5))}")
286: 
287:         # Check rule compatibility (log mismatches as warnings)
288:         expected_rels = set()
289:         expected_entities = set()
290:         for rule in self._expertise.inference_rules:
291:             for cond in rule.when:
292:                 if hasattr(cond, "relationship"):
293:                     expected_rels.add(cond.relationship)
294:                 if hasattr(cond, "source_type"):
295:                     expected_entities.add(cond.source_type)
296:                 if hasattr(cond, "target_type"):
297:                     expected_entities.add(cond.target_type)
298: 
299:         if expected_rels or expected_entities:
300:             actual_rel_types = {
301:                 r.relationship_type.value if hasattr(r.relationship_type, "value") else str(r.relationship_type)
302:                 for r in relationships
303:             }
304:             actual_entity_types = {
305:                 e.entity_type.value if hasattr(e.entity_type, "value") else str(e.entity_type) for e in entities
306:             }
307:             if not (actual_rel_types & expected_rels):
308:                 logger.debug(
309:                     f"No relationship type overlap: rules expect {sorted(expected_rels)}, "
310:                     f"graph has {sorted(actual_rel_types)}"
311:                 )
312:             if not (actual_entity_types & expected_entities):
313:                 logger.debug(
314:                     f"No entity type overlap: rules expect {sorted(expected_entities)}, "
315:                     f"graph has {sorted(actual_entity_types)}"
316:                 )
317: 
318:         all_inferred: list[InferredRelationship] = []
319:         current_relationships = list(relationships)
320: 
321:         for pass_num in range(depth):
322:             # Build context with current state
323:             context = RuleEvaluationContext.from_data(entities, current_relationships)
324: 
325:             # Evaluate inference rules
326:             matches = self._rule_engine.evaluate_inference_rules(context)
327:             logger.debug(f"Pass {pass_num + 1}: Rule engine returned {len(matches)} matches")
328: 
329:             # Log details of first few matches (debug only, avoids list() overhead)
330:             if logger._core.min_level <= 10:
331:                 for i, match in enumerate(matches[:5]):
332:                     logger.debug(
333:                         f"  Match {i + 1}: rule={match.rule_name}, "
334:                         f"confidence={match.confidence:.2f}, "
335:                         f"metadata_keys={list(match.metadata.keys())}"
336:                     )
337: 
338:             # Convert matches to inferred relationships
339:             pass_inferred = self._matches_to_relationships(matches, context)
340:             logger.debug(f"Pass {pass_num + 1}: Converted to {len(pass_inferred)} inferred relationships")
341: 
342:             if not pass_inferred:
343:                 logger.debug(f"Pass {pass_num + 1}: No new inferences, stopping early")
344:                 break
345: 
346:             # Filter out duplicates and already existing relationships
347:             new_inferred = self._filter_duplicates(pass_inferred, current_relationships, all_inferred)
348: 
349:             if not new_inferred:
350:                 break
351: 
352:             all_inferred.extend(new_inferred)
353: 
354:             # Add inferred to current for next pass (as mock relationships)
355:             current_relationships.extend(self._to_mock_relationships(new_inferred, entities))
356: 
357:             logger.debug(f"Inference pass {pass_num + 1}: {len(new_inferred)} new relationships")
358: 
359:         logger.debug(f"Inference complete: {len(all_inferred)} total relationships inferred")
360:         return all_inferred
361: 
362:     def infer_from_pattern(
363:         self,
364:         entities: list[Entity],
365:         relationships: list[Relationship],
366:         pattern: str,
367:     ) -> list[InferredRelationship]:
368:         """Infer relationships matching a specific pattern.
369: 
370:         Args:
371:             entities: Existing entities
372:             relationships: Existing relationships
373:             pattern: Pattern to match (e.g., "A -> WORKS_FOR -> B, B -> OWNS -> C")
374: 
375:         Returns:
376:             List of inferred relationships
377:         """
378:         # Parse pattern and find matches
379:         # For now, delegate to rule engine
380:         context = RuleEvaluationContext.from_data(entities, relationships)
381:         matches = self._rule_engine.evaluate_inference_rules(context)
382:         return self._matches_to_relationships(matches, context)
383: 
384:     def find_chains_indexed(
385:         self,
386:         first_type: str,
387:         second_type: str,
388:         entities: list[Entity],
389:     ) -> list[tuple[Relationship, Relationship]]:
390:         """Find relationship chains A-[first_type]->B-[second_type]->C using index.
391: 
392:         Uses RelationshipTypeIndex for O(1) type lookups followed by O(k) iteration
393:         over matching relationships, where k is the number of relationships of
394:         that type. This is significantly faster than O(n^2) nested iteration.
395: 
396:         Example: Find "MANAGES -> WORKS_ON" chains to infer STAKEHOLDER_OF.
397: 
398:         Args:
399:             first_type: Relationship type for first hop (e.g., "MANAGES")
400:             second_type: Relationship type for second hop (e.g., "WORKS_ON")
401:             entities: List of entities (for entity lookup by ID)
402: 
403:         Returns:
404:             List of (first_rel, second_rel) tuples forming valid chains
405:         """
406:         if not self._rel_index:
407:             logger.warning("Relationship index not built, returning empty chains")
408:             return []
409: 
410:         # Build entity lookup for validation
411:         entity_lookup: dict[UUID, Entity] = {e.id: e for e in entities}
412: 
413:         chains: list[tuple[Relationship, Relationship]] = []
414: 
415:         # O(1) lookup for first type
416:         first_rels = self._rel_index.get_by_type(first_type)
417: 
418:         for first_rel in first_rels:
419:             # The intermediate entity is the target of the first relationship
420:             intermediate_id = first_rel.target_entity_id
421: 
422:             # O(1) lookup for second type from the intermediate entity
423:             second_rels = self._rel_index.get_by_source_and_type(intermediate_id, second_type)
424: 
425:             for second_rel in second_rels:
426:                 # Validate that all entities exist
427:                 if (
428:                     first_rel.source_entity_id in entity_lookup
429:                     and intermediate_id in entity_lookup
430:                     and second_rel.target_entity_id in entity_lookup
431:                 ):
432:                     chains.append((first_rel, second_rel))
433: 
434:         logger.debug(
435:             f"Found {len(chains)} chains for {first_type} -> {second_type} "
436:             f"(checked {len(first_rels)} first-hop relationships)"
437:         )
438: 
439:         return chains
440: 
441:     def get_relationship_index(self) -> RelationshipTypeIndex | None:
442:         """Get the current relationship type index.
443: 
444:         Returns the index built during the last infer() call, or None if
445:         inference hasn't been run yet.
446: 
447:         Returns:
448:             RelationshipTypeIndex or None
449:         """
450:         return self._rel_index
451: 
452:     def _matches_to_relationships(
453:         self,
454:         matches: list[RuleMatch],
455:         context: RuleEvaluationContext,
456:     ) -> list[InferredRelationship]:
457:         """Convert rule matches to inferred relationships."""
458:         inferred = []
459:         rule_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
460: 
461:         # Diagnostic counters
462:         filtered_rule_limit = 0
463:         filtered_confidence = 0
464:         filtered_entity_resolution = 0
465:         filtered_self_ref = 0
466: 
467:         for match in matches:
468:             # Check rule limit
469:             rule_counts[match.rule_name] = rule_counts.get(match.rule_name, 0) + 1
470:             if rule_counts[match.rule_name] > self._max_inferences_per_rule:
471:                 filtered_rule_limit += 1
472:                 continue
473: 
474:             # Check confidence threshold
475:             if match.confidence < self._min_confidence:
476:                 filtered_confidence += 1
477:                 continue
478: 
479:             # Resolve source and target from metadata
480:             source_entity, target_entity = self._resolve_inference_entities(match, context)
481: 
482:             if not source_entity or not target_entity:
483:                 filtered_entity_resolution += 1
484:                 continue
485: 
486:             # Skip self-referential
487:             if source_entity.id == target_entity.id:
488:                 filtered_self_ref += 1
489:                 continue
490: 
491:             relationship_type = match.metadata.get("then_relationship", "RELATES_TO")
492: 
493:             inferred.append(
494:                 InferredRelationship(
495:                     source_entity_id=source_entity.id,
496:                     target_entity_id=target_entity.id,
497:                     relationship_type=relationship_type,
498:                     description=f"Inferred by rule: {match.rule_name}",
499:                     confidence=match.confidence,
500:                     rule_name=match.rule_name,
501:                     evidence=[r.id for r in match.matched_relationships],
502:                 )
503:             )
504: 
505:         # Log filtering breakdown
506:         total_filtered = filtered_rule_limit + filtered_confidence + filtered_entity_resolution + filtered_self_ref
507:         if total_filtered > 0 or len(matches) > 0:
508:             logger.debug(
509:                 f"Match filtering: {len(matches)} input -> {len(inferred)} output | "
510:                 f"rule_limit={filtered_rule_limit}, confidence={filtered_confidence}, "
511:                 f"entity_resolution={filtered_entity_resolution}, self_ref={filtered_self_ref}"
512:             )
513: 
514:         return inferred
515: 
516:     def _resolve_inference_entities(
517:         self,
518:         match: RuleMatch,
519:         context: RuleEvaluationContext,
520:     ) -> tuple[Entity | None, Entity | None]:
521:         """Resolve source and target entities from match metadata.
522: 
523:         The then_source and then_target specify which entity to use:
524:         - "first.source": Source entity of first matched relationship
525:         - "first.target": Target entity of first matched relationship
526:         - "second.source": Source entity of second matched relationship
527:         - "second.target": Target entity of second matched relationship
528:         """
529:         then_source = match.metadata.get("then_source", "first.source")
530:         then_target = match.metadata.get("then_target", "second.target")
531: 
532:         first = match.metadata.get("first", {})
533:         second = match.metadata.get("second", {})
534: 
535:         def resolve_ref(ref: str) -> Entity | None:
536:             parts = ref.split(".")
537:             if len(parts) != 2:
538:                 return None
539: 
540:             group, position = parts
541:             if group == "first":
542:                 data = first
543:             elif group == "second":
544:                 data = second
545:             else:
546:                 logger.warning(f"Rule '{match.rule_name}': invalid reference '{ref}' (only 'first'/'second' supported)")
547:                 return None
548: 
549:             return data.get(position)
550: 
551:         source = resolve_ref(then_source)
552:         target = resolve_ref(then_target)
553: 
554:         return source, target
555: 
556:     def _filter_duplicates(
557:         self,
558:         new_inferred: list[InferredRelationship],
559:         existing_relationships: list[Relationship],
560:         already_inferred: list[InferredRelationship],
561:     ) -> list[InferredRelationship]:
562:         """Filter out duplicate inferences."""
563:         # Build set of existing relationship keys
564:         existing_keys: set[tuple[UUID, UUID, str]] = set()
565: 
566:         for rel in existing_relationships:
567:             rel_type = str(
568:                 rel.relationship_type.value if hasattr(rel.relationship_type, "value") else rel.relationship_type
569:             )
570:             existing_keys.add((rel.source_entity_id, rel.target_entity_id, rel_type))
571: 
572:         for inf in already_inferred:
573:             existing_keys.add((inf.source_entity_id, inf.target_entity_id, inf.relationship_type))
574: 
575:         # Filter new inferences
576:         filtered = []
577:         for inf in new_inferred:
578:             key = (inf.source_entity_id, inf.target_entity_id, inf.relationship_type)
579:             if key not in existing_keys:
580:                 filtered.append(inf)
581:                 existing_keys.add(key)
582: 
583:         return filtered
584: 
585:     def _to_mock_relationships(
586:         self,
587:         inferred: list[InferredRelationship],
588:         entities: list[Entity],
589:     ) -> list[Relationship]:
590:         """Convert inferred relationships to mock Relationship objects for next pass."""
591:         from khora.core.models import Relationship
592:         from khora.core.models.entity import RelationshipType
593: 
594:         mock_rels = []
595:         # Get namespace from first entity if available
596:         namespace_id = entities[0].namespace_id if entities else uuid4()
597: 
598:         for inf in inferred:
599:             # Preserve original type string for domain-specific types
600:             try:
601:                 rel_type: RelationshipType | str = RelationshipType[inf.relationship_type]
602:             except (KeyError, AttributeError):
603:                 rel_type = inf.relationship_type
604: 
605:             mock_rels.append(
606:                 Relationship(
607:                     id=uuid4(),
608:                     namespace_id=namespace_id,
609:                     source_entity_id=inf.source_entity_id,
610:                     target_entity_id=inf.target_entity_id,
611:                     relationship_type=rel_type,
612:                     description=inf.description,
613:                     properties={},
614:                     source_document_ids=[],
615:                     source_chunk_ids=[],
616:                     confidence=inf.confidence,
617:                     metadata={"inferred": True, "rule": inf.rule_name},
618:                     created_at=datetime.now(UTC),
619:                     updated_at=datetime.now(UTC),
620:                 )
621:             )
622: 
623:         return mock_rels
624: 
625: 
626: def to_relationship(
627:     inferred: InferredRelationship,
628:     namespace_id: UUID,
629: ) -> Relationship:
630:     """Convert an InferredRelationship to a domain Relationship model.
631: 
632:     Args:
633:         inferred: The inferred relationship to convert
634:         namespace_id: Namespace ID for the relationship
635: 
636:     Returns:
637:         Domain Relationship model
638:     """
639:     from khora.core.models import Relationship
640:     from khora.core.models.entity import RelationshipType
641: 
642:     # Preserve original type string for domain-specific types
643:     try:
644:         rel_type: RelationshipType | str = RelationshipType[inferred.relationship_type]
645:     except (KeyError, AttributeError):
646:         rel_type = inferred.relationship_type
647: 
648:     return Relationship(
649:         id=uuid4(),
650:         namespace_id=namespace_id,
651:         source_entity_id=inferred.source_entity_id,
652:         target_entity_id=inferred.target_entity_id,
653:         relationship_type=rel_type,
654:         description=inferred.description,
655:         properties={
656:             "inferred": True,
657:             "rule_name": inferred.rule_name,
658:             "evidence": [str(e) for e in inferred.evidence],
659:         },
660:         source_document_ids=[],
661:         source_chunk_ids=[],
662:         confidence=inferred.confidence,
663:         metadata={"inferred": True},
664:         created_at=datetime.now(UTC),
665:         updated_at=datetime.now(UTC),
666:     )
````

## File: src/khora/storage/backends/pgvector.py
````python
  1: """pgvector backend for vector embeddings storage.
  2: 
  3: Handles storage and retrieval of embeddings for semantic search
  4: using pgvector extension in PostgreSQL.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: from datetime import UTC, datetime
 10: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
 11: from uuid import UUID
 12: 
 13: from loguru import logger
 14: from sqlalchemy import delete, func, select, text, update
 15: from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncEngine, AsyncSession, async_sessionmaker, create_async_engine
 16: from tenacity import AsyncRetrying, before_sleep_log, retry_if_exception, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential
 17: 
 18: from khora.core.models import Chunk, ChunkMetadata
 19: from khora.db.models import Base, ChunkModel, EntityModel
 20: 
 21: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 22:     pass
 23: 
 24: 
 25: _DEADLOCK_MAX_RETRIES = 3
 26: 
 27: 
 28: async def _retry_on_deadlock(coro_fn, *args, **kwargs):
 29:     """Retry an async operation on deadlock with exponential backoff."""
 30:     async for attempt in AsyncRetrying(
 31:         stop=stop_after_attempt(_DEADLOCK_MAX_RETRIES),
 32:         wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=0.1, min=0.1, max=0.4),
 33:         retry=retry_if_exception(lambda e: "deadlock" in str(e).lower()),
 34:         before_sleep=before_sleep_log(logger, "WARNING"),
 35:         reraise=True,
 36:     ):
 37:         with attempt:
 38:             return await coro_fn(*args, **kwargs)
 39: 
 40: 
 41: class PgVectorBackend:
 42:     """pgvector backend for vector embeddings.
 43: 
 44:     Handles all vector operations including chunk storage,
 45:     similarity search, and entity embeddings.
 46:     """
 47: 
 48:     def __init__(
 49:         self,
 50:         database_url: str,
 51:         *,
 52:         embedding_dimension: int = 1536,
 53:         echo: bool = False,
 54:         pool_size: int = 10,
 55:         max_overflow: int = 20,
 56:     ) -> None:
 57:         """Initialize the pgvector backend.
 58: 
 59:         Args:
 60:             database_url: PostgreSQL connection URL (with pgvector extension)
 61:             embedding_dimension: Dimension of embedding vectors
 62:             echo: Enable SQL echo logging
 63:             pool_size: Connection pool size
 64:             max_overflow: Maximum overflow connections
 65:         """
 66:         # Convert to async URL if needed
 67:         if database_url.startswith("postgresql://"):
 68:             database_url = database_url.replace("postgresql://", "postgresql+asyncpg://", 1)
 69:         elif database_url.startswith("postgres://"):
 70:             database_url = database_url.replace("postgres://", "postgresql+asyncpg://", 1)
 71: 
 72:         self._database_url = database_url
 73:         self._embedding_dimension = embedding_dimension
 74:         self._echo = echo
 75:         self._pool_size = pool_size
 76:         self._max_overflow = max_overflow
 77:         self._engine: AsyncEngine | None = None
 78:         self._session_factory: async_sessionmaker[AsyncSession] | None = None
 79: 
 80:     async def connect(self) -> None:
 81:         """Establish connection to the database."""
 82:         if self._engine is not None:
 83:             return
 84: 
 85:         logger.info("Connecting to pgvector...")
 86:         self._engine = create_async_engine(
 87:             self._database_url,
 88:             echo=self._echo,
 89:             pool_size=self._pool_size,
 90:             max_overflow=self._max_overflow,
 91:         )
 92:         self._session_factory = async_sessionmaker(
 93:             self._engine,
 94:             class_=AsyncSession,
 95:             expire_on_commit=False,
 96:         )
 97: 
 98:         # Ensure pgvector extension is enabled
 99:         async with self._engine.begin() as conn:
100:             await conn.execute(text("CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector"))
101: 
102:         logger.info("Connected to pgvector")
103: 
104:     async def disconnect(self) -> None:
105:         """Close database connections."""
106:         if self._engine is not None:
107:             logger.info("Disconnecting from pgvector...")
108:             await self._engine.dispose()
109:             self._engine = None
110:             self._session_factory = None
111:             logger.info("Disconnected from pgvector")
112: 
113:     async def is_healthy(self) -> bool:
114:         """Check if the backend is healthy and connected."""
115:         if self._engine is None or self._session_factory is None:
116:             return False
117:         try:
118:             async with self._session_factory() as session:
119:                 await session.execute(select(1))
120:             return True
121:         except Exception as e:
122:             logger.error(f"pgvector health check failed: {e}")
123:             return False
124: 
125:     def _get_session(self) -> AsyncSession:
126:         """Get a new database session."""
127:         if self._session_factory is None:
128:             raise RuntimeError("Backend not connected. Call connect() first.")
129:         return self._session_factory()
130: 
131:     async def create_tables(self) -> None:
132:         """Create all database tables (for testing/development)."""
133:         if self._engine is None:
134:             raise RuntimeError("Backend not connected. Call connect() first.")
135:         async with self._engine.begin() as conn:
136:             await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)
137: 
138:     # =========================================================================
139:     # Chunk operations
140:     # =========================================================================
141: 
142:     async def create_chunk(self, chunk: Chunk) -> Chunk:
143:         """Create a new chunk with its embedding."""
144:         async with self._get_session() as session:
145:             model = ChunkModel(
146:                 id=str(chunk.id),
147:                 namespace_id=str(chunk.namespace_id),
148:                 document_id=str(chunk.document_id),
149:                 content=chunk.content,
150:                 chunk_index=chunk.metadata.chunk_index,
151:                 start_char=chunk.metadata.start_char,
152:                 end_char=chunk.metadata.end_char,
153:                 token_count=chunk.metadata.token_count,
154:                 metadata_=chunk.metadata.custom,
155:                 embedding=chunk.embedding,
156:                 embedding_model=chunk.embedding_model,
157:                 created_at=chunk.created_at,
158:             )
159:             session.add(model)
160:             await session.commit()
161:             await session.refresh(model)
162:             return self._chunk_model_to_domain(model)
163: 
164:     async def create_chunks_batch(self, chunks: list[Chunk]) -> list[Chunk]:
165:         """Create multiple chunks in a batch."""
166:         if not chunks:
167:             return []
168: 
169:         async with self._get_session() as session:
170:             models = [
171:                 ChunkModel(
172:                     id=str(chunk.id),
173:                     namespace_id=str(chunk.namespace_id),
174:                     document_id=str(chunk.document_id),
175:                     content=chunk.content,
176:                     chunk_index=chunk.metadata.chunk_index,
177:                     start_char=chunk.metadata.start_char,
178:                     end_char=chunk.metadata.end_char,
179:                     token_count=chunk.metadata.token_count,
180:                     metadata_=chunk.metadata.custom,
181:                     embedding=chunk.embedding,
182:                     embedding_model=chunk.embedding_model,
183:                     created_at=chunk.created_at,
184:                 )
185:                 for chunk in chunks
186:             ]
187:             session.add_all(models)
188:             await session.commit()
189:             return chunks
190: 
191:     async def get_chunk(self, chunk_id: UUID) -> Chunk | None:
192:         """Get a chunk by ID."""
193:         async with self._get_session() as session:
194:             result = await session.execute(select(ChunkModel).where(ChunkModel.id == str(chunk_id)))
195:             model = result.scalar_one_or_none()
196:             return self._chunk_model_to_domain(model) if model else None
197: 
198:     async def get_chunks_batch(self, chunk_ids: list[UUID]) -> dict[UUID, Chunk]:
199:         """Get multiple chunks by ID in a single query.
200: 
201:         Args:
202:             chunk_ids: List of chunk IDs to fetch
203: 
204:         Returns:
205:             Dictionary mapping chunk ID to Chunk (only for existing chunks)
206:         """
207:         if not chunk_ids:
208:             return {}
209: 
210:         async with self._get_session() as session:
211:             result = await session.execute(select(ChunkModel).where(ChunkModel.id.in_([str(cid) for cid in chunk_ids])))
212:             models = result.scalars().all()
213:             return {UUID(m.id): self._chunk_model_to_domain(m) for m in models}
214: 
215:     async def get_chunks_by_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> list[Chunk]:
216:         """Get all chunks for a document."""
217:         async with self._get_session() as session:
218:             result = await session.execute(
219:                 select(ChunkModel).where(ChunkModel.document_id == str(document_id)).order_by(ChunkModel.chunk_index)
220:             )
221:             return [self._chunk_model_to_domain(m) for m in result.scalars().all()]
222: 
223:     async def delete_chunks_by_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> int:
224:         """Delete all chunks for a document."""
225:         async with self._get_session() as session:
226:             result = await session.execute(delete(ChunkModel).where(ChunkModel.document_id == str(document_id)))
227:             await session.commit()
228:             return result.rowcount
229: 
230:     async def search_similar(
231:         self,
232:         namespace_id: UUID,
233:         query_embedding: list[float],
234:         *,
235:         limit: int = 10,
236:         min_similarity: float = 0.0,
237:         filter_document_ids: list[UUID] | None = None,
238:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
239:         """Search for similar chunks using vector similarity.
240: 
241:         Uses cosine similarity for matching. Returns list of (chunk, similarity_score) tuples.
242:         """
243:         async with self._get_session() as session:
244:             # Build cosine similarity expression
245:             # pgvector uses <=> for cosine distance, so similarity = 1 - distance
246:             similarity = 1 - ChunkModel.embedding.cosine_distance(query_embedding)
247: 
248:             query = (
249:                 select(ChunkModel, similarity.label("similarity"))
250:                 .where(
251:                     ChunkModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id),
252:                     ChunkModel.embedding.is_not(None),
253:                 )
254:                 .order_by(similarity.desc())
255:                 .limit(limit)
256:             )
257: 
258:             if filter_document_ids:
259:                 query = query.where(ChunkModel.document_id.in_([str(d) for d in filter_document_ids]))
260: 
261:             if min_similarity > 0:
262:                 query = query.where(similarity >= min_similarity)
263: 
264:             result = await session.execute(query)
265:             rows = result.all()
266: 
267:             return [(self._chunk_model_to_domain(row.ChunkModel), row.similarity) for row in rows]
268: 
269:     def _chunk_model_to_domain(self, model: ChunkModel) -> Chunk:
270:         """Convert ChunkModel to domain Chunk."""
271:         return Chunk(
272:             id=UUID(model.id),
273:             namespace_id=UUID(model.namespace_id),
274:             document_id=UUID(model.document_id),
275:             content=model.content,
276:             metadata=ChunkMetadata(
277:                 document_id=UUID(model.document_id),
278:                 chunk_index=model.chunk_index,
279:                 start_char=model.start_char,
280:                 end_char=model.end_char,
281:                 token_count=model.token_count,
282:                 custom=model.metadata_,
283:             ),
284:             embedding=list(model.embedding) if model.embedding is not None else None,
285:             embedding_model=model.embedding_model,
286:             created_at=model.created_at,
287:         )
288: 
289:     # =========================================================================
290:     # Full-text search operations
291:     # =========================================================================
292: 
293:     async def search_fulltext(
294:         self,
295:         namespace_id: UUID,
296:         query_text: str,
297:         *,
298:         limit: int = 10,
299:         language: str = "english",
300:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
301:         """Search chunks using PostgreSQL full-text search with ts_rank.
302: 
303:         Uses the content_tsv generated column and GIN index for efficient
304:         full-text matching.
305:         """
306:         async with self._get_session() as session:
307:             tsquery = func.plainto_tsquery(language, query_text)
308:             rank = func.ts_rank(ChunkModel.content_tsv, tsquery)
309: 
310:             query = (
311:                 select(ChunkModel, rank.label("rank"))
312:                 .where(
313:                     ChunkModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id),
314:                     ChunkModel.content_tsv.op("@@")(tsquery),
315:                 )
316:                 .order_by(rank.desc())
317:                 .limit(limit)
318:             )
319: 
320:             result = await session.execute(query)
321:             rows = result.all()
322: 
323:             return [(self._chunk_model_to_domain(row.ChunkModel), float(row.rank)) for row in rows]
324: 
325:     # =========================================================================
326:     # Entity operations (for vector search via PostgreSQL)
327:     # =========================================================================
328: 
329:     async def create_entity(self, entity) -> None:
330:         """Create an entity record in PostgreSQL for vector search.
331: 
332:         This stores the entity metadata and embedding in PostgreSQL,
333:         complementing the Neo4j storage for graph traversal.
334: 
335:         Uses upsert pattern: if entity already exists, updates it instead.
336:         """
337:         await _retry_on_deadlock(self._upsert_entity, entity)
338: 
339:     async def update_entity(self, entity) -> None:
340:         """Update an entity record in PostgreSQL.
341: 
342:         Uses upsert to handle race conditions and entities created before
343:         dual-storage was implemented.
344:         """
345:         await _retry_on_deadlock(self._upsert_entity, entity)
346: 
347:     async def _upsert_entity(self, entity) -> None:
348:         """Internal upsert used by both create_entity and update_entity."""
349:         from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import insert
350: 
351:         async with self._get_session() as session:
352:             stmt = insert(EntityModel).values(
353:                 id=str(entity.id),
354:                 namespace_id=str(entity.namespace_id),
355:                 name=entity.name,
356:                 entity_type=(
357:                     entity.entity_type.value if hasattr(entity.entity_type, "value") else str(entity.entity_type)
358:                 ),
359:                 description=entity.description,
360:                 attributes=entity.attributes,
361:                 source_document_ids=[str(d) for d in entity.source_document_ids],
362:                 source_chunk_ids=[str(c) for c in entity.source_chunk_ids],
363:                 mention_count=entity.mention_count,
364:                 embedding=entity.embedding,
365:                 embedding_model=entity.embedding_model,
366:                 valid_from=entity.valid_from,
367:                 valid_until=entity.valid_until,
368:                 confidence=entity.confidence,
369:                 metadata_=entity.metadata,
370:                 created_at=entity.created_at,
371:                 updated_at=entity.updated_at,
372:             )
373:             stmt = stmt.on_conflict_do_update(
374:                 index_elements=["id"],
375:                 set_={
376:                     "name": stmt.excluded.name,
377:                     "description": stmt.excluded.description,
378:                     "attributes": stmt.excluded.attributes,
379:                     "source_document_ids": stmt.excluded.source_document_ids,
380:                     "source_chunk_ids": stmt.excluded.source_chunk_ids,
381:                     "mention_count": stmt.excluded.mention_count,
382:                     "embedding": stmt.excluded.embedding,
383:                     "embedding_model": stmt.excluded.embedding_model,
384:                     "valid_from": stmt.excluded.valid_from,
385:                     "valid_until": stmt.excluded.valid_until,
386:                     "confidence": stmt.excluded.confidence,
387:                     "metadata": stmt.excluded.metadata,
388:                     "updated_at": stmt.excluded.updated_at,
389:                 },
390:             )
391:             await session.execute(stmt)
392:             await session.commit()
393: 
394:     async def get_entity(self, entity_id: UUID):
395:         """Get an entity by ID from PostgreSQL."""
396:         async with self._get_session() as session:
397:             result = await session.execute(select(EntityModel).where(EntityModel.id == str(entity_id)))
398:             model = result.scalar_one_or_none()
399:             if model is None:
400:                 return None
401:             return self._entity_model_to_domain(model)
402: 
403:     async def entity_exists(self, entity_id: UUID) -> bool:
404:         """Check if an entity exists in PostgreSQL."""
405:         async with self._get_session() as session:
406:             result = await session.execute(select(func.count(EntityModel.id)).where(EntityModel.id == str(entity_id)))
407:             return result.scalar_one() > 0
408: 
409:     def _entity_model_to_domain(self, model: EntityModel):
410:         """Convert EntityModel to domain Entity."""
411:         from uuid import UUID as UUIDType
412: 
413:         from khora.core.models import Entity
414: 
415:         return Entity(
416:             id=UUIDType(model.id),
417:             namespace_id=UUIDType(model.namespace_id),
418:             name=model.name,
419:             entity_type=model.entity_type,
420:             description=model.description,
421:             attributes=model.attributes or {},
422:             source_document_ids=[UUIDType(d) for d in (model.source_document_ids or [])],
423:             source_chunk_ids=[UUIDType(c) for c in (model.source_chunk_ids or [])],
424:             mention_count=model.mention_count,
425:             embedding=list(model.embedding) if model.embedding is not None else None,
426:             embedding_model=model.embedding_model or "",
427:             valid_from=model.valid_from,
428:             valid_until=model.valid_until,
429:             confidence=model.confidence,
430:             metadata=model.metadata_ or {},
431:             created_at=model.created_at,
432:             updated_at=model.updated_at,
433:         )
434: 
435:     async def upsert_entities_batch(self, namespace_id: UUID, entities: list, *, batch_size: int = 200) -> list[tuple]:
436:         """Batch upsert entity records in PostgreSQL.
437: 
438:         Uses multi-row INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE statements, chunked
439:         into sub-batches to stay within asyncpg's parameter limit.
440:         Returns list of (entity, is_new) tuples (is_new is approximate).
441:         """
442:         if not entities:
443:             return []
444: 
445:         from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import insert
446: 
447:         # Process in sub-batches to avoid exceeding parameter limits
448:         # (17 columns × batch_size parameters per statement)
449:         for i in range(0, len(entities), batch_size):
450:             batch = entities[i : i + batch_size]
451:             values = [
452:                 {
453:                     "id": str(entity.id),
454:                     "namespace_id": str(entity.namespace_id),
455:                     "name": entity.name,
456:                     "entity_type": (
457:                         entity.entity_type.value if hasattr(entity.entity_type, "value") else str(entity.entity_type)
458:                     ),
459:                     "description": entity.description,
460:                     "attributes": entity.attributes,
461:                     "source_document_ids": [str(d) for d in entity.source_document_ids],
462:                     "source_chunk_ids": [str(c) for c in entity.source_chunk_ids],
463:                     "mention_count": entity.mention_count,
464:                     "embedding": entity.embedding,
465:                     "embedding_model": entity.embedding_model,
466:                     "valid_from": entity.valid_from,
467:                     "valid_until": entity.valid_until,
468:                     "confidence": entity.confidence,
469:                     "metadata_": entity.metadata,
470:                     "created_at": entity.created_at,
471:                     "updated_at": entity.updated_at,
472:                 }
473:                 for entity in batch
474:             ]
475: 
476:             async with self._get_session() as session:
477:                 stmt = insert(EntityModel).values(values)
478:                 stmt = stmt.on_conflict_do_update(
479:                     index_elements=["id"],
480:                     set_={
481:                         "name": stmt.excluded.name,
482:                         "description": stmt.excluded.description,
483:                         "attributes": stmt.excluded.attributes,
484:                         "source_document_ids": stmt.excluded.source_document_ids,
485:                         "source_chunk_ids": stmt.excluded.source_chunk_ids,
486:                         "mention_count": stmt.excluded.mention_count,
487:                         "embedding": stmt.excluded.embedding,
488:                         "embedding_model": stmt.excluded.embedding_model,
489:                         "valid_from": stmt.excluded.valid_from,
490:                         "valid_until": stmt.excluded.valid_until,
491:                         "confidence": stmt.excluded.confidence,
492:                         "metadata": stmt.excluded.metadata,
493:                         "updated_at": stmt.excluded.updated_at,
494:                     },
495:                 )
496:                 await session.execute(stmt)
497:                 await session.commit()
498: 
499:         return [(entity, True) for entity in entities]
500: 
501:     # =========================================================================
502:     # Entity embedding operations
503:     # =========================================================================
504: 
505:     async def update_entity_embedding(self, entity_id: UUID, embedding: list[float], model: str) -> None:
506:         """Update the embedding for an entity."""
507:         async with self._get_session() as session:
508:             await session.execute(
509:                 update(EntityModel)
510:                 .where(EntityModel.id == str(entity_id))
511:                 .values(
512:                     embedding=embedding,
513:                     embedding_model=model,
514:                     updated_at=datetime.now(UTC),
515:                 )
516:             )
517:             await session.commit()
518: 
519:     async def update_entity_embeddings_batch(self, updates: list[tuple[UUID, list[float], str]]) -> int:
520:         """Update embeddings for multiple entities in a single transaction.
521: 
522:         Uses executemany semantics to send all updates in a single round-trip
523:         instead of N individual UPDATE statements.
524: 
525:         Args:
526:             updates: List of (entity_id, embedding, model) tuples
527: 
528:         Returns:
529:             Number of entities updated
530:         """
531:         if not updates:
532:             return 0
533: 
534:         async def _do_batch():
535:             from sqlalchemy import bindparam
536: 
537:             # Sort by entity_id for consistent lock ordering across concurrent batches
538:             sorted_updates = sorted(updates, key=lambda u: str(u[0]))
539:             now = datetime.now(UTC)
540: 
541:             # Use Core table to avoid ORM bulk-update PK requirements
542:             tbl = EntityModel.__table__
543:             stmt = (
544:                 tbl.update()
545:                 .where(tbl.c.id == bindparam("eid"))
546:                 .values(
547:                     embedding=bindparam("emb"),
548:                     embedding_model=bindparam("mdl"),
549:                     updated_at=bindparam("ts"),
550:                 )
551:             )
552:             params = [{"eid": str(eid), "emb": emb, "mdl": mdl, "ts": now} for eid, emb, mdl in sorted_updates]
553: 
554:             async with self._get_session() as session:
555:                 await session.connection()
556:                 await session.execute(stmt, params)
557:                 await session.commit()
558:             return len(sorted_updates)
559: 
560:         return await _retry_on_deadlock(_do_batch)
561: 
562:     async def search_similar_entities(
563:         self,
564:         namespace_id: UUID,
565:         query_embedding: list[float],
566:         *,
567:         limit: int = 10,
568:         min_similarity: float = 0.0,
569:     ) -> list[tuple[UUID, float]]:
570:         """Search for similar entities by embedding.
571: 
572:         Returns list of (entity_id, similarity_score) tuples.
573:         """
574:         async with self._get_session() as session:
575:             similarity = 1 - EntityModel.embedding.cosine_distance(query_embedding)
576: 
577:             query = (
578:                 select(EntityModel.id, similarity.label("similarity"))
579:                 .where(
580:                     EntityModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id),
581:                     EntityModel.embedding.is_not(None),
582:                 )
583:                 .order_by(similarity.desc())
584:                 .limit(limit)
585:             )
586: 
587:             if min_similarity > 0:
588:                 query = query.where(similarity >= min_similarity)
589: 
590:             result = await session.execute(query)
591:             return [(UUID(row.id), row.similarity) for row in result.all()]
592: 
593:     # =========================================================================
594:     # Utility operations
595:     # =========================================================================
596: 
597:     async def count_chunks(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
598:         """Count total chunks in a namespace."""
599:         async with self._get_session() as session:
600:             result = await session.execute(
601:                 select(func.count(ChunkModel.id)).where(ChunkModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id))
602:             )
603:             return result.scalar_one()
604: 
605:     async def list_chunks(
606:         self,
607:         namespace_id: UUID,
608:         *,
609:         limit: int = 1000,
610:         offset: int = 0,
611:     ) -> list[Chunk]:
612:         """List chunks in a namespace.
613: 
614:         Args:
615:             namespace_id: Namespace ID
616:             limit: Maximum chunks to return
617:             offset: Offset for pagination
618: 
619:         Returns:
620:             List of chunks
621:         """
622:         async with self._get_session() as session:
623:             result = await session.execute(
624:                 select(ChunkModel)
625:                 .where(ChunkModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id))
626:                 .order_by(ChunkModel.created_at.desc())
627:                 .limit(limit)
628:                 .offset(offset)
629:             )
630:             rows = result.scalars().all()
631:             return [self._chunk_model_to_domain(row) for row in rows]
632: 
633:     async def get_embedding_stats(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> dict:
634:         """Get statistics about embeddings in a namespace."""
635:         async with self._get_session() as session:
636:             # Count chunks with embeddings
637:             chunk_count = await session.execute(
638:                 select(func.count(ChunkModel.id)).where(
639:                     ChunkModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id),
640:                     ChunkModel.embedding.is_not(None),
641:                 )
642:             )
643:             # Count entities with embeddings
644:             entity_count = await session.execute(
645:                 select(func.count(EntityModel.id)).where(
646:                     EntityModel.namespace_id == str(namespace_id),
647:                     EntityModel.embedding.is_not(None),
648:                 )
649:             )
650: 
651:             return {
652:                 "chunk_embeddings": chunk_count.scalar_one(),
653:                 "entity_embeddings": entity_count.scalar_one(),
654:             }
````

## File: src/khora/storage/coordinator.py
````python
  1: """Storage coordinator that orchestrates all backends.
  2: 
  3: The coordinator provides a unified interface to all storage backends
  4: (PostgreSQL, pgvector, Neo4j) and handles cross-cutting concerns like
  5: transaction coordination and consistency.
  6: """
  7: 
  8: from __future__ import annotations
  9: 
 10: import asyncio
 11: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 12: from datetime import datetime
 13: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 14: from uuid import UUID
 15: 
 16: from loguru import logger
 17: 
 18: from khora.core.models import (
 19:     Chunk,
 20:     Document,
 21:     Entity,
 22:     Episode,
 23:     MemoryEvent,
 24:     MemoryNamespace,
 25:     Organization,
 26:     Relationship,
 27:     Workspace,
 28: )
 29: 
 30: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 31:     from .backends.base import (
 32:         EventStoreProtocol,
 33:         GraphBackendProtocol,
 34:         RelationalBackendProtocol,
 35:         VectorBackendProtocol,
 36:     )
 37: 
 38: 
 39: @dataclass
 40: class StorageHealth:
 41:     """Health status of all storage backends."""
 42: 
 43:     relational: bool = False
 44:     vector: bool = False
 45:     graph: bool = False
 46:     event_store: bool = False
 47: 
 48:     @property
 49:     def is_healthy(self) -> bool:
 50:         """Check if all backends are healthy."""
 51:         return self.relational and self.vector
 52: 
 53:     @property
 54:     def summary(self) -> dict[str, bool]:
 55:         """Get health summary as a dictionary."""
 56:         return {
 57:             "relational": self.relational,
 58:             "vector": self.vector,
 59:             "graph": self.graph,
 60:             "event_store": self.event_store,
 61:         }
 62: 
 63: 
 64: @dataclass
 65: class StorageCoordinator:
 66:     """Coordinates operations across all storage backends.
 67: 
 68:     Provides a unified interface for storage operations and handles
 69:     cross-cutting concerns like transaction management and consistency.
 70:     """
 71: 
 72:     relational: RelationalBackendProtocol | None = None
 73:     vector: VectorBackendProtocol | None = None
 74:     graph: GraphBackendProtocol | None = None
 75:     event_store: EventStoreProtocol | None = None
 76: 
 77:     _connected: bool = field(default=False, init=False)
 78: 
 79:     async def connect(self) -> None:
 80:         """Connect all configured backends in parallel.
 81: 
 82:         All backend connections are initiated concurrently using asyncio.gather
 83:         for faster startup when multiple backends are configured.
 84:         """
 85:         if self._connected:
 86:             return
 87: 
 88:         logger.info("Connecting storage backends...")
 89: 
 90:         # Build list of connection tasks to run in parallel
 91:         tasks = []
 92:         if self.relational:
 93:             tasks.append(self.relational.connect())
 94:         if self.vector:
 95:             tasks.append(self.vector.connect())
 96:         if self.graph:
 97:             tasks.append(self.graph.connect())
 98:         if self.event_store:
 99:             tasks.append(self.event_store.connect())
100: 
101:         # Connect all backends concurrently
102:         if tasks:
103:             await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
104: 
105:         self._connected = True
106:         logger.info("All storage backends connected")
107: 
108:     async def disconnect(self) -> None:
109:         """Disconnect all backends in parallel.
110: 
111:         All backend disconnections are initiated concurrently for faster shutdown.
112:         """
113:         if not self._connected:
114:             return
115: 
116:         logger.info("Disconnecting storage backends...")
117: 
118:         # Build list of disconnection tasks to run in parallel
119:         tasks = []
120:         if self.event_store:
121:             tasks.append(self.event_store.disconnect())
122:         if self.graph:
123:             tasks.append(self.graph.disconnect())
124:         if self.vector:
125:             tasks.append(self.vector.disconnect())
126:         if self.relational:
127:             tasks.append(self.relational.disconnect())
128: 
129:         # Disconnect all backends concurrently
130:         if tasks:
131:             await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
132: 
133:         self._connected = False
134:         logger.info("All storage backends disconnected")
135: 
136:     async def health_check(self) -> StorageHealth:
137:         """Check health of all backends (parallel)."""
138:         health = StorageHealth()
139: 
140:         # Build list of health check coroutines to run in parallel
141:         checks: list[tuple[str, Any]] = []
142:         if self.relational:
143:             checks.append(("relational", self.relational.is_healthy()))
144:         if self.vector:
145:             checks.append(("vector", self.vector.is_healthy()))
146:         if self.graph:
147:             checks.append(("graph", self.graph.is_healthy()))
148:         if self.event_store:
149:             checks.append(("event_store", self.event_store.is_healthy()))
150: 
151:         if checks:
152:             results = await asyncio.gather(*[coro for _, coro in checks], return_exceptions=True)
153:             for (name, _), result in zip(checks, results):
154:                 # Treat exceptions as unhealthy
155:                 setattr(health, name, result is True)
156: 
157:         return health
158:

160:     # Tenancy operations (delegated to relational)

166:             raise RuntimeError("Relational backend not configured")
167:         return await self.relational.create_organization(org)

229:     async def create_namespace_version(
230:         self,
231:         workspace_id: UUID,
232:         slug: str,
233:         *,
234:         previous_version: MemoryNamespace | None = None,
235:     ) -> MemoryNamespace:
236:         """Create a new version of a namespace.
237: 
238:         If previous_version is provided, increments its version number and links to it.
239:         The previous version is marked as inactive.
240: 
241:         Args:
242:             workspace_id: Workspace ID
243:             slug: Namespace slug
244:             previous_version: The previous version to supersede (if any)
245: 
246:         Returns:
247:             New namespace version
248:         """
249:         if not self.relational:
250:             raise RuntimeError("Relational backend not configured")
251:         return await self.relational.create_namespace_version(workspace_id, slug, previous_version=previous_version)
252: 
253:     async def deactivate_namespace(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> None:

257:             namespace_id: ID of the namespace to deactivate
258:         """
259:         if not self.relational:
260:             raise RuntimeError("Relational backend not configured")

263:     # =========================================================================
264:     # Document operations (delegated to relational)
265:     # =========================================================================
266: 
267:     async def create_document(self, document: Document) -> Document:
268:         """Create a new document."""
269:         if not self.relational:
270:             raise RuntimeError("Relational backend not configured")
271:         import time as _time
272: 
273:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
274:         result = await self.relational.create_document(document)
275:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
276: 
277:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
278:             backend="postgresql",
279:             operation="create_document",
280:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
281:             record_count=1,
282:             namespace_id=document.namespace_id,
283:         )
284:         return result
285:

288:         if not self.relational:
289:             raise RuntimeError("Relational backend not configured")

291:

293:         self,
294:         namespace_id: UUID,
295:         *,
296:         status: str | None = None,
297:         limit: int = 100,
298:         offset: int = 0,
299:     ) -> list[Document]:

301:         if not self.relational:
302:             raise RuntimeError("Relational backend not configured")
303:         return await self.relational.list_documents(namespace_id, status=status, limit=limit, offset=offset)
304:

311:     async def delete_document(self, document_id: UUID) -> bool:
312:         """Delete a document and its chunks."""
313:         if not self.relational:
314:             raise RuntimeError("Relational backend not configured")
315: 
316:         # Delete chunks first
317:         if self.vector:

319: 
320:         return await self.relational.delete_document(document_id)
321:

327: 
328:     async def get_documents_by_checksums(self, namespace_id: UUID, checksums: list[str]) -> dict[str, Document]:
329:         """Fetch documents by content checksums in a single query.
330: 
331:         Used for batch deduplication to avoid N serial DB queries.
332: 
333:         Args:
334:             namespace_id: Namespace to search in
335:             checksums: List of content checksums to look up
336: 
337:         Returns:

339:         """

344:     # =========================================================================
345:     # Chunk operations (delegated to vector)

347: 
348:     async def create_chunk(self, chunk: Chunk) -> Chunk:
349:         """Create a new chunk with embedding."""

351:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")

354:     async def create_chunks_batch(self, chunks: list[Chunk]) -> list[Chunk]:
355:         """Create multiple chunks in a batch."""
356:         if not self.vector:
357:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")
358:         import time as _time
359: 
360:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
361:         result = await self.vector.create_chunks_batch(chunks)
362:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
363: 
364:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
365:             backend="pgvector",
366:             operation="create_chunks_batch",
367:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
368:             record_count=len(chunks),
369:             namespace_id=chunks[0].namespace_id if chunks else None,
370:         )
371:         return result

384: 
385:     async def get_chunks_batch(self, chunk_ids: list[UUID]) -> dict[UUID, Chunk]:
386:         """Fetch multiple chunks by ID in a single query.
387: 
388:         Args:
389:             chunk_ids: List of chunk IDs to fetch
390: 
391:         Returns:
392:             Dictionary mapping chunk ID to Chunk (only for existing chunks)
393:         """
394:         if not chunk_ids:
395:             return {}
396:         if not self.vector:
397:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")
398:         return await self.vector.get_chunks_batch(chunk_ids)
399: 
400:     async def search_similar_chunks(
401:         self,
402:         namespace_id: UUID,
403:         query_embedding: list[float],
404:         *,
405:         limit: int = 10,
406:         min_similarity: float = 0.0,
407:         filter_document_ids: list[UUID] | None = None,
408:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
409:         """Search for similar chunks."""
410:         if not self.vector:
411:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")
412:         import time as _time
413: 
414:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
415:         result = await self.vector.search_similar(
416:             namespace_id,
417:             query_embedding,
418:             limit=limit,
419:             min_similarity=min_similarity,
420:             filter_document_ids=filter_document_ids,
421:         )
422:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
423: 
424:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
425:             backend="pgvector",
426:             operation="search_similar_chunks",
427:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
428:             record_count=len(result),
429:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
430:         )
431:         return result
432: 
433:     async def search_fulltext_chunks(
434:         self,
435:         namespace_id: UUID,
436:         query_text: str,
437:         *,
438:         limit: int = 10,
439:         language: str = "english",
440:     ) -> list[tuple[Chunk, float]]:
441:         """Search chunks using PostgreSQL full-text search."""
442:         if not self.vector:
443:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")
444:         import time as _time
445: 
446:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
447:         result = await self.vector.search_fulltext(
448:             namespace_id,
449:             query_text,
450:             limit=limit,
451:             language=language,
452:         )
453:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
454: 
455:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
456:             backend="pgvector",
457:             operation="search_fulltext_chunks",
458:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
459:             record_count=len(result),
460:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
461:         )
462:         return result
463: 
464:     async def count_chunks(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
465:         """Count chunks in a namespace."""
466:         if not self.vector:
467:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")
468:         return await self.vector.count_chunks(namespace_id)
469: 
470:     async def list_chunks(
471:         self,
472:         namespace_id: UUID,
473:         *,
474:         limit: int = 1000,
475:         offset: int = 0,
476:     ) -> list[Chunk]:
477:         """List chunks in a namespace.
478: 
479:         Args:
480:             namespace_id: Namespace ID
481:             limit: Maximum chunks to return
482:             offset: Offset for pagination
483: 
484:         Returns:
485:             List of chunks
486:         """
487:         if not self.vector:
488:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")
489:         return await self.vector.list_chunks(namespace_id, limit=limit, offset=offset)
490: 
491:     async def count_entities(self, namespace_id: UUID) -> int:
492:         """Count entities in a namespace."""
493:         if self.graph:
494:             return await self.graph.count_entities(namespace_id)
495:         return 0
496: 
497:     # =========================================================================
498:     # Entity operations (cross-backend)
499:     # =========================================================================
500: 
501:     async def create_entity(self, entity: Entity) -> Entity:
502:         """Create an entity in both graph and vector stores (parallel)."""
503:         import time as _time
504: 
505:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
506:         # Parallel writes to graph + vector, matching update_entity pattern
507:         if self.graph and self.vector:
508:             graph_result, _ = await asyncio.gather(
509:                 self.graph.create_entity(entity),
510:                 self.vector.create_entity(entity),
511:             )
512:             entity = graph_result
513:         elif self.graph:
514:             entity = await self.graph.create_entity(entity)
515:         elif self.vector:
516:             await self.vector.create_entity(entity)
517:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
518: 
519:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
520:             backend="graph+vector",
521:             operation="create_entity",
522:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
523:             record_count=1,
524:             namespace_id=entity.namespace_id,
525:         )
526:         return entity
527: 
528:     async def get_entity(self, entity_id: UUID) -> Entity | None:
529:         """Get an entity by ID."""
530:         if self.graph:
531:             return await self.graph.get_entity(entity_id)
532:         return None
533: 
534:     async def get_entity_by_name(self, namespace_id: UUID, name: str, entity_type: str) -> Entity | None:
535:         """Get an entity by name and type."""
536:         if self.graph:
537:             return await self.graph.get_entity_by_name(namespace_id, name, entity_type)
538:         return None
539: 
540:     async def update_entity(self, entity: Entity) -> Entity:
541:         """Update an entity in both graph and vector stores (parallel)."""
542:         if self.graph and self.vector:
543:             graph_result, _ = await asyncio.gather(
544:                 self.graph.update_entity(entity),
545:                 self.vector.update_entity(entity),
546:             )
547:             return graph_result
548:         if self.graph:
549:             return await self.graph.update_entity(entity)
550:         if self.vector:
551:             await self.vector.update_entity(entity)
552:         return entity
553: 
554:     async def delete_entity(self, entity_id: UUID) -> bool:
555:         """Delete an entity."""
556:         if self.graph:
557:             return await self.graph.delete_entity(entity_id)
558:         return False
559: 
560:     async def list_entities(
561:         self,
562:         namespace_id: UUID,
563:         *,
564:         entity_type: str | None = None,
565:         limit: int = 100,
566:         offset: int = 0,
567:     ) -> list[Entity]:
568:         """List entities in a namespace."""
569:         if self.graph:
570:             return await self.graph.list_entities(namespace_id, entity_type=entity_type, limit=limit, offset=offset)
571:         return []
572: 
573:     async def update_entity_embedding(self, entity_id: UUID, embedding: list[float], model: str) -> None:
574:         """Update the embedding for an entity."""
575:         if self.vector:
576:             await self.vector.update_entity_embedding(entity_id, embedding, model)
577: 
578:     async def update_entity_embeddings_batch(self, updates: list[tuple[UUID, list[float], str]]) -> int:
579:         """Update embeddings for multiple entities in a single transaction."""
580:         if self.vector and hasattr(self.vector, "update_entity_embeddings_batch"):
581:             return await self.vector.update_entity_embeddings_batch(updates)
582:         # Fallback to individual updates
583:         if self.vector:
584:             for entity_id, embedding, model in updates:
585:                 await self.vector.update_entity_embedding(entity_id, embedding, model)
586:             return len(updates)
587:         return 0
588: 
589:     async def search_similar_entities(
590:         self,
591:         namespace_id: UUID,
592:         query_embedding: list[float],
593:         *,
594:         limit: int = 10,
595:         min_similarity: float = 0.0,
596:     ) -> list[tuple[UUID, float]]:
597:         """Search for similar entities."""
598:         if not self.vector:
599:             raise RuntimeError("Vector backend not configured")
600:         import time as _time
601: 
602:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
603:         result = await self.vector.search_similar_entities(
604:             namespace_id,
605:             query_embedding,
606:             limit=limit,
607:             min_similarity=min_similarity,
608:         )
609:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
610: 
611:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
612:             backend="pgvector",
613:             operation="search_similar_entities",
614:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
615:             record_count=len(result),
616:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
617:         )
618:         return result
619: 
620:     async def upsert_entities_batch(
621:         self,
622:         namespace_id: UUID,
623:         entities: list[Entity],
624:         *,
625:         batch_size: int = 200,
626:     ) -> list[tuple[Entity, bool]]:
627:         """Batch upsert entities across graph and vector backends.
628: 
629:         Uses MERGE semantics: creates new entities, updates existing ones
630:         matched by (namespace_id, name, entity_type).
631: 
632:         Returns list of (entity, is_new) tuples.
633:         """
634:         if not entities:
635:             return []
636: 
637:         import time as _time
638: 
639:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
640: 
641:         results: list[tuple[Entity, bool]] = []
642: 
643:         # Upsert in graph and vector backends in parallel
644:         has_graph = self.graph and hasattr(self.graph, "upsert_entities_batch")
645:         has_vector = self.vector and hasattr(self.vector, "upsert_entities_batch")
646:         logger.debug(
647:             f"upsert_entities_batch: {len(entities)} entities, " f"has_graph={has_graph}, has_vector={has_vector}"
648:         )
649: 
650:         if has_graph and has_vector:
651:             graph_results, _ = await asyncio.gather(
652:                 self.graph.upsert_entities_batch(namespace_id, entities, batch_size=batch_size),
653:                 self.vector.upsert_entities_batch(namespace_id, entities, batch_size=batch_size),
654:             )
655:             results = graph_results
656:         elif has_graph:
657:             results = await self.graph.upsert_entities_batch(namespace_id, entities, batch_size=batch_size)
658:         elif has_vector:
659:             results = await self.vector.upsert_entities_batch(namespace_id, entities, batch_size=batch_size)
660: 
661:         # Fallback: if no backend returned results, create synthetic results
662:         # to ensure callers always get one result per input entity
663:         if not results:
664:             logger.debug(f"upsert_entities_batch: using fallback synthetic results for {len(entities)} entities")
665:             results = [(entity, True) for entity in entities]
666: 
667:         logger.debug(f"upsert_entities_batch: returning {len(results)} results for {len(entities)} input entities")
668: 
669:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
670: 
671:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
672:             backend="graph+vector",
673:             operation="upsert_entities_batch",
674:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
675:             record_count=len(entities),
676:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
677:         )
678:         return results
679: 
680:     async def create_relationships_batch(
681:         self,
682:         relationships: list[Relationship],
683:         *,
684:         batch_size: int = 50,
685:     ) -> int:
686:         """Batch create relationships in the graph backend.
687: 
688:         Returns the number of relationships created.
689:         """
690:         if not relationships:
691:             return 0
692: 
693:         import time as _time
694: 
695:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
696: 
697:         count = 0
698:         if self.graph and hasattr(self.graph, "create_relationships_batch"):
699:             count = await self.graph.create_relationships_batch(relationships, batch_size=batch_size)
700: 
701:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
702: 
703:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
704:             backend="graph",
705:             operation="create_relationships_batch",
706:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
707:             record_count=count,
708:             namespace_id=relationships[0].namespace_id if relationships else None,
709:         )
710:         return count
711: 
712:     # =========================================================================
713:     # Relationship operations (delegated to graph)
714:     # =========================================================================
715: 
716:     async def create_relationship(self, relationship: Relationship) -> Relationship:
717:         """Create a relationship between entities."""
718:         if not self.graph:
719:             raise RuntimeError("Graph backend not configured")
720:         import time as _time
721: 
722:         _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
723:         result = await self.graph.create_relationship(relationship)
724:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
725: 
726:         get_collector().record_storage_op(
727:             backend="graph",
728:             operation="create_relationship",
729:             latency_ms=(_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000,
730:             record_count=1,
731:             namespace_id=relationship.namespace_id,
732:         )
733:         return result
734: 
735:     async def get_relationship(self, relationship_id: UUID) -> Relationship | None:
736:         """Get a relationship by ID."""
737:         if self.graph:
738:             return await self.graph.get_relationship(relationship_id)
739:         return None
740: 
741:     async def delete_relationship(self, relationship_id: UUID) -> bool:
742:         """Delete a relationship."""
743:         if self.graph:
744:             return await self.graph.delete_relationship(relationship_id)
745:         return False
746: 
747:     async def get_entity_relationships(
748:         self,
749:         entity_id: UUID,
750:         *,
751:         direction: str = "both",
752:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
753:         limit: int = 100,
754:     ) -> list[Relationship]:
755:         """Get relationships for an entity."""
756:         if self.graph:
757:             return await self.graph.get_entity_relationships(
758:                 entity_id, direction=direction, relationship_types=relationship_types, limit=limit
759:             )
760:         return []
761: 
762:     async def list_relationships(
763:         self,
764:         namespace_id: UUID,
765:         *,
766:         relationship_type: str | None = None,
767:         limit: int = 1000,
768:         offset: int = 0,
769:     ) -> list[Relationship]:
770:         """List all relationships in a namespace."""
771:         if self.graph:
772:             return await self.graph.list_relationships(
773:                 namespace_id, relationship_type=relationship_type, limit=limit, offset=offset
774:             )
775:         return []
776: 
777:     # =========================================================================
778:     # Episode operations (delegated to graph)
779:     # =========================================================================
780: 
781:     async def create_episode(self, episode: Episode) -> Episode:
782:         """Create an episode."""
783:         if not self.graph:
784:             raise RuntimeError("Graph backend not configured")
785:         return await self.graph.create_episode(episode)
786: 
787:     async def get_episode(self, episode_id: UUID) -> Episode | None:
788:         """Get an episode by ID."""
789:         if self.graph:
790:             return await self.graph.get_episode(episode_id)
791:         return None
792: 
793:     async def list_episodes(
794:         self,
795:         namespace_id: UUID,
796:         *,
797:         start_time: datetime | None = None,
798:         end_time: datetime | None = None,
799:         limit: int = 100,
800:     ) -> list[Episode]:
801:         """List episodes in a time range."""
802:         if self.graph:
803:             return await self.graph.list_episodes(namespace_id, start_time=start_time, end_time=end_time, limit=limit)
804:         return []
805: 
806:     # =========================================================================
807:     # Graph traversal (delegated to graph)
808:     # =========================================================================
809: 
810:     async def find_paths(
811:         self,
812:         namespace_id: UUID,
813:         source_entity_id: UUID,
814:         target_entity_id: UUID,
815:         *,
816:         max_depth: int = 3,
817:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
818:     ) -> list[list[dict[str, Any]]]:
819:         """Find paths between two entities."""
820:         if self.graph:
821:             return await self.graph.find_paths(
822:                 namespace_id,
823:                 source_entity_id,
824:                 target_entity_id,
825:                 max_depth=max_depth,
826:                 relationship_types=relationship_types,
827:             )
828:         return []
829: 
830:     async def get_neighborhood(
831:         self,
832:         entity_id: UUID,
833:         *,
834:         depth: int = 1,
835:         relationship_types: list[str] | None = None,
836:         limit: int = 50,
837:     ) -> dict[str, Any]:
838:         """Get the neighborhood of an entity."""
839:         if self.graph:
840:             return await self.graph.get_neighborhood(
841:                 entity_id, depth=depth, relationship_types=relationship_types, limit=limit
842:             )
843:         return {"entities": [], "relationships": []}
844: 
845:     # =========================================================================
846:     # Batch operations (optimized for parallel fetching)
847:     # =========================================================================
848: 
849:     async def get_entities_batch(self, entity_ids: list[UUID]) -> dict[UUID, Entity]:
850:         """Fetch multiple entities in a single query.
851: 
852:         Args:
853:             entity_ids: List of entity IDs to fetch
854: 
855:         Returns:
856:             Dictionary mapping entity ID to Entity object
857:         """
858:         if not entity_ids:
859:             return {}
860:         if self.graph:
861:             return await self.graph.get_entities_batch(entity_ids)
862:         return {}
863: 
864:     async def get_documents_batch(self, document_ids: list[UUID]) -> dict[UUID, Document]:
865:         """Fetch multiple documents in a single query.
866: 
867:         Args:
868:             document_ids: List of document IDs to fetch
869: 
870:         Returns:
871:             Dictionary mapping document ID to Document object
872:         """
````
